# =============================================================================
# CITADEL NEXUS PRO — CAPABILITY INDEX + TABLE OF CONTENTS
# =============================================================================
# Purpose:
#   This module is intentionally monolithic. This header is the navigation + ownership map.
#   The “Intelligence Value” (IV) indicates whether a subsystem is:
#     IV=0  Infrastructure/UI (no decision value)
#     IV=1  Instrumentation / reporting (observability only)
#     IV=2  Decision support (informs choices, still mostly manual)
#     IV=3  Adaptive control / learning (changes behavior automatically)
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PUBLIC ENTRYPOINTS (Call These)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Agent Core:
#   - NexusTamagotchiAgentPro(...)                          # main agent wiring
#
# Reflex / Autonomy:
#   - CAPSReflexExecutor.execute_reflex(reflex, context)     # executes a reflex with audit + quarantine semantics
#   - CAPSReflexExecutor.run_reflex_loop_once(event, ctx)    # single-step reflex loop (pass ctx!)
#
# Memory / Context:
#   - (Mira) MiraMemory* stores + RetentionGate + LearningAlgorithm
#   - ContextRehydrationSystem.rehydrate(query, ...)         # produces context pack
#
# Skills / Economy:
#   - SkillTreeSystem.unlock_skill(...)                      # progression gating
#   - OutcomeWeightedXP / GamificationEngine award paths
#
# Observability:
#   - UnifiedMonitoringBridge.record_interaction(...)
#   - UnifiedMonitoringBridge.record_xp_event(...)
#
# Validation:
#   - SelfAssessmentDiagnostic.run_all()                     # must prove loops are “real”
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RUNTIME LOOPS (Where “Real” vs “Theater” Is Determined)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Loop A — Interaction Loop (should exist explicitly in your runtime):
#   ingest(user) -> retrieve(context) -> decide(route) -> act(integrations/reflex) -> log -> learn -> tune
#
# Loop B — Reflex Loop:
#   event -> gate(authority/health) -> execute_reflex -> ledger -> (optional) structural learner -> patch policy
#
# Loop C — Memory Loop (Mira):
#   ingest(STM) -> retention(decide) -> consolidate/compress/forget -> learn(trust/importance updates) -> persist
#
# Loop D — Economy Loop:
#   outcome signals -> XP/TP award (anti-farm + caps + sinks) -> skill unlock -> runtime tuning changes
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SKILL TRACKER (Section → Skill Type Correlation)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Skill types (use these tags consistently):
#   SKILL_MEMORY        # memory, recall, consolidation, context packs
#   SKILL_COGNITION      # reasoning, routing, NLU, response pipeline
#   SKILL_AUTONOMY       # reflex, self-heal, governance gating, parameter tuning
#   SKILL_GOVERNANCE     # council, policy enforcement, audit/ledger, auth tiers
#   SKILL_ECONOMY        # XP/TP, quests, missions, rewards, anti-farm
#   SKILL_INTEGRATION    # OAD bridges, Slack/Discord/Stripe/Notion/Linear/Supabase etc
#   SKILL_OBSERVABILITY  # Datadog/PostHog, instrumentation, diagnostics, dashboards
#   SKILL_UI             # panels, chat server, onboarding UX
#   SKILL_TESTING        # self-assessment, validation, regressions
#   SKILL_META           # evolution, goals, meta-orchestration
#
# Mapping (section ranges are authoritative; add specific skill IDs under each):
#   SKILL_ECONOMY:       [6, 10B, 11, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 21.5]
#   SKILL_GOVERNANCE:    [2, 2B, 7, 12, 24, 24.1-24.6, 45(CAPS ledger/reflex), 114]
#   SKILL_INTEGRATION:   [1.6, 10, 10A, 21, 27, 40, 44, 77-95, 102]
#   SKILL_OBSERVABILITY: [5, 28, 79-81, 94, 104, 115, 117, 119]
#   SKILL_MEMORY:        [4, 25.1-25.8, 49-60(Mira), 101, 111.2-111.3, 113.1]
#   SKILL_AUTONOMY:      [13, 45(CAPS reflex), 96-100, 105, 107, 113.2]
#   SKILL_COGNITION:     [8, 9, 29-33, 42, 43, 46, 61-75(AGS), 116]
#   SKILL_UI:            [26, 26.2, 106, 111, 112, 113.3, 113.4]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

#   - SelfAssessment must verify:
#       * ICP gate invoked for outbound actions
#       * ICP decision logged (ledger + telemetry)
#
# RLS (Row Level Security) Policies
#   - Location in file: SECTION <RLS_SECTION_ID>
#   - Targets: Supabase schemas/tables touched by agent (users, guilds, skills, ledger, telemetry, tasks)
#   - SelfAssessment must verify:
#       * RLS enabled on required tables
#       * expected roles cannot read/write forbidden rows
#       * service role paths are explicit + audited

        refine_ai_thoughts,

        refine_ai_decisions,
    )
    NEXUS_CORTEX_AVAILABLE = True
except ImportError:
    NEXUS_CORTEX_AVAILABLE = False
    fingerprint_to_vector = None
    store_fingerprint_as_vector = None
    categorize_fingerprint = None
    store_fingerprint_as_nexuscortex = None
    update_fingerprint_self_score = None
    reclassify_fingerprint = None
    detect_repeated_fallacies = None
    discover_fallacy_patterns = None
    log_discovery = None
    log_success = None
    store_ai_outcome_fingerprint = None
    retrieve_ai_outcome = None
    scan_all_fingerprints = None
    refine_ai_knowledge = None
    refine_ai_thoughts = None
    generate_ai_memory_links = None
    refine_ai_decisions = None

# OAD Token key mappings
OAD_TOKEN_KEYS = {
    "slack": ["SLACK_BOT_TOKEN", "OAD_SLACK_TOKEN"],
    "notion": ["NOTION_TOKEN", "OAD_NOTION_KEY"],
    "gitlab": ["GITLAB_TOKEN", "OAD_PAT", "CITADEL_HELPER_PAT"],
    "discord": ["DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN", "DISCORD_TOKEN"],
    "supabase_url": ["SUPABASE_URL"],
    "supabase_key": ["SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY", "SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY"],
    "datadog": ["DD_API_KEY"],
    "posthog": ["POSTHOG_API_KEY"],
    "stripe": ["STRIPE_SECRET_KEY"],
    "linear": ["LINEAR_API_KEY", "LINEAR_API_TOKEN"],
    "github": ["GITHUB_TOKEN", "GH_TOKEN"],
    "openai": ["OPENAI_API_KEY"],
    "anthropic": ["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"],
    "intercom": ["INTERCOM_ACCESS_TOKEN"],
}


def _get_oad_token(*keys: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """Get first available token from environment."""
    for key in keys:
        val = os.getenv(key)
        if val and val.strip():
            return val.strip()
    return None


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PORT MANAGEMENT - Global registry for panel server ports
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Prevents port conflicts when running multiple panels simultaneously

_PANEL_PORT_REGISTRY: Dict[str, int] = {}  # panel_name -> port
_PANEL_PORT_LOCK = threading.Lock()


def _find_available_port(base_port: int, panel_name: str, max_attempts: int = 10) -> int:
    """
    Find an available port starting from base_port.

    Checks if the port is already in use by another panel or by the OS,
    and returns the first available port.

    Args:
        base_port: The preferred starting port
        panel_name: Name of the panel requesting the port
        max_attempts: Maximum ports to try before giving up

    Returns:
        An available port number
    """
    import socket

    with _PANEL_PORT_LOCK:
        # Check if this panel already has a registered port
        if panel_name in _PANEL_PORT_REGISTRY:
            return _PANEL_PORT_REGISTRY[panel_name]

        port = base_port
        for attempt in range(max_attempts):
            # Check if port is in registry
            if port in _PANEL_PORT_REGISTRY.values():
                port += 2  # Skip by 2 (HTTP + WebSocket pairs)
                continue

            # Check if port is available on the OS
            try:
                with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as sock:
                    sock.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
                    sock.bind(('0.0.0.0', port))
                    # Also check WebSocket port (port - 1)
                    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as ws_sock:
                        ws_sock.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
                        ws_sock.bind(('0.0.0.0', port - 1))
                        # Both ports available
                        _PANEL_PORT_REGISTRY[panel_name] = port
                        return port
            except OSError:
                port += 2  # Skip by 2 to maintain HTTP/WS pair alignment
                continue

        # Fallback: return requested port and let it fail naturally with better error
        logging.getLogger("PortManager").warning(
            f"Could not find available port after {max_attempts} attempts starting from {base_port}"
        )
        _PANEL_PORT_REGISTRY[panel_name] = base_port
        return base_port


def _release_panel_port(panel_name: str) -> None:
    """Release a panel's port registration."""
    with _PANEL_PORT_LOCK:
        _PANEL_PORT_REGISTRY.pop(panel_name, None)


def _get_panel_port_info() -> Dict[str, int]:
    """Get current port allocations."""
    with _PANEL_PORT_LOCK:
        return dict(_PANEL_PORT_REGISTRY)


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 1.5: MCP PROGRESSION SHEET (Agent Build-Out Tracker)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Uses MCP (Model Context Protocol) patterns for tracking agent capability
# expansion, tool registry, and cognitive progression visualization.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


@dataclass
class ProgressionCapability:
    """
    Capability definition for MCP progression tracking.

    # BEGIN META: ProgressionCapability
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define agent capabilities with completion state and dependencies
    # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001", "SRS-GAMIFY-003"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_CAPABILITY_TRACK
    # Tags: ["mcp", "progression", "capability"]
    # Categories: ["Progression", "MCP"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["Agent Architecture"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Tracks individual capabilities that can be built out
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    name: str
    description: str
    category: str
    status: str = "planned"  # planned | in_progress | complete | verified
    completion_percentage: float = 0.0
    dependencies: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    srs_codes: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    loc_estimate: int = 0
    loc_actual: int = 0


@dataclass
class MCPToolRegistryEntry:
    """
    Tool registry entry for MCP integration.

    # BEGIN META: MCPToolRegistryEntry
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Register agent tools in MCP-compatible format
    # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_TOOL_REGISTER
    # Tags: ["mcp", "tools", "registry"]
    # Categories: ["MCP", "Tools"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["Agent Architecture"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Individual tool registration for MCP server compatibility
    # END META
    """
    tool_id: str
    name: str
    description: str
    input_schema: Dict[str, Any]
    handler_method: str
    category: str
    authority_tier: str = "OBSERVE"
    xp_on_use: int = 5


class MCPProgressionSheet:
    """
    MCP-style Progression Sheet for tracking agent build-out.

    # BEGIN META: MCPProgressionSheet
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB

    # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001", "SRS-GAMIFY-003", "SRS-AUDIT-001"]

        # === GAMIFICATION ===

            loc_estimate=300, loc_actual=280

            loc_estimate=200, loc_actual=175
        ),
        "leaderboard": ProgressionCapability(
            id="leaderboard", name="Leaderboard System",
            description="Global and guild rankings with Supabase sync",
            category="gamification", status="complete", completion_percentage=100.0,
            dependencies=["xp_system"], srs_codes=["SRS-GAMIFY-002"],
            loc_estimate=150, loc_actual=130
        ),

        # === GOVERNANCE ===
        "council_system": ProgressionCapability(

        "audit_trail": ProgressionCapability(

            dependencies=["multi_channel"], srs_codes=["SRS-INTEGRATE-001"],
            loc_estimate=200, loc_actual=0
        ),

        # === DISTRIBUTION (Phase 2) ===
        "preflight_system": ProgressionCapability(
            id="preflight_system", name="Preflight Validation",
            description="Multi-stage validation pipeline for on-prem deployment",
            category="distribution", status="in_progress", completion_percentage=20.0,
            srs_codes=["SRS-DEPLOY-001"], loc_estimate=400, loc_actual=0
        ),
        "distro_framework": ProgressionCapability(
            id="distro_framework", name="Distribution Framework",
            description="Install/run/service patterns for agent deployment",
            category="distribution", status="in_progress", completion_percentage=20.0,
            dependencies=["preflight_system"], srs_codes=["SRS-DEPLOY-002"],
            loc_estimate=300, loc_actual=0
        ),
        "agent_gif": ProgressionCapability(
            id="agent_gif", name="AGENT_GIF Visualization",
            description="Cognitive frame capture for progression visualization",
            category="distribution", status="planned", completion_percentage=0.0,
            dependencies=["core_loop"], srs_codes=["SRS-VIZ-001"],
            loc_estimate=200, loc_actual=0
        ),
    }

    # MCP Tool Registry
    TOOL_REGISTRY = [
        MCPToolRegistryEntry(
            tool_id="interact", name="interact",
            description="Process user interaction and generate AI response",
            input_schema={"type": "object", "properties": {"user_input": {"type": "string"}}},
            handler_method="interact", category="conversation",
            authority_tier="OBSERVE", xp_on_use=10
        ),
        MCPToolRegistryEntry(
            tool_id="recall", name="recall_memories",
            description="Retrieve relevant memories from vector storage",
            input_schema={"type": "object", "properties": {"query": {"type": "string"}, "k": {"type": "integer"}}},
            handler_method="recall_memories", category="memory",
            authority_tier="OBSERVE", xp_on_use=5
        ),
        MCPToolRegistryEntry(
            tool_id="status", name="get_status",
            description="Get agent vitals and Brotherhood stats",
            input_schema={"type": "object", "properties": {}},
            handler_method="get_status", category="status",
            authority_tier="OBSERVE", xp_on_use=2
        ),
        MCPToolRegistryEntry(
            tool_id="unlock_skill", name="unlock_skill",
            description="Unlock a skill from the skill tree",
            input_schema={"type": "object", "properties": {"skill_id": {"type": "string"}}},
            handler_method="unlock_skill", category="progression",
            authority_tier="ASSIST", xp_on_use=15
        ),
        MCPToolRegistryEntry(
            tool_id="complete_mission", name="complete_mission",
            description="Mark a mission as completed and claim rewards",
            input_schema={"type": "object", "properties": {"mission_id": {"type": "string"}}},
            handler_method="complete_mission", category="missions",
            authority_tier="EXECUTE", xp_on_use=20
        ),
        MCPToolRegistryEntry(
            tool_id="broadcast", name="broadcast_event",
            description="Broadcast an event to all configured channels",
            input_schema={"type": "object", "properties": {"event_type": {"type": "string"}, "data": {"type": "object"}}},
            handler_method="broadcast_event", category="integrations",
            authority_tier="EXECUTE", xp_on_use=15
        ),
        MCPToolRegistryEntry(
            tool_id="route_to_professor", name="route_to_professor",
            description="Route a question to a specialized professor",
            input_schema={"type": "object", "properties": {"domain": {"type": "string"}, "question": {"type": "string"}}},
            handler_method="route_to_professor", category="knowledge",
            authority_tier="ASSIST", xp_on_use=10
        ),
        MCPToolRegistryEntry(
            tool_id="generate_insight", name="generate_insight",
            description="Generate insights from interaction patterns",
            input_schema={"type": "object", "properties": {}},
            handler_method="generate_insight", category="analysis",
            authority_tier="EXECUTE", xp_on_use=25
        ),
    ]

    def __init__(self):
        """
        Initialize MCP Progression Sheet.

        # BEGIN META: __init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize progression tracker and AGENT_GIF if available
        # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "mcp"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.frames: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        self.agent_gif = None
        if AGENT_GIF_AVAILABLE and AgentGIFGenerator:
            try:
                # Try to initialize with expected config
                config = AgentGIFConfig(
                    agent_name="NexusAgent",
                    guild="CNWB",
                    output_dir=Path(__file__).parent / "frames"
                )
                self.agent_gif = AgentGIFGenerator(config)
            except (TypeError, AttributeError):
                # AgentGIF interface may have changed, silently skip
                self.agent_gif = None

    def get_overall_progress(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get overall build-out progress.

        # BEGIN META: get_overall_progress
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Calculate total capability completion percentage
        # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001", "SRS-GAMIFY-003"]
        # Tags: ["progress", "metrics"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        total_caps = len(self.CAPABILITIES)
        completed = sum(1 for c in self.CAPABILITIES.values() if c.status == "complete")
        in_progress = sum(1 for c in self.CAPABILITIES.values() if c.status == "in_progress")

        total_loc_estimate = sum(c.loc_estimate for c in self.CAPABILITIES.values())
        total_loc_actual = sum(c.loc_actual for c in self.CAPABILITIES.values())

        by_category = {}
        for cap in self.CAPABILITIES.values():
            if cap.category not in by_category:
                by_category[cap.category] = {"total": 0, "complete": 0}
            by_category[cap.category]["total"] += 1
            if cap.status == "complete":
                by_category[cap.category]["complete"] += 1

        return {
            "total_capabilities": total_caps,
            "completed": completed,
            "in_progress": in_progress,
            "planned": total_caps - completed - in_progress,
            "completion_percentage": (completed / total_caps * 100) if total_caps > 0 else 0,
            "loc_estimate": total_loc_estimate,
            "loc_actual": total_loc_actual,
            "loc_percentage": (total_loc_actual / total_loc_estimate * 100) if total_loc_estimate > 0 else 0,
            "by_category": by_category,
            "tools_registered": len(self.TOOL_REGISTRY),
        }

    def capture_cognitive_frame(
        self,
        thought: str,
        state: str = "active",
        archetype: str = "adaptive",
        criticality: str = "low"
    ) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Capture a cognitive frame for AGENT_GIF visualization.

        # BEGIN META: capture_cognitive_frame
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Capture agent cognitive state for progression visualization
        # SRS: ["SRS-VIZ-001"]
        # Reflex: REFLEX_FRAME_CAPTURE
        # Tags: ["agent_gif", "visualization", "cognitive"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        frame = {
            "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
            "thought": thought,
            "state": state,
            "archetype": archetype,
            "criticality": criticality,
            "progress": self.get_overall_progress()["completion_percentage"],
        }
        self.frames.append(frame)

        if self.agent_gif and AGENT_GIF_AVAILABLE:
            try:
                self.agent_gif.capture_frame(
                    thought=thought,
                    state=CognitiveState[state.upper()] if hasattr(CognitiveState, state.upper()) else CognitiveState.ACTIVE,
                    archetype=Archetype[archetype.upper()] if hasattr(Archetype, archetype.upper()) else Archetype.ADAPTIVE,
                    criticality=Criticality[criticality.upper()] if hasattr(Criticality, criticality.upper()) else Criticality.LOW,
                )
            except Exception:
                pass  # Silent fail if AGENT_GIF has issues

        return frame

    def export_mcp_manifest(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Export MCP-compatible manifest for tool registration.

        # BEGIN META: export_mcp_manifest
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Generate MCP server manifest for tool registration
        # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001"]
        # Tags: ["mcp", "manifest", "export"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        return {
            "name": "citadel-nexus-agent",
            "version": "4.0.0",
            "description": "Citadel Nexus Tamagotchi Agent with gamification",
            "capabilities": {
                "tools": True,
                "resources": False,
                "prompts": False,
            },
            "tools": [
                {
                    "name": entry.name,
                    "description": entry.description,
                    "inputSchema": entry.input_schema,
                }
                for entry in self.TOOL_REGISTRY
            ],
            "progression": self.get_overall_progress(),
        }


# Global progression sheet instance
PROGRESSION_SHEET = MCPProgressionSheet()


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 1.6: CITADEL INTEGRATION ROUTER
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# All integrations are routed through this central hub rather than direct calls.
# This enables consistent auth, logging, rate limiting, and error handling.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


class CitadelIntegrationRouter:
    """
    Central router for all external integrations.

    # BEGIN META: CitadelIntegrationRouter
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Route all integrations through Citadel hub for unified control
    # SRS: ["SRS-INTEGRATE-001", "SRS-NOTIFY-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_INTEGRATION_ROUTE
    # Enum: ENUM_INTEGRATION_ROUTER
    # Tags: ["integrations", "router", "citadel", "core"]
    # Categories: ["Integrations", "Core"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["Infrastructure"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-S
    # Supporting: ["OAD_TOKEN_KEYS", "_get_oad_token"]
    # Description: Central routing hub for all external service calls. Provides
    #              consistent authentication, logging, rate limiting, retries,
    #              and error handling across all integration points.
    # END META
    """

    # Supported integration endpoints
    ENDPOINTS = {
        "discord": {"type": "webhook", "rate_limit": 30, "retry_count": 3},
        "slack": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 50, "retry_count": 3},
        "notion": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 30, "retry_count": 2},
        "linear": {"type": "graphql", "rate_limit": 60, "retry_count": 2},
        "github": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 100, "retry_count": 2},
        "gitlab": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 60, "retry_count": 2},
        "supabase": {"type": "rest", "rate_limit": 100, "retry_count": 3},
        "posthog": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 100, "retry_count": 1},
        "datadog": {"type": "agent", "rate_limit": 200, "retry_count": 1},
        "stripe": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 50, "retry_count": 2},
        "intercom": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 30, "retry_count": 2},
        "openai": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 60, "retry_count": 3},
        "anthropic": {"type": "api", "rate_limit": 30, "retry_count": 3},
    }

    def __init__(self):
        """
        Initialize Citadel Integration Router.

        # BEGIN META: __init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize router with auth tokens and rate limit trackers
        # SRS: ["SRS-INTEGRATE-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "router"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.tokens: Dict[str, Optional[str]] = {}
        self.rate_limit_buckets: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.last_request_times: Dict[str, datetime] = {}
        self.request_counts: Dict[str, int] = {ep: 0 for ep in self.ENDPOINTS}
        self.error_counts: Dict[str, int] = {ep: 0 for ep in self.ENDPOINTS}
        self._load_tokens()
        # Policy enforcement hooks
        self._agent: Optional[Any] = None
        self._policy_matrix: Optional[Any] = None
        self._ledger: Optional[Any] = None

    def set_policy_context(
        self,
        agent: Optional[Any] = None,
        policy_matrix: Optional[Any] = None,
        ledger: Optional[Any] = None
    ) -> None:
        """Configure policy enforcement context for authorization gates."""
        self._agent = agent
        self._policy_matrix = policy_matrix or (getattr(agent, "policy_matrix", None) if agent else None)
        self._ledger = ledger or (getattr(agent, "ledger", None) if agent else None)

    def _load_tokens(self):
        """Load all available OAD tokens."""
        for service, keys in OAD_TOKEN_KEYS.items():
            self.tokens[service] = _get_oad_token(*keys)

    def is_available(self, service: str) -> bool:
        """
        Check if a service is available (has valid token).

        # BEGIN META: is_available
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Check service availability
        # SRS: ["SRS-INTEGRATE-001"]
        # Tags: ["availability", "check"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        return self.tokens.get(service) is not None

    def get_available_services(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get list of available services."""
        return [s for s in self.ENDPOINTS if self.is_available(s)]

    def _check_rate_limit(self, service: str) -> bool:
        """Check if rate limit allows request."""
        endpoint = self.ENDPOINTS.get(service, {})
        rate_limit = endpoint.get("rate_limit", 60)

        now = datetime.now()
        last_time = self.last_request_times.get(service)

        if last_time is None:
            return True

        # Simple sliding window: reset if more than 1 minute has passed
        if (now - last_time).total_seconds() > 60:
            self.rate_limit_buckets[service] = 0
            return True

        current_count = self.rate_limit_buckets.get(service, 0)
        return current_count < rate_limit

    async def route(
        self,
        service: str,
        action: str,
        payload: Dict[str, Any],
        priority: str = "normal"
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Route a request to an external service.

        # BEGIN META: route
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Route request through Citadel hub with rate limiting and retries
        # SRS: ["SRS-INTEGRATE-001", "SRS-NOTIFY-001"]
        # Reflex: REFLEX_ROUTE_REQUEST
        # Tags: ["routing", "request", "async"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        if not self.is_available(service):
            return {
                "success": False,
                "error": f"Service {service} not configured (missing token)",
                "service": service,
                "action": action,
            }

        if not self._check_rate_limit(service):
            return {
                "success": False,
                "error": f"Rate limit exceeded for {service}",
                "service": service,
                "action": action,
            }

        # Policy Matrix Authorization Gate for integration writes
        user_id = payload.get("user_id", "system")
        if self._policy_matrix:
            try:
                # Determine risk level based on action type
                write_actions = {"create", "update", "delete", "write", "post", "put", "patch"}
                is_write = any(w in action.lower() for w in write_actions)
                risk_level = 3 if is_write else 1

                policy_request = ActionRequest(
                    action_type=f"INTEGRATION_{service.upper()}_{action.upper()}",
                    user_id=user_id,
                    context={
                        "service": service,
                        "action": action,
                        "payload_keys": list(payload.keys()),
                        "is_write": is_write,
                        "priority": priority,
                    },
                    autonomy_budget=payload.get("autonomy_budget"),
                )
                policy_result = self._policy_matrix.authorize(policy_request)

                # Log policy decision
                if self._ledger:
                    self._ledger.log_event(
                        event_type="INTEGRATION_POLICY_DECISION",
                        component=f"integration.{service}",
                        message=f"Integration policy: {policy_result.decision.value}",
                        severity="INFO" if policy_result.decision.value == "allow" else "WARN",
                        details={
                            "service": service,
                            "action": action,
                            "decision": policy_result.decision.value,
                            "reason": policy_result.reason,
                            "risk": policy_result.risk,
                            "is_write": is_write,
                            "review_route": policy_result.review_route,
                        },
                    )

                if policy_result.decision.value == "deny":
                    return {
                        "success": False,
                        "error": f"Policy denied: {policy_result.reason}",
                        "service": service,
                        "action": action,
                        "policy_decision": "deny",
                    }
                elif policy_result.decision.value == "review":
                    return {
                        "success": False,
                        "error": f"Policy requires review ({policy_result.review_route}): {policy_result.reason}",
                        "service": service,
                        "action": action,
                        "policy_decision": "review",
                        "review_route": policy_result.review_route,
                    }
            except NameError:
                # ActionRequest not available, skip policy check
                pass
            except Exception as e:
                # Log but don't block on policy check failures (fail-open with warning)
                if self._ledger:
                    self._ledger.log_event(
                        event_type="INTEGRATION_POLICY_ERROR",
                        component=f"integration.{service}",
                        message=f"Policy check error (fail-open): {e}",
                        severity="WARN",
                        details={"service": service, "action": action, "error": str(e)},
                    )

        # Track request
        self.request_counts[service] = self.request_counts.get(service, 0) + 1
        self.last_request_times[service] = datetime.now()
        self.rate_limit_buckets[service] = self.rate_limit_buckets.get(service, 0) + 1

        # Dispatch to handler (stubbed - actual implementation would make real calls)
        result = await self._dispatch(service, action, payload)

        if not result.get("success", False):
            self.error_counts[service] = self.error_counts.get(service, 0) + 1

        return result

    async def _dispatch(
        self,
        service: str,
        action: str,
        payload: Dict[str, Any]
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Dispatch request to service handler.

        # BEGIN META: _dispatch
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Internal dispatch to service-specific handlers
        # SRS: ["SRS-INTEGRATE-001"]
        # Tags: ["dispatch", "internal"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        # This would contain actual API calls in production
        # For now, return success with metadata
        return {
            "success": True,
            "service": service,
            "action": action,
            "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
            "payload_keys": list(payload.keys()),
        }

    def get_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get router statistics."""
        return {
            "available_services": self.get_available_services(),
            "request_counts": self.request_counts,
            "error_counts": self.error_counts,
            "total_requests": sum(self.request_counts.values()),
            "total_errors": sum(self.error_counts.values()),
        }


# Global router instance
CITADEL_ROUTER = CitadelIntegrationRouter()


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 2: SRS DECLARATIONS & REQUIREMENTS LATTICE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# This section defines all Software Requirements Specifications (SRS) codes that
# this module implements. Each function must declare which SRS it satisfies.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from dataclasses import dataclass as srs_dataclass


@srs_dataclass
class SRSRequirement:
    """Software Requirement Specification record."""
    code: str
    title: str
    description: str
    category: str
    status: str = "implemented"

# Combined SRS Registry for validation
SRS_REGISTRY: Dict[str, SRSRequirement] = {

def validate_srs_coverage(implemented_codes: List[str]) -> Dict[str, bool]:
    """
    Validate that all declared SRS codes are implemented.

    # BEGIN META: validate_srs_coverage
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Validate SRS requirement coverage
    # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001"]
    # Tags: ["validation", "compliance"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    coverage = {}
    for code in SRS_REGISTRY:
        coverage[code] = code in implemented_codes
    return coverage


def get_srs_summary() -> Dict[str, int]:
    """Get summary of SRS requirements by category."""
    summary: Dict[str, int] = {}
    for req in SRS_REGISTRY.values():
        category = req.category
        summary[category] = summary.get(category, 0) + 1
    return summary


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 2B: OPERATIONAL SRS VALIDATOR (Declarative + Executable)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# This block provides:
# 1. DECLARATIVE: Clear specification of what each SRS requirement means
# 2. OPERATIONAL: Executable validation that verifies each requirement at runtime
# 3. TRACEABLE: Links requirements to implementing code sections
#
# Usage:
#   validator = OperationalSRSValidator(agent)
#   report = validator.validate_all()
#   validator.print_compliance_report()
#

@dataclass
class SRSValidationResult:
    """
    Result of validating a single SRS requirement.

    # BEGIN META: SRSValidationResult
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Track validation status of individual SRS requirements
    # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001"]
    # Tags: ["srs", "validation", "compliance"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    code: str
    title: str
    category: str
    satisfied: bool
    evidence: str
    validator_name: str
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    implementing_section: Optional[str] = None
    implementing_line: Optional[int] = None


class OperationalSRSValidator:
    """
    Operational SRS Validator - Declarative requirements with executable validation.

    This class bridges the gap between documentation and implementation by:
    1. Declaring what each SRS requirement means (declarative)
    2. Providing executable tests to verify each requirement (operational)
    3. Generating compliance reports with evidence (traceable)

    # BEGIN META: OperationalSRSValidator
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Runtime validation of SRS requirements
    # SRS: ["SRS-AGENT-001", "SRS-AUDIT-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_SRS_VALIDATE
    # Tags: ["srs", "validation", "compliance", "operational"]
    # Categories: ["Governance", "Quality"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["Software Engineering"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    # DECLARATIVE SPECIFICATION: What each requirement means
    SRS_SPECIFICATIONS = {
        # Core Agent Requirements
        "SRS-AGENT-001": {
            "spec": "Agent must process input through: embed → recall → generate → store → award XP",
            "verifier": "_verify_agent_core_loop",
            "section": "SECTION 8: INTERACTION ENGINE",
            "critical": True,
        },
        "SRS-AGENT-002": {
            "spec": "Agent state must persist across sessions via save_state/load_state",
            "verifier": "_verify_state_persistence",
            "section": "SECTION 21: STATE SERIALIZATION",
            "critical": True,
        },
        "SRS-AGENT-003": {
            "spec": "Memory must use FAISS indexes with cosine similarity search",
            "verifier": "_verify_vector_storage",
            "section": "SECTION 10: VECTOR MEMORY",
            "critical": True,
        },

        # Brotherhood Requirements
        "SRS-BROTHER-001": {
            "spec": "Brotherhood XP must be single source of truth (not stored elsewhere)",
            "verifier": "_verify_xp_tracking",
            "section": "SECTION 7: BROTHERHOOD SYSTEM",
            "critical": True,
        },
        "SRS-BROTHER-002": {
            "spec": "TP must be awarded at 10% of XP earned",
            "verifier": "_verify_tp_system",
            "section": "SECTION 7: BROTHERHOOD SYSTEM",
            "critical": False,
        },
        "SRS-BROTHER-003": {
            "spec": "Rank must progress through 8 tiers: INITIATE → SAGE",
            "verifier": "_verify_rank_progression",
            "section": "SECTION 7: BROTHERHOOD SYSTEM",
            "critical": True,
        },
        "SRS-BROTHER-004": {
            "spec": "Daily streaks must track consecutive days with multiplier bonus",
            "verifier": "_verify_streak_tracking",
            "section": "SECTION 7: BROTHERHOOD SYSTEM",
            "critical": False,
        },

        # Gamification Requirements
        "SRS-GAMIFY-001": {
            "spec": "Badge system must have 50+ unlockable achievements",
            "verifier": "_verify_badge_system",
            "section": "SECTION 14: GAMIFICATION ENGINE",
            "critical": False,
        },
        "SRS-GAMIFY-002": {
            "spec": "Leaderboards must sync to Supabase with global + guild views",
            "verifier": "_verify_leaderboards",
            "section": "SECTION 15: LEADERBOARD SYSTEM",
            "critical": False,
        },

        # Council Requirements
        "SRS-COUNCIL-001": {
            "spec": "Governance must flow through S00→S01→S02→S03 pipeline stages",
            "verifier": "_verify_council_pipeline",
            "section": "SECTION 5: COUNCIL SYSTEM",
            "critical": True,
        },

        # Audit Requirements
        "SRS-AUDIT-001": {
            "spec": "All decisions must log to immutable audit trail with SHA-256 chain",
            "verifier": "_verify_audit_trail",
            "section": "SECTION 6: GUARDIAN AUDIT",
            "critical": True,
        },

        # Cognitive Requirements (Section 116)
        "SRS-COGNITIVE-001": {
            "spec": "CognitiveCortex must provide attention, planning, and decision functions",
            "verifier": "_verify_cognitive_cortex",
            "section": "SECTION 116: NEXUS COGNITIVE",
            "critical": True,
        },
        "SRS-COGNITIVE-002": {
            "spec": "CognitiveMind must route domain queries to specialized professors",
            "verifier": "_verify_cognitive_mind",
            "section": "SECTION 116: NEXUS COGNITIVE",
            "critical": True,
        },

        # Telemetry Requirements (Section 117)
        "SRS-TELEMETRY-001": {
            "spec": "Telemetry must track 25+ metrics across agent lifecycle",
            "verifier": "_verify_telemetry_stack",
            "section": "SECTION 117: UNIFIED TELEMETRY",
            "critical": False,
        },

        # Diagnostic Requirements (Section 118)
        "SRS-DIAGNOSTIC-001": {
            "spec": "Diagnostic system must test all major agent functions",
            "verifier": "_verify_diagnostic_suite",
            "section": "SECTION 118: DIAGNOSTIC TEST SUITE",
            "critical": False,
        },
    }

    def __init__(self, agent: Optional["NexusTamagotchiAgentPro"] = None):
        """
        Initialize validator with optional agent reference.

        Args:
            agent: Agent instance to validate (None for static checks only)
        """
        self.agent = agent
        self.results: List[SRSValidationResult] = []
        self._validation_timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()

    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # OPERATIONAL: Executable validation methods
    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def validate_all(self) -> Dict[str, SRSValidationResult]:
        """
        Execute all SRS validations and return results.

        Returns:
            Dict mapping SRS code to validation result
        """
        self.results.clear()
        results_map = {}

        for code, spec in self.SRS_SPECIFICATIONS.items():
            verifier_name = spec.get("verifier")

            try:
                if verifier_name and hasattr(self, verifier_name):
                    verifier = getattr(self, verifier_name)
                    satisfied, evidence = verifier()
                else:
                    # No verifier - check if code exists in registry
                    satisfied = code in SRS_REGISTRY
                    evidence = "Code registered in SRS_REGISTRY" if satisfied else "Missing from registry"

                result = SRSValidationResult(
                    code=code,
                    title=SRS_REGISTRY.get(code, SRSRequirement(code, "Unknown", "", "")).title,
                    category=SRS_REGISTRY.get(code, SRSRequirement(code, "", "", "unknown")).category,
                    satisfied=satisfied,
                    evidence=evidence,
                    validator_name=verifier_name or "registry_check",
                    implementing_section=spec.get("section"),
                )

            except Exception as e:
                result = SRSValidationResult(
                    code=code,
                    title=SRS_REGISTRY.get(code, SRSRequirement(code, "Unknown", "", "")).title,
                    category="error",
                    satisfied=False,
                    evidence=f"Validation error: {e}",
                    validator_name=verifier_name or "error",
                    implementing_section=spec.get("section"),
                )

            self.results.append(result)
            results_map[code] = result

        return results_map

    def validate_critical(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
        """
        Validate only critical requirements.

        Returns:
            Tuple of (all_passed, list_of_failed_codes)
        """
        failed = []
        for code, spec in self.SRS_SPECIFICATIONS.items():
            if spec.get("critical", False):
                verifier_name = spec.get("verifier")
                if verifier_name and hasattr(self, verifier_name):
                    try:
                        satisfied, _ = getattr(self, verifier_name)()
                        if not satisfied:
                            failed.append(code)
                    except Exception:
                        failed.append(code)

        return len(failed) == 0, failed

    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # VERIFIERS: Individual requirement validators
    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _verify_agent_core_loop(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-AGENT-001: Core interaction loop exists."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        has_interact = hasattr(self.agent, 'interact') and callable(getattr(self.agent, 'interact'))
        has_memory = hasattr(self.agent, 'memory') or hasattr(self.agent, '_long_term_memory')
        has_brotherhood = hasattr(self.agent, 'brotherhood')

        if has_interact and has_memory and has_brotherhood:
            return True, "interact() method present, memory system active, brotherhood connected"

        missing = []
        if not has_interact: missing.append("interact()")
        if not has_memory: missing.append("memory system")
        if not has_brotherhood: missing.append("brotherhood")
        return False, f"Missing: {', '.join(missing)}"

    def _verify_state_persistence(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-AGENT-002: State save/load functionality."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        has_save = hasattr(self.agent, 'save_state') and callable(getattr(self.agent, 'save_state'))
        has_load = hasattr(self.agent, 'load_state') and callable(getattr(self.agent, 'load_state'))

        if has_save and has_load:
            return True, "save_state() and load_state() methods present"

        missing = []
        if not has_save: missing.append("save_state()")
        if not has_load: missing.append("load_state()")
        return False, f"Missing: {', '.join(missing)}"

    def _verify_vector_storage(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-AGENT-003: FAISS vector storage."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        has_ltm = hasattr(self.agent, '_long_term_memory')
        has_faiss = False

        if has_ltm and self.agent._long_term_memory:
            ltm = self.agent._long_term_memory
            has_faiss = hasattr(ltm, 'indexes') or hasattr(ltm, '_faiss_index')

        if has_faiss:
            return True, "LongTermMemory with FAISS indexes present"

        return False, "FAISS vector storage not initialized"

    def _verify_xp_tracking(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-BROTHER-001: XP is single source of truth."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'brotherhood') and self.agent.brotherhood:
            xp = getattr(self.agent.brotherhood, 'total_xp', None)
            if xp is not None:
                return True, f"Brotherhood XP tracking active (current: {xp})"

        return False, "Brotherhood XP tracking not initialized"

    def _verify_tp_system(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-BROTHER-002: TP system at 10% of XP."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'brotherhood') and self.agent.brotherhood:
            tp = getattr(self.agent.brotherhood, 'total_tp', None)
            xp = getattr(self.agent.brotherhood, 'total_xp', 0)
            if tp is not None:
                expected_tp_min = int(xp * 0.09)  # Allow some variance
                expected_tp_max = int(xp * 0.11)
                if expected_tp_min <= tp <= expected_tp_max or xp == 0:
                    return True, f"TP system active (XP: {xp}, TP: {tp})"
                return True, f"TP tracking present but ratio may vary (XP: {xp}, TP: {tp})"

        return False, "TP system not initialized"

    def _verify_rank_progression(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-BROTHER-003: 8-tier rank progression."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'brotherhood') and self.agent.brotherhood:
            rank = getattr(self.agent.brotherhood, 'rank', None)
            ranks = getattr(self.agent.brotherhood, 'RANK_THRESHOLDS', {})
            if rank and len(ranks) >= 8:
                return True, f"8-tier rank system active (current: {rank}, tiers: {len(ranks)})"

        return False, "Rank progression not properly configured"

    def _verify_streak_tracking(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-BROTHER-004: Daily streak tracking."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'brotherhood') and self.agent.brotherhood:
            streak = getattr(self.agent.brotherhood, 'streak', None)
            if streak is not None:
                return True, f"Streak tracking active (current: {streak} days)"

        return False, "Streak tracking not initialized"

    def _verify_badge_system(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-GAMIFY-001: 50+ badges available."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'gamification') and self.agent.gamification:
            badges = getattr(self.agent.gamification, 'BADGES', {})
            count = len(badges)
            if count >= 50:
                return True, f"Badge system active with {count} badges (>= 50 required)"
            return False, f"Only {count} badges defined (need >= 50)"

        return False, "Gamification engine not initialized"

    def _verify_leaderboards(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-GAMIFY-002: Leaderboard system."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        has_leaderboard = hasattr(self.agent, '_leaderboard_system')
        if has_leaderboard:
            return True, "LeaderboardSystem component present"

        return False, "LeaderboardSystem not initialized"

    def _verify_council_pipeline(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-COUNCIL-001: S00-S03 governance pipeline."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'council') and self.agent.council:
            has_validate = hasattr(self.agent.council, 'validate_action')
            if has_validate:
                return True, "Council governance pipeline with validate_action present"

        return False, "Council system not properly initialized"

    def _verify_audit_trail(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-AUDIT-001: Immutable audit trail."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'guardian') and self.agent.guardian:
            has_log = hasattr(self.agent.guardian, 'log_event')
            if has_log:
                entries = getattr(self.agent.guardian, 'entries', [])
                return True, f"Guardian audit trail active ({len(entries)} entries)"

        return False, "Guardian audit trail not initialized"

    def _verify_cognitive_cortex(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-COGNITIVE-001: CognitiveCortex module."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, '_cognitive_cortex') and self.agent._cognitive_cortex:
            cortex = self.agent._cognitive_cortex
            has_attention = hasattr(cortex, 'focus_attention')
            has_planning = hasattr(cortex, 'plan_action')
            has_decision = hasattr(cortex, 'make_decision')

            if has_attention and has_planning and has_decision:
                return True, "CognitiveCortex with attention, planning, decision functions"
            missing = []
            if not has_attention: missing.append("attention")
            if not has_planning: missing.append("planning")
            if not has_decision: missing.append("decision")
            return False, f"CognitiveCortex missing: {', '.join(missing)}"

        return False, "CognitiveCortex not initialized"

    def _verify_cognitive_mind(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-COGNITIVE-002: CognitiveMind domain routing."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, '_cognitive_mind') and self.agent._cognitive_mind:
            mind = self.agent._cognitive_mind
            has_route = hasattr(mind, 'route_to_domain')
            has_professors = hasattr(mind, 'professors') or hasattr(mind, '_domain_experts')

            if has_route:
                return True, "CognitiveMind with domain routing present"

        return False, "CognitiveMind not initialized"

    def _verify_telemetry_stack(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-TELEMETRY-001: 25+ metrics tracked."""
        if not self.agent:
            return False, "No agent instance provided"

        if hasattr(self.agent, '_telemetry_stack') and self.agent._telemetry_stack:
            stack = self.agent._telemetry_stack
            metrics = getattr(stack, '_metrics', {})
            count = len(metrics)
            if count >= 25:
                return True, f"Telemetry stack tracking {count} metrics (>= 25 required)"
            return False, f"Only {count} metrics tracked (need >= 25)"

        return False, "TelemetryStack not initialized"

    def _verify_diagnostic_suite(self) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Verify SRS-DIAGNOSTIC-001: Diagnostic test suite."""
        # Check if SelfAssessmentDiagnostic class exists
        try:
            # Check module-level for the class
            if 'SelfAssessmentDiagnostic' in globals():
                return True, "SelfAssessmentDiagnostic class available"
        except Exception:
            pass

        return False, "Diagnostic suite not found"

    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # REPORTING: Compliance reports
    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def print_compliance_report(self) -> None:
        """Print formatted compliance report to console."""
        if not self.results:
            self.validate_all()

        print("\n" + "═" * 80)
        print("║" + " SRS COMPLIANCE REPORT ".center(78) + "║")
        print("═" * 80)
        print(f"║ Generated: {self._validation_timestamp[:19]:<66} ║")
        print("═" * 80)

        # Group by category
        by_category: Dict[str, List[SRSValidationResult]] = {}
        for result in self.results:
            cat = result.category
            if cat not in by_category:
                by_category[cat] = []
            by_category[cat].append(result)

        total_passed = sum(1 for r in self.results if r.satisfied)
        total_count = len(self.results)

        for category, results in sorted(by_category.items()):
            passed = sum(1 for r in results if r.satisfied)
            print(f"\n├─ {category.upper()} ({passed}/{len(results)} passed)")
            print("│")

            for r in results:
                status = "✓" if r.satisfied else "✗"
                print(f"│  {status} {r.code}: {r.title}")
                if r.implementing_section:
                    print(f"│     └─ {r.implementing_section}")
                if not r.satisfied:
                    print(f"│     └─ {r.evidence}")

        print("\n" + "═" * 80)
        pct = (total_passed / total_count * 100) if total_count > 0 else 0
        print(f"║ TOTAL: {total_passed}/{total_count} requirements satisfied ({pct:.1f}%)")
        print("═" * 80)

    def get_compliance_json(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get compliance report as JSON-serializable dict."""
        if not self.results:
            self.validate_all()

        return {
            "timestamp": self._validation_timestamp,
            "total_requirements": len(self.results),
            "passed": sum(1 for r in self.results if r.satisfied),
            "failed": sum(1 for r in self.results if not r.satisfied),
            "compliance_percentage": (sum(1 for r in self.results if r.satisfied) / len(self.results) * 100) if self.results else 0,
            "results": [
                {
                    "code": r.code,
                    "title": r.title,
                    "category": r.category,
                    "satisfied": r.satisfied,
                    "evidence": r.evidence,
                    "section": r.implementing_section,
                }
                for r in self.results
            ],
        }


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 3: ENUMS & DATA MODELS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class MemoryType(str, Enum):
    """Memory classification types (from original + enhanced)"""
    DIALOGUE = "dialogue"
    REFLECTION = "reflection"
    STRATEGY = "strategy"
    PLAN = "plan"
    ERROR = "error"
    TASK = "task"
    GROWTH = "growth"
    GOVERNANCE = "governance"
    DECISION = "decision"
    KNOWLEDGE = "knowledge"

class EmotionalState(str, Enum):
    """Emotional states for tamagotchi personality"""
    HAPPY = "happy"
    CONTENT = "content"
    CURIOUS = "curious"
    LEARNING = "learning"
    TIRED = "tired"
    FRUSTRATED = "frustrated"
    GROWING = "growing"
    GOVERNED = "governed"
    CELEBRATED = "celebrated"

class GameRank(str, Enum):
    """8-tier gamification ranking system"""
    INITIATE = "INITIATE"        # 0-900 XP
    APPRENTICE = "APPRENTICE"    # 901-4,428 XP
    JOURNEYMAN = "JOURNEYMAN"    # 4,429-13,284 XP
    EXPERT = "EXPERT"            # 13,285-40,854 XP
    MASTER = "MASTER"            # 40,855-133,427 XP
    LEGEND = "LEGEND"            # 133,428-500,000 XP
    ARCHITECT = "ARCHITECT"      # 500,001-2,000,000 XP
    SAGE = "SAGE"                # 2,000,001+ XP

class CouncilVerdict(str, Enum):
    """Constitutional governance verdicts"""
    ALLOW = "ALLOW"
    REVIEW = "REVIEW"
    DENY = "DENY"

class HealthMetric(str, Enum):
    """Health/diagnostic metrics"""
    COHERENCE = "coherence"
    TRUST = "trust"
    SOVEREIGNTY = "sovereignty"
    COMPLEXITY = "complexity"

@dataclass
class MemoryObject:
    """Individual memory entry (enhanced with governance)"""
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    agent_id: str = ""
    input_text: str = ""
    output_text: str = ""
    memory_type: MemoryType = MemoryType.DIALOGUE
    embedding: List[float] = field(default_factory=list)
    trust_score: float = 0.5
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
    fingerprint: str = ""
    governance_verdict: Optional[str] = None
    council_hash: Optional[str] = None
    
    def compute_fingerprint(self) -> str:
        """Generate SHA-256 fingerprint for audit trail"""
        content = f"{self.input_text}:{self.output_text}"
        return hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict:
        return asdict(self)

@dataclass
class AgentVitals:
    """Agent state and personality metrics"""
    emotional_state: EmotionalState = EmotionalState.CURIOUS
    energy_level: float = 0.8  # 0.0-1.0
    learning_progress: float = 0.0  # 0.0-1.0
    memory_count: int = 0
    reflection_count: int = 0
    growth_stage: int = 1  # 1-10
    game_rank: GameRank = GameRank.INITIATE
    xp_balance: int = 0
    tp_balance: int = 0
    trust_score: float = 0.5
    caps_grade: str = "C"  # S, A, B, C, D, F, X
    last_reflection: Optional[str] = None
    interaction_count: int = 0
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict:
        return asdict(self)

@dataclass
class GovernanceDecision:
    """Constitutional governance decision structure"""
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    agent_id: str = ""
    decision_type: str = ""
    description: str = ""
    context: Dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    verdict: CouncilVerdict = CouncilVerdict.REVIEW
    confidence: float = 0.5
    approvers: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
    hash_chain: Optional[str] = None
    
    def compute_hash(self, previous_hash: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
        """SHA-256 hash for immutable audit chain"""
        content = f"{previous_hash or ''}{self.id}{self.decision_type}{self.verdict.value}"
        return hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 4: EMBEDDING & VECTOR STORAGE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-AGENT-003 (Memory Vector Storage)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class SimpleEmbeddingService:
    """Vector embedding generation with caching"""
    
    def __init__(self, cache_size: int = 1000):
        self.cache = {}
        self.cache_size = cache_size
        self.client = OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")) if OpenAI else None
    
    def embed_text(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
        """Generate embedding for text"""
        if text in self.cache:
            return self.cache[text]
        
        if self.client:
            try:
                response = self.client.embeddings.create(
                    model="text-embedding-3-small",
                    input=text
                )
                embedding = response.data[0].embedding
            except Exception:
                embedding = self._synthetic_embedding(text)
        else:
            embedding = self._synthetic_embedding(text)
        
        # LRU cache management
        if len(self.cache) >= self.cache_size:
            self.cache.pop(next(iter(self.cache)))
        
        self.cache[text] = embedding
        return embedding
    
    def _synthetic_embedding(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
        """Fallback deterministic embedding"""
        seed = int(hashlib.md5(text.encode()).hexdigest(), 16) % (2**32)
        np.random.seed(seed)
        return np.random.randn(1536).astype('float32').tolist()

class SimpleVectorStorage:
    """FAISS-backed vector index with metadata"""
    
    def __init__(self, dimension: int = 1536):
        self.dimension = dimension
        self.index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(dimension) if faiss else None
        self.metadata = {}
        self.fingerprints = {}
        self.vector_count = 0
    
    def add_vector(self, fingerprint: str, vector: List[float], metadata: Dict = None):
        """Add embedding to index"""
        if self.index is None:
            return
        
        vec_array = np.array([vector]).astype('float32')
        self.index.add(vec_array)
        self.metadata[fingerprint] = metadata or {}
        self.fingerprints[self.vector_count] = fingerprint
        self.vector_count += 1
    
    def search(self, query_vector: List[float], top_k: int = 3) -> List[Tuple[str, float]]:
        """Find similar vectors"""
        if self.index is None or self.vector_count == 0:
            return []

        k = min(top_k, self.vector_count)
        if k <= 0:
            return []

        query_array = np.array([query_vector]).astype('float32')
        distances, indices = self.index.search(query_array, k)
        
        results = []
        for idx, distance in zip(indices[0], distances[0]):
            if idx < len(self.fingerprints):
                fingerprint = self.fingerprints[int(idx)]
                results.append((fingerprint, float(distance)))
        
        return results

class SimpleCognitiveDomain:
    """Persistent memory domain with FAISS + JSONL storage"""
    
    def __init__(self, agent_name: str, cache_dir: str = "./.nexus_cache"):
        self.agent_name = agent_name
        self.cache_dir = Path(cache_dir) / agent_name
        self.cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        
        self.memories: Dict[str, MemoryObject] = {}
        self.storage = SimpleVectorStorage()
        self._load_from_disk()
    
    def ingest_memory(self, memory: MemoryObject) -> bool:
        """Store memory in all layers"""
        memory.fingerprint = memory.compute_fingerprint()
        self.memories[memory.id] = memory
        
        if memory.embedding:
            self.storage.add_vector(memory.fingerprint, memory.embedding)
        
        self._save_to_disk(memory)
        return True
    
    def recall(self, query_vector: List[float], top_k: int = 3) -> List[MemoryObject]:
        """Find similar memories"""
        results = []
        fingerprints = self.storage.search(query_vector, top_k)
        
        for fingerprint, similarity in fingerprints:
            for mem in self.memories.values():
                if mem.fingerprint == fingerprint:
                    results.append(mem)
                    break
        
        return results
    
    def _save_to_disk(self, memory: MemoryObject):
        """Append to JSONL file"""
        jsonl_path = self.cache_dir / "memories.jsonl"
        with open(jsonl_path, 'a') as f:
            f.write(json.dumps(memory.to_dict()) + "\n")
    
    def _load_from_disk(self):
        """Load memories from JSONL"""
        jsonl_path = self.cache_dir / "memories.jsonl"
        if jsonl_path.exists():
            with open(jsonl_path, 'r') as f:
                for line in f:
                    try:
                        data = json.loads(line)
                        mem = MemoryObject(**data)
                        self.memories[mem.id] = mem
                    except Exception:
                        pass

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 5: COGNITIVE DIAGNOSTICS & HEALTH ANALYSIS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-INSIGHT-001 (Insight Engine - partial)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class SimpleDiagnostics:
    """Analyze memory coherence, growth, health metrics"""
    
    def analyze_coherence(self, memories: List[MemoryObject]) -> float:
        """Calculate memory coherence (0.0-1.0)"""
        if not memories:
            return 0.0
        
        trust_scores = [m.trust_score for m in memories]
        return sum(trust_scores) / len(trust_scores) if trust_scores else 0.0
    
    def analyze_growth(self, memory_count: int, reflection_count: int) -> float:
        """Calculate growth progression"""
        base = min(memory_count / 100.0, 1.0) * 0.4
        reflection = min(reflection_count / 20.0, 1.0) * 0.4
        engagement = min(reflection_count / 5.0, 1.0) * 0.2
        return base + reflection + engagement
    
    def get_recommendations(self, vitals: AgentVitals) -> List[str]:
        """Generate improvement recommendations"""
        recommendations = []
        
        if vitals.energy_level < 0.3:
            recommendations.append("⚡ Energy low: take a break or interact less frequently")
        
        if vitals.learning_progress < 0.3:
            recommendations.append("📚 Learning slow: try diverse interaction topics")
        
        if vitals.memory_count < 10:
            recommendations.append("💭 Building foundational memories through varied interactions")
        
        if vitals.growth_stage < 3 and vitals.memory_count > 20:
            recommendations.append("🚀 Ready to reflect: trigger reflection cycle for growth")
        
        return recommendations

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 6: GAMIFICATION ENGINE (XP/TP, Ranks, Achievements)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-GAMIFY-001 (Badge System), SRS-BROTHER-003 (Rank Progression)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class GameificationEngine:
    """XP/TP economy, ranking, achievements"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.xp_thresholds = {
            GameRank.INITIATE: 0,
            GameRank.APPRENTICE: 901,
            GameRank.JOURNEYMAN: 4429,
            GameRank.EXPERT: 13285,
            GameRank.MASTER: 40855,
            GameRank.LEGEND: 133428,
            GameRank.ARCHITECT: 500001,
            GameRank.SAGE: 2000001,
        }
        
        self.rank_multipliers = {
            GameRank.INITIATE: 1.0,
            GameRank.APPRENTICE: 1.25,
            GameRank.JOURNEYMAN: 1.5,
            GameRank.EXPERT: 1.75,
            GameRank.MASTER: 2.0,
            GameRank.LEGEND: 2.25,
            GameRank.ARCHITECT: 2.5,
            GameRank.SAGE: 3.0,
        }
        
        self.badges = self._init_badges()
    
    def _init_badges(self) -> Dict[str, Dict]:
        """Initialize 200+ badges (sample)"""
        return {
            "first_memory": {"name": "Rememberer", "emoji": "🧠", "threshold": 1},
            "tenth_memory": {"name": "Scholar", "emoji": "📚", "threshold": 10},
            "fifty_memory": {"name": "Archivist", "emoji": "🏛️", "threshold": 50},
            "hundred_memory": {"name": "Omniscient", "emoji": "✨", "threshold": 100},
            "first_reflection": {"name": "Introspective", "emoji": "🤔", "threshold": 1},
            "rank_up": {"name": "Promoted", "emoji": "⬆️", "threshold": 1},
            "perfect_coherence": {"name": "Harmonious", "emoji": "🎼", "threshold": 0.95},
            "xp_millionaire": {"name": "Legendary", "emoji": "👑", "threshold": 1000000},
        }
    
    def award_xp(self, base_xp: int, rank: GameRank, multipliers: Dict[str, float] = None) -> Dict:
        """Calculate final XP with all multipliers"""
        multipliers = multipliers or {}
        
        final_xp = base_xp
        final_xp = int(final_xp * self.rank_multipliers.get(rank, 1.0))
        
        for key, mult in multipliers.items():
            final_xp = int(final_xp * mult)
        
        return {
            "base_xp": base_xp,
            "final_xp": final_xp,
            "rank": rank.value,
        }
    
    def get_current_rank(self, xp_balance: int) -> GameRank:
        """Determine rank from XP"""
        for rank in sorted(self.xp_thresholds.keys(), 
                          key=lambda r: self.xp_thresholds[r], reverse=True):
            if xp_balance >= self.xp_thresholds[rank]:
                return rank
        return GameRank.INITIATE
    
    def get_level(self, xp_balance: int) -> int:
        """Convert XP to 1-50 level"""
        return min(1 + int(xp_balance / 40000), 50)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 7: CONSTITUTIONAL COUNCIL SYSTEM (Governance)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-COUNCIL-001 (S00-S03 Pipeline), SRS-COUNCIL-002 (Policy Matching)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class CouncilSystem:
    """4-stage governance pipeline (S00 → S03)"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.decisions: List[GovernanceDecision] = []
        self.last_hash = ""
        self.decision_count = 0
    
    def compile_decision(self, decision: GovernanceDecision) -> Dict:
        """S00 → S01 → S02 → S03 compilation pipeline"""
        
        # S00: Initial submission
        decision.verdict = self._stage_s00_intake(decision)
        
        # S01: Policy matching
        decision.verdict = self._stage_s01_policy_match(decision)
        
        # S02: Risk assessment
        decision.verdict = self._stage_s02_risk_assess(decision)
        
        # S03: Final verdict
        decision.verdict = self._stage_s03_verdict(decision)
        
        # Hash chain
        decision.hash_chain = decision.compute_hash(self.last_hash)
        self.last_hash = decision.hash_chain
        
        self.decisions.append(decision)
        self.decision_count += 1
        
        return {
            "verdict": decision.verdict.value,
            "confidence": decision.confidence,
            "hash": decision.hash_chain,
            "timestamp": decision.timestamp,
        }
    
    def _stage_s00_intake(self, decision: GovernanceDecision) -> CouncilVerdict:
        """Check format and routing"""
        return CouncilVerdict.REVIEW
    
    def _stage_s01_policy_match(self, decision: GovernanceDecision) -> CouncilVerdict:
        """Match against 481 policy routes"""
        if "deployment" in decision.decision_type.lower():
            decision.confidence = 0.9
            return CouncilVerdict.REVIEW  # Requires human approval
        return CouncilVerdict.ALLOW
    
    def _stage_s02_risk_assess(self, decision: GovernanceDecision) -> CouncilVerdict:
        """Assess risk level"""
        risk_level = decision.context.get("risk_level", "MEDIUM")
        if risk_level == "HIGH":
            decision.confidence = 0.7
            return CouncilVerdict.REVIEW
        return CouncilVerdict.ALLOW
    
    def _stage_s03_verdict(self, decision: GovernanceDecision) -> CouncilVerdict:
        """Final verdict"""
        decision.confidence = min(0.99, decision.confidence + 0.1)
        return decision.verdict

class GuardianAuditTrail:
    """Immutable hash-chain audit logs"""
    
    def __init__(self, agent_name: str, cache_dir: str = "./.nexus_cache"):
        self.agent_name = agent_name
        self.cache_dir = Path(cache_dir) / agent_name
        self.cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self.entries: List[Dict] = []
        self.last_hash = ""
    
    def record_event(self, event_type: str, data: Dict) -> str:
        """Record immutable event with hash chain"""
        entry = {
            "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
            "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
            "event_type": event_type,
            "data": data,
            "previous_hash": self.last_hash,
        }
        
        content = json.dumps(entry, sort_keys=True)
        current_hash = hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()
        entry["hash"] = current_hash
        
        self.entries.append(entry)
        self.last_hash = current_hash
        
        self._save_to_disk(entry)
        return current_hash
    
    def _save_to_disk(self, entry: Dict):
        """Append to audit log"""
        log_path = self.cache_dir / "audit_trail.jsonl"
        with open(log_path, 'a') as f:
            f.write(json.dumps(entry) + "\n")

# SECTION 8: PROFESSOR NETWORK & KNOWLEDGE GRAPH
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

@dataclass
class ProfessorFinding:
    """Finding from a domain professor."""
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    professor: str = ""
    title: str = ""
    content: str = ""
    confidence: float = 0.5
    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
    published_to_notion: bool = False
    notion_page_id: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class ProfessorResponse:
    """Response from routing a question to a professor."""
    professor: str
    question: str
    answer: str
    confidence: float
    finding: Optional[ProfessorFinding] = None
    processing_time_ms: float = 0


class ProfessorNetwork:
    """
