                ws.send(JSON.stringify({{ type: 'init', settings: settings }}));
            }};

            ws.onclose = () => {{
                document.getElementById('statusDot').classList.add('disconnected');
                if (reconnectAttempts < maxReconnectAttempts) {{
                    reconnectAttempts++;
                    setTimeout(connect, 1000 * reconnectAttempts);
                }}
            }};

            ws.onmessage = (event) => {{
                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
                handleMessage(data);
            }};

            ws.onerror = (error) => {{
                console.error('WebSocket error:', error);
            }};
        }}

        function sendWS(payload) {{
            if (!ws || ws.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN) return;
            ws.send(JSON.stringify(payload));
        }}

        function requestPanelData() {{
            const domainSelect = document.getElementById('memoryDomain');
            sendWS({{ type: 'get_professors' }});
            sendWS({{ type: 'get_memories', domain: domainSelect ? domainSelect.value : 'all' }});
            sendWS({{ type: 'get_missions' }});
            sendWS({{ type: 'get_skills' }});
            sendWS({{ type: 'get_integrations' }});
            sendWS({{ type: 'get_analytics' }});
        }}

        function handleMessage(data) {{
            switch (data.type) {{
                case 'response':
                    // Update logic steps
                    updateLogicStep('generate', 'completed', 'Response generated', data.generate_time || 0);
                    updateLogicStep('memory', 'completed', 'Stored in STM/LTM', data.memory_time || 0);
                    addMessage('assistant', data.content, data.reasoning, data.tool_calls);
                    hideTyping();
                    break;
                case 'status':
                    updateStatus(data);
                    break;
                case 'typing':
                    showTyping();
                    break;
                case 'info':
                    document.getElementById('agentInfo').innerHTML = data.content;
                    break;
                case 'logic_step':
                    // Handle logic step updates from server
                    updateLogicStep(data.step_id, data.status, data.detail, data.time_ms);
                    break;
                case 'domain_status':
                    // Update domain status from server
                    const statusDot = document.getElementById(data.domain + '-status');
                    const statusText = document.getElementById(data.domain + '-status-text');
                    if (statusDot) statusDot.className = 'domain-status-dot ' + data.status;
                    if (statusText) statusText.textContent = data.message;
                    break;
                case 'error':
                    addMessage('system', '⚠️ ' + data.content);
                    hideTyping();
                    break;
            }}
        }}

        function updateStatus(data) {{
            if (data.xp !== undefined) document.getElementById('xpValue').textContent = data.xp;
            if (data.rank) document.getElementById('rankValue').textContent = data.rank;
            if (data.emotion) document.getElementById('emotionValue').textContent = data.emotion;
            if (data.memory_usage !== undefined) {{
                document.getElementById('memoryBar').style.width = data.memory_usage + '%';
            }}
        }}

        async function sendMessage() {{
            const input = document.getElementById('messageInput');
            const message = input.value.trim();
            if (!message || !ws || ws.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN) return;

            // Reset logic steps for new message
            resetLogicSteps();
            const startTime = Date.now();

            // Step 1: Receive Input
            updateLogicStep('receive', 'completed', 'Input received: ' + message.substring(0, 30) + '...', Date.now() - startTime);

            addMessage('user', message);
            input.value = '';
            autoResize(input);

            // Step 2: Taskir Router (API call to citadel-nexus.com)
            const taskResult = await callTaskirAPI(message);

            // Step 3: College RAG (API call)
            const collegeResult = await callCollegeAPI(message, {{ domain: activeDomain }});

            // Step 4: SAKE Analysis (API call)
            const sakeResult = await callSakeAPI(message);

            // Step 5: Council Review (API call)
            const councilResult = await callCouncilAPI('respond', {{ message: message, domain: activeDomain }});

            // Step 6: Generate Response (via WebSocket)
            updateLogicStep('generate', 'active', 'Generating response...', null);
            const generateStart = Date.now();

            ws.send(JSON.stringify({{
                type: 'message',
                content: message,
                settings: settings,
                context: {{
                    taskir: taskResult,
                    college: collegeResult,
                    sake: sakeResult,
                    council: councilResult,
                    domain: activeDomain
                }}
            }}));
        }}

        function addMessage(role, content, reasoning = null, toolCalls = []) {{
            const messages = document.getElementById('messages');
            const div = document.createElement('div');
            div.className = 'message ' + role;

            const avatarEmoji = role === 'user' ? '👤' : role === 'assistant' ? '🤖' : 'ℹ️';
            let html = `
                <div class="message-avatar">${{avatarEmoji}}</div>
                <div class="message-content">
                    ${{settings.markdown ? renderMarkdown(content) : escapeHtml(content)}}
            `;

            if (reasoning && settings.reasoning) {{
                html += `<div class="message-reasoning">${{escapeHtml(reasoning)}}</div>`;
            }}

            html += `
                    <div class="message-timestamp">${{new Date().toLocaleTimeString()}}</div>
                </div>
            `;

            div.innerHTML = html;
            messages.appendChild(div);
            messages.scrollTop = messages.scrollHeight;
        }}

        function showTyping() {{
            if (document.getElementById('typingIndicator')) return;
            const messages = document.getElementById('messages');
            const div = document.createElement('div');
            div.id = 'typingIndicator';
            div.className = 'message assistant';
            div.innerHTML = `
                <div class="message-avatar">🤖</div>
                <div class="message-content">
                    <div class="typing-indicator">
                        <div class="typing-dot"></div>
                        <div class="typing-dot"></div>
                        <div class="typing-dot"></div>
                    </div>
                </div>
            `;
            messages.appendChild(div);
            messages.scrollTop = messages.scrollHeight;
        }}

        function hideTyping() {{
            const indicator = document.getElementById('typingIndicator');
            if (indicator) indicator.remove();
        }}

        function handleKeydown(event) {{
            if (event.key === 'Enter' && !event.shiftKey) {{
                event.preventDefault();
                sendMessage();
            }}
        }}

        function autoResize(el) {{
            el.style.height = 'auto';
            el.style.height = Math.min(el.scrollHeight, 200) + 'px';
        }}

        function toggleSettings() {{
            document.getElementById('settingsPanel').classList.toggle('open');
        }}

        function toggleOption(option) {{
            settings[option] = !settings[option];
            const toggle = document.getElementById('toggle' + option.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + option.slice(1));
            toggle.classList.toggle('active');
        }}

        function updateResponsePreset() {{
            settings.responsePreset = document.getElementById('responsePreset').value;
        }}

        function clearChat() {{
            const messages = document.getElementById('messages');
            messages.innerHTML = '';
            if (ws && ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {{
                ws.send(JSON.stringify({{ type: 'clear' }}));
            }}
        }}

        function exportChat() {{
            const messages = document.querySelectorAll('.message');
            let text = '';
            messages.forEach(msg => {{
                const role = msg.classList.contains('user') ? 'User' : 'Assistant';
                const content = msg.querySelector('.message-content').textContent;
                text += role + ': ' + content + '\\n\\n';
            }});
            const blob = new Blob([text], {{ type: 'text/plain' }});
            const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
            const a = document.createElement('a');
            a.href = url;
            a.download = 'nexus-chat-' + new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0] + '.txt';
            a.click();
        }}

        function escapeHtml(text) {{
            const div = document.createElement('div');
            div.textContent = text;
            return div.innerHTML;
        }}

        function renderMarkdown(text) {{
            // Simple markdown rendering
            return text
                .replace(/```([\\s\\S]*?)```/g, '<pre><code>$1</code></pre>')
                .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '<code>$1</code>')
                .replace(/\\*\\*([^*]+)\\*\\*/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')
                .replace(/\\*([^*]+)\\*/g, '<em>$1</em>')
                .replace(/^### (.+)$/gm, '<h3>$1</h3>')
                .replace(/^## (.+)$/gm, '<h2>$1</h2>')
                .replace(/^# (.+)$/gm, '<h1>$1</h1>')
                .replace(/^- (.+)$/gm, '<li>$1</li>')
                .replace(/\\n/g, '<br>');
        }}

        // Initialize
        connect();
        checkDomainHealth();
        initThoughtVector();

        // Re-check health periodically
        setInterval(checkDomainHealth, 60000);
    </script>
</body>
</html>'''

    async def _handle_websocket(self, websocket, path=None):
        """Handle WebSocket connection (path is optional for newer websockets lib)."""
        self._connections.add(websocket)
        self.state.connected = True
        self.state.connection_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

        try:
            # Send initial status
            await self._send_status(websocket)
            await self._send_agent_info(websocket)

            async for message in websocket:
                await self._handle_message(websocket, message)

        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[UI] WebSocket error: {e}")
        finally:
            self._connections.discard(websocket)
            if not self._connections:
                self.state.connected = False

    async def _handle_message(self, websocket, message: str):
        """Handle incoming WebSocket message."""
        try:
            data = json.loads(message)
            msg_type = data.get("type", "")

            if msg_type == "init":
                settings = data.get("settings", {})
                if "responsePreset" in settings:
                    self.response_config.set_preset(settings["responsePreset"])

            elif msg_type == "message":
                user_input = data.get("content", "")
                settings = data.get("settings", {})

                # Update response config
                if "responsePreset" in settings:
                    self.response_config.set_preset(settings["responsePreset"])

                # Send typing indicator
                await websocket.send(json.dumps({"type": "typing"}))

                # Process with agent
                response = await self._process_message(user_input)

                # Send response
                await websocket.send(json.dumps({
                    "type": "response",
                    "content": response.get("content", ""),
                    "reasoning": response.get("reasoning"),
                    "tool_calls": response.get("tool_calls", []),
                }))

                # Send updated status
                await self._send_status(websocket)

            elif msg_type == "clear":
                self._message_history.clear()

        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            await websocket.send(json.dumps({
                "type": "error",
                "content": "Invalid message format",
            }))

    async def _process_message(self, user_input: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Process message with agent."""
        try:
            # Get response from agent
            if hasattr(self.agent, 'interact'):
                response_content = self.agent.interact(user_input)
            else:
                response_content = "Agent interaction not available"

            # Store in history
            self._message_history.append(UIMessage(
                role="user",
                content=user_input,
            ))
            self._message_history.append(UIMessage(
                role="assistant",
                content=response_content,
            ))

            return {
                "content": response_content,
                "reasoning": None,  # Could extract from agent if available
                "tool_calls": [],
            }

        except Exception as e:
            return {
                "content": f"Error processing message: {str(e)}",
                "reasoning": None,
                "tool_calls": [],
            }

    async def _process_message_streaming(
        self,
        websocket,
        session_id: str,
        user_input: str
    ) -> None:
        """
        Process message with streaming response via WebSocket.

        Sends tokens as they arrive for real-time display.

        # BEGIN META: _process_message_streaming
        # SRS: ["SRS-STREAM-002"]
        # Purpose: Real-time streaming chat responses over WebSocket
        # END META
        """
        try:
            # Send typing indicator
            await websocket.send(json.dumps({
                "type": "typing",
                "session_id": session_id
            }))

            # Check if agent has streaming capability
            if not hasattr(self.agent, '_generate_response_streaming'):
                # Fallback to non-streaming
                result = await self._process_message(user_input)
                await websocket.send(json.dumps({
                    "type": "response",
                    "session_id": session_id,
                    "content": result["content"],
                    "complete": True
                }))
                return

            # Build context from recent history
            context = ""
            if self._message_history:
                recent = self._message_history[-4:]  # Last 2 exchanges
                context = "\n".join([f"{m.role}: {m.content[:100]}" for m in recent])

            # Stream tokens
            full_response = ""
            async for token in self.agent._generate_response_streaming(user_input, context):
                full_response += token
                await websocket.send(json.dumps({
                    "type": "stream_token",
                    "session_id": session_id,
                    "token": token
                }))

            # Send completion signal
            await websocket.send(json.dumps({
                "type": "stream_complete",
                "session_id": session_id,
                "full_response": full_response
            }))

            # Store in history
            self._message_history.append(UIMessage(
                role="user",
                content=user_input,
            ))
            self._message_history.append(UIMessage(
                role="assistant",
                content=full_response,
            ))

        except Exception as e:
            await websocket.send(json.dumps({
                "type": "error",
                "session_id": session_id,
                "error": str(e)
            }))

    async def _send_status(self, websocket):
        """Send agent status update."""
        xp = 0
        rank = "INITIATE"
        emotion = "neutral"
        memory_usage = 0

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'brotherhood_system'):
            bs = self.agent.brotherhood_system
            xp = bs.total_xp if hasattr(bs, 'total_xp') else 0
            rank = bs.rank if hasattr(bs, 'rank') else "INITIATE"

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'vitals'):
            emotion = self.agent.vitals.emotional_state if hasattr(self.agent.vitals, 'emotional_state') else "neutral"
            memory_usage = (self.agent.vitals.memory_count / 1000) * 100 if hasattr(self.agent.vitals, 'memory_count') else 0

        await websocket.send(json.dumps({
            "type": "status",
            "xp": xp,
            "rank": rank,
            "emotion": emotion,
            "memory_usage": min(memory_usage, 100),
        }))

    async def _send_agent_info(self, websocket):
        """Send agent info for settings panel."""
        info = []

        if hasattr(self.agent, 'agent_name'):
            info.append(f"<b>Name:</b> {self.agent.agent_name}")
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'model'):
            info.append(f"<b>Model:</b> {self.agent.model}")
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'brotherhood_system'):
            bs = self.agent.brotherhood_system
            info.append(f"<b>XP:</b> {getattr(bs, 'total_xp', 0)}")
            info.append(f"<b>TP:</b> {getattr(bs, 'total_tp', 0)}")
            info.append(f"<b>Rank:</b> {getattr(bs, 'rank', 'INITIATE')}")
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'vitals'):
            info.append(f"<b>Interactions:</b> {getattr(self.agent.vitals, 'interaction_count', 0)}")
            info.append(f"<b>Memories:</b> {getattr(self.agent.vitals, 'memory_count', 0)}")

        await websocket.send(json.dumps({
            "type": "info",
            "content": "<br>".join(info),
        }))

    async def start(self):
        """
        Start the WebSocket server and optionally open browser.

        # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer.start
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Launch WebSocket server and UI
        # SRS: ["SRS-UI-001"]
        # Tags: ["start", "server", "launch"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        try:
            import websockets
            from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler
            import threading
            import webbrowser

            html_content = self._generate_html()

            # Simple HTTP handler for the HTML page
            class HTMLHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
                def do_GET(self):
                    self.send_response(200)
                    self.send_header('Content-type', 'text/html; charset=utf-8')
                    self.end_headers()
                    self.wfile.write(html_content.encode('utf-8'))

                def log_message(self, format, *args):
                    pass  # Suppress HTTP logs

            # Start HTTP server in thread
            http_port = self.config.port + 1
            http_server = HTTPServer((self.config.host, http_port), HTMLHandler)
            http_thread = threading.Thread(target=http_server.serve_forever)
            http_thread.daemon = True
            http_thread.start()

            print(f"[UI] HTTP server started at http://{self.config.host}:{http_port}")

            # Open browser
            if self.config.auto_open_browser:
                webbrowser.open(f"http://{self.config.host}:{http_port}")

            # Start WebSocket server
            print(f"[UI] WebSocket server starting at ws://{self.config.host}:{self.config.port}")
            async with websockets.serve(
                self._handle_websocket,
                self.config.host,
                self.config.port,
            ):
                await asyncio.Future()  # Run forever

        except ImportError:
            print("[UI] Warning: websockets package not installed. Run: pip install websockets")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[UI] Error starting server: {e}")

    def launch_ui(self):
        """
        Launch the UI (blocking call).

        # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer.launch_ui
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Convenience method to launch UI
        # SRS: ["SRS-UI-001"]
        # Tags: ["launch", "ui", "blocking"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        asyncio.run(self.start())


# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 26.3: UI LAUNCHER
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

def launch_agent_ui(
    agent: Any,
    port: int = 8765,
    auto_open: bool = True,
    response_preset: str = "balanced",
):
    """
    Launch the agent UI.

    # BEGIN META: launch_agent_ui
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Convenience function to launch agent UI
    # SRS: ["SRS-UI-001"]
    # Tags: ["launch", "ui", "function"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META

    Args:
        agent: The NexusTamagotchiAgentPro instance
        port: WebSocket server port
        auto_open: Whether to auto-open browser
        response_preset: Initial response length preset

    Example:
        agent = NexusTamagotchiAgentPro(agent_name="Aurora")
        launch_agent_ui(agent, response_preset="elongated")
    """
    config = UIConfig(
        port=port,
        auto_open_browser=auto_open,
    )
    response_config = ResponseLengthConfig(response_preset)

    server = AgentChatServer(agent, config, response_config)
    server.launch_ui()


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 27: WORKSHOP INTEGRATION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-WORKSHOP-001, SRS-WORKSHOP-002
# Purpose: Integration with CitadelWorkshop for knowledge sync and tool registry
# Pattern: CitadelWorkshop tool registry pattern
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 27.1: WORKSHOP TOOL DEFINITIONS
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class WorkshopToolDefinition:
    """
    Tool definition for Workshop registry.

    # BEGIN META: WorkshopToolDefinition
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define tool for Workshop integration
    # SRS: ["SRS-WORKSHOP-001"]
    # Tags: ["workshop", "tool", "definition"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    name: str
    description: str
    category: str = "agent"
    requires: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Dependencies
    enabled_by_default: bool = True
    schema: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    handler: Optional[Any] = None  # Callable


@dataclass
class WorkshopSyncResult:
    """
    Result of Workshop sync operation.

    # BEGIN META: WorkshopSyncResult
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Track Workshop sync operation result
    # SRS: ["SRS-WORKSHOP-001"]
    # Tags: ["workshop", "sync", "result"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    success: bool
    items_synced: int = 0
    items_failed: int = 0
    errors: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    sync_timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())


class WorkshopIntegration:
    """
    Integration with CitadelWorkshop.

    # BEGIN META: WorkshopIntegration
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Connect agent to CitadelWorkshop for knowledge sync and tools
    # SRS: ["SRS-WORKSHOP-001", "SRS-WORKSHOP-002"]
    # Pattern: CitadelWorkshop tool registry
    # Tags: ["workshop", "integration", "sync", "tools"]
    # Categories: ["Workshop Integration"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["System Integration"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Provides two-way integration with CitadelWorkshop including
    #              knowledge sync, tool registration, and brain map visualization.
    # END META
    """

    DEFAULT_WORKSHOP_URL: str = "http://localhost:8000"
    SYNC_INTERVAL_MINUTES: int = 5

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent: Any,
        workshop_url: Optional[str] = None,
        api_key: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """
        Initialize Workshop integration.

        # BEGIN META: WorkshopIntegration.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize connection to CitadelWorkshop
        # SRS: ["SRS-WORKSHOP-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "workshop"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.agent = agent
        self.workshop_url = workshop_url or os.getenv(
            "CITADEL_WORKSHOP_URL", self.DEFAULT_WORKSHOP_URL
        )
        self.api_key = api_key or os.getenv("CITADEL_WORKSHOP_API_KEY")
        self._registered_tools: Dict[str, WorkshopToolDefinition] = {}
        self._last_sync: Optional[datetime] = None
        self._sync_errors: List[str] = []

        # Register default tools
        self._register_default_tools()

    def _register_default_tools(self):
        """Register default agent tools for Workshop."""
        # Memory search tool
        self.register_tool(WorkshopToolDefinition(
            name="nexus_memory_search",
            description="Search agent's semantic memory (STM/LTM)",
            category="memory",
            schema={
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "query": {"type": "string", "description": "Search query"},
                    "top_k": {"type": "integer", "default": 5},
                },
                "required": ["query"],
            },
            handler=self._handle_memory_search,
        ))

        # Introspection tool
        self.register_tool(WorkshopToolDefinition(
            name="nexus_introspect",
            description="Get agent self-awareness report",
            category="awareness",
            schema={"type": "object", "properties": {}},
            handler=self._handle_introspect,
        ))

        # Brain map tool
        self.register_tool(WorkshopToolDefinition(
            name="nexus_brain_map",
            description="Generate brain synapse visualization data",
            category="visualization",
            schema={"type": "object", "properties": {}},
            handler=self._handle_brain_map,
        ))

        # Learn tool
        self.register_tool(WorkshopToolDefinition(
            name="nexus_learn",
            description="Add knowledge to agent's learning database",
            category="learning",
            schema={
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "content": {"type": "string"},
                    "domain": {"type": "string"},
                },
                "required": ["content"],
            },
            handler=self._handle_learn,
        ))

    def register_tool(self, tool: WorkshopToolDefinition):
        """
        Register a tool for Workshop.

        # BEGIN META: WorkshopIntegration.register_tool
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Register tool in Workshop registry
        # SRS: ["SRS-WORKSHOP-001"]
        # Tags: ["register", "tool", "workshop"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self._registered_tools[tool.name] = tool

    def get_registered_tools(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get list of registered tools."""
        return [
            {
                "name": t.name,
                "description": t.description,
                "category": t.category,
                "schema": t.schema,
            }
            for t in self._registered_tools.values()
        ]

    async def _handle_memory_search(self, query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Handle memory search tool invocation."""
        results = []

        # Search STM if available
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'stm') and self.agent.stm:
            stm_results = self.agent.stm.search(query, top_k)
            for r in stm_results:
                results.append({
                    "source": "stm",
                    "content": r.entry.content,
                    "similarity": r.similarity,
                    "emotion": r.entry.emotion,
                })

        # Search LTM if available
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'ltm') and self.agent.ltm:
            ltm_results = self.agent.ltm.retrieve(query, top_k=top_k)
            for r in ltm_results:
                results.append({
                    "source": "ltm",
                    "content": r.entry.content,
                    "similarity": r.similarity,
                    "domain": r.entry.domain,
                })

        return {"results": results, "count": len(results)}

    async def _handle_introspect(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Handle introspection tool invocation."""
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'self_awareness') and self.agent.self_awareness:
            report = self.agent.self_awareness.introspect()
            return {
                "agent_name": report.agent_name,
                "stm_entries": report.stm_entry_count,
                "ltm_entries": report.ltm_total_vectors,
                "learning_velocity": report.learning_velocity,
                "emotion": report.current_emotion,
                "integrations": {
                    k: v.status for k, v in report.integrations.items()
                },
            }
        return {"error": "Self-awareness module not available"}

    async def _handle_brain_map(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Handle brain map tool invocation."""
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'memory_renderer') and self.agent.memory_renderer:
            graph_json = self.agent.memory_renderer.render_to_json()
            return json.loads(graph_json)
        return {"error": "Memory renderer not available"}

    async def _handle_learn(self, content: str, domain: str = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Handle learn tool invocation."""
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'learning_engine') and self.agent.learning_engine:
            result = self.agent.learning_engine.learn(content, domain=domain)
            return {
                "success": result.success,
                "domain": result.domain,
                "entry_id": result.entry_id,
            }
        return {"error": "Learning engine not available"}

    async def sync_to_workshop(
        self,
        include_memories: bool = True,
        include_learnings: bool = True,
        include_brain_map: bool = True,
    ) -> WorkshopSyncResult:
        """
        Sync agent data to Workshop.

        # BEGIN META: WorkshopIntegration.sync_to_workshop
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Push agent data to CitadelWorkshop
        # SRS: ["SRS-WORKSHOP-001", "SRS-WORKSHOP-002"]
        # Tags: ["sync", "workshop", "push"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        result = WorkshopSyncResult(success=True)

        try:
            import aiohttp

            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
                headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}"} if self.api_key else {}

                # Sync memories
                if include_memories and hasattr(self.agent, 'ltm') and self.agent.ltm:
                    for domain, entries in self.agent.ltm.entries.items():
                        for entry_id, entry in entries.items():
                            try:
                                async with session.post(
                                    f"{self.workshop_url}/api/v1/documents",
                                    headers=headers,
                                    json={
                                        "content": entry.content,
                                        "metadata": {
                                            "source": "nexus_agent",
                                            "domain": domain,
                                            "agent_id": self.agent.agent_id if hasattr(self.agent, 'agent_id') else "",
                                        },
                                    },
                                ) as resp:
                                    if resp.status == 200:
                                        result.items_synced += 1
                                    else:
                                        result.items_failed += 1
                            except Exception as e:
                                result.items_failed += 1
                                result.errors.append(str(e))

                # Sync brain map
                if include_brain_map:
                    brain_data = await self._handle_brain_map()
                    if "error" not in brain_data:
                        try:
                            async with session.post(
                                f"{self.workshop_url}/api/v1/visualizations/brain-map",
                                headers=headers,
                                json=brain_data,
                            ) as resp:
                                if resp.status == 200:
                                    result.items_synced += 1
                        except Exception as e:
                            result.errors.append(f"Brain map sync failed: {e}")

                self._last_sync = datetime.now()

        except ImportError:
            result.success = False
            result.errors.append("aiohttp not installed")
        except Exception as e:
            result.success = False
            result.errors.append(str(e))

        return result

    async def fetch_from_workshop(
        self,
        query: str = "",
        limit: int = 10,
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Fetch relevant content from Workshop.

        # BEGIN META: WorkshopIntegration.fetch_from_workshop
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Pull relevant content from CitadelWorkshop
        # SRS: ["SRS-WORKSHOP-001"]
        # Tags: ["fetch", "workshop", "pull"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        try:
            import aiohttp

            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
                headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}"} if self.api_key else {}

                async with session.post(
                    f"{self.workshop_url}/api/v1/search",
                    headers=headers,
                    json={"query": query, "limit": limit},
                ) as resp:
                    if resp.status == 200:
                        data = await resp.json()
                        return data.get("results", [])

        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[Workshop] Fetch error: {e}")

        return []

    def get_sync_status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get Workshop sync status."""
        return {
            "connected": self.api_key is not None,
            "workshop_url": self.workshop_url,
            "last_sync": self._last_sync.isoformat() if self._last_sync else None,
            "registered_tools": len(self._registered_tools),
            "recent_errors": self._sync_errors[-5:],
        }


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 28: ANALYTICS & AUTOMATION (DATADOG/POSTHOG WIRING)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-ANALYTICS-001, SRS-AUTOMATION-001
# Purpose: Wire agent events to Datadog and PostHog for observability
# Features: Event tracking, metrics, automated workflows
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 28.1: ANALYTICS EVENT MODELS
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class AnalyticsEvent:
    """
    Analytics event for tracking.

    # BEGIN META: AnalyticsEvent
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Structured analytics event
    # SRS: ["SRS-ANALYTICS-001"]
    # Tags: ["analytics", "event", "tracking"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    event_name: str
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
    properties: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    user_id: Optional[str] = None
    agent_id: Optional[str] = None
    session_id: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class MetricPoint:
    """
    Metric data point.

    # BEGIN META: MetricPoint
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Single metric measurement
    # SRS: ["SRS-ANALYTICS-001"]
    # Tags: ["metric", "measurement"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    metric_name: str
    value: float
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
    tags: Dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
    metric_type: str = "gauge"  # gauge, counter, histogram


class AgentAnalytics:
    """
    Analytics integration for agent observability.

    # BEGIN META: AgentAnalytics
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Wire agent events to Datadog and PostHog
    # SRS: ["SRS-ANALYTICS-001"]
    # Tags: ["analytics", "datadog", "posthog", "observability"]
    # Categories: ["Analytics"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["Observability", "Metrics"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Comprehensive analytics integration sending events and metrics
    #              to Datadog and PostHog for full observability.
    # END META
    """

    # Event types to track
    EVENT_TYPES: List[str] = [
        "agent.interaction",
        "agent.memory.inject",
        "agent.memory.search",
        "agent.learning",
        "agent.xp.award",
        "agent.rank.change",
        "agent.badge.unlock",
        "agent.mission.complete",
        "agent.skill.unlock",
        "agent.error",
        "agent.session.start",
        "agent.session.end",
    ]

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent_id: str = "",
        agent_name: str = "",
        enable_datadog: bool = True,
        enable_posthog: bool = True,
    ):
        """
        Initialize analytics.

        # BEGIN META: AgentAnalytics.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize analytics connections
        # SRS: ["SRS-ANALYTICS-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "analytics"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.agent_id = agent_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.agent_name = agent_name or "NexusAgent"
        self.enable_datadog = enable_datadog
        self.enable_posthog = enable_posthog

        self._datadog_client = None
        self._posthog_client = None
        self._event_buffer: List[AnalyticsEvent] = []
        self._metric_buffer: List[MetricPoint] = []
        self._buffer_size: int = 100

        # Initialize clients
        self._init_datadog()
        self._init_posthog()

    def _init_datadog(self):
        """Initialize Datadog client."""
        if not self.enable_datadog or not DATADOG_AVAILABLE:
            return

        api_key = _get_oad_token(*OAD_TOKEN_KEYS.get("datadog", []))
        if api_key:
            try:
                dd_initialize(api_key=api_key)
                self._datadog_client = statsd
                print("[Analytics] Datadog initialized")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[Analytics] Datadog init failed: {e}")

    def _init_posthog(self):
        """Initialize PostHog client."""
        if not self.enable_posthog or not POSTHOG_AVAILABLE:
            return

        api_key = _get_oad_token(*OAD_TOKEN_KEYS.get("posthog", []))
        if api_key:
            try:
                self._posthog_client = Posthog(
                    project_api_key=api_key,
                    host=os.getenv("POSTHOG_HOST", "https://app.posthog.com"),
                )
                print("[Analytics] PostHog initialized")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[Analytics] PostHog init failed: {e}")

    def track_event(
        self,
        event_name: str,
        properties: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        user_id: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """
        Track an analytics event.

        # BEGIN META: AgentAnalytics.track_event
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Send event to analytics platforms
        # SRS: ["SRS-ANALYTICS-001"]
        # Tags: ["track", "event", "analytics"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        event = AnalyticsEvent(
            event_name=event_name,
            properties=properties or {},
            user_id=user_id,
            agent_id=self.agent_id,
        )

        # Add to buffer
        self._event_buffer.append(event)

        # Send to PostHog
        if self._posthog_client:
            try:
                self._posthog_client.capture(
                    distinct_id=user_id or self.agent_id,
                    event=event_name,
                    properties={
                        **event.properties,
                        "agent_id": self.agent_id,
                        "agent_name": self.agent_name,
                    },
                )
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[Analytics] PostHog error: {e}")

        # Send to Datadog as custom event
        if self._datadog_client:
            try:
                self._datadog_client.event(
                    title=event_name,
                    text=json.dumps(event.properties),
                    tags=[
                        f"agent_id:{self.agent_id}",
                        f"agent_name:{self.agent_name}",
                    ],
                )
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[Analytics] Datadog event error: {e}")

        # Flush if buffer is full
        if len(self._event_buffer) >= self._buffer_size:
            self._flush_events()

    def track_metric(
        self,
        metric_name: str,
        value: float,
        tags: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        metric_type: str = "gauge",
    ):
        """
        Track a metric.

        # BEGIN META: AgentAnalytics.track_metric
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Send metric to Datadog
        # SRS: ["SRS-ANALYTICS-001"]
        # Tags: ["track", "metric", "datadog"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        metric = MetricPoint(
            metric_name=metric_name,
            value=value,
            tags=tags or {},
            metric_type=metric_type,
        )

        self._metric_buffer.append(metric)

        if self._datadog_client:
            try:
                full_tags = [
                    f"agent_id:{self.agent_id}",
                    f"agent_name:{self.agent_name}",
                ] + [f"{k}:{v}" for k, v in (tags or {}).items()]

                if metric_type == "gauge":
                    self._datadog_client.gauge(metric_name, value, tags=full_tags)
                elif metric_type == "counter":
                    self._datadog_client.increment(metric_name, value, tags=full_tags)
                elif metric_type == "histogram":
                    self._datadog_client.histogram(metric_name, value, tags=full_tags)

            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[Analytics] Datadog metric error: {e}")

    def track_interaction(
        self,
        user_input: str,
        response_length: int,
        response_time_ms: float,
        xp_earned: int = 0,
    ):
        """
        Track an agent interaction.

        # BEGIN META: AgentAnalytics.track_interaction
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Track interaction metrics and events
        # SRS: ["SRS-ANALYTICS-001"]
        # Tags: ["track", "interaction"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        # Track event
        self.track_event("agent.interaction", {
            "input_length": len(user_input),
            "response_length": response_length,
            "response_time_ms": response_time_ms,
            "xp_earned": xp_earned,
        })

        # Track metrics
        self.track_metric("agent.interaction.response_time", response_time_ms, metric_type="histogram")
        self.track_metric("agent.interaction.response_length", response_length, metric_type="gauge")
        if xp_earned > 0:
            self.track_metric("agent.xp.earned", xp_earned, metric_type="counter")

    def track_memory_operation(
        self,
        operation: str,  # inject, search, consolidate
        source: str,  # stm, ltm
        count: int = 1,
    ):
        """Track memory operation."""
        self.track_event(f"agent.memory.{operation}", {
            "source": source,
            "count": count,
        })
        self.track_metric(f"agent.memory.{operation}", count, {"source": source}, metric_type="counter")

    def track_learning(self, domain: str, content_length: int):
        """Track learning event."""
        self.track_event("agent.learning", {
            "domain": domain,
            "content_length": content_length,
        })
        self.track_metric("agent.learning.count", 1, {"domain": domain}, metric_type="counter")

    def track_rank_change(self, old_rank: str, new_rank: str, total_xp: int):
        """Track rank change."""
        self.track_event("agent.rank.change", {
            "old_rank": old_rank,
            "new_rank": new_rank,
            "total_xp": total_xp,
        })

    def track_badge_unlock(self, badge_name: str, badge_xp: int):
        """Track badge unlock."""
        self.track_event("agent.badge.unlock", {
            "badge_name": badge_name,
            "badge_xp": badge_xp,
        })

    def _flush_events(self):
        """Flush event buffer."""
        self._event_buffer = []

    def get_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get analytics stats."""
        return {
            "agent_id": self.agent_id,
            "datadog_connected": self._datadog_client is not None,
            "posthog_connected": self._posthog_client is not None,
            "events_buffered": len(self._event_buffer),
            "metrics_buffered": len(self._metric_buffer),
        }


# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 28.2: AUTOMATION ENGINE
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class AutomationRule:
    """
    Automation rule definition.

    # BEGIN META: AutomationRule
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define automated action trigger
    # SRS: ["SRS-AUTOMATION-001"]
    # Tags: ["automation", "rule", "trigger"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    name: str = ""
    description: str = ""
    trigger_event: str = ""  # Event that triggers this rule
    conditions: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Conditions to match
    actions: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)  # Actions to execute
    enabled: bool = True
    priority: int = 0
    cooldown_seconds: int = 0
    last_triggered: Optional[str] = None


class AutomationEngine:
    """
    Event-driven automation engine.

    # BEGIN META: AutomationEngine
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Execute automated actions based on agent events
    # SRS: ["SRS-AUTOMATION-001"]
    # Tags: ["automation", "engine", "events"]
    # Categories: ["Automation"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["Event Processing", "Workflows"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Event-driven automation that triggers actions based on
    #              agent events like rank changes, badge unlocks, etc.
    # END META
    """

    def __init__(self, agent: Any):
        """
        Initialize automation engine.

        # BEGIN META: AutomationEngine.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize automation with default rules
        # SRS: ["SRS-AUTOMATION-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "automation"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.agent = agent
        self._rules: Dict[str, AutomationRule] = {}
        self._event_handlers: Dict[str, List[AutomationRule]] = {}
        self._execution_log: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

        # Register default rules
        self._register_default_rules()

    def _register_default_rules(self):
        """Register default automation rules."""
        # Rank up notification
        self.register_rule(AutomationRule(
            name="rank_up_broadcast",
            description="Broadcast rank changes to configured channels",
            trigger_event="rank_change",
            conditions={},
            actions=[
                {"type": "discord_broadcast", "template": "rank_up"},
                {"type": "slack_broadcast", "template": "rank_up"},
            ],
            cooldown_seconds=60,
        ))

        # Badge unlock notification
        self.register_rule(AutomationRule(
            name="badge_unlock_broadcast",
            description="Broadcast badge unlocks",
            trigger_event="badge_unlock",
            conditions={},
            actions=[
                {"type": "discord_broadcast", "template": "badge"},
            ],
            cooldown_seconds=30,
        ))

        # Memory consolidation trigger
        self.register_rule(AutomationRule(
            name="auto_consolidate",
            description="Automatically consolidate STM to LTM",
            trigger_event="stm_high_usage",
            conditions={"usage_percent": {"gte": 80}},
            actions=[
                {"type": "consolidate_memory"},
            ],
            cooldown_seconds=300,
        ))

        # Workshop sync trigger
        self.register_rule(AutomationRule(
            name="auto_workshop_sync",
            description="Sync to Workshop on significant learning",
            trigger_event="learning",
            conditions={"count": {"gte": 10}},
            actions=[
                {"type": "workshop_sync"},
            ],
            cooldown_seconds=600,
        ))

    def register_rule(self, rule: AutomationRule):
        """
        Register an automation rule.

        # BEGIN META: AutomationEngine.register_rule
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Add rule to automation engine
        # SRS: ["SRS-AUTOMATION-001"]
        # Tags: ["register", "rule"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self._rules[rule.id] = rule

        # Index by trigger event
        if rule.trigger_event not in self._event_handlers:
            self._event_handlers[rule.trigger_event] = []
        self._event_handlers[rule.trigger_event].append(rule)

        # Sort by priority
        self._event_handlers[rule.trigger_event].sort(key=lambda r: r.priority, reverse=True)

    def trigger_event(self, event_name: str, event_data: Dict[str, Any]):
        """
        Trigger an event and execute matching rules.

        # BEGIN META: AutomationEngine.trigger_event
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Process event and execute matching automation rules
        # SRS: ["SRS-AUTOMATION-001"]
        # Tags: ["trigger", "event", "execute"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        rules = self._event_handlers.get(event_name, [])

        for rule in rules:
            if not rule.enabled:
                continue

            # Check cooldown
            if rule.last_triggered:
                try:
                    last = datetime.fromisoformat(rule.last_triggered)
                    elapsed = (datetime.now() - last).total_seconds()
                    if elapsed < rule.cooldown_seconds:
                        continue
                except (ValueError, TypeError):
                    pass

            # Check conditions
            if not self._check_conditions(rule.conditions, event_data):
                continue

            # Execute actions
            for action in rule.actions:
                self._execute_action(action, event_data)

            # Update last triggered
            rule.last_triggered = datetime.now().isoformat()

            # Log execution
            self._execution_log.append({
                "rule_id": rule.id,
                "rule_name": rule.name,
                "event": event_name,
                "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
            })

    def _check_conditions(self, conditions: Dict[str, Any], data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """Check if conditions are met."""
        for key, condition in conditions.items():
            value = data.get(key)

            if isinstance(condition, dict):
                # Comparison operators
                if "gte" in condition and value < condition["gte"]:
                    return False
                if "lte" in condition and value > condition["lte"]:
                    return False
                if "eq" in condition and value != condition["eq"]:
                    return False
            else:
                # Direct equality
                if value != condition:
                    return False

        return True

    def _execute_action(self, action: Dict[str, Any], event_data: Dict[str, Any]):
        """Execute an automation action."""
        action_type = action.get("type", "")

        if action_type == "discord_broadcast":
            self._action_discord_broadcast(action, event_data)
        elif action_type == "slack_broadcast":
            self._action_slack_broadcast(action, event_data)
        elif action_type == "consolidate_memory":
            self._action_consolidate_memory()
        elif action_type == "workshop_sync":
            self._action_workshop_sync()
        elif action_type == "webhook":
            self._action_webhook(action, event_data)

    def _action_discord_broadcast(self, action: Dict[str, Any], event_data: Dict[str, Any]):
        """Execute Discord broadcast action."""
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'broadcast_integrations'):
            template = action.get("template", "default")
            message = self._format_broadcast_message(template, event_data)
            # Would call broadcast integration here
            print(f"[Automation] Discord broadcast: {message[:100]}...")

    def _action_slack_broadcast(self, action: Dict[str, Any], event_data: Dict[str, Any]):
        """Execute Slack broadcast action."""
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'broadcast_integrations'):
            template = action.get("template", "default")
            message = self._format_broadcast_message(template, event_data)
            print(f"[Automation] Slack broadcast: {message[:100]}...")

    def _action_consolidate_memory(self):
        """Execute memory consolidation action."""
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'stm') and hasattr(self.agent, 'ltm'):
            candidates = self.agent.stm.get_candidates_for_consolidation()
            if candidates:
                count = self.agent.ltm.consolidate_from_stm(candidates)
                print(f"[Automation] Consolidated {count} memories to LTM")

    def _action_workshop_sync(self):
        """Execute Workshop sync action."""
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'workshop_integration'):
            asyncio.create_task(self.agent.workshop_integration.sync_to_workshop())
            print("[Automation] Workshop sync triggered")

    def _action_webhook(self, action: Dict[str, Any], event_data: Dict[str, Any]):
        """Execute webhook action."""
        url = action.get("url")
        if url:
            try:
                import requests
                requests.post(url, json=event_data, timeout=5)
                print(f"[Automation] Webhook called: {url}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[Automation] Webhook error: {e}")

    def _format_broadcast_message(self, template: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
        """Format broadcast message from template."""
        templates = {
            "rank_up": f"🎉 **Rank Up!** {data.get('old_rank', '')} → {data.get('new_rank', '')}",
            "badge": f"🏆 **Badge Unlocked!** {data.get('badge_name', '')}",
            "default": str(data),
        }
        return templates.get(template, templates["default"])

    def get_rules(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get all registered rules."""
        return [
            {
                "id": r.id,
                "name": r.name,
                "description": r.description,
                "trigger": r.trigger_event,
                "enabled": r.enabled,
                "last_triggered": r.last_triggered,
            }
            for r in self._rules.values()
        ]

    def get_execution_log(self, limit: int = 50) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get recent execution log."""
        return self._execution_log[-limit:]


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 29: COGNITIVE SYSTEM INTEGRATION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-INTEGRATE-001
# Purpose: Wire cognitive architecture into NexusTamagotchiAgentPro
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


def integrate_cognitive_systems(agent: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Integrate cognitive architecture systems into agent.

    # BEGIN META: integrate_cognitive_systems
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Wire STM, LTM, Self-Awareness, and other systems into agent
    # SRS: ["SRS-INTEGRATE-001"]
    # Tags: ["integration", "cognitive", "wire"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META

    Args:
        agent: The NexusTamagotchiAgentPro instance

    Returns:
        Dictionary of initialized systems
    """
    systems = {}

    agent_name = getattr(agent, 'agent_name', 'NexusAgent')
    agent_id = getattr(agent, 'agent_id', str(uuid.uuid4()))

    # Get OpenAI client if available
    openai_client = None
    if OpenAI:
        api_key = _get_oad_token(*OAD_TOKEN_KEYS.get("openai", []))
        if api_key:
            openai_client = OpenAI(api_key=api_key)

    # Initialize STM
    try:
        stm = ShortTermMemoryBuffer(openai_client=openai_client)
        agent.stm = stm
        systems["stm"] = stm
        print(f"[Cognitive] STM initialized with {stm.get_stats()['total_entries']} entries")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] STM init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize LTM
    try:
        ltm = LongTermMemory(openai_client=openai_client)
        agent.ltm = ltm
        systems["ltm"] = ltm
        print(f"[Cognitive] LTM initialized with {ltm.get_stats()['total_entries']} entries")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] LTM init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize Learning Engine
    try:
        learning = DomainLearningEngine(openai_client=openai_client)
        agent.learning_engine = learning
        systems["learning_engine"] = learning
        print("[Cognitive] Learning engine initialized")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] Learning engine init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize Context Rehydrator
    try:
        rehydrator = ContextRehydrator(
            stm=systems.get("stm"),
            ltm=systems.get("ltm"),
            learning_engine=systems.get("learning_engine"),
        )
        agent.context_rehydrator = rehydrator
        systems["rehydrator"] = rehydrator
        print("[Cognitive] Context rehydrator initialized")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] Rehydrator init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize Self-Awareness
    try:
        awareness = SelfAwarenessModule(
            agent_id=agent_id,
            agent_name=agent_name,
            stm=systems.get("stm"),
            ltm=systems.get("ltm"),
            learning_engine=systems.get("learning_engine"),
        )
        agent.self_awareness = awareness
        systems["self_awareness"] = awareness
        print("[Cognitive] Self-awareness module initialized")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] Self-awareness init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize Memory Graph Renderer
    try:
        if systems.get("ltm"):
            renderer = MemoryGraphRenderer(
                ltm=systems["ltm"],
                stm=systems.get("stm"),
            )
            agent.memory_renderer = renderer
            systems["memory_renderer"] = renderer
            print("[Cognitive] Memory graph renderer initialized")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] Memory renderer init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize Workshop Integration
    try:
        workshop = WorkshopIntegration(agent)
        agent.workshop_integration = workshop
        systems["workshop"] = workshop
        print(f"[Cognitive] Workshop integration initialized ({len(workshop.get_registered_tools())} tools)")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] Workshop init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize Analytics
    try:
        analytics = AgentAnalytics(
            agent_id=agent_id,
            agent_name=agent_name,
        )
        agent.analytics = analytics
        systems["analytics"] = analytics
        print(f"[Cognitive] Analytics initialized: {analytics.get_stats()}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] Analytics init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize Automation Engine
    try:
        automation = AutomationEngine(agent)
        agent.automation = automation
        systems["automation"] = automation
        print(f"[Cognitive] Automation engine initialized ({len(automation.get_rules())} rules)")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] Automation init failed: {e}")

    # Initialize Response Config
    try:
        response_config = ResponseLengthConfig("balanced")
        agent.response_config = response_config
        systems["response_config"] = response_config
        print("[Cognitive] Response configuration initialized")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[Cognitive] Response config init failed: {e}")

    return systems


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 30: EXTENDED KNOWLEDGE GRAPH SYSTEM
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001, SRS-KNOWLEDGE-002
# Purpose: Build and manage semantic knowledge graph from agent interactions
# Features: Entity extraction, relationship mapping, concept clustering
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 30.1: KNOWLEDGE GRAPH DATA MODELS
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class KnowledgeEntity:
    """
    Entity in the knowledge graph.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeEntity
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Represent an entity/concept in the knowledge graph
    # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001"]
    # Tags: ["knowledge", "entity", "graph"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    name: str = ""
    entity_type: str = "concept"  # concept, person, place, thing, action, topic
    description: str = ""
    attributes: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    embedding: Optional[List[float]] = None
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
    updated_at: Optional[str] = None
    mention_count: int = 1
    last_mentioned_at: Optional[str] = None
    source_interactions: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    confidence: float = 1.0
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert to dictionary."""
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "name": self.name,
            "entity_type": self.entity_type,
            "description": self.description,
            "attributes": self.attributes,
            "mention_count": self.mention_count,
            "confidence": self.confidence,
            "created_at": self.created_at,
            "metadata": self.metadata,
        }

    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "KnowledgeEntity":
        """Create from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            id=data.get("id", str(uuid.uuid4())),
            name=data.get("name", ""),
            entity_type=data.get("entity_type", "concept"),
            description=data.get("description", ""),
            attributes=data.get("attributes", {}),
            embedding=data.get("embedding"),
            mention_count=data.get("mention_count", 1),
            confidence=data.get("confidence", 1.0),
            created_at=data.get("created_at", ""),
            metadata=data.get("metadata", {}),
        )


@dataclass
class KnowledgeRelationship:
    """
    Relationship between entities in the knowledge graph.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeRelationship
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Represent relationship between entities
    # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001"]
    # Tags: ["knowledge", "relationship", "edge"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    source_id: str = ""
    target_id: str = ""
    relationship_type: str = "related_to"  # related_to, is_a, has_a, part_of, causes, etc.
    strength: float = 1.0  # 0.0-1.0
    bidirectional: bool = True
    description: str = ""
    evidence: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Interaction IDs that support this
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
    confidence: float = 1.0
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class KnowledgeCluster:
    """
    Cluster of related entities.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeCluster
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Group related entities into semantic clusters
    # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-002"]
    # Tags: ["knowledge", "cluster", "grouping"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    name: str = ""
    description: str = ""
    entity_ids: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    centroid: Optional[List[float]] = None
    coherence_score: float = 0.0
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())
    representative_terms: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class ConceptPath:
    """
    Path between two concepts in the knowledge graph.

    # BEGIN META: ConceptPath
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Represent traversal path between concepts
    # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-002"]
    # Tags: ["knowledge", "path", "traversal"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    source_id: str
    target_id: str
    path_nodes: List[str]  # Entity IDs in order
    path_edges: List[str]  # Relationship IDs in order
    total_distance: float
    relationship_types: List[str]


class KnowledgeGraph:
    """
    Semantic knowledge graph built from agent interactions.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeGraph
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Build and query semantic knowledge graph
    # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001", "SRS-KNOWLEDGE-002"]
    # Tags: ["knowledge", "graph", "semantic"]
    # Categories: ["Knowledge Management"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["Knowledge Graphs", "NLP"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Builds semantic knowledge graph from interactions with entity
    #              extraction, relationship mapping, and concept clustering.
    # END META
    """

    RELATIONSHIP_TYPES: List[str] = [
        "related_to",
        "is_a",
        "has_a",
        "part_of",
        "causes",
        "caused_by",
        "similar_to",
        "opposite_of",
        "used_for",
        "created_by",
        "contains",
        "depends_on",
        "influences",
        "precedes",
        "follows",
    ]

    ENTITY_TYPES: List[str] = [
        "concept",
        "person",
        "organization",
        "place",
        "thing",
        "action",
        "event",
        "topic",
        "skill",
        "technology",
    ]

    def __init__(
        self,
        storage_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
        openai_client: Optional[Any] = None,
    ):
        """
        Initialize knowledge graph.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeGraph.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize graph storage and entity/relationship maps
        # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "knowledge", "graph"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.storage_dir = storage_dir or Path.home() / ".nexus_agent_data" / "knowledge_graph"
        self.storage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self._openai_client = openai_client

        # Entity and relationship storage
        self.entities: Dict[str, KnowledgeEntity] = {}
        self.relationships: Dict[str, KnowledgeRelationship] = {}
        self.clusters: Dict[str, KnowledgeCluster] = {}

        # Indexes for fast lookup
        self._entity_by_name: Dict[str, str] = {}  # name.lower() -> entity_id
        self._entity_by_type: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}  # type -> [entity_ids]
        self._relationships_by_source: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
        self._relationships_by_target: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}

        # FAISS index for semantic search
        self._entity_faiss: Optional[AgentFAISSWrapper] = None

        # Load existing data
        self._load()

    def _load(self):
        """Load knowledge graph from disk."""
        entities_path = self.storage_dir / "entities.json"
        rels_path = self.storage_dir / "relationships.json"
        clusters_path = self.storage_dir / "clusters.json"

        if entities_path.exists():
            try:
                with open(entities_path, "r") as f:
                    data = json.load(f)
                    for eid, edata in data.items():
                        entity = KnowledgeEntity.from_dict(edata)
                        self.entities[eid] = entity
                        self._index_entity(entity)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[KnowledgeGraph] Failed to load entities: {e}")

        if rels_path.exists():
            try:
                with open(rels_path, "r") as f:
                    data = json.load(f)
                    for rid, rdata in data.items():
                        rel = KnowledgeRelationship(
                            id=rdata.get("id", rid),
                            source_id=rdata.get("source_id", ""),
                            target_id=rdata.get("target_id", ""),
                            relationship_type=rdata.get("relationship_type", "related_to"),
                            strength=rdata.get("strength", 1.0),
                            confidence=rdata.get("confidence", 1.0),
                        )
                        self.relationships[rid] = rel
                        self._index_relationship(rel)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[KnowledgeGraph] Failed to load relationships: {e}")

    def _save(self):
        """Save knowledge graph to disk."""
        entities_path = self.storage_dir / "entities.json"
        rels_path = self.storage_dir / "relationships.json"

        try:
            with open(entities_path, "w") as f:
                json.dump({eid: e.to_dict() for eid, e in self.entities.items()}, f, indent=2)

            with open(rels_path, "w") as f:
                rels_data = {}
                for rid, r in self.relationships.items():
                    rels_data[rid] = {
                        "id": r.id,
                        "source_id": r.source_id,
                        "target_id": r.target_id,
                        "relationship_type": r.relationship_type,
                        "strength": r.strength,
                        "confidence": r.confidence,
                    }
                json.dump(rels_data, f, indent=2)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[KnowledgeGraph] Failed to save: {e}")

    def _index_entity(self, entity: KnowledgeEntity):
        """Index entity for fast lookup."""
        self._entity_by_name[entity.name.lower()] = entity.id

        if entity.entity_type not in self._entity_by_type:
            self._entity_by_type[entity.entity_type] = []
        if entity.id not in self._entity_by_type[entity.entity_type]:
            self._entity_by_type[entity.entity_type].append(entity.id)

    def _index_relationship(self, rel: KnowledgeRelationship):
        """Index relationship for fast lookup."""
        if rel.source_id not in self._relationships_by_source:
            self._relationships_by_source[rel.source_id] = []
        if rel.id not in self._relationships_by_source[rel.source_id]:
            self._relationships_by_source[rel.source_id].append(rel.id)

        if rel.target_id not in self._relationships_by_target:
            self._relationships_by_target[rel.target_id] = []
        if rel.id not in self._relationships_by_target[rel.target_id]:
            self._relationships_by_target[rel.target_id].append(rel.id)

    def add_entity(
        self,
        name: str,
        entity_type: str = "concept",
        description: str = "",
        attributes: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        source_interaction_id: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> KnowledgeEntity:
        """
        Add or update an entity in the knowledge graph.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeGraph.add_entity
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Add new entity or update existing
        # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001"]
        # Tags: ["add", "entity", "graph"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        name_lower = name.lower()

        # Check if entity exists
        if name_lower in self._entity_by_name:
            entity = self.entities[self._entity_by_name[name_lower]]
            entity.mention_count += 1
            entity.last_mentioned_at = datetime.now().isoformat()
            entity.updated_at = datetime.now().isoformat()
            if source_interaction_id:
                entity.source_interactions.append(source_interaction_id)
            if attributes:
                entity.attributes.update(attributes)
            self._save()
            return entity

        # Create new entity
        entity = KnowledgeEntity(
            name=name,
            entity_type=entity_type,
            description=description,
            attributes=attributes or {},
            source_interactions=[source_interaction_id] if source_interaction_id else [],
        )

        self.entities[entity.id] = entity
        self._index_entity(entity)
        self._save()

        return entity

    def add_relationship(
        self,
        source_name: str,
        target_name: str,
        relationship_type: str = "related_to",
        strength: float = 1.0,
        evidence_interaction_id: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Optional[KnowledgeRelationship]:
        """
        Add relationship between entities.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeGraph.add_relationship
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Create relationship between entities
        # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001"]
        # Tags: ["add", "relationship", "edge"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        # Get or create entities
        source = self.get_entity_by_name(source_name)
        target = self.get_entity_by_name(target_name)

        if not source:
            source = self.add_entity(source_name)
        if not target:
            target = self.add_entity(target_name)

        # Check for existing relationship
        for rid in self._relationships_by_source.get(source.id, []):
            rel = self.relationships.get(rid)
            if rel and rel.target_id == target.id and rel.relationship_type == relationship_type:
                # Update existing
                rel.strength = min(1.0, rel.strength + 0.1)
                if evidence_interaction_id:
                    rel.evidence.append(evidence_interaction_id)
                self._save()
                return rel

        # Create new relationship
        rel = KnowledgeRelationship(
            source_id=source.id,
            target_id=target.id,
            relationship_type=relationship_type,
            strength=strength,
            evidence=[evidence_interaction_id] if evidence_interaction_id else [],
        )

        self.relationships[rel.id] = rel
        self._index_relationship(rel)
        self._save()

        return rel

    def get_entity_by_name(self, name: str) -> Optional[KnowledgeEntity]:
        """Get entity by name (case-insensitive)."""
        entity_id = self._entity_by_name.get(name.lower())
        return self.entities.get(entity_id) if entity_id else None

    def get_entity_by_id(self, entity_id: str) -> Optional[KnowledgeEntity]:
        """Get entity by ID."""
        return self.entities.get(entity_id)

    def get_entities_by_type(self, entity_type: str) -> List[KnowledgeEntity]:
        """Get all entities of a type."""
        entity_ids = self._entity_by_type.get(entity_type, [])
        return [self.entities[eid] for eid in entity_ids if eid in self.entities]

    def get_relationships_for_entity(
        self,
        entity_id: str,
        direction: str = "both",  # source, target, both
    ) -> List[KnowledgeRelationship]:
        """Get all relationships for an entity."""
        relationships = []

        if direction in ("source", "both"):
            for rid in self._relationships_by_source.get(entity_id, []):
                if rid in self.relationships:
                    relationships.append(self.relationships[rid])

        if direction in ("target", "both"):
            for rid in self._relationships_by_target.get(entity_id, []):
                if rid in self.relationships:
                    relationships.append(self.relationships[rid])

        return relationships

    def find_path(
        self,
        source_name: str,
        target_name: str,
        max_depth: int = 5,
    ) -> Optional[ConceptPath]:
        """
        Find shortest path between two concepts.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeGraph.find_path
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Find shortest path between concepts using BFS
        # SRS: ["SRS-KNOWLEDGE-002"]
        # Tags: ["path", "traversal", "bfs"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        source = self.get_entity_by_name(source_name)
        target = self.get_entity_by_name(target_name)

        if not source or not target:
            return None

        if source.id == target.id:
            return ConceptPath(
                source_id=source.id,
                target_id=target.id,
