        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-003"]
        # Tags: ["init", "ltm", "manager"]
        # Categories: ["Memory"]
        # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Constructor for MiraLTMManager
        # END META
        """
        self.config = config or MiraMemoryConfig()
        self.embedding_fn = embedding_fn
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(__name__)

        # Storage
        self.storage_path = Path(self.config.storage_base_path) / "ltm"
        self.storage_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        # Domain indexes
        self._domains: Dict[str, MiraLTMDomainIndex] = {}
        self._initialize_domains()

        # Cross-domain linking graph
        self._links: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}  # entry_id -> related_entry_ids

        # Metrics
        self._metrics = {
            "stores": 0,
            "retrievals": 0,
            "consolidations": 0,
            "cross_domain_links": 0,
        }

    def _initialize_domains(self) -> None:
        """Initialize all domain indexes."""
        for domain in self.config.ltm_domains:
            self._domains[domain] = MiraLTMDomainIndex(
                domain=domain,
                storage_path=self.storage_path,
                config=self.config,
                logger=self.logger,
            )
        self.logger.info(f"Initialized {len(self._domains)} LTM domain indexes")

    def _generate_embedding(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
        """Generate embedding for text."""
        if self.embedding_fn:
            return self.embedding_fn(text)
        return [0.0] * self.config.embedding_dimension

    def store(
        self,
        content: str,
        domain: Optional[str] = None,
        source: str = "direct",
        user_id: Optional[str] = None,
        tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
        consolidation_source: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Optional[MiraLTMEntry]:
        """
        Store content in LTM.

        # BEGIN META: MiraLTMManager.store
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Store new entry in appropriate LTM domain
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-003", "SRS-LTM-001"]
        # Reflex: REFLEX_MEMORY_STORE
        # Tags: ["ltm", "store", "memory"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "LTM"]
        # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Store content in LTM with automatic domain routing
        # END META

        Args:
            content: Content to store
            domain: Optional specific domain (auto-routed if not provided)
            source: Source of entry
            user_id: Optional user ID
            tags: Optional tags
            consolidation_source: Optional STM entry ID if from consolidation

        Returns:
            Created MiraLTMEntry or None
        """
        if not content or not content.strip():
            return None

        # Auto-route to domain if not specified
        if not domain:
            domain, confidence = self._route_to_domain(content)
        else:
            confidence = 0.9

        if domain not in self._domains:
            domain = "conversation"  # Default domain

        # Generate embedding
        embedding = self._generate_embedding(content)

        # Create entry
        entry = MiraLTMEntry(
            domain=domain,
            content=content.strip(),
            content_hash=hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()[:16],
            summary=content[:200] if len(content) > 200 else content,
            embedding=embedding,
            trust_score=0.7,
            consolidation_source=consolidation_source or "",
            consolidation_method="direct" if not consolidation_source else "consolidation",
            domain_confidence=confidence,
            tags=tags or [],
            source=source,
            user_id=user_id,
        )

        # Insert into domain index
        domain_index = self._domains[domain]
        if domain_index.insert(entry):
            self._metrics["stores"] += 1
            self.logger.debug(f"Stored in LTM/{domain}: {content[:50]}...")
            return entry

        return None

    def _route_to_domain(self, content: str) -> Tuple[str, float]:
        """Route content to most appropriate domain."""
        content_lower = content.lower()

        domain_patterns = {
            "facts": [r"\b(is|are|was|were)\b.*\b(true|fact|known)\b", r"\bdefinition\b"],
            "skills": [r"\b(can|able|skill|capability|learn)\b"],
            "user_preferences": [r"\b(like|prefer|favorite|want|love|hate)\b"],
            "emotional_patterns": [r"\b(feel|emotion|happy|sad|angry|excited|worried)\b"],
            "procedural": [r"\bhow to\b", r"\bsteps?\b", r"\bprocess\b"],
            "episodic": [r"\bremember\b", r"\byesterday\b", r"\blast time\b"],
            "semantic_knowledge": [r"\bmeans?\b", r"\bconcept\b", r"\btheory\b"],
        }

        for domain, patterns in domain_patterns.items():
            for pattern in patterns:
                if re.search(pattern, content_lower):
                    return domain, 0.7

        return "conversation", 0.5

    def retrieve(
        self,
        query: str,
        domain: Optional[str] = None,
        top_k: int = 5,
    ) -> List[MiraSearchResult]:
        """
        Retrieve from LTM.

        # BEGIN META: MiraLTMManager.retrieve
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Retrieve relevant entries from LTM
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-003", "SRS-SEARCH-001"]
        # Reflex: REFLEX_MEMORY_RETRIEVE
        # Tags: ["ltm", "retrieve", "search"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Search"]
        # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Retrieve from LTM with optional domain filter
        # END META

        Args:
            query: Search query
            domain: Optional domain filter
            top_k: Number of results

        Returns:
            List of MiraSearchResult
        """
        if not query:
            return []

        self._metrics["retrievals"] += 1

        # Generate query embedding
        query_embedding = self._generate_embedding(query)

        results = []

        if domain and domain in self._domains:
            # Search specific domain
            domain_results = self._domains[domain].search(query_embedding, top_k)
            for similarity, entry in domain_results:
                results.append(MiraSearchResult(
                    entry=entry,
                    similarity_score=similarity,
                    attention_score=self._compute_ltm_attention(entry),
                    source="ltm",
                    domain=entry.domain,
                ))
        else:
            # Search all domains
            for domain_name, domain_index in self._domains.items():
                domain_results = domain_index.search(query_embedding, top_k)
                for similarity, entry in domain_results:
                    results.append(MiraSearchResult(
                        entry=entry,
                        similarity_score=similarity,
                        attention_score=self._compute_ltm_attention(entry),
                        source="ltm",
                        domain=entry.domain,
                    ))

        # Sort by combined score and return top_k
        results.sort(key=lambda r: r.combined_score, reverse=True)
        return results[:top_k]

    def _compute_ltm_attention(self, entry: MiraLTMEntry) -> float:
        """Compute attention score for LTM entry."""
        score = 0.0

        # Trust score
        score += entry.trust_score * 0.4

        # Access frequency
        if entry.access_count > 0:
            score += min(0.3, entry.access_count * 0.03)

        # Domain confidence
        score += entry.domain_confidence * 0.2

        # Recency boost
        try:
            last_access = datetime.fromisoformat(entry.last_access_timestamp.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
            hours_old = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - last_access).total_seconds() / 3600
            recency = max(0.0, 1.0 - (hours_old * 0.01))
            score += recency * 0.1
        except:
            pass

        return min(1.0, score)

    def cross_domain_search(
        self,
        query: str,
        domain_weights: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        top_k: int = 5,
    ) -> List[MiraSearchResult]:
        """
        Search across domains with custom weights.

        # BEGIN META: MiraLTMManager.cross_domain_search
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Cross-domain search with custom weighting
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-003", "SRS-SEARCH-001"]
        # Tags: ["ltm", "cross-domain", "search"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Search"]
        # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Cross-domain LTM search with weighted results
        # END META
        """
        if not query:
            return []

        # Default equal weights
        if not domain_weights:
            domain_weights = {d: 1.0 for d in self._domains.keys()}

        query_embedding = self._generate_embedding(query)
        all_results = []

        for domain_name, weight in domain_weights.items():
            if domain_name not in self._domains or weight <= 0:
                continue

            domain_results = self._domains[domain_name].search(query_embedding, top_k * 2)
            for similarity, entry in domain_results:
                # Apply domain weight
                weighted_similarity = similarity * weight
                all_results.append(MiraSearchResult(
                    entry=entry,
                    similarity_score=weighted_similarity,
                    attention_score=self._compute_ltm_attention(entry),
                    source="ltm",
                    domain=entry.domain,
                ))

        # Sort and return top_k
        all_results.sort(key=lambda r: r.combined_score, reverse=True)
        return all_results[:top_k]

    def consolidate_from_stm(
        self,
        stm_entries: List[MiraSTMEntry],
    ) -> List[MiraConsolidationResult]:
        """
        Consolidate STM entries to LTM.

        # BEGIN META: MiraLTMManager.consolidate_from_stm
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Consolidate STM entries to appropriate LTM domains
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-004", "SRS-CONSOLIDATION-001"]
        # Reflex: REFLEX_MEMORY_CONSOLIDATE
        # Tags: ["ltm", "consolidation", "stm"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Consolidation"]
        # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Consolidate STM entries to LTM
        # END META
        """
        results = []

        for stm_entry in stm_entries:
            if stm_entry.consolidated_to_ltm:
                continue

            # Determine target domain
            domain = stm_entry.predicted_domain or "conversation"

            # Store in LTM
            ltm_entry = self.store(
                content=stm_entry.content,
                domain=domain,
                source="consolidation",
                user_id=stm_entry.user_id,
                tags=stm_entry.tags,
                consolidation_source=stm_entry.entry_id,
            )

            result = MiraConsolidationResult(
                stm_entry_id=stm_entry.entry_id,
                ltm_entry_id=ltm_entry.entry_id if ltm_entry else "",
                domain=domain,
                method="direct",
                success=ltm_entry is not None,
                error=None if ltm_entry else "Failed to create LTM entry",
            )
            results.append(result)

            if ltm_entry:
                self._metrics["consolidations"] += 1

        return results

    def apply_trust_decay(self, hours_elapsed: float = 1.0) -> int:
        """
        Apply trust decay to all LTM entries.

        # BEGIN META: MiraLTMManager.apply_trust_decay
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Decay trust scores over time
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-003"]
        # Tags: ["ltm", "trust", "decay"]
        # Categories: ["Memory"]
        # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Apply trust decay to LTM entries
        # END META
        """
        if not self.config.enable_trust_decay:
            return 0

        count = 0
        for domain_index in self._domains.values():
            for entry in domain_index._entries.values():
                old_trust = entry.trust_score
                entry.decay_trust(self.config.decay_rate, hours_elapsed)
                if entry.trust_score != old_trust:
                    count += 1

        return count

    def save_all(self) -> None:
        """Save all domain indexes."""
        for domain_index in self._domains.values():
            domain_index.save()
        self.logger.info("Saved all LTM domain indexes")

    def get_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get LTM manager statistics."""
        domain_stats = {}
        total_entries = 0

        for domain, index in self._domains.items():
            stats = index.get_stats()
            domain_stats[domain] = stats
            total_entries += stats["entry_count"]

        return {
            "total_entries": total_entries,
            "domain_count": len(self._domains),
            "domains": domain_stats,
            "metrics": self._metrics,
        }


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 49-51 AUTO-INTEGRATION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Store reference for further patching
_section48_enhanced_init = NexusTamagotchiAgentPro.__init__

def _ensure_state_dir(agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro") -> Path:
    state_dir = getattr(agent, "state_dir", None)
    if state_dir:
        return Path(state_dir)
    base_dir = Path(".nexus_agent_data") / getattr(agent, "agent_name", "agent")
    base_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    agent.state_dir = base_dir
    return base_dir

def _mira_memory_enhanced_agent_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
    """Enhanced __init__ with Mira memory system."""
    _section48_enhanced_init(self, *args, **kwargs)

    # Add Mira memory integration
    try:
        state_dir = _ensure_state_dir(self)
        # Initialize Mira memory config
        mira_config = MiraMemoryConfig(
            storage_base_path=str(state_dir / "mira_memory"),
        )

        # Initialize STM buffer
        self._mira_stm = MiraSTMBuffer(
            config=mira_config,
            embedding_fn=getattr(self, '_generate_embedding', None),
            logger=self.logger,
        )

        # Initialize LTM manager
        self._mira_ltm = MiraLTMManager(
            config=mira_config,
            embedding_fn=getattr(self, '_generate_embedding', None),
            logger=self.logger,
        )

        # Store config
        self._mira_config = mira_config

        self.logger.info("Mira memory system initialized (STM + LTM)")

    except Exception as e:
        self.logger.warning(f"Mira memory initialization failed: {e}")

NexusTamagotchiAgentPro.__init__ = _mira_memory_enhanced_agent_init


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 52: MIRA ATTENTION MECHANISM (ATTENTIONAL BIAS PILLAR)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Behrouz et al. (2025) - Pillar 2: Attentional Bias
# Controls what memories receive focus during retrieval
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


class MiraAttentionType(Enum):
    """
    Types of attention mechanisms.

    # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionType
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Attention mechanism types for Mira memory
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005", "SRS-ATTENTION-001"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "attention", "enum"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "CognitiveScience"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Attention types in Mira framework
    # END META
    """
    TEMPORAL = "temporal"           # Recent memories get more attention
    SEMANTIC = "semantic"           # Semantically similar get more attention
    EMOTIONAL = "emotional"         # Emotionally resonant get more attention
    GOAL_DIRECTED = "goal_directed" # Task-relevant get more attention
    NOVELTY = "novelty"             # Novel/surprising get more attention
    SOCIAL = "social"               # User-related get more attention
    HYBRID = "hybrid"               # Combination of all mechanisms


class MiraAttentionWeight(Enum):
    """
    Predefined attention weight profiles.

    # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionWeight
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Attention weight profiles
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "attention", "weights"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    BALANCED = "balanced"           # Equal weights
    RECENCY_FOCUSED = "recency"     # Prioritize recent
    RELEVANCE_FOCUSED = "relevance" # Prioritize semantic match
    EMOTIONAL_FOCUSED = "emotional" # Prioritize emotional resonance
    TASK_FOCUSED = "task"           # Prioritize goal relevance


@dataclass
class MiraAttentionHead:
    """
    Single attention head for multi-head attention.

    # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionHead
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Individual attention head in multi-head attention
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005", "SRS-ATTENTION-001"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "attention", "head"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Single attention head for multi-head mechanism
    # END META
    """
    head_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4())[:8])
    attention_type: MiraAttentionType = MiraAttentionType.SEMANTIC
    weight: float = 1.0
    enabled: bool = True
    last_activation: float = 0.0
    activation_count: int = 0


@dataclass
class MiraAttentionContext:
    """
    Context for attention computation.

    # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionContext
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Context for attention mechanism computation
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005", "SRS-ATTENTION-001"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "attention", "context"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Attention context with query, goals, and emotion
    # END META
    """
    query: str = ""
    query_embedding: Optional[List[float]] = None
    current_goal: Optional[str] = None
    current_emotion: str = "neutral"
    user_id: Optional[str] = None
    conversation_id: Optional[str] = None
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    domain_filter: Optional[str] = None
    novelty_threshold: float = 0.5
    recency_window_hours: float = 24.0


@dataclass
class MiraAttentionScore:
    """
    Computed attention score for a memory.

    # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionScore
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Computed attention score result
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "attention", "score"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    memory_id: str = ""
    total_score: float = 0.0
    component_scores: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
    winning_head: str = ""
    is_above_threshold: bool = False
    attention_timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())


class MiraAttentionMechanism:
    """
    Multi-head attention mechanism for memory retrieval.

    # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionMechanism
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Implement Mira attentional bias pillar with multi-head attention
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005", "SRS-ATTENTION-001", "SRS-COGNITION-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_ATTENTION_COMPUTE
    # Enum: ENUM_MIRA_ATTENTION
    # Tags: ["mira", "attention", "multi-head", "pillar-2"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention", "Cognition"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "CognitiveScience"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Supporting: ["compute_attention", "apply_attention_to_results", "_compute_head_score"]
    # Description: Mira Attentional Bias pillar - controls memory focus during retrieval
    # END META

    Implements Pillar 2 of Behrouz et al. (2025) Mira framework.
    Uses multi-head attention to determine which memories receive focus.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: MiraMemoryConfig,
        embedding_fn: Optional[Callable[[str], List[float]]] = None,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
    ):
        """Initialize attention mechanism with config."""
        self.config = config
        self._embedding_fn = embedding_fn
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(__name__)

        # Initialize attention heads
        self._heads: Dict[str, MiraAttentionHead] = {}
        self._initialize_default_heads()

        # Attention threshold
        self.attention_threshold = getattr(config, "attention_threshold", 0.6)

        # Metrics
        self._metrics = {
            "attention_computations": 0,
            "above_threshold_count": 0,
            "below_threshold_count": 0,
            "avg_attention_score": 0.0,
        }

    def _initialize_default_heads(self) -> None:
        """
        Initialize default attention heads.

        # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionMechanism._initialize_default_heads
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Set up default multi-head attention configuration
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005"]
        # Tags: ["attention", "initialization"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # END META
        """
        default_heads = [
            MiraAttentionHead(head_id="temporal", attention_type=MiraAttentionType.TEMPORAL, weight=0.15),
            MiraAttentionHead(head_id="semantic", attention_type=MiraAttentionType.SEMANTIC, weight=0.30),
            MiraAttentionHead(head_id="emotional", attention_type=MiraAttentionType.EMOTIONAL, weight=0.15),
            MiraAttentionHead(head_id="goal", attention_type=MiraAttentionType.GOAL_DIRECTED, weight=0.20),
            MiraAttentionHead(head_id="novelty", attention_type=MiraAttentionType.NOVELTY, weight=0.10),
            MiraAttentionHead(head_id="social", attention_type=MiraAttentionType.SOCIAL, weight=0.10),
        ]

        for head in default_heads:
            self._heads[head.head_id] = head

    def add_head(self, head: MiraAttentionHead) -> None:
        """Add custom attention head."""
        self._heads[head.head_id] = head
        self.logger.info(f"Added attention head: {head.head_id} ({head.attention_type.value})")

    def remove_head(self, head_id: str) -> bool:
        """Remove attention head."""
        if head_id in self._heads:
            del self._heads[head_id]
            return True
        return False

    def set_attention_profile(self, profile: MiraAttentionWeight) -> None:
        """
        Apply predefined attention weight profile.

        # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionMechanism.set_attention_profile
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Apply predefined attention weight profile
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005"]
        # Tags: ["attention", "profile"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # END META
        """
        profiles = {
            MiraAttentionWeight.BALANCED: {
                "temporal": 0.17, "semantic": 0.17, "emotional": 0.17,
                "goal": 0.17, "novelty": 0.16, "social": 0.16,
            },
            MiraAttentionWeight.RECENCY_FOCUSED: {
                "temporal": 0.40, "semantic": 0.25, "emotional": 0.10,
                "goal": 0.15, "novelty": 0.05, "social": 0.05,
            },
            MiraAttentionWeight.RELEVANCE_FOCUSED: {
                "temporal": 0.10, "semantic": 0.45, "emotional": 0.10,
                "goal": 0.25, "novelty": 0.05, "social": 0.05,
            },
            MiraAttentionWeight.EMOTIONAL_FOCUSED: {
                "temporal": 0.10, "semantic": 0.20, "emotional": 0.40,
                "goal": 0.10, "novelty": 0.10, "social": 0.10,
            },
            MiraAttentionWeight.TASK_FOCUSED: {
                "temporal": 0.10, "semantic": 0.30, "emotional": 0.05,
                "goal": 0.45, "novelty": 0.05, "social": 0.05,
            },
        }

        if profile in profiles:
            for head_id, weight in profiles[profile].items():
                if head_id in self._heads:
                    self._heads[head_id].weight = weight
            self.logger.info(f"Applied attention profile: {profile.value}")

    def compute_attention(
        self,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        memory_entry: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> MiraAttentionScore:
        """
        Compute attention score for a memory entry.

        # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionMechanism.compute_attention
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Compute multi-head attention score for memory
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005", "SRS-ATTENTION-001"]
        # Reflex: REFLEX_ATTENTION_COMPUTE
        # Tags: ["attention", "computation", "multi-head"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
        # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Core attention computation with multi-head aggregation
        # END META
        """
        component_scores: Dict[str, float] = {}
        total_score = 0.0
        max_head_score = 0.0
        winning_head = ""

        # Compute each head's score
        for head_id, head in self._heads.items():
            if not head.enabled:
                continue

            head_score = self._compute_head_score(head, context, memory_entry)
            weighted_score = head_score * head.weight
            component_scores[head_id] = weighted_score
            total_score += weighted_score

            # Track activation
            head.last_activation = weighted_score
            head.activation_count += 1

            # Track winning head
            if weighted_score > max_head_score:
                max_head_score = weighted_score
                winning_head = head_id

        # Normalize to 0-1 range
        total_score = min(1.0, total_score)

        # Update metrics
        self._metrics["attention_computations"] += 1
        n = self._metrics["attention_computations"]
        self._metrics["avg_attention_score"] = (
            (self._metrics["avg_attention_score"] * (n - 1) + total_score) / n
        )

        is_above = total_score >= self.attention_threshold
        if is_above:
            self._metrics["above_threshold_count"] += 1
        else:
            self._metrics["below_threshold_count"] += 1

        return MiraAttentionScore(
            memory_id=getattr(memory_entry, 'entry_id', ''),
            total_score=total_score,
            component_scores=component_scores,
            winning_head=winning_head,
            is_above_threshold=is_above,
        )

    def _compute_head_score(
        self,
        head: MiraAttentionHead,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        memory: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> float:
        """
        Compute individual head attention score.

        # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionMechanism._compute_head_score
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Compute attention score for single head
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005"]
        # Tags: ["attention", "head", "computation"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # END META
        """
        if head.attention_type == MiraAttentionType.TEMPORAL:
            return self._compute_temporal_attention(context, memory)
        elif head.attention_type == MiraAttentionType.SEMANTIC:
            return self._compute_semantic_attention(context, memory)
        elif head.attention_type == MiraAttentionType.EMOTIONAL:
            return self._compute_emotional_attention(context, memory)
        elif head.attention_type == MiraAttentionType.GOAL_DIRECTED:
            return self._compute_goal_attention(context, memory)
        elif head.attention_type == MiraAttentionType.NOVELTY:
            return self._compute_novelty_attention(context, memory)
        elif head.attention_type == MiraAttentionType.SOCIAL:
            return self._compute_social_attention(context, memory)
        else:
            return 0.5  # Default for hybrid or unknown

    def _compute_temporal_attention(
        self,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        memory: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> float:
        """Temporal attention - recency bias."""
        try:
            memory_time = datetime.fromisoformat(memory.timestamp.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
            context_time = datetime.fromisoformat(context.timestamp.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
            hours_old = (context_time - memory_time).total_seconds() / 3600

            # Exponential decay within recency window
            if hours_old <= context.recency_window_hours:
                decay = math.exp(-hours_old / context.recency_window_hours)
                return decay
            else:
                # Beyond window, use slower decay
                beyond = hours_old - context.recency_window_hours
                return max(0.1, 0.5 * math.exp(-beyond / 168))  # 168 = 1 week
        except:
            return 0.5

    def _compute_semantic_attention(
        self,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        memory: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> float:
        """Semantic attention - relevance to query."""
        if not context.query_embedding or not memory.embedding:
            # Fallback to text similarity
            if context.query and memory.content:
                query_words = set(context.query.lower().split())
                memory_words = set(memory.content.lower().split())
                if query_words:
                    overlap = len(query_words & memory_words) / len(query_words)
                    return min(1.0, overlap * 1.5)
            return 0.3

        # Cosine similarity
        try:
            dot_product = sum(a * b for a, b in zip(context.query_embedding, memory.embedding))
            magnitude1 = math.sqrt(sum(a ** 2 for a in context.query_embedding))
            magnitude2 = math.sqrt(sum(b ** 2 for b in memory.embedding))
            if magnitude1 > 0 and magnitude2 > 0:
                similarity = dot_product / (magnitude1 * magnitude2)
                return max(0.0, (similarity + 1) / 2)  # Normalize to 0-1
        except:
            pass
        return 0.3

    def _compute_emotional_attention(
        self,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        memory: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> float:
        """Emotional attention - emotional resonance."""
        memory_emotion = getattr(memory, 'emotion', 'neutral')
        emotional_intensity = getattr(memory, 'emotional_intensity', 0.5)

        # Same emotion gets boost
        if context.current_emotion == memory_emotion:
            return min(1.0, 0.6 + emotional_intensity * 0.4)

        # Strong emotions get more attention regardless
        if emotional_intensity > 0.7:
            return emotional_intensity * 0.8

        # Neutral gets less attention
        if memory_emotion == "neutral":
            return 0.3

        return 0.5

    def _compute_goal_attention(
        self,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        memory: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> float:
        """Goal-directed attention - task relevance."""
        if not context.current_goal:
            return 0.5

        # Check tags for goal relevance
        tags = getattr(memory, 'tags', [])
        goal_words = set(context.current_goal.lower().split())

        # Tag match
        for tag in tags:
            if tag.lower() in goal_words or any(gw in tag.lower() for gw in goal_words):
                return 0.9

        # Content match to goal
        if memory.content:
            content_words = set(memory.content.lower().split())
            overlap = len(goal_words & content_words)
            if overlap > 0:
                return min(1.0, 0.4 + overlap * 0.15)

        return 0.3

    def _compute_novelty_attention(
        self,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        memory: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> float:
        """Novelty attention - unusual or surprising memories."""
        # Access count as proxy for novelty (less accessed = more novel)
        access_count = getattr(memory, 'access_count', 0)
        if access_count == 0:
            return 0.9  # Never accessed = very novel
        elif access_count < 3:
            return 0.7
        elif access_count < 10:
            return 0.5
        else:
            return 0.3  # Frequently accessed = not novel

    def _compute_social_attention(
        self,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        memory: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> float:
        """Social attention - user relevance."""
        memory_user = getattr(memory, 'user_id', None)

        # Same user gets high attention
        if context.user_id and memory_user == context.user_id:
            return 0.9

        # Same conversation
        memory_conv = getattr(memory, 'conversation_id', None)
        if context.conversation_id and memory_conv == context.conversation_id:
            return 0.8

        # Different user/conversation
        if memory_user:
            return 0.4
        return 0.5

    def apply_attention_to_results(
        self,
        context: MiraAttentionContext,
        results: List[MiraSearchResult],
        min_attention: float = 0.0,
    ) -> List[MiraSearchResult]:
        """
        Apply attention mechanism to search results.

        # BEGIN META: MiraAttentionMechanism.apply_attention_to_results
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Rerank search results using attention scores
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-005", "SRS-ATTENTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["attention", "rerank", "search"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Attention", "Search"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Apply attention mechanism to rerank search results
        # END META
        """
        scored_results = []

        for result in results:
            # Compute attention for the entry
            attention_score = self.compute_attention(context, result.entry)

            if attention_score.total_score >= min_attention:
                # Update result with attention score
                result.attention_score = attention_score.total_score

                # Recompute combined score with attention
                result_copy = MiraSearchResult(
                    entry=result.entry,
                    similarity_score=result.similarity_score,
                    attention_score=attention_score.total_score,
                    source=result.source,
                    domain=result.domain,
                )
                scored_results.append(result_copy)

        # Sort by combined score (now includes attention)
        scored_results.sort(key=lambda r: r.combined_score, reverse=True)
        return scored_results

    def get_head_activations(self) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get activation statistics for all heads."""
        return {
            head_id: {
                "type": head.attention_type.value,
                "weight": head.weight,
                "enabled": head.enabled,
                "last_activation": head.last_activation,
                "activation_count": head.activation_count,
            }
            for head_id, head in self._heads.items()
        }

    def get_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get attention mechanism statistics."""
        return {
            "head_count": len(self._heads),
            "threshold": self.attention_threshold,
            "heads": self.get_head_activations(),
            "metrics": self._metrics,
        }


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 53: MIRA RETENTION GATE (RETENTION GATE PILLAR)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Behrouz et al. (2025) - Pillar 3: Retention Gate
# Decides what memories to keep vs. forget
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


class MiraRetentionSignal(Enum):
    """
    Signals that influence retention decisions.

    # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionSignal
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Retention decision signals
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006", "SRS-RETENTION-001"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "retention", "signals"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    HIGH_IMPORTANCE = "high_importance"
    FREQUENT_ACCESS = "frequent_access"
    EMOTIONAL_SALIENCE = "emotional_salience"
    GOAL_RELEVANCE = "goal_relevance"
    USER_EXPLICIT = "user_explicit"       # User explicitly marked
    CONSOLIDATION = "consolidation"       # From STM to LTM
    DECAY_THRESHOLD = "decay_threshold"   # Below decay threshold
    REDUNDANCY = "redundancy"             # Duplicate information
    CONTRADICTION = "contradiction"       # Conflicts with other memories


@dataclass
class MiraRetentionCandidate:
    """
    Memory being evaluated for retention.

    # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionCandidate
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Memory candidate for retention evaluation
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "retention", "candidate"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    entry: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry]
    retention_score: float = 0.0
    signals: List[MiraRetentionSignal] = field(default_factory=list)
    decision: MiraRetentionDecision = MiraRetentionDecision.PENDING
    evaluated_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    reason: str = ""


@dataclass
class MiraRetentionConfig:
    """
    Configuration for retention gate.

    # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionConfig
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Retention gate configuration
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "retention", "config"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    # Thresholds
    retain_threshold: float = 0.6        # Above this = definitely retain
    forget_threshold: float = 0.2        # Below this = definitely forget
    consolidate_threshold: float = 0.75  # Above this = consolidate to LTM

    # Weights for retention score
    importance_weight: float = 0.25
    access_weight: float = 0.20
    emotional_weight: float = 0.15
    recency_weight: float = 0.15
    trust_weight: float = 0.15
    uniqueness_weight: float = 0.10

    # Decay settings
    min_age_for_forget_hours: float = 24.0  # Don't forget memories < 24h old
    max_forgotten_per_cycle: int = 50       # Limit forgetting per cycle

    # Special handling
    never_forget_tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: [
        "important", "user_marked", "core_knowledge", "identity"
    ])
    always_forget_tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: [
        "temporary", "ephemeral", "test"
    ])


@dataclass
class MiraRetentionResult:
    """
    Result of retention gate evaluation cycle.

    # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionResult
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Retention evaluation cycle result
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "retention", "result"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    cycle_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4())[:8])
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    evaluated_count: int = 0
    retained_count: int = 0
    forgotten_count: int = 0
    consolidated_count: int = 0
    compressed_count: int = 0
    pending_count: int = 0
    decisions: List[MiraRetentionCandidate] = field(default_factory=list)


class MiraRetentionGate:
    """
    Retention gate for memory lifecycle management.

    # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionGate
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Implement Mira retention gate pillar
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006", "SRS-RETENTION-001", "SRS-MEMORY-LIFECYCLE-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_RETENTION_EVALUATE
    # Enum: ENUM_MIRA_RETENTION
    # Tags: ["mira", "retention", "gate", "pillar-3"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention", "Lifecycle"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "CognitiveScience"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Supporting: ["evaluate_retention", "compute_retention_score", "execute_decision"]
    # Description: Mira Retention Gate pillar - decides what to keep vs forget
    # END META

    Implements Pillar 3 of Behrouz et al. (2025) Mira framework.
    Controls the memory lifecycle: retain, forget, consolidate, or compress.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: MiraRetentionConfig = None,
        stm: Optional[MiraSTMBuffer] = None,
        ltm: Optional[MiraLTMManager] = None,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
    ):
        """Initialize retention gate."""
        self.config = config or MiraRetentionConfig()
        self._stm = stm
        self._ltm = ltm
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(__name__)

        # Metrics
        self._metrics = {
            "evaluations": 0,
            "retained": 0,
            "forgotten": 0,
            "consolidated": 0,
            "compressed": 0,
        }

        # Track recent decisions for pattern analysis
        self._recent_decisions: List[MiraRetentionCandidate] = []
        self._max_decision_history = 1000

    def evaluate_entry(
        self,
        entry: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> MiraRetentionCandidate:
        """
        Evaluate single entry for retention.

        # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionGate.evaluate_entry
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Evaluate single memory for retention decision
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006", "SRS-RETENTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["retention", "evaluation", "single"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Evaluate single memory for retention decision
        # END META
        """
        candidate = MiraRetentionCandidate(entry=entry)

        # Check never-forget tags
        entry_tags = getattr(entry, 'tags', [])
        if any(tag in self.config.never_forget_tags for tag in entry_tags):
            candidate.decision = MiraRetentionDecision.RETAIN
            candidate.signals.append(MiraRetentionSignal.USER_EXPLICIT)
            candidate.reason = "Protected by never-forget tag"
            return candidate

        # Check always-forget tags
        if any(tag in self.config.always_forget_tags for tag in entry_tags):
            candidate.decision = MiraRetentionDecision.FORGET
            candidate.signals.append(MiraRetentionSignal.REDUNDANCY)
            candidate.reason = "Marked with always-forget tag"
            return candidate

        # Compute retention score
        retention_score = self._compute_retention_score(entry)
        candidate.retention_score = retention_score

        # Collect signals
        candidate.signals = self._collect_signals(entry, retention_score)

        # Make decision based on score and signals
        candidate.decision = self._make_decision(candidate)

        # Set reason
        candidate.reason = self._generate_reason(candidate)

        self._metrics["evaluations"] += 1
        return candidate

    def _compute_retention_score(
        self,
        entry: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
    ) -> float:
        """
        Compute retention score for entry.

        # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionGate._compute_retention_score
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Calculate retention score from multiple factors
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006"]
        # Tags: ["retention", "score", "computation"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # END META
        """
        score = 0.0

        # Importance score
        importance = getattr(entry, 'importance_score', 0.5)
        score += importance * self.config.importance_weight

        # Access frequency
        access_count = getattr(entry, 'access_count', 0)
        access_score = min(1.0, access_count * 0.1)
        score += access_score * self.config.access_weight

        # Emotional intensity
        emotional_intensity = getattr(entry, 'emotional_intensity', 0.5)
        score += emotional_intensity * self.config.emotional_weight

        # Recency
        try:
            entry_time = datetime.fromisoformat(entry.timestamp.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
            hours_old = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - entry_time).total_seconds() / 3600
            recency_score = max(0.0, 1.0 - (hours_old / 168))  # 168 = 1 week
            score += recency_score * self.config.recency_weight
        except:
            score += 0.5 * self.config.recency_weight

        # Trust score
        trust = getattr(entry, 'trust_score', 1.0)
        score += trust * self.config.trust_weight

        # Uniqueness (inverse of redundancy potential)
        # Simple proxy: shorter content = less unique
        content = getattr(entry, 'content', '')
        uniqueness = min(1.0, len(content) / 200)
        score += uniqueness * self.config.uniqueness_weight

        return min(1.0, score)

    def _collect_signals(
        self,
        entry: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
        retention_score: float,
    ) -> List[MiraRetentionSignal]:
        """Collect signals that influence retention decision."""
        signals = []

        # High importance
        if getattr(entry, 'importance_score', 0) > 0.7:
            signals.append(MiraRetentionSignal.HIGH_IMPORTANCE)

        # Frequent access
        if getattr(entry, 'access_count', 0) > 5:
            signals.append(MiraRetentionSignal.FREQUENT_ACCESS)

        # Emotional salience
        if getattr(entry, 'emotional_intensity', 0) > 0.6:
            signals.append(MiraRetentionSignal.EMOTIONAL_SALIENCE)

        # Check if entry is from STM (candidate for consolidation)
        if isinstance(entry, MiraSTMEntry) and not entry.consolidated_to_ltm:
            if retention_score >= self.config.consolidate_threshold:
                signals.append(MiraRetentionSignal.CONSOLIDATION)

        # Decay threshold
        trust = getattr(entry, 'trust_score', 1.0)
        if trust < 0.3:
            signals.append(MiraRetentionSignal.DECAY_THRESHOLD)

        return signals

    def _make_decision(
        self,
        candidate: MiraRetentionCandidate,
    ) -> MiraRetentionDecision:
        """Make retention decision based on score and signals."""
        score = candidate.retention_score
        signals = candidate.signals

        # High score = retain
        if score >= self.config.retain_threshold:
            # Check for consolidation opportunity
            if MiraRetentionSignal.CONSOLIDATION in signals:
                return MiraRetentionDecision.CONSOLIDATE
            return MiraRetentionDecision.RETAIN

        # Low score = forget (if old enough)
        if score < self.config.forget_threshold:
            try:
                entry_time = datetime.fromisoformat(
                    candidate.entry.timestamp.replace("Z", "+00:00")
                )
                hours_old = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - entry_time).total_seconds() / 3600
                if hours_old >= self.config.min_age_for_forget_hours:
                    return MiraRetentionDecision.FORGET
            except:
                pass

        # Medium score with decay signal = compress
        if MiraRetentionSignal.DECAY_THRESHOLD in signals:
            return MiraRetentionDecision.COMPRESS

        # Default = retain but deprioritize
        return MiraRetentionDecision.RETAIN

    def _generate_reason(self, candidate: MiraRetentionCandidate) -> str:
        """Generate human-readable reason for decision."""
        decision = candidate.decision
        score = candidate.retention_score
        signals = candidate.signals

        if decision == MiraRetentionDecision.RETAIN:
            if MiraRetentionSignal.HIGH_IMPORTANCE in signals:
                return f"Retained: High importance (score={score:.2f})"
            elif MiraRetentionSignal.FREQUENT_ACCESS in signals:
                return f"Retained: Frequently accessed (score={score:.2f})"
            return f"Retained: Above threshold (score={score:.2f})"

        elif decision == MiraRetentionDecision.FORGET:
            if MiraRetentionSignal.DECAY_THRESHOLD in signals:
                return f"Forgotten: Trust decayed below threshold (score={score:.2f})"
            return f"Forgotten: Below threshold (score={score:.2f})"

        elif decision == MiraRetentionDecision.CONSOLIDATE:
            return f"Consolidate to LTM: High value STM entry (score={score:.2f})"

        elif decision == MiraRetentionDecision.COMPRESS:
            return f"Compressed: Medium value, decay detected (score={score:.2f})"

        return f"Pending decision (score={score:.2f})"

    def evaluate_stm_batch(
        self,
        entries: Optional[List[MiraSTMEntry]] = None,
        limit: int = 100,
    ) -> MiraRetentionResult:
        """
        Evaluate batch of STM entries.

        # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionGate.evaluate_stm_batch
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Batch evaluate STM entries for retention
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006", "SRS-RETENTION-001"]
        # Reflex: REFLEX_RETENTION_BATCH
        # Tags: ["retention", "batch", "stm"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Batch evaluate STM for retention decisions
        # END META
        """
        if not entries and self._stm:
            entries = self._stm.get_recent(limit=limit)

        if not entries:
            return MiraRetentionResult()

        result = MiraRetentionResult(evaluated_count=len(entries))
        forgotten_count = 0

        for entry in entries:
            candidate = self.evaluate_entry(entry)
            result.decisions.append(candidate)

            if candidate.decision == MiraRetentionDecision.RETAIN:
                result.retained_count += 1
            elif candidate.decision == MiraRetentionDecision.FORGET:
                if forgotten_count < self.config.max_forgotten_per_cycle:
                    result.forgotten_count += 1
                    forgotten_count += 1
                else:
                    candidate.decision = MiraRetentionDecision.RETAIN
                    candidate.reason = "Retained: Forget quota reached"
                    result.retained_count += 1
            elif candidate.decision == MiraRetentionDecision.CONSOLIDATE:
                result.consolidated_count += 1
            elif candidate.decision == MiraRetentionDecision.COMPRESS:
                result.compressed_count += 1
            else:
                result.pending_count += 1

            # Track decision
            self._recent_decisions.append(candidate)

        # Prune decision history
        if len(self._recent_decisions) > self._max_decision_history:
            self._recent_decisions = self._recent_decisions[-self._max_decision_history:]

        return result

    def execute_decisions(
        self,
        result: MiraRetentionResult,
    ) -> Dict[str, int]:
        """
        Execute retention decisions.

        # BEGIN META: MiraRetentionGate.execute_decisions
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Execute retention decisions (forget, consolidate, compress)
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-006", "SRS-RETENTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["retention", "execution"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Retention"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Execute retention decisions on memory systems
        # END META
        """
        executed = {
            "forgotten": 0,
            "consolidated": 0,
            "compressed": 0,
        }

        for candidate in result.decisions:
            try:
                if candidate.decision == MiraRetentionDecision.FORGET:
                    if self._execute_forget(candidate.entry):
                        executed["forgotten"] += 1
                        self._metrics["forgotten"] += 1

                elif candidate.decision == MiraRetentionDecision.CONSOLIDATE:
                    if self._execute_consolidate(candidate.entry):
                        executed["consolidated"] += 1
                        self._metrics["consolidated"] += 1

                elif candidate.decision == MiraRetentionDecision.COMPRESS:
                    if self._execute_compress(candidate.entry):
                        executed["compressed"] += 1
                        self._metrics["compressed"] += 1

                elif candidate.decision == MiraRetentionDecision.RETAIN:
                    self._metrics["retained"] += 1

            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.warning(f"Failed to execute retention decision: {e}")

        return executed

    def _execute_forget(self, entry: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry]) -> bool:
        """Execute forget decision - remove from memory."""
        entry_id = getattr(entry, 'entry_id', '')
        if not entry_id:
            return False

        # Remove from STM
        if isinstance(entry, MiraSTMEntry) and self._stm:
            return self._stm.remove_entry(entry_id)

        # For LTM entries, mark as forgotten (soft delete)
        if isinstance(entry, MiraLTMEntry):
            entry.trust_score = 0.0
            return True

        return False

    def _execute_consolidate(self, entry: MiraSTMEntry) -> bool:
        """Execute consolidate decision - move from STM to LTM."""
        if not self._ltm or not isinstance(entry, MiraSTMEntry):
            return False

        if entry.consolidated_to_ltm:
            return False

        # Store in LTM
        ltm_entry = self._ltm.store(
            content=entry.content,
            domain=entry.predicted_domain or "conversation",
            source="consolidation",
            user_id=entry.user_id,
            tags=entry.tags,
            consolidation_source=entry.entry_id,
        )

        if ltm_entry:
            entry.consolidated_to_ltm = True
            entry.ltm_entry_id = ltm_entry.entry_id
            return True

        return False

    def _execute_compress(self, entry: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry]) -> bool:
        """Execute compress decision - reduce memory footprint."""
        # Simple compression: truncate content, clear embedding
        if hasattr(entry, 'content') and len(entry.content) > 200:
            # Keep first 200 chars as summary
            entry.content = entry.content[:200] + "..."
            entry.embedding = None  # Will need re-embedding if searched
            return True
        return False

    def get_retention_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get retention gate statistics."""
        return {
            "config": {
                "retain_threshold": self.config.retain_threshold,
                "forget_threshold": self.config.forget_threshold,
                "consolidate_threshold": self.config.consolidate_threshold,
            },
            "metrics": self._metrics,
            "recent_decisions_count": len(self._recent_decisions),
        }


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 54: MIRA MEMORY LEARNING ALGORITHM (LEARNING PILLAR)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Behrouz et al. (2025) - Pillar 4: Memory Learning Algorithm
# Adapts memory system based on experience
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


class MiraLearningSignal(Enum):
    """
    Learning signals that update memory parameters.

    # BEGIN META: MiraLearningSignal
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Learning signal types for memory adaptation
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "learning", "signals"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    RETRIEVAL_SUCCESS = "retrieval_success"     # Memory helped answer query
    RETRIEVAL_FAILURE = "retrieval_failure"     # Memory didn't help
    USER_FEEDBACK = "user_feedback"             # User rated response
    GOAL_ACHIEVED = "goal_achieved"             # Task completed successfully
    GOAL_FAILED = "goal_failed"                 # Task failed
    CONTRADICTION_DETECTED = "contradiction"    # Memory conflicts detected
    PATTERN_DISCOVERED = "pattern_discovered"   # New pattern found
    REINFORCEMENT = "reinforcement"             # Memory accessed again


class MiraLearningMode(Enum):
    """
    Learning modes for different contexts.

    # BEGIN META: MiraLearningMode
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Learning mode types
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "learning", "modes"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    ONLINE = "online"           # Learn in real-time
    BATCH = "batch"             # Learn from accumulated experiences
    REINFORCEMENT = "rl"        # Reinforcement learning style
    HEBBIAN = "hebbian"         # Strengthen co-activated memories
    CONTRASTIVE = "contrastive" # Learn from positive/negative pairs


@dataclass
class MiraLearningEvent:
    """
    Learning event that triggers memory updates.

    # BEGIN META: MiraLearningEvent
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Learning event record
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "learning", "event"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    event_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4())[:8])
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    signal: MiraLearningSignal = MiraLearningSignal.REINFORCEMENT
    memory_ids: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    context: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    reward: float = 0.0  # -1 to 1 scale
    query: Optional[str] = None
    feedback: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class MiraLearningUpdate:
    """
    Update to apply to a memory based on learning.

    # BEGIN META: MiraLearningUpdate
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Learning update to apply to memory
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "learning", "update"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    memory_id: str = ""
    trust_delta: float = 0.0
    importance_delta: float = 0.0
    access_increment: int = 0
    tags_to_add: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    tags_to_remove: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    reason: str = ""


@dataclass
class MiraLearningConfig:
    """
    Configuration for learning algorithm.

    # BEGIN META: MiraLearningConfig
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Learning algorithm configuration
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "learning", "config"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    # Learning rates
    base_learning_rate: float = 0.1
    trust_learning_rate: float = 0.05
    importance_learning_rate: float = 0.03

    # Reward scaling
    success_reward: float = 0.5
    failure_penalty: float = -0.3
    user_feedback_weight: float = 1.5

    # Hebbian parameters
    hebbian_strength: float = 0.1
    hebbian_decay: float = 0.01

    # Reinforcement parameters
    discount_factor: float = 0.95      # For temporal credit
    eligibility_trace: float = 0.7     # Memory of past activations

    # Batch learning
    batch_size: int = 32
    min_batch_for_update: int = 10

    # Bounds
    max_trust: float = 1.0
    min_trust: float = 0.0
    max_importance: float = 1.0
    min_importance: float = 0.0


@dataclass
class MiraLearningResult:
    """
    Result of learning cycle.

    # BEGIN META: MiraLearningResult
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Learning cycle result
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
    # Tags: ["mira", "learning", "result"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # END META
    """
    cycle_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4())[:8])
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    events_processed: int = 0
    memories_updated: int = 0
    trust_increases: int = 0
    trust_decreases: int = 0
    importance_increases: int = 0
    importance_decreases: int = 0
    patterns_discovered: int = 0
    updates: List[MiraLearningUpdate] = field(default_factory=list)


class MiraMemoryLearningAlgorithm:
    """
    Memory learning algorithm for adaptive memory system.

    # BEGIN META: MiraMemoryLearningAlgorithm
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Implement Mira memory learning algorithm pillar
    # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007", "SRS-LEARNING-001", "SRS-ADAPTATION-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_LEARNING_UPDATE
    # Enum: ENUM_MIRA_LEARNING
    # Tags: ["mira", "learning", "algorithm", "pillar-4"]
    # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning", "Adaptation"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "CognitiveScience", "MachineLearning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Supporting: ["process_event", "apply_hebbian_update", "compute_reward"]
    # Description: Mira Memory Learning pillar - adapts memory based on experience
    # END META

    Implements Pillar 4 of Behrouz et al. (2025) Mira framework.
    Adapts memory parameters based on retrieval success, user feedback, and goals.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: MiraLearningConfig = None,
        stm: Optional[MiraSTMBuffer] = None,
        ltm: Optional[MiraLTMManager] = None,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
    ):
        """Initialize learning algorithm."""
        self.config = config or MiraLearningConfig()
        self._stm = stm
        self._ltm = ltm
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(__name__)

        # Event buffer for batch learning
        self._event_buffer: List[MiraLearningEvent] = []
        self._max_buffer_size = 1000

        # Co-activation matrix for Hebbian learning (simplified)
        self._coactivation_counts: Dict[Tuple[str, str], int] = {}

        # Eligibility traces for RL
        self._eligibility: Dict[str, float] = {}

        # Metrics
        self._metrics = {
            "events_processed": 0,
            "memories_updated": 0,
            "total_reward": 0.0,
            "avg_reward": 0.0,
        }

        # Mode
        self.mode = MiraLearningMode.ONLINE

    def record_retrieval_outcome(
        self,
        query: str,
        retrieved_memories: List[str],
        was_helpful: bool,
        user_feedback: Optional[float] = None,
    ) -> MiraLearningEvent:
        """
        Record outcome of memory retrieval.

        # BEGIN META: MiraMemoryLearningAlgorithm.record_retrieval_outcome
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Record retrieval outcome for learning
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
        # Tags: ["learning", "retrieval", "outcome"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Record retrieval success/failure for learning
        # END META
        """
        signal = (
            MiraLearningSignal.RETRIEVAL_SUCCESS if was_helpful
            else MiraLearningSignal.RETRIEVAL_FAILURE
        )

        reward = self.config.success_reward if was_helpful else self.config.failure_penalty
        if user_feedback is not None:
            reward = user_feedback * self.config.user_feedback_weight

        event = MiraLearningEvent(
            signal=signal,
            memory_ids=retrieved_memories,
            query=query,
            reward=reward,
            context={"was_helpful": was_helpful},
        )

        self._event_buffer.append(event)

        # Update co-activation for Hebbian learning
        if len(retrieved_memories) > 1:
            self._record_coactivation(retrieved_memories)

        # Online learning
        if self.mode == MiraLearningMode.ONLINE:
            self.process_event(event)

        return event

    def record_user_feedback(
        self,
        memory_ids: List[str],
        rating: float,
        feedback_text: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> MiraLearningEvent:
        """
        Record explicit user feedback on memories.

        # BEGIN META: MiraMemoryLearningAlgorithm.record_user_feedback
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Record user feedback for learning
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
        # Tags: ["learning", "feedback", "user"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # END META
        """
        event = MiraLearningEvent(
            signal=MiraLearningSignal.USER_FEEDBACK,
            memory_ids=memory_ids,
            reward=rating,
            feedback=feedback_text,
            context={"rating": rating},
        )

        self._event_buffer.append(event)

        if self.mode == MiraLearningMode.ONLINE:
            self.process_event(event)

        return event

    def record_goal_outcome(
        self,
        goal: str,
        contributing_memories: List[str],
        achieved: bool,
    ) -> MiraLearningEvent:
        """
        Record goal achievement/failure for temporal credit.

        # BEGIN META: MiraMemoryLearningAlgorithm.record_goal_outcome
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Record goal outcome for learning
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
        # Tags: ["learning", "goal", "outcome"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # END META
        """
        signal = (
            MiraLearningSignal.GOAL_ACHIEVED if achieved
            else MiraLearningSignal.GOAL_FAILED
        )

        event = MiraLearningEvent(
            signal=signal,
            memory_ids=contributing_memories,
            reward=self.config.success_reward if achieved else self.config.failure_penalty,
            context={"goal": goal, "achieved": achieved},
        )

        self._event_buffer.append(event)

        if self.mode == MiraLearningMode.ONLINE:
            self.process_event(event)

        return event

    def process_event(
        self,
        event: MiraLearningEvent,
    ) -> List[MiraLearningUpdate]:
        """
        Process single learning event.

        # BEGIN META: MiraMemoryLearningAlgorithm.process_event
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Process learning event and generate updates
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
        # Reflex: REFLEX_LEARNING_PROCESS
        # Tags: ["learning", "event", "processing"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Process learning event and generate memory updates
        # END META
        """
        updates = []

        for memory_id in event.memory_ids:
            update = self._compute_update(memory_id, event)
            if update:
                updates.append(update)

                # Apply update
                self._apply_update(update)

        # Update eligibility traces for RL
        self._update_eligibility(event.memory_ids, event.reward)

        # Update metrics
        self._metrics["events_processed"] += 1
        self._metrics["total_reward"] += event.reward
        self._metrics["avg_reward"] = (
            self._metrics["total_reward"] / self._metrics["events_processed"]
        )

        return updates

    def _compute_update(
        self,
        memory_id: str,
        event: MiraLearningEvent,
    ) -> Optional[MiraLearningUpdate]:
        """Compute update for single memory based on event."""
        update = MiraLearningUpdate(memory_id=memory_id)

        # Base learning rate scaled by reward
        lr = self.config.base_learning_rate
        reward = event.reward

        if event.signal == MiraLearningSignal.RETRIEVAL_SUCCESS:
            update.trust_delta = self.config.trust_learning_rate * reward
            update.importance_delta = self.config.importance_learning_rate * reward
            update.access_increment = 1
            update.reason = "Retrieval success"

        elif event.signal == MiraLearningSignal.RETRIEVAL_FAILURE:
            update.trust_delta = self.config.trust_learning_rate * reward  # Negative
            update.reason = "Retrieval failure"

        elif event.signal == MiraLearningSignal.USER_FEEDBACK:
            update.trust_delta = self.config.trust_learning_rate * reward * 1.5
            update.importance_delta = self.config.importance_learning_rate * reward
            update.reason = f"User feedback: {reward:.2f}"

        elif event.signal == MiraLearningSignal.GOAL_ACHIEVED:
            # Temporal credit assignment via eligibility
            eligibility = self._eligibility.get(memory_id, 1.0)
            update.trust_delta = self.config.trust_learning_rate * reward * eligibility
            update.importance_delta = self.config.importance_learning_rate * reward * eligibility
            update.tags_to_add = ["goal_contributor"]
            update.reason = "Contributed to goal"

        elif event.signal == MiraLearningSignal.GOAL_FAILED:
            eligibility = self._eligibility.get(memory_id, 1.0)
            update.trust_delta = self.config.trust_learning_rate * reward * eligibility
            update.reason = "Associated with goal failure"

        elif event.signal == MiraLearningSignal.REINFORCEMENT:
            update.access_increment = 1
            update.trust_delta = 0.01  # Small positive reinforcement
            update.reason = "Memory reinforcement"

        return update if (update.trust_delta != 0 or update.importance_delta != 0 or
                         update.access_increment > 0) else None

    def _apply_update(self, update: MiraLearningUpdate) -> bool:
        """Apply learning update to memory."""
        # Try STM first
        if self._stm:
            entry = self._stm.get_entry(update.memory_id)
            if entry:
                return self._apply_update_to_entry(entry, update)

        # Try LTM
        if self._ltm:
            entry = self._ltm.get_entry(update.memory_id)
            if entry:
                return self._apply_update_to_entry(entry, update)

        return False

    def _apply_update_to_entry(
        self,
        entry: Union[MiraSTMEntry, MiraLTMEntry],
        update: MiraLearningUpdate,
    ) -> bool:
        """Apply update to specific entry."""
        try:
            # Update trust
            if update.trust_delta != 0:
                current_trust = getattr(entry, 'trust_score', 1.0)
                new_trust = current_trust + update.trust_delta
                entry.trust_score = max(self.config.min_trust,
                                        min(self.config.max_trust, new_trust))

            # Update importance
            if update.importance_delta != 0:
                current_importance = getattr(entry, 'importance_score', 0.5)
                new_importance = current_importance + update.importance_delta
                entry.importance_score = max(self.config.min_importance,
                                             min(self.config.max_importance, new_importance))

            # Update access count
            if update.access_increment > 0:
                entry.access_count = getattr(entry, 'access_count', 0) + update.access_increment

            # Update tags
            entry_tags = set(getattr(entry, 'tags', []))
            for tag in update.tags_to_add:
                entry_tags.add(tag)
            for tag in update.tags_to_remove:
                entry_tags.discard(tag)
            entry.tags = list(entry_tags)

            self._metrics["memories_updated"] += 1
            return True

        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to apply update to {update.memory_id}: {e}")
            return False

    def _record_coactivation(self, memory_ids: List[str]) -> None:
        """Record co-activation for Hebbian learning."""
        for i, id1 in enumerate(memory_ids):
            for id2 in memory_ids[i + 1:]:
                key = tuple(sorted([id1, id2]))
                self._coactivation_counts[key] = self._coactivation_counts.get(key, 0) + 1

    def _update_eligibility(self, memory_ids: List[str], reward: float) -> None:
        """Update eligibility traces for temporal credit."""
        # Decay all eligibilities
        for mid in list(self._eligibility.keys()):
            self._eligibility[mid] *= self.config.discount_factor
            if self._eligibility[mid] < 0.01:
                del self._eligibility[mid]

        # Set eligibility for activated memories
        for mid in memory_ids:
            self._eligibility[mid] = self.config.eligibility_trace

    def apply_hebbian_update(self) -> int:
        """
        Apply Hebbian learning to strengthen co-activated memories.

        # BEGIN META: MiraMemoryLearningAlgorithm.apply_hebbian_update
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Hebbian learning for co-activated memories
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
        # Tags: ["learning", "hebbian", "coactivation"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Strengthen associations between co-activated memories
        # END META
        """
        updates_applied = 0

        for (id1, id2), count in self._coactivation_counts.items():
            if count >= 3:  # Minimum co-activations for Hebbian update
                strength = min(1.0, count * self.config.hebbian_strength)

                # Update both memories
                update1 = MiraLearningUpdate(
                    memory_id=id1,
                    trust_delta=strength * 0.01,
                    tags_to_add=[f"associated_with:{id2[:8]}"],
                    reason=f"Hebbian: co-activated with {id2[:8]} ({count}x)",
                )
                update2 = MiraLearningUpdate(
                    memory_id=id2,
                    trust_delta=strength * 0.01,
                    tags_to_add=[f"associated_with:{id1[:8]}"],
                    reason=f"Hebbian: co-activated with {id1[:8]} ({count}x)",
                )

                if self._apply_update(update1):
                    updates_applied += 1
                if self._apply_update(update2):
                    updates_applied += 1

        # Decay co-activation counts
        for key in list(self._coactivation_counts.keys()):
            self._coactivation_counts[key] = int(
                self._coactivation_counts[key] * (1 - self.config.hebbian_decay)
            )
            if self._coactivation_counts[key] == 0:
                del self._coactivation_counts[key]

        return updates_applied

    def run_batch_learning(self) -> MiraLearningResult:
        """
        Run batch learning on buffered events.

        # BEGIN META: MiraMemoryLearningAlgorithm.run_batch_learning
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Batch learning from accumulated events
        # SRS: ["SRS-MIRA-007"]
        # Tags: ["learning", "batch"]
        # Categories: ["Memory", "Learning"]
        # Guild: "CNWB"
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # Description: Process accumulated events in batch
        # END META
        """
        result = MiraLearningResult()

        if len(self._event_buffer) < self.config.min_batch_for_update:
            return result

        # Process events in batches
        batch = self._event_buffer[:self.config.batch_size]
        result.events_processed = len(batch)

        for event in batch:
            updates = self.process_event(event)
            result.updates.extend(updates)

            for update in updates:
                result.memories_updated += 1
                if update.trust_delta > 0:
                    result.trust_increases += 1
                elif update.trust_delta < 0:
                    result.trust_decreases += 1
                if update.importance_delta > 0:
                    result.importance_increases += 1
                elif update.importance_delta < 0:
                    result.importance_decreases += 1

        # Apply Hebbian updates
        hebbian_updates = self.apply_hebbian_update()
        result.patterns_discovered = hebbian_updates

        # Clear processed events
        self._event_buffer = self._event_buffer[self.config.batch_size:]

        return result

    def get_learning_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get learning algorithm statistics."""
        return {
            "mode": self.mode.value,
            "event_buffer_size": len(self._event_buffer),
            "coactivation_pairs": len(self._coactivation_counts),
            "eligibility_traces": len(self._eligibility),
            "metrics": self._metrics,
            "config": {
                "base_lr": self.config.base_learning_rate,
                "trust_lr": self.config.trust_learning_rate,
                "importance_lr": self.config.importance_learning_rate,
            },
        }


# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION ZZ: MIRA MEMORY ORCHESTRATOR + NESTED/RECURSIVE LEARNING
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Purpose:
#   - Close the loop: ingest -> retention -> learn -> retrieve -> persist
#   - Bind Mira memory to NexusMindAdapter for recursive refinement
#   - Provide autonomy gating + structural self-heal signals

def _mira_utc_iso() -> str:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()


def _mira_stable_hash(text: str) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    h.update((text or "").encode("utf-8", errors="ignore"))
    return h.hexdigest()[:12]


def _mira_clamp(x: float, lo: float, hi: float) -> float:
    return max(lo, min(hi, x))


def _mira_safe_float(x: Any, default: float = 0.0) -> float:
    try:
        return float(x)
    except Exception:
        return default


@dataclass
class MiraLoopIngestResult:
    stm_entry_id: str
    retention_candidate: Optional[MiraRetentionCandidate]
    consolidated_ltm_id: Optional[str]
    notes: str = ""


class MiraMemorySystem:
    """
