                self.logger.info(f"Self-model update: {domain.value} {old_level.name} -> {cap.level.name}")

        # Add notes if provided
        if notes:
            cap.notes.append(notes)

        # Update confidence based on evidence count
        cap.confidence = min(0.5 + (cap.evidence_count * 0.01), 0.95)

        return update

    def discover_limitation(
        self,
        description: str,
        domain: CapabilityDomain,
        severity: str,
        context: str,
        workarounds: List[str] = None
    ) -> Limitation:
        """
        Record a discovered limitation.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.discover_limitation
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Document newly discovered agent limitation
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001", "SRS-AWARENESS-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "limitation"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            description: Description of the limitation
            domain: Capability domain affected
            severity: "minor", "moderate", "major", "critical"
            context: Context in which limitation was discovered
            workarounds: Known workarounds

        Returns:
            The created Limitation record
        """
        # Check for duplicate
        for existing in self.limitations:
            if existing.description.lower() == description.lower() and not existing.is_overcome:
                existing.context += f"\n{context}"
                if workarounds:
                    existing.workarounds.extend(workarounds)
                return existing

        limitation = Limitation(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            description=description,
            domain=domain,
            severity=severity,
            discovered_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            context=context,
            workarounds=workarounds or []
        )
        self.limitations.append(limitation)

        # Record update
        update = SelfModelUpdate(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            update_type=SelfModelUpdateType.LIMITATION_DISCOVERED,
            domain=domain,
            description=f"Limitation discovered: {description}",
            evidence=context,
            old_value=None,
            new_value=description,
            confidence=0.7
        )
        self.update_history.append(update)

        self.logger.info(f"Limitation discovered in {domain.value}: {description}")
        return limitation

    def overcome_limitation(self, limitation_id: str, evidence: str) -> bool:
        """
        Mark a limitation as overcome.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.overcome_limitation
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Record limitation being overcome
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "limitation", "growth"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            limitation_id: ID of the limitation to mark overcome
            evidence: Evidence that limitation is overcome

        Returns:
            True if limitation was found and marked, False otherwise
        """
        for limitation in self.limitations:
            if limitation.id == limitation_id and not limitation.is_overcome:
                limitation.is_overcome = True
                limitation.overcome_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

                # Record update
                update = SelfModelUpdate(
                    id=str(uuid4()),
                    timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
                    update_type=SelfModelUpdateType.LIMITATION_OVERCOME,
                    domain=limitation.domain,
                    description=f"Limitation overcome: {limitation.description}",
                    evidence=evidence,
                    old_value=False,
                    new_value=True,
                    confidence=0.8
                )
                self.update_history.append(update)

                self.logger.info(f"Limitation overcome: {limitation.description}")
                return True
        return False

    def identify_strength(
        self,
        description: str,
        domain: CapabilityDomain,
        evidence: List[str],
        confidence: float = 0.7
    ) -> Strength:
        """
        Record an identified strength.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.identify_strength
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Document identified agent strength
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001", "SRS-AWARENESS-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "strength"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            description: Description of the strength
            domain: Capability domain of the strength
            evidence: Evidence supporting the strength
            confidence: Confidence in this strength (0.0-1.0)

        Returns:
            The created Strength record
        """
        # Check for existing strength to reinforce
        for existing in self.strengths:
            if existing.description.lower() == description.lower():
                existing.evidence.extend(evidence)
                existing.confidence = min(existing.confidence + 0.05, 1.0)
                existing.usage_count += 1
                existing.last_used = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
                return existing

        strength = Strength(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            description=description,
            domain=domain,
            confidence=confidence,
            evidence=evidence,
            identified_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        )
        self.strengths.append(strength)

        # Record update
        update = SelfModelUpdate(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            update_type=SelfModelUpdateType.STRENGTH_IDENTIFIED,
            domain=domain,
            description=f"Strength identified: {description}",
            evidence="; ".join(evidence[:3]),
            old_value=None,
            new_value=description,
            confidence=confidence
        )
        self.update_history.append(update)

        self.logger.info(f"Strength identified in {domain.value}: {description}")
        return strength

    def update_personality_trait(self, trait: str, delta: float) -> None:
        """
        Adjust a personality trait.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.update_personality_trait
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Adjust personality trait value
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "personality"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            trait: Name of the trait to adjust
            delta: Amount to adjust (-1.0 to 1.0)
        """
        if trait in self.personality:
            old_value = self.personality[trait]
            self.personality[trait] = max(-1.0, min(1.0, old_value + delta))

            if abs(delta) >= 0.1:
                update = SelfModelUpdate(
                    id=str(uuid4()),
                    timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
                    update_type=SelfModelUpdateType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTED,
                    domain=None,
                    description=f"Personality trait adjusted: {trait}",
                    evidence="Interaction pattern analysis",
                    old_value=old_value,
                    new_value=self.personality[trait],
                    confidence=0.6
                )
                self.update_history.append(update)

    def record_behavioral_pattern(self, pattern: str) -> None:
        """
        Record occurrence of a behavioral pattern.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.record_behavioral_pattern
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Track behavioral pattern frequency
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "behavior"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            pattern: Name of the behavioral pattern
        """
        if pattern not in self.behavioral_patterns:
            self.behavioral_patterns[pattern] = 0
        self.behavioral_patterns[pattern] += 1

    def get_capability_level(self, domain: CapabilityDomain) -> CapabilityLevel:
        """
        Get current capability level for a domain.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.get_capability_level
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Retrieve capability level for domain
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "query"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            domain: Capability domain to query

        Returns:
            Current capability level
        """
        cap = self.capabilities.get(domain.value)
        if cap:
            return cap.level
        return CapabilityLevel.NOVICE

    def get_active_limitations(self, domain: CapabilityDomain = None) -> List[Limitation]:
        """
        Get active (not overcome) limitations.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.get_active_limitations
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Query active limitations
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001", "SRS-AWARENESS-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "query"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            domain: Optional domain filter

        Returns:
            List of active limitations
        """
        active = [lim for lim in self.limitations if not lim.is_overcome]
        if domain:
            active = [lim for lim in active if lim.domain == domain]
        return active

    def get_top_strengths(self, limit: int = 5) -> List[Strength]:
        """
        Get top strengths by confidence.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.get_top_strengths
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Query top strengths
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001", "SRS-AWARENESS-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "query"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            limit: Maximum number to return

        Returns:
            Top strengths sorted by confidence
        """
        return sorted(self.strengths, key=lambda s: s.confidence, reverse=True)[:limit]

    def generate_self_description(self) -> str:
        """
        Generate a natural language self-description.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.generate_self_description
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Create self-description from self-model
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001", "SRS-AWARENESS-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "introspection"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Returns:
            Natural language self-description
        """
        # Find strongest capabilities
        strong_caps = sorted(
            [(domain, cap) for domain, cap in self.capabilities.items() if cap.level.value >= CapabilityLevel.PROFICIENT.value],
            key=lambda x: x[1].level.value,
            reverse=True
        )[:3]

        # Find areas for improvement
        weak_caps = sorted(
            [(domain, cap) for domain, cap in self.capabilities.items() if cap.level.value <= CapabilityLevel.APPRENTICE.value],
            key=lambda x: x[1].level.value
        )[:2]

        # Build description
        parts = [
            f"I am an AI agent with {self._total_interactions} interactions of experience.",
        ]

        if strong_caps:
            strengths = ", ".join([cap[0].replace("_", " ") for cap in strong_caps])
            parts.append(f"My strongest capabilities are in {strengths}.")

        if self.strengths:
            top_strength = self.get_top_strengths(1)[0]
            parts.append(f"A particular strength is {top_strength.description.lower()}.")

        if weak_caps:
            areas = ", ".join([cap[0].replace("_", " ") for cap in weak_caps])
            parts.append(f"I am still developing my abilities in {areas}.")

        active_lims = self.get_active_limitations()
        if active_lims:
            parts.append(f"I am aware of {len(active_lims)} current limitation(s) I am working to address.")

        # Personality description
        dominant_traits = sorted(
            self.personality.items(),
            key=lambda x: abs(x[1]),
            reverse=True
        )[:3]
        trait_descs = []
        for trait, value in dominant_traits:
            if value > 0.5:
                trait_descs.append(f"highly {trait}")
            elif value > 0:
                trait_descs.append(f"somewhat {trait}")
        if trait_descs:
            parts.append(f"I tend to be {', '.join(trait_descs)}.")

        return " ".join(parts)

    def create_snapshot(self) -> SelfModelSnapshot:
        """
        Create a complete snapshot of current self-model.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.create_snapshot
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Create complete self-model snapshot
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001", "SRS-CONTINUITY-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "snapshot"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Returns:
            Complete self-model snapshot
        """
        return SelfModelSnapshot(
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            agent_id=self.agent_id,
            capabilities=dict(self.capabilities),
            limitations=list(self.limitations),
            strengths=list(self.strengths),
            personality_traits=dict(self.personality),
            behavioral_patterns=dict(self.behavioral_patterns),
            total_interactions=self._total_interactions,
            total_learnings=self._total_learnings
        )

    def get_introspection_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get a summary for introspection reporting.

        # BEGIN META: SelfModelSystem.get_introspection_summary
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Generate introspection summary
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-001", "SRS-AWARENESS-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "introspection"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Returns:
            Introspection summary dictionary
        """
        return {
            'agent_id': self.agent_id,
            'total_interactions': self._total_interactions,
            'total_learnings': self._total_learnings,
            'capabilities': {
                domain: {
                    'level': cap.level.name,
                    'confidence': cap.confidence,
                    'trend': cap.trend
                }
                for domain, cap in self.capabilities.items()
            },
            'active_limitations': len(self.get_active_limitations()),
            'overcome_limitations': len([l for l in self.limitations if l.is_overcome]),
            'strengths_count': len(self.strengths),
            'top_strengths': [s.description for s in self.get_top_strengths(3)],
            'personality': self.personality,
            'dominant_behaviors': sorted(
                self.behavioral_patterns.items(),
                key=lambda x: x[1],
                reverse=True
            )[:3],
            'recent_updates': len([
                u for u in self.update_history
                if (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - u.timestamp).days < 7
            ]),
            'self_description': self.generate_self_description()
        }


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 97: AGENT EVOLUTION FRAMEWORK
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


class EvolutionaryPressure(Enum):
    """
    Types of evolutionary pressure that drive agent adaptation.

    # BEGIN META: EvolutionaryPressure
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Categorize forces that drive agent evolution
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_EVOLUTIONARY_PRESSURE
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "enum"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionEngine"]
    # Description: Evolutionary pressure type enumeration.
    # END META
    """
    USER_FEEDBACK = "user_feedback"
    PERFORMANCE_METRICS = "performance_metrics"
    ERROR_PATTERNS = "error_patterns"
    SUCCESS_PATTERNS = "success_patterns"
    USAGE_FREQUENCY = "usage_frequency"
    DOMAIN_SHIFT = "domain_shift"
    RESOURCE_CONSTRAINTS = "resource_constraints"
    EXTERNAL_CHANGES = "external_changes"


class AdaptationType(Enum):
    """
    Types of adaptations the agent can make.

    # BEGIN META: AdaptationType
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Categorize adaptation types for evolution
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_ADAPTATION_TYPE
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "enum"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionEngine"]
    # Description: Adaptation type enumeration.
    # END META
    """
    BEHAVIORAL = "behavioral"
    KNOWLEDGE = "knowledge"
    STRATEGY = "strategy"
    COMMUNICATION = "communication"
    MEMORY = "memory"
    INTEGRATION = "integration"
    GOVERNANCE = "governance"


class EvolutionPhase(Enum):
    """
    Phases of the evolutionary cycle.

    # BEGIN META: EvolutionPhase
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define evolutionary cycle phases
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_EVOLUTION_PHASE
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "enum"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionEngine"]
    # Description: Evolution phase enumeration.
    # END META
    """
    OBSERVATION = "observation"
    ANALYSIS = "analysis"
    HYPOTHESIS = "hypothesis"
    EXPERIMENT = "experiment"
    EVALUATION = "evaluation"
    INTEGRATION = "integration"
    CONSOLIDATION = "consolidation"


@dataclass
class EvolutionarySignal:
    """
    A signal that may trigger evolutionary adaptation.

    # BEGIN META: EvolutionarySignal
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Structured evolutionary signal for adaptation triggers
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_EVOLUTIONARY_SIGNAL
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionEngine"]
    # Description: Evolutionary signal dataclass.
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    pressure_type: EvolutionaryPressure
    signal_strength: float  # 0.0 to 1.0
    description: str
    evidence: List[str]
    affected_domains: List[CapabilityDomain]
    context: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class AdaptationCandidate:
    """
    A proposed adaptation to evaluate.

    # BEGIN META: AdaptationCandidate
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Proposed adaptation for evolutionary evaluation
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_ADAPTATION_CANDIDATE
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionEngine"]
    # Description: Adaptation candidate dataclass.
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    adaptation_type: AdaptationType
    description: str
    rationale: str
    triggering_signals: List[str]  # Signal IDs
    expected_benefit: float  # 0.0 to 1.0
    risk_level: float  # 0.0 to 1.0
    reversibility: float  # 0.0 to 1.0 (1.0 = fully reversible)
    implementation_plan: List[str]
    created_at: datetime
    status: str = "proposed"  # proposed, approved, testing, applied, rejected


@dataclass
class EvolutionExperiment:
    """
    An experiment to test an adaptation.

    # BEGIN META: EvolutionExperiment
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Track adaptation experiments
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_EVOLUTION_EXPERIMENT
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionEngine"]
    # Description: Evolution experiment dataclass.
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    adaptation_id: str
    hypothesis: str
    success_criteria: Dict[str, float]
    started_at: datetime
    duration_hours: int
    interactions_during: int = 0
    metrics_before: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
    metrics_after: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
    status: str = "running"  # running, completed, failed, aborted
    result: Optional[str] = None
    ended_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class AppliedAdaptation:
    """
    Record of an adaptation that was applied.

    # BEGIN META: AppliedAdaptation
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Record applied adaptations for history
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_APPLIED_ADAPTATION
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionEngine"]
    # Description: Applied adaptation record.
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    adaptation: AdaptationCandidate
    applied_at: datetime
    experiment_id: Optional[str]
    success_metrics: Dict[str, float]
    is_active: bool = True
    reverted_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    revert_reason: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class EvolutionState:
    """
    Complete state of the evolution system.

    # BEGIN META: EvolutionState
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Complete evolution system state
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-CONTINUITY-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_EVOLUTION_STATE
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionEngine"]
    # Description: Evolution state snapshot.
    # END META
    """
    generation: int
    current_phase: EvolutionPhase
    pending_signals: List[EvolutionarySignal]
    candidate_adaptations: List[AdaptationCandidate]
    active_experiments: List[EvolutionExperiment]
    applied_adaptations: List[AppliedAdaptation]
    evolution_velocity: float  # Adaptations per week
    stability_score: float  # How stable current state is
    last_evolution_at: Optional[datetime]


class EvolutionEngine:
    """
    Engine for agent self-evolution and adaptation.

    # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Drive agent evolution through adaptation cycles
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_EVOLUTION
    # Enum: ENUM_EVOLUTION_ENGINE
    # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "core"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Evolution", "Learning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["EvolutionarySignal", "AdaptationCandidate", "EvolutionExperiment"]
    # Description: Core evolution engine for agent adaptation.
    # END META

    The Evolution Engine enables the agent to:
    1. Detect evolutionary pressures from environment and feedback
    2. Generate adaptation candidates based on signals
    3. Design and run experiments to test adaptations
    4. Apply successful adaptations
    5. Revert unsuccessful changes

    Architecture:
    - Signal detection: Identify pressures requiring adaptation
    - Candidate generation: Propose possible adaptations
    - Experiment design: Test adaptations safely
    - Application: Apply successful adaptations
    - Monitoring: Track adaptation effectiveness
    """

    # Evolution parameters
    SIGNAL_THRESHOLD = 0.5  # Minimum signal strength to consider
    ADAPTATION_THRESHOLD = 0.7  # Minimum expected benefit to propose
    EXPERIMENT_MIN_INTERACTIONS = 10  # Minimum interactions for experiment
    EXPERIMENT_DEFAULT_HOURS = 24  # Default experiment duration
    MAX_ACTIVE_EXPERIMENTS = 3  # Maximum concurrent experiments
    REVERT_THRESHOLD = 0.3  # If metrics drop below this, revert

    def __init__(
        self,
        self_model: SelfModelSystem,
        storage_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None
    ):
        """
        Initialize the Evolution Engine.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize evolution engine
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "init"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            self_model: Self-model system for capability tracking
            storage_dir: Directory for persisting evolution state
            logger: Optional logger instance
        """
        self.self_model = self_model
        self.storage_dir = storage_dir or Path(".nexus_agent_data/evolution")
        self.storage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(__name__)

        # Evolution state
        self._generation = 0
        self._current_phase = EvolutionPhase.OBSERVATION
        self._pending_signals: List[EvolutionarySignal] = []
        self._candidate_adaptations: List[AdaptationCandidate] = []
        self._active_experiments: List[EvolutionExperiment] = []
        self._applied_adaptations: List[AppliedAdaptation] = []
        self._last_evolution_at: Optional[datetime] = None

        # Metrics tracking
        self._baseline_metrics: Dict[str, float] = {}
        self._current_metrics: Dict[str, float] = {}

        # Load existing state
        self._load_if_exists()

        self.logger.info("Evolution Engine initialized")

    def _load_if_exists(self) -> None:
        """
        Load existing evolution state from storage.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine._load_if_exists
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Restore evolution state from persistence
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-CONTINUITY-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "persistence"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META
        """
        state_path = self.storage_dir / "evolution_state.json"
        if state_path.exists():
            try:
                with open(state_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                    data = json.load(f)

                self._generation = data.get('generation', 0)
                self._current_phase = EvolutionPhase(data.get('current_phase', 'observation'))
                self._baseline_metrics = data.get('baseline_metrics', {})

                if data.get('last_evolution_at'):
                    self._last_evolution_at = datetime.fromisoformat(data['last_evolution_at'])

                # Restore applied adaptations
                for adapt_data in data.get('applied_adaptations', []):
                    try:
                        candidate = AdaptationCandidate(
                            id=adapt_data['adaptation']['id'],
                            adaptation_type=AdaptationType(adapt_data['adaptation']['adaptation_type']),
                            description=adapt_data['adaptation']['description'],
                            rationale=adapt_data['adaptation']['rationale'],
                            triggering_signals=adapt_data['adaptation'].get('triggering_signals', []),
                            expected_benefit=adapt_data['adaptation'].get('expected_benefit', 0.5),
                            risk_level=adapt_data['adaptation'].get('risk_level', 0.3),
                            reversibility=adapt_data['adaptation'].get('reversibility', 0.8),
                            implementation_plan=adapt_data['adaptation'].get('implementation_plan', []),
                            created_at=datetime.fromisoformat(adapt_data['adaptation']['created_at']),
                            status=adapt_data['adaptation'].get('status', 'applied')
                        )
                        applied = AppliedAdaptation(
                            id=adapt_data['id'],
                            adaptation=candidate,
                            applied_at=datetime.fromisoformat(adapt_data['applied_at']),
                            experiment_id=adapt_data.get('experiment_id'),
                            success_metrics=adapt_data.get('success_metrics', {}),
                            is_active=adapt_data.get('is_active', True)
                        )
                        self._applied_adaptations.append(applied)
                    except (ValueError, KeyError):
                        pass

                self.logger.info(f"Evolution state restored: generation {self._generation}")

            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.warning(f"Failed to load evolution state: {e}")

    def save(self) -> None:
        """
        Persist current evolution state to storage.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.save
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Persist evolution state
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-CONTINUITY-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "persistence"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META
        """
        state_path = self.storage_dir / "evolution_state.json"

        data = {
            'generation': self._generation,
            'current_phase': self._current_phase.value,
            'baseline_metrics': self._baseline_metrics,
            'current_metrics': self._current_metrics,
            'last_evolution_at': self._last_evolution_at.isoformat() if self._last_evolution_at else None,
            'pending_signals': [
                {
                    'id': sig.id,
                    'timestamp': sig.timestamp.isoformat(),
                    'pressure_type': sig.pressure_type.value,
                    'signal_strength': sig.signal_strength,
                    'description': sig.description
                }
                for sig in self._pending_signals[-50:]
            ],
            'candidate_adaptations': [
                {
                    'id': cand.id,
                    'adaptation_type': cand.adaptation_type.value,
                    'description': cand.description,
                    'rationale': cand.rationale,
                    'expected_benefit': cand.expected_benefit,
                    'risk_level': cand.risk_level,
                    'status': cand.status,
                    'created_at': cand.created_at.isoformat()
                }
                for cand in self._candidate_adaptations[-20:]
            ],
            'active_experiments': [
                {
                    'id': exp.id,
                    'adaptation_id': exp.adaptation_id,
                    'hypothesis': exp.hypothesis,
                    'started_at': exp.started_at.isoformat(),
                    'status': exp.status
                }
                for exp in self._active_experiments
            ],
            'applied_adaptations': [
                {
                    'id': applied.id,
                    'adaptation': {
                        'id': applied.adaptation.id,
                        'adaptation_type': applied.adaptation.adaptation_type.value,
                        'description': applied.adaptation.description,
                        'rationale': applied.adaptation.rationale,
                        'triggering_signals': applied.adaptation.triggering_signals,
                        'expected_benefit': applied.adaptation.expected_benefit,
                        'risk_level': applied.adaptation.risk_level,
                        'reversibility': applied.adaptation.reversibility,
                        'implementation_plan': applied.adaptation.implementation_plan,
                        'created_at': applied.adaptation.created_at.isoformat(),
                        'status': applied.adaptation.status
                    },
                    'applied_at': applied.applied_at.isoformat(),
                    'experiment_id': applied.experiment_id,
                    'success_metrics': applied.success_metrics,
                    'is_active': applied.is_active
                }
                for applied in self._applied_adaptations[-100:]
            ]
        }

        try:
            with open(state_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
            self.logger.debug(f"Evolution state saved to {state_path}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to save evolution state: {e}")

    def receive_signal(
        self,
        pressure_type: EvolutionaryPressure,
        signal_strength: float,
        description: str,
        evidence: List[str],
        affected_domains: List[CapabilityDomain],
        context: Dict[str, Any] = None
    ) -> EvolutionarySignal:
        """
        Receive an evolutionary signal.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.receive_signal
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Process incoming evolutionary pressure signal
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "evolution"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            pressure_type: Type of evolutionary pressure
            signal_strength: Strength of the signal (0.0-1.0)
            description: Description of the signal
            evidence: Evidence supporting the signal
            affected_domains: Capability domains affected
            context: Additional context

        Returns:
            The created EvolutionarySignal
        """
        signal = EvolutionarySignal(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            pressure_type=pressure_type,
            signal_strength=min(1.0, max(0.0, signal_strength)),
            description=description,
            evidence=evidence,
            affected_domains=affected_domains,
            context=context or {}
        )
        self._pending_signals.append(signal)

        # Prune old signals
        if len(self._pending_signals) > 100:
            self._pending_signals = self._pending_signals[-100:]

        self.logger.debug(f"Received evolutionary signal: {pressure_type.value} (strength: {signal_strength:.2f})")

        # Auto-advance phase if we have strong signals
        if signal_strength >= self.SIGNAL_THRESHOLD and self._current_phase == EvolutionPhase.OBSERVATION:
            self._current_phase = EvolutionPhase.ANALYSIS

        return signal

    def analyze_signals(self) -> List[AdaptationCandidate]:
        """
        Analyze pending signals and generate adaptation candidates.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.analyze_signals
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Analyze signals and propose adaptations
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "analysis"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Returns:
            List of new adaptation candidates
        """
        # Filter strong signals
        strong_signals = [
            sig for sig in self._pending_signals
            if sig.signal_strength >= self.SIGNAL_THRESHOLD
        ]

        if not strong_signals:
            return []

        new_candidates = []

        # Group signals by pressure type
        by_pressure: Dict[EvolutionaryPressure, List[EvolutionarySignal]] = {}
        for sig in strong_signals:
            if sig.pressure_type not in by_pressure:
                by_pressure[sig.pressure_type] = []
            by_pressure[sig.pressure_type].append(sig)

        # Generate candidates for each pressure type
        for pressure_type, signals in by_pressure.items():
            avg_strength = sum(s.signal_strength for s in signals) / len(signals)

            if avg_strength < self.ADAPTATION_THRESHOLD:
                continue

            candidate = self._generate_candidate_for_pressure(pressure_type, signals)
            if candidate:
                self._candidate_adaptations.append(candidate)
                new_candidates.append(candidate)

        if new_candidates:
            self._current_phase = EvolutionPhase.HYPOTHESIS
            self.logger.info(f"Generated {len(new_candidates)} adaptation candidates")

        return new_candidates

    def _generate_candidate_for_pressure(
        self,
        pressure_type: EvolutionaryPressure,
        signals: List[EvolutionarySignal]
    ) -> Optional[AdaptationCandidate]:
        """
        Generate an adaptation candidate for a pressure type.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine._generate_candidate_for_pressure
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Generate adaptation proposal from signals
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "evolution"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            pressure_type: Type of pressure
            signals: Related signals

        Returns:
            Adaptation candidate or None
        """
        # Collect affected domains
        affected_domains = set()
        for sig in signals:
            affected_domains.update(sig.affected_domains)

        # Collect evidence
        all_evidence = []
        for sig in signals:
            all_evidence.extend(sig.evidence[:3])

        # Generate candidate based on pressure type
        avg_strength = sum(s.signal_strength for s in signals) / len(signals)

        adaptation_map = {
            EvolutionaryPressure.USER_FEEDBACK: (
                AdaptationType.COMMUNICATION,
                "Adjust communication style based on user feedback",
                "Repeated user feedback suggests communication adjustments"
            ),
            EvolutionaryPressure.PERFORMANCE_METRICS: (
                AdaptationType.STRATEGY,
                "Optimize strategy based on performance metrics",
                "Performance metrics indicate optimization opportunities"
            ),
            EvolutionaryPressure.ERROR_PATTERNS: (
                AdaptationType.BEHAVIORAL,
                "Adjust behavior to reduce error patterns",
                "Repeated error patterns detected requiring behavioral adjustment"
            ),
            EvolutionaryPressure.SUCCESS_PATTERNS: (
                AdaptationType.STRATEGY,
                "Reinforce successful patterns",
                "Identified success patterns to reinforce"
            ),
            EvolutionaryPressure.USAGE_FREQUENCY: (
                AdaptationType.MEMORY,
                "Optimize memory based on usage patterns",
                "Usage frequency suggests memory optimization"
            ),
            EvolutionaryPressure.DOMAIN_SHIFT: (
                AdaptationType.KNOWLEDGE,
                "Expand knowledge for new domains",
                "Domain shift detected requiring knowledge expansion"
            ),
            EvolutionaryPressure.RESOURCE_CONSTRAINTS: (
                AdaptationType.STRATEGY,
                "Optimize resource usage",
                "Resource constraints requiring efficiency improvements"
            ),
            EvolutionaryPressure.EXTERNAL_CHANGES: (
                AdaptationType.INTEGRATION,
                "Adapt to external system changes",
                "External changes requiring integration adjustments"
            ),
        }

        if pressure_type not in adaptation_map:
            return None

        adapt_type, description, rationale = adaptation_map[pressure_type]

        # Calculate risk based on adaptation type
        risk_map = {
            AdaptationType.BEHAVIORAL: 0.3,
            AdaptationType.KNOWLEDGE: 0.2,
            AdaptationType.STRATEGY: 0.4,
            AdaptationType.COMMUNICATION: 0.2,
            AdaptationType.MEMORY: 0.3,
            AdaptationType.INTEGRATION: 0.5,
            AdaptationType.GOVERNANCE: 0.6,
        }

        return AdaptationCandidate(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            adaptation_type=adapt_type,
            description=description,
            rationale=f"{rationale}. Evidence: {'; '.join(all_evidence[:3])}",
            triggering_signals=[s.id for s in signals],
            expected_benefit=avg_strength,
            risk_level=risk_map.get(adapt_type, 0.3),
            reversibility=0.8,  # Most adaptations should be reversible
            implementation_plan=[
                f"Analyze current {adapt_type.value} patterns",
                f"Identify specific changes for {pressure_type.value}",
                "Design experiment to test changes",
                "Apply changes if experiment succeeds"
            ],
            created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            status="proposed"
        )

    def approve_candidate(self, candidate_id: str) -> bool:
        """
        Approve an adaptation candidate for experimentation.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.approve_candidate
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Approve adaptation for experiment
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "governance"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            candidate_id: ID of the candidate to approve

        Returns:
            True if approved, False if not found
        """
        for candidate in self._candidate_adaptations:
            if candidate.id == candidate_id and candidate.status == "proposed":
                candidate.status = "approved"
                self._current_phase = EvolutionPhase.EXPERIMENT
                self.logger.info(f"Approved adaptation candidate: {candidate.description}")
                return True
        return False

    def start_experiment(
        self,
        candidate_id: str,
        hypothesis: str,
        success_criteria: Dict[str, float],
        duration_hours: int = None
    ) -> Optional[EvolutionExperiment]:
        """
        Start an experiment for an approved adaptation.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.start_experiment
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Begin adaptation experiment
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "experiment"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            candidate_id: ID of the approved candidate
            hypothesis: Hypothesis for the experiment
            success_criteria: Metrics and thresholds for success
            duration_hours: Duration of the experiment

        Returns:
            The created experiment or None
        """
        # Check experiment limit
        running = [e for e in self._active_experiments if e.status == "running"]
        if len(running) >= self.MAX_ACTIVE_EXPERIMENTS:
            self.logger.warning("Maximum active experiments reached")
            return None

        # Find approved candidate
        candidate = None
        for c in self._candidate_adaptations:
            if c.id == candidate_id and c.status == "approved":
                candidate = c
                break

        if not candidate:
            return None

        # Record baseline metrics
        baseline = self._capture_current_metrics()

        experiment = EvolutionExperiment(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            adaptation_id=candidate_id,
            hypothesis=hypothesis,
            success_criteria=success_criteria,
            started_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            duration_hours=duration_hours or self.EXPERIMENT_DEFAULT_HOURS,
            metrics_before=baseline,
            status="running"
        )

        self._active_experiments.append(experiment)
        candidate.status = "testing"

        self.logger.info(f"Started experiment for: {candidate.description}")
        return experiment

    def _capture_current_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """
        Capture current performance metrics.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine._capture_current_metrics
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Capture metrics for experiment comparison
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "metrics"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Returns:
            Current metrics dictionary
        """
        metrics = {}

        # From self-model
        summary = self.self_model.get_introspection_summary()

        metrics['total_interactions'] = float(summary.get('total_interactions', 0))
        metrics['total_learnings'] = float(summary.get('total_learnings', 0))
        metrics['active_limitations'] = float(summary.get('active_limitations', 0))
        metrics['strengths_count'] = float(summary.get('strengths_count', 0))

        # Calculate average capability level
        caps = summary.get('capabilities', {})
        if caps:
            level_map = {'NOVICE': 1, 'APPRENTICE': 2, 'COMPETENT': 3, 'PROFICIENT': 4, 'EXPERT': 5, 'MASTER': 6}
            levels = [level_map.get(c.get('level', 'NOVICE'), 1) for c in caps.values()]
            metrics['avg_capability_level'] = sum(levels) / len(levels)

        return metrics

    def evaluate_experiment(self, experiment_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Evaluate an experiment's results.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.evaluate_experiment
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Evaluate experiment success/failure
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "evaluation"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            experiment_id: ID of the experiment to evaluate

        Returns:
            Evaluation results or None
        """
        experiment = None
        for e in self._active_experiments:
            if e.id == experiment_id:
                experiment = e
                break

        if not experiment or experiment.status != "running":
            return None

        # Check if experiment has enough data
        if experiment.interactions_during < self.EXPERIMENT_MIN_INTERACTIONS:
            return {
                'ready': False,
                'reason': f"Need {self.EXPERIMENT_MIN_INTERACTIONS - experiment.interactions_during} more interactions"
            }

        # Capture current metrics
        current_metrics = self._capture_current_metrics()
        experiment.metrics_after = current_metrics

        # Evaluate against success criteria
        success_count = 0
        total_criteria = len(experiment.success_criteria)

        evaluation = {
            'ready': True,
            'criteria_results': {}
        }

        for metric, threshold in experiment.success_criteria.items():
            before = experiment.metrics_before.get(metric, 0)
            after = current_metrics.get(metric, 0)

            if before > 0:
                improvement = (after - before) / before
            else:
                improvement = after

            passed = improvement >= threshold
            if passed:
                success_count += 1

            evaluation['criteria_results'][metric] = {
                'before': before,
                'after': after,
                'improvement': improvement,
                'threshold': threshold,
                'passed': passed
            }

        evaluation['success_rate'] = success_count / total_criteria if total_criteria > 0 else 0
        evaluation['passed'] = evaluation['success_rate'] >= 0.5

        return evaluation

    def complete_experiment(
        self,
        experiment_id: str,
        apply_on_success: bool = True
    ) -> Optional[AppliedAdaptation]:
        """
        Complete an experiment and optionally apply the adaptation.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.complete_experiment
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Complete experiment and apply if successful
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "application"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            experiment_id: ID of the experiment to complete
            apply_on_success: Whether to apply adaptation if successful

        Returns:
            Applied adaptation if successful and applied, None otherwise
        """
        # Evaluate first
        evaluation = self.evaluate_experiment(experiment_id)
        if not evaluation or not evaluation.get('ready'):
            return None

        # Find experiment and candidate
        experiment = None
        for e in self._active_experiments:
            if e.id == experiment_id:
                experiment = e
                break

        if not experiment:
            return None

        candidate = None
        for c in self._candidate_adaptations:
            if c.id == experiment.adaptation_id:
                candidate = c
                break

        if not candidate:
            return None

        # Update experiment status
        experiment.ended_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        if evaluation['passed']:
            experiment.status = "completed"
            experiment.result = "success"
            candidate.status = "applied" if apply_on_success else "approved"

            self.logger.info(f"Experiment completed successfully: {candidate.description}")

            if apply_on_success:
                # Create applied adaptation record
                applied = AppliedAdaptation(
                    id=str(uuid4()),
                    adaptation=candidate,
                    applied_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
                    experiment_id=experiment_id,
                    success_metrics=experiment.metrics_after,
                    is_active=True
                )
                self._applied_adaptations.append(applied)
                self._generation += 1
                self._last_evolution_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
                self._current_phase = EvolutionPhase.CONSOLIDATION

                return applied
        else:
            experiment.status = "failed"
            experiment.result = "failure"
            candidate.status = "rejected"

            self.logger.info(f"Experiment failed: {candidate.description}")

        return None

    def revert_adaptation(self, adaptation_id: str, reason: str) -> bool:
        """
        Revert an applied adaptation.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.revert_adaptation
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Revert applied adaptation
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-EVOLUTION-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "evolution", "revert"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            adaptation_id: ID of the applied adaptation
            reason: Reason for reverting

        Returns:
            True if reverted, False if not found
        """
        for applied in self._applied_adaptations:
            if applied.id == adaptation_id and applied.is_active:
                applied.is_active = False
                applied.reverted_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
                applied.revert_reason = reason

                self.logger.info(f"Reverted adaptation: {applied.adaptation.description} ({reason})")
                return True
        return False

    def record_interaction_in_experiment(self) -> None:
        """
        Record an interaction in active experiments.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.record_interaction_in_experiment
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Track interactions during experiments
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "tracking"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META
        """
        for experiment in self._active_experiments:
            if experiment.status == "running":
                experiment.interactions_during += 1

    def get_evolution_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get summary of evolution state.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.get_evolution_summary
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Generate evolution summary
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "introspection"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Returns:
            Evolution summary dictionary
        """
        active_count = len([a for a in self._applied_adaptations if a.is_active])
        reverted_count = len([a for a in self._applied_adaptations if not a.is_active])

        # Calculate evolution velocity (adaptations per week)
        week_ago = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=7)
        recent_adaptations = [
            a for a in self._applied_adaptations
            if a.applied_at > week_ago
        ]
        velocity = len(recent_adaptations)

        return {
            'generation': self._generation,
            'current_phase': self._current_phase.value,
            'pending_signals': len(self._pending_signals),
            'candidate_adaptations': len(self._candidate_adaptations),
            'active_experiments': len([e for e in self._active_experiments if e.status == "running"]),
            'completed_experiments': len([e for e in self._active_experiments if e.status == "completed"]),
            'applied_adaptations': active_count,
            'reverted_adaptations': reverted_count,
            'evolution_velocity': velocity,
            'last_evolution_at': self._last_evolution_at.isoformat() if self._last_evolution_at else None,
            'stability': 1.0 - (reverted_count / max(1, active_count + reverted_count))
        }

    def get_state(self) -> EvolutionState:
        """
        Get complete evolution state.

        # BEGIN META: EvolutionEngine.get_state
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Create evolution state snapshot
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-002", "SRS-CONTINUITY-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "snapshot"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Returns:
            Complete evolution state
        """
        summary = self.get_evolution_summary()
        return EvolutionState(
            generation=self._generation,
            current_phase=self._current_phase,
            pending_signals=list(self._pending_signals),
            candidate_adaptations=list(self._candidate_adaptations),
            active_experiments=list(self._active_experiments),
            applied_adaptations=list(self._applied_adaptations),
            evolution_velocity=summary['evolution_velocity'],
            stability_score=summary['stability'],
            last_evolution_at=self._last_evolution_at
        )


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 98: KNOWLEDGE SYNTHESIS ENGINE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


class KnowledgeType(Enum):
    """
    Types of knowledge for synthesis.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeType
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Categorize knowledge types for synthesis
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-003", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_KNOWLEDGE
    # Enum: ENUM_KNOWLEDGE_TYPE
    # Tags: ["meta", "knowledge", "enum"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Knowledge"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["KnowledgeSynthesisEngine"]
    # Description: Knowledge type enumeration.
    # END META
    """
    FACTUAL = "factual"
    PROCEDURAL = "procedural"
    CONCEPTUAL = "conceptual"
    METACOGNITIVE = "metacognitive"
    EXPERIENTIAL = "experiential"
    RELATIONAL = "relational"


class SynthesisMethod(Enum):
    """
    Methods for synthesizing knowledge.

    # BEGIN META: SynthesisMethod
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define knowledge synthesis methods
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-003"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_KNOWLEDGE
    # Enum: ENUM_SYNTHESIS_METHOD
    # Tags: ["meta", "knowledge", "enum"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Knowledge"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["KnowledgeSynthesisEngine"]
    # Description: Synthesis method enumeration.
    # END META
    """
    ANALOGY = "analogy"
    ABSTRACTION = "abstraction"
    COMPOSITION = "composition"
    DECOMPOSITION = "decomposition"
    GENERALIZATION = "generalization"
    SPECIALIZATION = "specialization"
    CROSS_DOMAIN = "cross_domain"
    PATTERN_EXTRACTION = "pattern_extraction"


@dataclass
class KnowledgeFragment:
    """
    A piece of knowledge for synthesis.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeFragment
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Structured knowledge fragment
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-003", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_KNOWLEDGE
    # Enum: ENUM_KNOWLEDGE_FRAGMENT
    # Tags: ["meta", "knowledge", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Knowledge"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["KnowledgeSynthesisEngine"]
    # Description: Knowledge fragment dataclass.
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    content: str
    knowledge_type: KnowledgeType
    domain: str
    confidence: float
    source: str
    created_at: datetime
    embedding: Optional[List[float]] = None
    related_fragments: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class SynthesizedKnowledge:
    """
    Knowledge created through synthesis.

    # BEGIN META: SynthesizedKnowledge
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Record synthesized knowledge
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-003", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_KNOWLEDGE
    # Enum: ENUM_SYNTHESIZED_KNOWLEDGE
    # Tags: ["meta", "knowledge", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Knowledge"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["KnowledgeSynthesisEngine"]
    # Description: Synthesized knowledge record.
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    content: str
    synthesis_method: SynthesisMethod
    source_fragments: List[str]
    knowledge_type: KnowledgeType
    domain: str
    confidence: float
    novelty_score: float  # How novel is this synthesis
    utility_score: float  # How useful is this knowledge
    created_at: datetime
    validated: bool = False
    validation_evidence: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class KnowledgeRelation:
    """
    A relationship between knowledge fragments.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeRelation
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define knowledge relationships
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-003"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_KNOWLEDGE
    # Enum: ENUM_KNOWLEDGE_RELATION
    # Tags: ["meta", "knowledge", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Knowledge"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["KnowledgeSynthesisEngine"]
    # Description: Knowledge relation dataclass.
    # END META
    """
    source_id: str
    target_id: str
    relation_type: str  # "supports", "contradicts", "extends", "specializes", "generalizes"
    strength: float
    discovered_at: datetime
    evidence: str = ""


@dataclass
class KnowledgeCluster:
    """
    A cluster of related knowledge.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeCluster
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Group related knowledge fragments
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-003"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_KNOWLEDGE
    # Enum: ENUM_KNOWLEDGE_CLUSTER
    # Tags: ["meta", "knowledge", "dataclass"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Knowledge"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["KnowledgeSynthesisEngine"]
    # Description: Knowledge cluster dataclass.
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    theme: str
    fragment_ids: List[str]
    centroid_embedding: Optional[List[float]]
    coherence_score: float
    size: int
    domains: List[str]
    created_at: datetime


class KnowledgeSynthesisEngine:
    """
    Engine for synthesizing knowledge across domains.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Synthesize and integrate knowledge across domains
    # SRS: ["SRS-META-003", "SRS-LEARNING-001", "SRS-MEMORY-002"]
    # Reflex: REFLEX_KNOWLEDGE
    # Enum: ENUM_KNOWLEDGE_SYNTHESIS
    # Tags: ["meta", "knowledge", "core"]
    # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Knowledge", "Learning"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-M
    # Supporting: ["KnowledgeFragment", "SynthesizedKnowledge", "KnowledgeRelation"]
    # Description: Core knowledge synthesis engine.
    # END META

    The Knowledge Synthesis Engine enables the agent to:
    1. Store and organize knowledge fragments
    2. Identify relationships between knowledge pieces
    3. Synthesize new knowledge through various methods
    4. Cluster related knowledge for efficient retrieval
    5. Validate synthesized knowledge through evidence

    Architecture:
    - Fragment storage: Individual knowledge pieces
    - Relation graph: Connections between knowledge
    - Synthesis methods: Ways to create new knowledge
    - Cluster detection: Group related knowledge
    - Validation: Verify synthesized knowledge
    """

    # Synthesis parameters
    MIN_CONFIDENCE_FOR_SYNTHESIS = 0.5
    MIN_FRAGMENTS_FOR_SYNTHESIS = 2
    MAX_SYNTHESIS_ATTEMPTS = 5
    RELATION_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.7

    def __init__(
        self,
        storage_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None
    ):
        """
        Initialize the Knowledge Synthesis Engine.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize knowledge synthesis engine
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-003"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "init"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            storage_dir: Directory for persisting knowledge
            logger: Optional logger instance
        """
        self.storage_dir = storage_dir or Path(".nexus_agent_data/knowledge")
        self.storage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(__name__)

        # Knowledge storage
        self._fragments: Dict[str, KnowledgeFragment] = {}
        self._synthesized: Dict[str, SynthesizedKnowledge] = {}
        self._relations: List[KnowledgeRelation] = []
        self._clusters: Dict[str, KnowledgeCluster] = {}

        # Indexes
        self._domain_index: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
        self._type_index: Dict[KnowledgeType, List[str]] = {}

        # Statistics
        self._total_syntheses = 0
        self._successful_validations = 0

        # Load existing knowledge
        self._load_if_exists()

        self.logger.info("Knowledge Synthesis Engine initialized")

    def _load_if_exists(self) -> None:
        """
        Load existing knowledge from storage.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine._load_if_exists
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Restore knowledge from persistence
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-003", "SRS-CONTINUITY-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "persistence"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META
        """
        knowledge_path = self.storage_dir / "knowledge_base.json"
        if knowledge_path.exists():
            try:
                with open(knowledge_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                    data = json.load(f)

                # Restore fragments
                for frag_data in data.get('fragments', []):
                    try:
                        fragment = KnowledgeFragment(
                            id=frag_data['id'],
                            content=frag_data['content'],
                            knowledge_type=KnowledgeType(frag_data['knowledge_type']),
                            domain=frag_data['domain'],
                            confidence=frag_data['confidence'],
                            source=frag_data['source'],
                            created_at=datetime.fromisoformat(frag_data['created_at']),
                            related_fragments=frag_data.get('related_fragments', []),
                            metadata=frag_data.get('metadata', {})
                        )
                        self._fragments[fragment.id] = fragment
                        self._index_fragment(fragment)
                    except (ValueError, KeyError):
                        pass

                # Restore synthesized knowledge
                for synth_data in data.get('synthesized', []):
                    try:
                        synthesized = SynthesizedKnowledge(
                            id=synth_data['id'],
                            content=synth_data['content'],
                            synthesis_method=SynthesisMethod(synth_data['synthesis_method']),
                            source_fragments=synth_data['source_fragments'],
                            knowledge_type=KnowledgeType(synth_data['knowledge_type']),
                            domain=synth_data['domain'],
                            confidence=synth_data['confidence'],
                            novelty_score=synth_data['novelty_score'],
                            utility_score=synth_data['utility_score'],
                            created_at=datetime.fromisoformat(synth_data['created_at']),
                            validated=synth_data.get('validated', False),
                            validation_evidence=synth_data.get('validation_evidence', [])
                        )
                        self._synthesized[synthesized.id] = synthesized
                    except (ValueError, KeyError):
                        pass

                # Restore relations
                for rel_data in data.get('relations', []):
                    try:
                        relation = KnowledgeRelation(
                            source_id=rel_data['source_id'],
                            target_id=rel_data['target_id'],
                            relation_type=rel_data['relation_type'],
                            strength=rel_data['strength'],
                            discovered_at=datetime.fromisoformat(rel_data['discovered_at']),
                            evidence=rel_data.get('evidence', '')
                        )
                        self._relations.append(relation)
                    except (ValueError, KeyError):
                        pass

                self._total_syntheses = data.get('total_syntheses', 0)
                self._successful_validations = data.get('successful_validations', 0)

                self.logger.info(f"Knowledge base restored: {len(self._fragments)} fragments, {len(self._synthesized)} syntheses")

            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.warning(f"Failed to load knowledge base: {e}")

    def _index_fragment(self, fragment: KnowledgeFragment) -> None:
        """
        Add fragment to indexes.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine._index_fragment
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Index fragment for efficient retrieval
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-003"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "indexing"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META
        """
        # Domain index
        if fragment.domain not in self._domain_index:
            self._domain_index[fragment.domain] = []
        if fragment.id not in self._domain_index[fragment.domain]:
            self._domain_index[fragment.domain].append(fragment.id)

        # Type index
        if fragment.knowledge_type not in self._type_index:
            self._type_index[fragment.knowledge_type] = []
        if fragment.id not in self._type_index[fragment.knowledge_type]:
            self._type_index[fragment.knowledge_type].append(fragment.id)

    def save(self) -> None:
        """
        Persist current knowledge to storage.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine.save
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Persist knowledge base
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-003", "SRS-CONTINUITY-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "persistence"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META
        """
        knowledge_path = self.storage_dir / "knowledge_base.json"

        data = {
            'fragments': [
                {
                    'id': frag.id,
                    'content': frag.content,
                    'knowledge_type': frag.knowledge_type.value,
                    'domain': frag.domain,
                    'confidence': frag.confidence,
                    'source': frag.source,
                    'created_at': frag.created_at.isoformat(),
                    'related_fragments': frag.related_fragments,
                    'metadata': frag.metadata
                }
                for frag in list(self._fragments.values())[-500:]  # Keep last 500
            ],
            'synthesized': [
                {
                    'id': synth.id,
                    'content': synth.content,
                    'synthesis_method': synth.synthesis_method.value,
                    'source_fragments': synth.source_fragments,
                    'knowledge_type': synth.knowledge_type.value,
                    'domain': synth.domain,
                    'confidence': synth.confidence,
                    'novelty_score': synth.novelty_score,
                    'utility_score': synth.utility_score,
                    'created_at': synth.created_at.isoformat(),
                    'validated': synth.validated,
                    'validation_evidence': synth.validation_evidence
                }
                for synth in list(self._synthesized.values())[-200:]  # Keep last 200
            ],
            'relations': [
                {
                    'source_id': rel.source_id,
                    'target_id': rel.target_id,
                    'relation_type': rel.relation_type,
                    'strength': rel.strength,
                    'discovered_at': rel.discovered_at.isoformat(),
                    'evidence': rel.evidence
                }
                for rel in self._relations[-1000:]  # Keep last 1000
            ],
            'total_syntheses': self._total_syntheses,
            'successful_validations': self._successful_validations
        }

        try:
            with open(knowledge_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
            self.logger.debug(f"Knowledge base saved to {knowledge_path}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to save knowledge base: {e}")

    def add_fragment(
        self,
        content: str,
        knowledge_type: KnowledgeType,
        domain: str,
        source: str,
        confidence: float = 0.7,
        metadata: Dict[str, Any] = None
    ) -> KnowledgeFragment:
        """
        Add a knowledge fragment.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine.add_fragment
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Add new knowledge fragment
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-003", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "knowledge"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            content: Content of the knowledge
            knowledge_type: Type of knowledge
            domain: Domain of the knowledge
            source: Source of the knowledge
            confidence: Confidence in this knowledge
            metadata: Additional metadata

        Returns:
            The created KnowledgeFragment
        """
        fragment = KnowledgeFragment(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            content=content,
            knowledge_type=knowledge_type,
            domain=domain,
            confidence=confidence,
            source=source,
            created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            metadata=metadata or {}
        )

        self._fragments[fragment.id] = fragment
        self._index_fragment(fragment)

        # Auto-discover relations
        self._discover_relations_for_fragment(fragment)

        self.logger.debug(f"Added knowledge fragment in {domain}: {content[:50]}...")
        return fragment

    def _discover_relations_for_fragment(self, fragment: KnowledgeFragment) -> List[KnowledgeRelation]:
        """
        Discover relations for a new fragment.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine._discover_relations_for_fragment
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Find relations with existing fragments
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-003"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "relations"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            fragment: The new fragment

        Returns:
            List of discovered relations
        """
        new_relations = []

        # Check same-domain fragments
        domain_fragments = self._domain_index.get(fragment.domain, [])
        for frag_id in domain_fragments:
            if frag_id == fragment.id:
                continue

            other = self._fragments.get(frag_id)
            if not other:
                continue

            # Simple relation detection based on content overlap
            relation_type, strength = self._detect_relation_type(fragment, other)
            if strength >= 0.3:
                relation = KnowledgeRelation(
                    source_id=fragment.id,
                    target_id=frag_id,
                    relation_type=relation_type,
                    strength=strength,
                    discovered_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
                    evidence=f"Auto-discovered based on content similarity"
                )
                self._relations.append(relation)
                new_relations.append(relation)

                # Update fragment's related list
                if frag_id not in fragment.related_fragments:
                    fragment.related_fragments.append(frag_id)
                if fragment.id not in other.related_fragments:
                    other.related_fragments.append(fragment.id)

        return new_relations

    def _detect_relation_type(
        self,
        fragment1: KnowledgeFragment,
        fragment2: KnowledgeFragment
    ) -> Tuple[str, float]:
        """
        Detect relation type between two fragments.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine._detect_relation_type
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Determine relation type and strength
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-003"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "relations"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            fragment1: First fragment
            fragment2: Second fragment

        Returns:
            Tuple of (relation_type, strength)
        """
        content1 = fragment1.content.lower()
        content2 = fragment2.content.lower()

        # Simple word overlap for similarity
        words1 = set(content1.split())
        words2 = set(content2.split())
        overlap = len(words1 & words2)
        union = len(words1 | words2)

        if union == 0:
            return ("unrelated", 0.0)

        similarity = overlap / union

        # Determine relation type based on knowledge types
        if fragment1.knowledge_type == fragment2.knowledge_type:
            if similarity > 0.5:
                return ("extends", similarity)
            elif similarity > 0.3:
                return ("supports", similarity)
        else:
            if fragment1.knowledge_type == KnowledgeType.CONCEPTUAL and fragment2.knowledge_type == KnowledgeType.FACTUAL:
                return ("generalizes", similarity)
            elif fragment1.knowledge_type == KnowledgeType.FACTUAL and fragment2.knowledge_type == KnowledgeType.CONCEPTUAL:
                return ("specializes", similarity)

        return ("relates", similarity)

    def synthesize(
        self,
        method: SynthesisMethod,
        fragment_ids: List[str],
        target_domain: str = None
    ) -> Optional[SynthesizedKnowledge]:
        """
        Synthesize new knowledge from fragments.

        # BEGIN META: KnowledgeSynthesisEngine.synthesize
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Create new knowledge through synthesis
        # SRS: ["SRS-META-003", "SRS-LEARNING-001"]
        # Tags: ["meta", "synthesis"]
        # Categories: ["Meta-Systems"]
        # END META

        Args:
            method: Synthesis method to use
            fragment_ids: IDs of source fragments
            target_domain: Target domain for synthesized knowledge

        Returns:
            Synthesized knowledge or None if synthesis failed
        """
        # Validate fragments
        fragments = []
        for frag_id in fragment_ids:
            fragment = self._fragments.get(frag_id)
            if fragment and fragment.confidence >= self.MIN_CONFIDENCE_FOR_SYNTHESIS:
                fragments.append(fragment)

        if len(fragments) < self.MIN_FRAGMENTS_FOR_SYNTHESIS:
            self.logger.warning(f"Not enough valid fragments for synthesis (have {len(fragments)}, need {self.MIN_FRAGMENTS_FOR_SYNTHESIS})")
            return None

        # Perform synthesis based on method
        synthesized_content = self._apply_synthesis_method(method, fragments)
        if not synthesized_content:
            return None

        # Determine knowledge type of synthesis
        type_counts: Dict[KnowledgeType, int] = {}
        for frag in fragments:
            type_counts[frag.knowledge_type] = type_counts.get(frag.knowledge_type, 0) + 1
        dominant_type = max(type_counts, key=type_counts.get)

        # Calculate scores
        avg_confidence = sum(f.confidence for f in fragments) / len(fragments)
        novelty = self._calculate_novelty(synthesized_content)
        utility = self._calculate_utility(fragments, method)

        # Determine domain
        if not target_domain:
            domains = set(f.domain for f in fragments)
            target_domain = list(domains)[0] if len(domains) == 1 else "cross_domain"

        synthesized = SynthesizedKnowledge(
            id=str(uuid4()),
            content=synthesized_content,
            synthesis_method=method,
            source_fragments=fragment_ids,
            knowledge_type=dominant_type,
