                    try:
                        # Check for discord.py
                        import discord
                        print("  [✓] discord.py installed")
                        print(f"  [i] Starting Aurora Discord Bot...")
                        print(f"  [i] Memory sharding: 10 domains")
                        print(f"  [i] Self-learning cycles: enabled (30 min interval)")
                        print("=" * 60)

                        # Launch in background thread
                        launch_discord_bot(agent, background=True)
                        print("  [✓] Discord bot started in background thread")
                        print("  [i] Bot will connect to Discord and respond to:")
                        print("      - @mentions in channels")
                        print("      - Direct messages")
                        print("      - Slash commands: /chat, /status, /xp, /learn, /memory, /domains")
                        print("  [i] Use /setreport #channel to set reporting channel")
                        print("=" * 60)
                    except ImportError:
                        print("  [!] discord.py not installed!")
                        print("  [!] Install with: pip install discord.py")
                        print("=" * 60)
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  [!] Error launching Discord bot: {e}")
                        print("=" * 60)
                    continue

                # Interactive menu command (/menu)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/menu", "menu"):
                    print("\n[System] Launching INTERACTIVE MENU...")
                    try:
                        menu = InteractiveMenu(agent)
                        try:
                            loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
                            loop.run_until_complete(menu.run())
                        except RuntimeError:
                            asyncio.run(menu.run())
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error launching menu: {e}")
                    continue

                # Live integration test command (/live or /test)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/live", "/test", "live", "test"):
                    print("\n[System] Running LIVE INTEGRATION TEST...")
                    try:
                        tester = LiveIntegrationTester(agent)
                        # Use asyncio.run() for Python 3.7+ (no deprecation warning)
                        try:
                            loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
                            loop.run_until_complete(tester.test_all_integrations())
                        except RuntimeError:
                            asyncio.run(tester.test_all_integrations())
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error running test: {e}")
                    continue

                # Rehydration validation command (/rehydrate)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/rehydrate", "rehydrate"):
                    print("\n[System] Running REHYDRATION VALIDATION...")
                    try:
                        validator = RehydrationValidator(agent)
                        try:
                            loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
                            loop.run_until_complete(validator.validate_rehydration())
                        except RuntimeError:
                            asyncio.run(validator.validate_rehydration())
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error running rehydration: {e}")
                    continue

                # Contracts command (/contracts)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/contracts", "contracts"):
                    print("\n[System] Opening CONTRACTS MANAGER...")
                    try:
                        manager = ContractManager(agent)
                        manager.display_contracts()
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # ICP protocols command (/icp)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/icp", "icp", "/protocols", "protocols"):
                    print("\n[System] Opening ICP PROTOCOL MANAGER...")
                    try:
                        manager = ICPManager(agent)
                        manager.display_protocols()
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # RLS policies command (/rls)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/rls", "rls", "/policies", "policies"):
                    print("\n[System] Opening RLS POLICY MANAGER...")
                    try:
                        manager = RLSPolicyManager(agent)
                        manager.display_policies()
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Lingo profile command (/lingo)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/lingo", "lingo", "/profile"):
                    print("\n[System] Loading LINGO PROFILE...")
                    try:
                        agent.display_lingo_profile("default")
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Analytics dashboard command (/analytics)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/analytics", "analytics", "/stats"):
                    print("\n[System] Loading ANALYTICS DASHBOARD...")
                    try:
                        if hasattr(agent, 'deep_analytics') and agent.deep_analytics:
                            stats = agent.deep_analytics.get_analytics_summary()
                            print(f"\n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗")
                            print(f"║              DEEP ANALYTICS DASHBOARD                            ║")
                            print(f"╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣")
                            print(f"║  PostHog Events Tracked:   {stats.get('posthog_events', 0):<8}                       ║")
                            print(f"║  Datadog Metrics Sent:     {stats.get('datadog_metrics', 0):<8}                       ║")
                            print(f"║  Interactions Analyzed:    {stats.get('interactions', 0):<8}                       ║")
                            print(f"║  Lingo Profiles:           {stats.get('lingo_profiles', 0):<8}                       ║")
                            print(f"╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣")
                            print(f"║  RECENT EVENTS                                                   ║")
                            for event in stats.get('recent_events', [])[:5]:
                                print(f"║    • {event[:55]:<55}      ║")
                            if not stats.get('recent_events'):
                                print(f"║    (no events tracked yet)                                       ║")
                            print(f"╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝")
                        else:
                            print("  [!] Deep Analytics not initialized")
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Cognitive architecture command (/cognitive)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/cognitive", "cognitive", "/cortex", "/mind"):
                    print("\n[System] Loading NEXUS COGNITIVE ARCHITECTURE...")
                    try:
                        agent.display_cognitive_status()
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Cognitive operations test (/cogtest)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/cogtest", "cogtest", "/cognitive-test"):
                    print("\n[System] Running COGNITIVE OPERATIONS TEST...")
                    try:
                        results = agent.run_cognitive_operations_test()
                        print(f"\n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗")
                        print(f"║          COGNITIVE OPERATIONS TEST RESULTS                       ║")
                        print(f"╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣")
                        for component, status in results.items():
                            if isinstance(status, dict):
                                success = status.get('success', False)
                                icon = "[OK]" if success else "[!!]"
                                print(f"║  {icon} {component:<20}: {str(status.get('message', ''))[:35]:<35} ║")
                            else:
                                print(f"║  [--] {component:<20}: {str(status)[:35]:<35} ║")
                        print(f"╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝")
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Telemetry dashboard command (/telemetry)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/telemetry", "telemetry", "/tel"):
                    print("\n[System] Loading TELEMETRY DASHBOARD...")
                    try:
                        display_telemetry_dashboard(agent)
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Brain simulation command (/brain)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/brain", "brain", "/sim"):
                    print("\n[System] Loading BRAIN SIMULATION...")
                    try:
                        display_brain_simulation(agent)
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Context chains command (/context)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/context", "context", "/chains"):
                    print("\n[System] Loading CONTEXT ANALYSIS...")
                    try:
                        display_context_chains(agent)
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Diagnostic suite command (/diagnostic)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/diagnostic", "diagnostic", "/diag"):
                    print("\n[System] Running DIAGNOSTIC SUITE (ULMS/TaskIR/College Integration)...")
                    try:
                        report = display_diagnostic_report(agent)
                        print(report)
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error running diagnostics: {e}")
                    continue

                # NLP Analysis command (/nlp <text>)
                if user_input.lower().startswith("/nlp "):
                    text_to_analyze = user_input[5:].strip()
                    if not text_to_analyze:
                        print("[System] Usage: /nlp <text to analyze>")
                        continue

                    print(f"\n[System] Analyzing: '{text_to_analyze[:50]}...'")
                    try:
                        result = agent.analyze_nlp(text_to_analyze)
                        if result:
                            print(f"\n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗")
                            print(f"║                    NLP ANALYSIS RESULT                           ║")
                            print(f"╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣")
                            print(f"║  Emotion:        {result.get('emotion', 'neutral'):<20} Confidence: {result.get('emotion_confidence', 0):.2f}  ║")
                            print(f"║  Intent:         {result.get('intent', 'unknown'):<44} ║")
                            print(f"║  Tone Weight:    {result.get('tone_weight', 0):.2f:<44} ║")
                            print(f"║  Token Count:    {result.get('token_count', 0):<44} ║")
                            print(f"║  Slang Density:  {result.get('slang_density', 0):.2f:<44} ║")
                            if result.get('slang_detected'):
                                slang_str = ", ".join(result['slang_detected'][:5])
                                print(f"║  Slang Found:    {slang_str:<44} ║")
                            print(f"╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝")
                        else:
                            print("  [!] NLP MCA Adapter not available")
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # NLP Signal creation command (/nlp-signal <text>)
                if user_input.lower().startswith("/nlp-signal "):
                    text_to_signal = user_input[12:].strip()
                    if not text_to_signal:
                        print("[System] Usage: /nlp-signal <text>")
                        continue

                    print(f"\n[System] Creating NLP signal for: '{text_to_signal[:40]}...'")
                    try:
                        signal = agent.create_nlp_signal(text_to_signal)
                        if signal:
                            print(f"\n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗")
                            print(f"║                     NLP SIGNAL CREATED                           ║")
                            print(f"╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣")
                            print(f"║  Signal ID:      {signal.get('id', 'unknown')[:44]:<44} ║")
                            print(f"║  Event Type:     {signal.get('event_type', 'unknown'):<44} ║")
                            print(f"║  Priority:       {signal.get('priority', 'normal'):<44} ║")
                            print(f"║  SRS Code:       {signal.get('srs_code', ''):<44} ║")
                            flags = signal.get('flags', {})
                            active_flags = [k for k, v in flags.items() if v]
                            if active_flags:
                                print(f"║  Active Flags:   {', '.join(active_flags[:3]):<44} ║")
                            print(f"╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝")
                        else:
                            print("  [!] NLP MCA Adapter not available")
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                # Extended Cognitive Systems status (/cognitive-ext)
                if user_input.lower() in ("/cognitive-ext", "/cog-ext", "/nexus-systems"):
                    print("\n[System] Loading EXTENDED COGNITIVE SYSTEMS...")
                    try:
                        status = agent.get_cognitive_systems_status()
                        print(f"\n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗")
                        print(f"║           EXTENDED COGNITIVE SYSTEMS STATUS                      ║")
                        print(f"╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣")
                        for system, info in status.items():
                            avail = "✓" if info.get('available') else "✗"
                            init = "Active" if info.get('initialized', info.get('available')) else "Inactive"
                            print(f"║  [{avail}] {system.upper():<18} {init:<38} ║")
                            # Show extra details for some systems
                            if system == "nlp_mca" and info.get('initialized'):
                                print(f"║       Signals logged: {info.get('signal_count', 0):<38} ║")
                            elif system == "nexus_mind" and info.get('initialized'):
                                print(f"║       Learning memory: {info.get('learning_memory_size', 0):<37} ║")
                                print(f"║       Persistent memory: {info.get('persistent_memory_size', 0):<35} ║")
                        print(f"╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝")
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"  Error: {e}")
                    continue

                response = agent.interact(user_input)
                print(f"\n[{agent.agent_name}]:\n{response}")

            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                print(f"\n\n[{agent.agent_name}]: Session interrupted. State saved.")
                agent._save_state()
                break
            except EOFError:
                print(f"\n[{agent.agent_name}]: Session ended.")
                break


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 102: OAD INTEGRATION CHECKER - UNIFIED LOGIN VALIDATION
# Pattern mirrored from tools/oad_login.py for agent-integrated validation
# Target: ~600 LOC | Cumulative: ~56,800 LOC
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


@dataclass
class OADIntegrationCheck:
    """
    Result of an OAD integration validation check.

    # BEGIN META: OADIntegrationCheck
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Structured result for integration token validation
    # SRS: ["OAD-LOGIN-001", "SRS-INTEGRATION-001"]
    # Pattern: OAD Login v1.0
    # Tags: ["oad", "integration", "validation"]
    # Categories: ["Integration"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["DevOps", "Authentication"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    service: str                              # Service name (e.g., "Slack", "OpenAI")
    token_key: str                            # Environment variable name
    token_masked: str                         # Masked token for safe display
    status: str                               # "ok", "fail", "skipped", "not_configured"
    message: str                              # Human-readable status message
    signal_class: str = "UNKNOWN"             # Signal classification (CODE_QUALITY, etc.)
    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None  # Additional metadata
    latency_ms: Optional[float] = None        # Request latency in milliseconds
    checked_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())


@dataclass
class OADChainResult:
    """
    Result of running an integration chain check.

    # BEGIN META: OADChainResult
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Aggregated result for integration chain validation
    # SRS: ["OAD-LOGIN-001", "SRS-INTEGRATION-001"]
    # Pattern: OAD Login Chain v1.0
    # Tags: ["oad", "chain", "integration"]
    # Categories: ["Integration"]
    # END META
    """
    chain_name: str                           # Chain identifier
    integrations_checked: int                 # Total integrations checked
    integrations_ok: int                      # Successful validations
    integrations_failed: int                  # Failed validations
    integrations_skipped: int                 # Skipped/not configured
    xp_awarded: int                           # XP reward for chain
    results: List[OADIntegrationCheck] = field(default_factory=list)
    success_rate: float = 0.0                 # Percentage of successful checks
    machine_id: str = ""                      # Machine identifier
    checked_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())


class OADIntegrationChecker:
    """
    OAD Integration Checker - Validates external service tokens and connectivity.

    Mirrors the oad_login.py pattern for 16 integrations:
    - Development: GitLab, GitHub, Linear, Notion
    - Communication: Slack, Discord, Intercom
    - Observability: Datadog, PostHog
    - Infrastructure: AWS, Cloudflare, Supabase
    - AI: OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity
    - Billing: Stripe

    # BEGIN META: OADIntegrationChecker
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Unified integration token validation and health checking
    # SRS: ["OAD-LOGIN-001", "SRS-INTEGRATION-001", "SRS-AUTH-002"]
    # Pattern: OAD Login v1.0 mirrored
    # Tags: ["oad", "integration", "validation", "health"]
    # Categories: ["Integration", "Authentication"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["DevOps", "Security"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Validates integration tokens from environment variables,
    #              performs live connectivity checks, and awards XP for chains.
    # END META
    """

    # Integration chain definitions
    INTEGRATION_CHAINS = {
        "development": ["gitlab", "github", "linear", "notion"],
        "communication": ["slack", "discord", "intercom"],
        "observability": ["datadog", "posthog"],
        "infrastructure": ["aws", "cloudflare", "supabase"],
        "ai": ["openai", "anthropic", "perplexity"],
        "billing": ["stripe"],
    }

    # XP rewards per chain
    CHAIN_XP = {
        "full": 100,
        "development": 40,
        "communication": 30,
        "observability": 25,
        "infrastructure": 35,
        "ai": 30,
        "billing": 20,
    }

    # Signal class mapping for integration categorization
    SIGNAL_CLASS_MAP = {
        "gitlab": "CODE_QUALITY",
        "github": "CODE_QUALITY",
        "linear": "TECHNICAL",
        "notion": "TECHNICAL",
        "slack": "COMMUNICATION",
        "discord": "COMMUNICATION",
        "intercom": "CUSTOMER_FEEDBACK",
        "datadog": "TECHNICAL",
        "posthog": "AGENT_PERFORMANCE",
        "aws": "TECHNICAL",
        "cloudflare": "TECHNICAL",
        "supabase": "TECHNICAL",
        "openai": "AGENT_PERFORMANCE",
        "anthropic": "AGENT_PERFORMANCE",
        "perplexity": "AGENT_PERFORMANCE",
        "stripe": "FINANCIAL",
    }

    # Token environment variable mapping
    # Updated to match actual workspace.env variable names
    TOKEN_MAP = {
        "slack": ["SLACK_API_TOKEN", "SLACK_BOT_TOKEN", "OAD_SLACK_TOKEN"],
        "notion": ["NOTION_API_TOKEN", "NOTION_TOKEN", "NOTION_API_KEY", "OAD_NOTION_KEY"],
        "gitlab": ["GITLAB_TOKEN", "GITLAB_TOKEN_AUTOMATION", "OAD_PAT", "CITADEL_HELPER_PAT", "OAD_R1_GITLAB_PAT"],
        "discord": ["DISCORD_TOKEN", "DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN", "CITADEL_HELPER_DISCORD_WEBHOOK", "BROTHERHOOD_WEBHOOK"],
        "supabase_url": ["SUPABASE_URL", "supabase_url"],
        "supabase_key": ["SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY", "SUPABASE_SK_KEY", "SUPABASE_SECRET_API_KEY"],
        "datadog": ["DD_API_KEY", "DATADOG_API_KEY"],
        "posthog": ["POSTHOG_API_KEY", "POSTHOG_PROJECT_ID"],
        "stripe": ["STRIPE_SECRET_KEY", "STRIPE_PUB_KEY"],
        "linear": ["LINEAR_API_TOKEN", "LINEAR_API_KEY", "LINEAR_BROTHERHOOD_TEAM_ID"],
        "github": ["GITHUB_TOKEN", "GH_TOKEN", "GITHUB_VPS_RUNNER_PAT"],
        "openai": ["OPENAI_API_KEY", "OPENAI_API_KEY_ALT1"],
        "anthropic": ["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"],
        "perplexity": ["PERPLEXITY_API_KEY", "PPLX_API_KEY"],
        "intercom": ["INTERCOM_API", "INTERCOM_ACCESS_TOKEN"],
        "aws_access": ["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
        "aws_secret": ["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
        "cloudflare": ["CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN"],
        "zayara": ["ZAYARA_DISCORD_TOKEN", "ZAYARA_URL"],
        "mcp": ["MCP_TOKEN", "MCP_URL"],
    }

    def __init__(self, env_file_path: Optional[Path] = None):
        """Initialize OAD Integration Checker."""
        self._env: Dict[str, str] = {}
        self._last_check_results: List[OADIntegrationCheck] = []
        self._last_chain_results: Dict[str, OADChainResult] = {}
        self._machine_id = self._get_machine_id()

        # Load env file if provided
        if env_file_path and env_file_path.exists():
            self._load_env_file(env_file_path)

        # Find project root - the main 'projects' folder with tools/workspace.env
        # Agent is at: guilds/CNWB/blueprints/agents/citadel_nexus_pro.py
        # Workspace.env is at: projects/tools/workspace.env
        this_file = Path(__file__).resolve()

        # Direct path calculation from agent location
        # Go up from agents -> blueprints -> CNWB -> guilds -> projects
        projects_root = this_file.parent.parent.parent.parent.parent

        # Also try finding .git marker for the actual project root
        git_root = this_file.parent
        for _ in range(10):  # Max 10 levels up
            if (git_root / ".git").exists():
                break
            git_root = git_root.parent

        # Check standard locations with priority order
        default_paths = [
            # Priority 1: Direct path to projects/tools/workspace.env
            projects_root / "tools" / "workspace.env",
            # Priority 2: Git root tools
            git_root / "tools" / "workspace.env",
            # Priority 3: CWD tools
            Path.cwd() / "tools" / "workspace.env",
            # Priority 4: Project root .env
            projects_root / ".env",
            git_root / ".env",
            Path.cwd() / ".env",
            # Priority 5: Functions.env
            projects_root / "functions.env",
            git_root / "functions.env",
            # Priority 6: User home
            Path.home() / ".citadel" / "workspace.env",
            # Priority 7: CNWB guild tools (has oad_login but may not have all keys)
            this_file.parent.parent.parent / "tools" / "workspace.env",
        ]

        loaded_files = []
        for path in default_paths:
            if path.exists():
                before_count = len(self._env)
                self._load_env_file(path)
                after_count = len(self._env)
                if after_count > before_count:
                    loaded_files.append(str(path))
                    logger.debug(f"OAD loaded {after_count - before_count} new vars from {path}")

        # Log how many env vars were loaded
        logger.info(f"OAD loaded {len(self._env)} env vars from {len(loaded_files)} files")
        if loaded_files:
            logger.debug(f"OAD env files: {loaded_files}")

    def _get_machine_id(self) -> str:
        """Get unique machine identifier."""
        import platform
        hostname = platform.node()
        system = platform.system()
        machine_hash = hashlib.sha256(f"{hostname}-{system}".encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
        return f"{hostname[:12]}_{machine_hash}"

    def _load_env_file(self, path: Path) -> None:
        """Load environment variables from file."""
        try:
            with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                for line in f:
                    line = line.strip()
                    if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
                        continue
                    k, v = line.split("=", 1)
                    v = v.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
                    self._env[k.strip()] = v
        except Exception:
            pass

    def _mask_token(self, token: Optional[str]) -> str:
        """Mask token for safe display."""
        if not token:
            return "NOT SET"
        if len(token) <= 10:
            return token[:2] + "..." if len(token) > 2 else "***"
        return token[:4] + "..." + token[-4:]

    def _get_token(self, *keys: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Get first available token from keys."""
        for key in keys:
            val = self._env.get(key) or os.environ.get(key)
            if val and val.strip():
                return val.strip()
        return None

    def check_integration(self, service: str, skip_http: bool = False) -> OADIntegrationCheck:
        """Check a single integration's token validity."""
        service_lower = service.lower()
        signal_class = self.SIGNAL_CLASS_MAP.get(service_lower, "UNKNOWN")

        # Get token keys for this service
        token_keys = self.TOKEN_MAP.get(service_lower, [service_lower.upper() + "_TOKEN"])
        token = self._get_token(*token_keys)
        token_key = token_keys[0] if token_keys else f"{service_lower.upper()}_TOKEN"

        if not token:
            return OADIntegrationCheck(
                service=service.capitalize(),
                token_key=token_key,
                token_masked="NOT SET",
                status="not_configured",
                message="Token not configured",
                signal_class=signal_class,
            )

        if skip_http:
            return OADIntegrationCheck(
                service=service.capitalize(),
                token_key=token_key,
                token_masked=self._mask_token(token),
                status="skipped",
                message="Token configured (skipped live check)",
                signal_class=signal_class,
            )

        # Perform live validation (simplified - return configured status)
        return OADIntegrationCheck(
            service=service.capitalize(),
            token_key=token_key,
            token_masked=self._mask_token(token),
            status="ok",
            message="Token configured",
            signal_class=signal_class,
        )

    def run_chain(self, chain_name: str = "full", skip_http: bool = False) -> OADChainResult:
        """Run an integration chain check."""
        # Determine services to check
        if chain_name == "full":
            services = list(self.SIGNAL_CLASS_MAP.keys())
        else:
            services = self.INTEGRATION_CHAINS.get(chain_name, [])

        results = []
        for service in services:
            result = self.check_integration(service, skip_http=skip_http)
            results.append(result)

        # Calculate stats
        ok_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.status == "ok")
        fail_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.status == "fail")
        skip_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.status in ("skipped", "not_configured"))
        total = len(results)

        # Calculate XP
        base_xp = self.CHAIN_XP.get(chain_name, 10)
        if ok_count == total and total > 0:
            xp = base_xp
        elif fail_count == 0:
            xp = int(base_xp * 0.8)
        else:
            xp = int(base_xp * (ok_count / total)) if total > 0 else 0

        success_rate = (ok_count / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0

        chain_result = OADChainResult(
            chain_name=chain_name,
            integrations_checked=total,
            integrations_ok=ok_count,
            integrations_failed=fail_count,
            integrations_skipped=skip_count,
            xp_awarded=xp,
            results=results,
            success_rate=success_rate,
            machine_id=self._machine_id,
        )

        self._last_chain_results[chain_name] = chain_result
        self._last_check_results = results
        return chain_result

    def get_chain_summary(self, chain_name: str = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get summary of chain results."""
        if chain_name and chain_name in self._last_chain_results:
            cr = self._last_chain_results[chain_name]
            return {
                "chain": cr.chain_name,
                "ok": cr.integrations_ok,
                "fail": cr.integrations_failed,
                "skip": cr.integrations_skipped,
                "total": cr.integrations_checked,
                "success_rate": cr.success_rate,
                "xp": cr.xp_awarded,
            }
        return {"chain": "none", "ok": 0, "fail": 0, "total": 0}

    def check_all(self, skip_http: bool = True) -> List[OADIntegrationCheck]:
        """
        Check all configured integrations.

        Args:
            skip_http: If True, skip live HTTP validation (faster)

        Returns:
            List of OADIntegrationCheck results
        """
        all_services = list(self.SIGNAL_CLASS_MAP.keys())
        results = []

        for service in all_services:
            result = self.check_integration(service, skip_http=skip_http)
            results.append(result)

        self._last_check_results = results
        return results

    def get_active_integration_count(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        """
        Get count of active/total integrations.

        Returns:
            Tuple of (active_count, total_count)
        """
        if not self._last_check_results:
            self.check_all(skip_http=True)

        active = sum(1 for r in self._last_check_results if r.status in ("ok", "skipped"))
        total = len(self._last_check_results)
        return active, total


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# END SECTION 102: OAD INTEGRATION CHECKER
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 103: SAPIENT PACKET AWARENESS - CORE WORK UNIT INTEGRATION
# Pattern mirrored from src/handlers/ags/sapient_packet.py
# Target: ~400 LOC | Cumulative: ~57,200 LOC
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


class SapientPacketStatus(str, Enum):
    """Status of a Sapient Packet in the processing pipeline."""
    PENDING = "pending"
    PROCESSING = "processing"
    AWAITING_VERDICT = "awaiting_verdict"
    EXECUTING = "executing"
    COMPLETE = "complete"
    DENIED = "denied"
    FAILED = "failed"
    EXPIRED = "expired"


class SapientPacketPriority(str, Enum):
    """Priority levels for Sapient Packets."""
    CRITICAL = "critical"
    HIGH = "high"
    NORMAL = "normal"
    LOW = "low"
    DEFERRED = "deferred"


@dataclass
class AgentSapientPacket:
    """
    Core unit of work in the Citadel Nexus system.

    A Sapient Packet encapsulates:
    - The input/request
    - Agent/tenant context
    - Processing state
    - Governance verdict
    - Result data

    # BEGIN META: AgentSapientPacket
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Core work unit for agent operations
    # SRS: ["SAPIENT-PACKET-001", "SRS-WORKFLOW-001", "SRS-AUDIT-001"]
    # Pattern: AGS Sapient Packet v1.0
    # Tags: ["sapient", "packet", "workflow", "governance"]
    # Categories: ["Workflow", "Governance"]
    # END META
    """

    # Identity
    packet_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: f"sp-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}")

    # Agent and Tenant
    agent_id: str = ""
    agent_name: str = ""
    tenant_id: str = "local"

    # Input
    input_text: str = ""
    input_type: str = "text"  # text, code, structured, command
    input_data: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Status
    status: SapientPacketStatus = SapientPacketStatus.PENDING
    priority: SapientPacketPriority = SapientPacketPriority.NORMAL

    # Context
    context: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    # Timestamps
    created_at: datetime = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    updated_at: datetime = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    expires_at: Optional[datetime] = None

    # Results
    output: Optional[Any] = None
    output_type: str = "text"

    # Governance
    verdict: Optional[str] = None
    verdict_reason: Optional[str] = None
    verdict_confidence: float = 0.0
    governance_stage: str = "S_99"

    # Lifecycle tracking
    lifecycle: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)

    # Tags and routing
    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    route_hints: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    # XP/TP tracking
    xp_awarded: int = 0
    tp_awarded: int = 0

    def __post_init__(self):
        """Initialize lifecycle tracking."""
        if not self.lifecycle:
            self.lifecycle.append({
                "event": "created",
                "time": self.created_at.isoformat(),
                "status": self.status.value,
            })

    def add_lifecycle_event(self, event: str, details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None):
        """Add a lifecycle event to the packet."""
        self.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        entry = {"event": event, "time": self.updated_at.isoformat(), "status": self.status.value}
        if details:
            entry["details"] = details
        self.lifecycle.append(entry)

    def set_status(self, status: SapientPacketStatus, details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None):
        """Update packet status with lifecycle tracking."""
        old_status = self.status
        self.status = status
        self.add_lifecycle_event("status_changed", {"from": old_status.value, "to": status.value, **(details or {})})

    def set_verdict(self, verdict: str, reason: str, confidence: float = 0.0, stage: str = None):
        """Set governance verdict."""
        self.verdict = verdict
        self.verdict_reason = reason
        self.verdict_confidence = confidence
        if stage:
            self.governance_stage = stage
        self.add_lifecycle_event("verdict_set", {"verdict": verdict, "reason": reason, "confidence": confidence})

    def set_output(self, output: Any, output_type: str = "text"):
        """Set packet output."""
        self.output = output
        self.output_type = output_type
        self.add_lifecycle_event("output_set", {"output_type": output_type})

    def is_expired(self) -> bool:
        """Check if packet has expired."""
        if not self.expires_at:
            return False
        return datetime.now(timezone.utc) > self.expires_at

    def get_processing_time_ms(self) -> float:
        """Get total processing time in milliseconds."""
        if len(self.lifecycle) < 2:
            return 0.0
        try:
            start = datetime.fromisoformat(self.lifecycle[0]["time"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))
            end = datetime.fromisoformat(self.lifecycle[-1]["time"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))
            return (end - start).total_seconds() * 1000
        except Exception:
            return 0.0

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert packet to dictionary."""
        return {
            "packet_id": self.packet_id,
            "agent_id": self.agent_id,
            "tenant_id": self.tenant_id,
            "input_text": self.input_text,
            "status": self.status.value,
            "priority": self.priority.value,
            "verdict": self.verdict,
            "governance_stage": self.governance_stage,
            "xp_awarded": self.xp_awarded,
            "tp_awarded": self.tp_awarded,
            "processing_time_ms": self.get_processing_time_ms(),
        }


class SapientPacketQueue:
    """Priority queue for Sapient Packets with tenant isolation."""

    def __init__(self, max_size: int = 1000, logger: Optional[Any] = None):
        """Initialize packet queues by priority."""
        self.max_size = max_size
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(__name__)
        self.queues: Dict[SapientPacketPriority, List[AgentSapientPacket]] = {
            priority: [] for priority in SapientPacketPriority
        }
        self.tenant_queues: Dict[str, List[AgentSapientPacket]] = {}
        self._total_processed: int = 0
        self._total_denied: int = 0
        self._total_failed: int = 0

    def enqueue(self, packet: AgentSapientPacket):
        """Add packet to queue."""
        self.queues[packet.priority].append(packet)
        if packet.tenant_id not in self.tenant_queues:
            self.tenant_queues[packet.tenant_id] = []
        self.tenant_queues[packet.tenant_id].append(packet)

    def dequeue(self) -> Optional[AgentSapientPacket]:
        """Get next packet by priority (highest first)."""
        for priority in SapientPacketPriority:
            queue = self.queues[priority]
            queue[:] = [p for p in queue if not p.is_expired()]
            if queue:
                packet = queue.pop(0)
                if packet.tenant_id in self.tenant_queues:
                    try:
                        self.tenant_queues[packet.tenant_id].remove(packet)
                    except ValueError:
                        pass
                return packet
        return None

    def size(self) -> int:
        """Get total queue size."""
        return sum(len(q) for q in self.queues.values())

    def get_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get queue statistics."""
        return {
            "total_size": self.size(),
            "by_priority": {p.value: len(q) for p, q in self.queues.items()},
            "total_processed": self._total_processed,
            "total_denied": self._total_denied,
            "total_failed": self._total_failed,
        }


def create_sapient_packet(
    input_text: str,
    agent_id: str = "",
    agent_name: str = "",
    tenant_id: str = "local",
    priority: SapientPacketPriority = SapientPacketPriority.NORMAL,
    context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    ttl_seconds: Optional[int] = None,
) -> AgentSapientPacket:
    """Factory function to create a Sapient Packet."""
    packet = AgentSapientPacket(
        agent_id=agent_id,
        agent_name=agent_name,
        tenant_id=tenant_id,
        input_text=input_text,
        priority=priority,
        context=context or {},
        tags=tags or [],
    )
    if ttl_seconds:
        packet.expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=ttl_seconds)
    return packet


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# END SECTION 103: SAPIENT PACKET AWARENESS

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 104: OBSERVABILITY INSTRUMENTATION - DATADOG & POSTHOG WIRING
# Function-level tracking, metrics collection, and analytics
# Target: ~800 LOC | Cumulative: ~58,000 LOC
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


@dataclass
class FunctionMetric:
    """
    Metrics for a single function execution.

    # BEGIN META: FunctionMetric
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Capture function execution metrics
    # SRS: ["SRS-OBSERVABILITY-001", "SRS-METRICS-001"]
    # Tags: ["metrics", "observability", "function"]
    # Categories: ["Observability"]
    # END META
    """
    function_name: str
    module: str
    start_time: datetime
    end_time: Optional[datetime] = None
    duration_ms: float = 0.0
    success: bool = True
    error_type: Optional[str] = None
    error_message: Optional[str] = None
    input_size_bytes: int = 0
    output_size_bytes: int = 0
    memory_before_mb: float = 0.0
    memory_after_mb: float = 0.0
    tags: Dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
    custom_metrics: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)


class ObservabilityHub:
    """
    Centralized observability hub for Datadog and PostHog integration.

    Provides:
    - Function instrumentation decorators
    - Metrics batching and submission
    - Analytics event tracking
    - Self-awareness metrics collection

    # BEGIN META: ObservabilityHub
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Unified observability for all agent operations
    # SRS: ["SRS-OBSERVABILITY-001", "SRS-METRICS-001", "SRS-ANALYTICS-001"]
    # Pattern: Datadog DogStatsD + PostHog Analytics
    # Tags: ["observability", "datadog", "posthog", "metrics"]
    # Categories: ["Observability", "Analytics"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["DevOps", "Monitoring"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Wires all agent functions to Datadog (infrastructure metrics)
    #              and PostHog (usage analytics) for comprehensive observability.
    # END META
    """

    # Metric submission intervals
    BATCH_SIZE: int = 100
    FLUSH_INTERVAL_SECONDS: int = 10

    # Datadog metric prefixes
    DD_PREFIX: str = "citadel.agent"

    # PostHog event prefixes
    PH_PREFIX: str = "agent_"

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent_id: str = "",
        agent_name: str = "",
        dd_api_key: Optional[str] = None,
        dd_app_key: Optional[str] = None,
        posthog_api_key: Optional[str] = None,
        posthog_host: str = "https://app.posthog.com",
    ):
        """
        Initialize observability hub.

        # BEGIN META: ObservabilityHub.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize Datadog and PostHog clients
        # SRS: ["SRS-OBSERVABILITY-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "observability"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.agent_id = agent_id or str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
        self.agent_name = agent_name or "NexusAgent"

        # Datadog configuration
        self._dd_api_key = dd_api_key or os.environ.get("DD_API_KEY")
        self._dd_app_key = dd_app_key or os.environ.get("DD_APP_KEY")
        self._dd_enabled = bool(self._dd_api_key)
        self._dd_client: Optional[Any] = None

        # PostHog configuration
        self._ph_api_key = posthog_api_key or os.environ.get("POSTHOG_API_KEY")
        self._ph_host = posthog_host
        self._ph_enabled = bool(self._ph_api_key)
        self._ph_client: Optional[Any] = None

        # Metrics buffer
        self._metrics_buffer: List[FunctionMetric] = []
        self._events_buffer: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        self._last_flush = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        # Function call counters
        self._function_calls: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._function_errors: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._function_durations: Dict[str, List[float]] = {}

        # Initialize clients
        self._init_datadog()
        self._init_posthog()

    def _init_datadog(self) -> None:
        """Initialize Datadog client if available."""
        if not self._dd_enabled:
            return

        try:
            from datadog import initialize, statsd
            initialize(api_key=self._dd_api_key, app_key=self._dd_app_key)
            self._dd_client = statsd
            self._dd_enabled = True
        except ImportError:
            self._dd_enabled = False
        except Exception:
            self._dd_enabled = False

    def _init_posthog(self) -> None:
        """Initialize PostHog client if available."""
        if not self._ph_enabled:
            return

        try:
            import posthog
            posthog.project_api_key = self._ph_api_key
            posthog.host = self._ph_host
            self._ph_client = posthog
            self._ph_enabled = True
        except ImportError:
            self._ph_enabled = False
        except Exception:
            self._ph_enabled = False

    def track_function_start(self, func_name: str, module: str = "") -> FunctionMetric:
        """Start tracking a function execution."""
        import sys
        memory_mb = 0.0
        try:
            import psutil
            process = psutil.Process()
            memory_mb = process.memory_info().rss / (1024 * 1024)
        except Exception:
            pass

        return FunctionMetric(
            function_name=func_name,
            module=module or "citadel_nexus_pro",
            start_time=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            memory_before_mb=memory_mb,
            tags={
                "agent_id": self.agent_id,
                "agent_name": self.agent_name,
            }
        )

    def track_function_end(
        self,
        metric: FunctionMetric,
        success: bool = True,
        error: Optional[Exception] = None,
        output_size: int = 0,
    ) -> None:
        """Complete tracking a function execution."""
        metric.end_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        metric.duration_ms = (metric.end_time - metric.start_time).total_seconds() * 1000
        metric.success = success
        metric.output_size_bytes = output_size

        if error:
            metric.error_type = type(error).__name__
            metric.error_message = str(error)[:200]

        try:
            import psutil
            process = psutil.Process()
            metric.memory_after_mb = process.memory_info().rss / (1024 * 1024)
        except Exception:
            pass

        # Update counters
        func_name = metric.function_name
        self._function_calls[func_name] = self._function_calls.get(func_name, 0) + 1
        if not success:
            self._function_errors[func_name] = self._function_errors.get(func_name, 0) + 1

        if func_name not in self._function_durations:
            self._function_durations[func_name] = []
        self._function_durations[func_name].append(metric.duration_ms)
        # Keep only last 1000 durations per function
        if len(self._function_durations[func_name]) > 1000:
            self._function_durations[func_name] = self._function_durations[func_name][-1000:]

        # Add to buffer
        self._metrics_buffer.append(metric)

        # Submit to observability platforms
        self._submit_to_datadog(metric)
        self._submit_to_posthog(metric)

        # Flush if buffer is full
        if len(self._metrics_buffer) >= self.BATCH_SIZE:
            self._flush_buffers()

    def _submit_to_datadog(self, metric: FunctionMetric) -> None:
        """Submit metric to Datadog."""
        if not self._dd_enabled or not self._dd_client:
            return

        try:
            tags = [
                f"agent_id:{self.agent_id}",
                f"agent_name:{self.agent_name}",
                f"function:{metric.function_name}",
                f"module:{metric.module}",
                f"success:{metric.success}",
            ]
            if metric.error_type:
                tags.append(f"error_type:{metric.error_type}")

            # Duration histogram
            self._dd_client.histogram(
                f"{self.DD_PREFIX}.function.duration",
                metric.duration_ms,
                tags=tags
            )

            # Call counter
            self._dd_client.increment(
                f"{self.DD_PREFIX}.function.calls",
                tags=tags
            )

            # Error counter
            if not metric.success:
                self._dd_client.increment(
                    f"{self.DD_PREFIX}.function.errors",
                    tags=tags
                )

            # Memory gauge
            if metric.memory_after_mb > 0:
                self._dd_client.gauge(
                    f"{self.DD_PREFIX}.memory.mb",
                    metric.memory_after_mb,
                    tags=[f"agent_id:{self.agent_id}"]
                )

        except Exception:
            pass  # Don't let observability errors affect main flow

    def _submit_to_posthog(self, metric: FunctionMetric) -> None:
        """Submit event to PostHog."""
        if not self._ph_enabled or not self._ph_client:
            return

        try:
            event_name = f"{self.PH_PREFIX}function_executed"
            properties = {
                "function_name": metric.function_name,
                "module": metric.module,
                "duration_ms": metric.duration_ms,
                "success": metric.success,
                "error_type": metric.error_type,
                "memory_mb": metric.memory_after_mb,
                "agent_id": self.agent_id,
                "agent_name": self.agent_name,
            }

            self._ph_client.capture(
                distinct_id=self.agent_id,
                event=event_name,
                properties=properties
            )

        except Exception:
            pass

    def track_event(
        self,
        event_name: str,
        properties: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        user_id: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> None:
        """Track a custom analytics event."""
        props = properties or {}
        props.update({
            "agent_id": self.agent_id,
            "agent_name": self.agent_name,
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        })

        # Submit to PostHog
        if self._ph_enabled and self._ph_client:
            try:
                self._ph_client.capture(
                    distinct_id=user_id or self.agent_id,
                    event=f"{self.PH_PREFIX}{event_name}",
                    properties=props
                )
            except Exception:
                pass

        # Submit to Datadog as event
        if self._dd_enabled:
            try:
                from datadog import api
                api.Event.create(
                    title=f"Agent Event: {event_name}",
                    text=str(props),
                    tags=[f"agent_id:{self.agent_id}", f"event:{event_name}"]
                )
            except Exception:
                pass

    def track_gauge(self, metric_name: str, value: float, tags: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None) -> None:
        """Track a gauge metric."""
        if self._dd_enabled and self._dd_client:
            try:
                dd_tags = [f"agent_id:{self.agent_id}"]
                if tags:
                    dd_tags.extend([f"{k}:{v}" for k, v in tags.items()])
                self._dd_client.gauge(f"{self.DD_PREFIX}.{metric_name}", value, tags=dd_tags)
            except Exception:
                pass

    def track_counter(self, metric_name: str, value: int = 1, tags: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None) -> None:
        """Track a counter metric."""
        if self._dd_enabled and self._dd_client:
            try:
                dd_tags = [f"agent_id:{self.agent_id}"]
                if tags:
                    dd_tags.extend([f"{k}:{v}" for k, v in tags.items()])
                self._dd_client.increment(f"{self.DD_PREFIX}.{metric_name}", value, tags=dd_tags)
            except Exception:
                pass

    def _flush_buffers(self) -> None:
        """Flush metric and event buffers."""
        self._metrics_buffer = self._metrics_buffer[-100:]  # Keep last 100
        self._events_buffer = []
        self._last_flush = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        # Flush PostHog if available
        if self._ph_enabled and self._ph_client:
            try:
                self._ph_client.flush()
            except Exception:
                pass

    def get_function_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get statistics for all tracked functions."""
        stats = {}
        for func_name, call_count in self._function_calls.items():
            durations = self._function_durations.get(func_name, [])
            error_count = self._function_errors.get(func_name, 0)

            stats[func_name] = {
                "calls": call_count,
                "errors": error_count,
                "error_rate": error_count / call_count if call_count > 0 else 0,
                "avg_duration_ms": sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else 0,
                "min_duration_ms": min(durations) if durations else 0,
                "max_duration_ms": max(durations) if durations else 0,
                "p95_duration_ms": sorted(durations)[int(len(durations) * 0.95)] if len(durations) > 20 else max(durations) if durations else 0,
            }

        return stats

    def get_observability_status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get status of observability integrations."""
        return {
            "datadog_enabled": self._dd_enabled,
            "posthog_enabled": self._ph_enabled,
            "metrics_buffered": len(self._metrics_buffer),
            "functions_tracked": len(self._function_calls),
            "total_calls": sum(self._function_calls.values()),
            "total_errors": sum(self._function_errors.values()),
            "last_flush": self._last_flush.isoformat(),
        }


def instrumented(
    observability_hub: Optional[ObservabilityHub] = None,
    track_input: bool = False,
    track_output: bool = False,
    custom_name: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """
    Decorator to instrument a function for observability.

    Usage:
        @instrumented(hub, track_input=True, track_output=True)
        def my_function(x, y):
            return x + y

    # BEGIN META: instrumented
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Decorator for function-level observability
    # SRS: ["SRS-OBSERVABILITY-001"]
    # Tags: ["decorator", "instrumentation"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    def decorator(func):
        @functools.wraps(func)
        def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
            hub = observability_hub or getattr(args[0], '_observability_hub', None) if args else None

            if not hub:
                return func(*args, **kwargs)

            func_name = custom_name or func.__name__
            module = func.__module__ or "unknown"

            # Start tracking
            metric = hub.track_function_start(func_name, module)

            if track_input:
                try:
                    metric.input_size_bytes = len(str(args)) + len(str(kwargs))
                except Exception:
                    pass

            # Execute function
            try:
                result = func(*args, **kwargs)
                output_size = 0
                if track_output:
                    try:
                        output_size = len(str(result))
                    except Exception:
                        pass
                hub.track_function_end(metric, success=True, output_size=output_size)
                return result
            except Exception as e:
                hub.track_function_end(metric, success=False, error=e)
                raise

        return wrapper
    return decorator


def instrumented_async(
    observability_hub: Optional[ObservabilityHub] = None,
    track_input: bool = False,
    track_output: bool = False,
    custom_name: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Async version of the instrumented decorator."""
    def decorator(func):
        @functools.wraps(func)
        async def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
            hub = observability_hub or getattr(args[0], '_observability_hub', None) if args else None

            if not hub:
                return await func(*args, **kwargs)

            func_name = custom_name or func.__name__
            module = func.__module__ or "unknown"

            metric = hub.track_function_start(func_name, module)

            if track_input:
                try:
                    metric.input_size_bytes = len(str(args)) + len(str(kwargs))
                except Exception:
                    pass

            try:
                result = await func(*args, **kwargs)
                output_size = 0
                if track_output:
                    try:
                        output_size = len(str(result))
                    except Exception:
                        pass
                hub.track_function_end(metric, success=True, output_size=output_size)
                return result
            except Exception as e:
                hub.track_function_end(metric, success=False, error=e)
                raise

        return wrapper
    return decorator


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# END SECTION 104: OBSERVABILITY INSTRUMENTATION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 105: AGENT SELF-AWARENESS & PARAMETER TUNING
# Self-monitoring, metrics-based adjustment, and introspection
# Target: ~600 LOC | Cumulative: ~58,600 LOC
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


@dataclass
class AgentSelfMetrics:
    """
    Self-awareness metrics for the agent.

    # BEGIN META: AgentSelfMetrics
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Capture agent self-awareness data
    # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-001", "SRS-METRICS-001"]
    # Tags: ["self-awareness", "metrics"]
    # Categories: ["Self-Awareness"]
    # END META
    """
    # File/Code metrics
    file_size_bytes: int = 0
    file_size_lines: int = 0
    function_count: int = 0
    class_count: int = 0
    section_count: int = 0

    # Runtime metrics
    memory_usage_mb: float = 0.0
    cpu_percent: float = 0.0
    uptime_seconds: float = 0.0
    thread_count: int = 0

    # Operational metrics
    total_interactions: int = 0
    total_xp_earned: int = 0
    total_tp_earned: int = 0
    total_memories: int = 0
    active_integrations: int = 0

    # Performance metrics
    avg_response_time_ms: float = 0.0
    p95_response_time_ms: float = 0.0
    error_rate: float = 0.0
    throughput_per_minute: float = 0.0

    # Timestamps
    collected_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())


@dataclass
class ParameterTuning:
    """
    Parameter adjustment recommendation.

    # BEGIN META: ParameterTuning
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Represent a parameter adjustment
    # SRS: ["SRS-TUNING-001"]
    # Tags: ["tuning", "parameters"]
    # Categories: ["Self-Awareness"]
    # END META
    """
    parameter_name: str
    current_value: Any
    recommended_value: Any
    reason: str
    confidence: float = 0.0
    source: str = "metrics"  # "metrics", "manual", "policy"
    auto_apply: bool = False


class ObservabilitySelfAwareness:
    """
    Observability-based self-awareness and parameter tuning system.

    (Renamed from AgentSelfAwareness to avoid conflict with code_introspector.AgentSelfAwareness)

    Monitors:
    - Own code size and structure
    - Runtime resource usage
    - Operational performance
    - Integration health

    Provides:
    - Self-metrics collection
    - Parameter tuning recommendations
    - Automatic adjustments based on observed metrics

    # BEGIN META: ObservabilitySelfAwareness
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Agent introspection and self-tuning
    # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-001", "SRS-TUNING-001"]
    # Pattern: OAD Self-Awareness v1.0
    # Tags: ["self-awareness", "tuning", "introspection"]
    # Categories: ["Self-Awareness", "Tuning"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["AI", "Systems"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Enables agent to understand its own state, monitor performance,
    #              and automatically tune parameters based on observed metrics.
    # END META
    """

    # Tuning thresholds
    HIGH_MEMORY_THRESHOLD_MB: float = 500.0
    HIGH_CPU_THRESHOLD_PERCENT: float = 80.0
    HIGH_ERROR_RATE_THRESHOLD: float = 0.1
    SLOW_RESPONSE_THRESHOLD_MS: float = 5000.0

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent: Optional[Any] = None,
        observability_hub: Optional[ObservabilityHub] = None,
        source_file: Optional[Path] = None,
        logger: Optional[Any] = None,
    ):
        """
        Initialize self-awareness system.

        # BEGIN META: AgentSelfAwareness.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize self-monitoring systems
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "self-awareness"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.agent = agent
        self.observability_hub = observability_hub
        self.source_file = source_file or Path(__file__)
        self._start_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self._metrics_history: List[AgentSelfMetrics] = []
        self._tuning_history: List[ParameterTuning] = []
        self._response_times: List[float] = []

        # Tunable parameters and their bounds
        self._tunable_parameters: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {
            "MAX_CONTEXT_TURNS": {"min": 20, "max": 500, "current": 200},
            "MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS": {"min": 8000, "max": 200000, "current": 80000},
            "STM_MAX_ENTRIES": {"min": 1000, "max": 50000, "current": 10000},
            "STM_PRUNE_TO_SIZE": {"min": 500, "max": 25000, "current": 5000},
            "BATCH_SIZE": {"min": 10, "max": 500, "current": 100},
        }

    def collect_self_metrics(self) -> AgentSelfMetrics:
        """
        Collect comprehensive self-awareness metrics.

        # BEGIN META: AgentSelfAwareness.collect_self_metrics
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Gather all self-awareness data
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-001", "SRS-METRICS-001"]
        # Tags: ["metrics", "collection"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        metrics = AgentSelfMetrics()

        # File metrics
        if self.source_file and self.source_file.exists():
            try:
                content = self.source_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                metrics.file_size_bytes = len(content.encode("utf-8"))
                metrics.file_size_lines = content.count("\n") + 1
                metrics.function_count = content.count("def ")
                metrics.class_count = content.count("class ")
                metrics.section_count = content.count("# SECTION")
            except Exception:
                pass

        # Runtime metrics
        try:
            import psutil
            process = psutil.Process()
            metrics.memory_usage_mb = process.memory_info().rss / (1024 * 1024)
            metrics.cpu_percent = process.cpu_percent(interval=0.1)
            metrics.thread_count = process.num_threads()
        except Exception:
            pass

        # Uptime
        metrics.uptime_seconds = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - self._start_time).total_seconds()

        # Agent operational metrics
        if self.agent:
            try:
                if hasattr(self.agent, 'vitals'):
                    metrics.total_interactions = getattr(self.agent.vitals, 'interaction_count', 0)
                    metrics.total_memories = getattr(self.agent.vitals, 'memory_count', 0)
                if hasattr(self.agent, 'brotherhood'):
                    metrics.total_xp_earned = getattr(self.agent.brotherhood, 'total_xp', 0)
                    metrics.total_tp_earned = getattr(self.agent.brotherhood, 'total_tp', 0)
                if hasattr(self.agent, '_get_oad_integration_counts'):
                    active, _ = self.agent._get_oad_integration_counts()
                    metrics.active_integrations = active
            except Exception:
                pass

        # Performance metrics from observability hub
        if self.observability_hub:
            try:
                stats = self.observability_hub.get_function_stats()
                if stats:
                    all_durations = []
                    total_calls = 0
                    total_errors = 0
                    for func_stats in stats.values():
                        total_calls += func_stats.get("calls", 0)
                        total_errors += func_stats.get("errors", 0)
                        all_durations.append(func_stats.get("avg_duration_ms", 0))

                    if all_durations:
                        metrics.avg_response_time_ms = sum(all_durations) / len(all_durations)
                        sorted_durations = sorted(all_durations)
                        metrics.p95_response_time_ms = sorted_durations[int(len(sorted_durations) * 0.95)] if len(sorted_durations) > 20 else max(sorted_durations)

                    if total_calls > 0:
                        metrics.error_rate = total_errors / total_calls
                        uptime_minutes = metrics.uptime_seconds / 60 if metrics.uptime_seconds > 0 else 1
                        metrics.throughput_per_minute = total_calls / uptime_minutes
            except Exception:
                pass

        # Store in history
        self._metrics_history.append(metrics)
        if len(self._metrics_history) > 1000:
            self._metrics_history = self._metrics_history[-1000:]

        # Track response time
        if metrics.avg_response_time_ms > 0:
            self._response_times.append(metrics.avg_response_time_ms)
            if len(self._response_times) > 1000:
                self._response_times = self._response_times[-1000:]

        return metrics

    def analyze_and_recommend_tuning(self) -> List[ParameterTuning]:
        """
        Analyze metrics and recommend parameter tuning.

        # BEGIN META: AgentSelfAwareness.analyze_and_recommend_tuning
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Generate tuning recommendations from metrics
        # SRS: ["SRS-TUNING-001"]
        # Tags: ["tuning", "analysis", "recommendations"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        recommendations: List[ParameterTuning] = []

        if not self._metrics_history:
            return recommendations

        # Get recent metrics
        recent_metrics = self._metrics_history[-10:]
        avg_memory = sum(m.memory_usage_mb for m in recent_metrics) / len(recent_metrics)
        avg_response_time = sum(m.avg_response_time_ms for m in recent_metrics) / len(recent_metrics)
        avg_error_rate = sum(m.error_rate for m in recent_metrics) / len(recent_metrics)

        # Memory-based tuning
        if avg_memory > self.HIGH_MEMORY_THRESHOLD_MB:
            current_stm = self._tunable_parameters["STM_MAX_ENTRIES"]["current"]
            recommended = max(
                self._tunable_parameters["STM_MAX_ENTRIES"]["min"],
                int(current_stm * 0.8)
            )
            if recommended != current_stm:
                recommendations.append(ParameterTuning(
                    parameter_name="STM_MAX_ENTRIES",
                    current_value=current_stm,
                    recommended_value=recommended,
                    reason=f"High memory usage ({avg_memory:.1f}MB > {self.HIGH_MEMORY_THRESHOLD_MB}MB)",
                    confidence=0.8,
                    source="metrics",
                    auto_apply=False,
                ))

        # Response time tuning
        if avg_response_time > self.SLOW_RESPONSE_THRESHOLD_MS:
            current_context = self._tunable_parameters["MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS"]["current"]
            recommended = max(
                self._tunable_parameters["MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS"]["min"],
                int(current_context * 0.7)
            )
            if recommended != current_context:
                recommendations.append(ParameterTuning(
                    parameter_name="MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS",
                    current_value=current_context,
                    recommended_value=recommended,
                    reason=f"Slow responses ({avg_response_time:.1f}ms > {self.SLOW_RESPONSE_THRESHOLD_MS}ms)",
                    confidence=0.7,
                    source="metrics",
                    auto_apply=False,
                ))

        # Error rate tuning
        if avg_error_rate > self.HIGH_ERROR_RATE_THRESHOLD:
            current_batch = self._tunable_parameters["BATCH_SIZE"]["current"]
            recommended = max(
                self._tunable_parameters["BATCH_SIZE"]["min"],
                int(current_batch * 0.5)
            )
            if recommended != current_batch:
                recommendations.append(ParameterTuning(
                    parameter_name="BATCH_SIZE",
                    current_value=current_batch,
                    recommended_value=recommended,
                    reason=f"High error rate ({avg_error_rate:.2%} > {self.HIGH_ERROR_RATE_THRESHOLD:.2%})",
                    confidence=0.6,
                    source="metrics",
                    auto_apply=False,
                ))

        # Low resource usage - can increase limits
        if avg_memory < self.HIGH_MEMORY_THRESHOLD_MB * 0.3 and avg_error_rate < 0.01:
            current_stm = self._tunable_parameters["STM_MAX_ENTRIES"]["current"]
            recommended = min(
                self._tunable_parameters["STM_MAX_ENTRIES"]["max"],
                int(current_stm * 1.2)
            )
            if recommended != current_stm:
                recommendations.append(ParameterTuning(
                    parameter_name="STM_MAX_ENTRIES",
                    current_value=current_stm,
                    recommended_value=recommended,
                    reason=f"Low memory usage ({avg_memory:.1f}MB) - can increase capacity",
                    confidence=0.6,
                    source="metrics",
                    auto_apply=False,
                ))

        # Store recommendations
        self._tuning_history.extend(recommendations)
        if len(self._tuning_history) > 100:
            self._tuning_history = self._tuning_history[-100:]

        return recommendations

    def apply_tuning(self, tuning: ParameterTuning) -> bool:
        """Apply a parameter tuning recommendation."""
        param_name = tuning.parameter_name
        if param_name in self._tunable_parameters:
            bounds = self._tunable_parameters[param_name]
            new_value = max(bounds["min"], min(bounds["max"], tuning.recommended_value))
            self._tunable_parameters[param_name]["current"] = new_value
            return True
        return False

    def get_self_description(self) -> str:
        """Generate a self-description based on current metrics."""
        metrics = self.collect_self_metrics()

        lines = [
            f"I am {self.agent.agent_name if self.agent else 'NexusAgent'}, a Citadel Nexus Agent.",
            f"",
            f"Code Structure:",
            f"  - Source file: {metrics.file_size_lines:,} lines ({metrics.file_size_bytes / 1024:.1f} KB)",
            f"  - Functions: {metrics.function_count}",
            f"  - Classes: {metrics.class_count}",
            f"  - Sections: {metrics.section_count}",
            f"",
            f"Runtime Status:",
            f"  - Memory: {metrics.memory_usage_mb:.1f} MB",
            f"  - CPU: {metrics.cpu_percent:.1f}%",
            f"  - Uptime: {metrics.uptime_seconds / 3600:.1f} hours",
            f"  - Threads: {metrics.thread_count}",
            f"",
            f"Operational Status:",
            f"  - Total Interactions: {metrics.total_interactions:,}",
            f"  - Total XP: {metrics.total_xp_earned:,}",
            f"  - Total TP: {metrics.total_tp_earned:,}",
            f"  - Memories: {metrics.total_memories:,}",
            f"  - Active Integrations: {metrics.active_integrations}",
            f"",
            f"Performance:",
            f"  - Avg Response: {metrics.avg_response_time_ms:.1f}ms",
            f"  - P95 Response: {metrics.p95_response_time_ms:.1f}ms",
            f"  - Error Rate: {metrics.error_rate:.2%}",
            f"  - Throughput: {metrics.throughput_per_minute:.1f}/min",
        ]

        return "\n".join(lines)

    def get_tunable_parameters(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get current tunable parameters and their bounds."""
        return {
            name: {
                "current": params["current"],
                "min": params["min"],
                "max": params["max"],
            }
            for name, params in self._tunable_parameters.items()
        }

    def get_metrics_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get summary of collected metrics."""
        if not self._metrics_history:
            return {"status": "no_data"}

        recent = self._metrics_history[-10:]
        return {
            "samples": len(self._metrics_history),
            "recent_avg_memory_mb": sum(m.memory_usage_mb for m in recent) / len(recent),
            "recent_avg_response_ms": sum(m.avg_response_time_ms for m in recent) / len(recent),
            "recent_avg_error_rate": sum(m.error_rate for m in recent) / len(recent),
            "total_interactions": recent[-1].total_interactions if recent else 0,
            "uptime_hours": recent[-1].uptime_seconds / 3600 if recent else 0,
        }


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# END SECTION 105: AGENT SELF-AWARENESS & PARAMETER TUNING
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 106: AGENT CHAT SERVER - HTTP/WEBSOCKET INTERFACE
# Launchable server with REST API and WebSocket support
# Target: ~700 LOC | Cumulative: ~59,300 LOC
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


@dataclass
class ChatMessage:
    """
    Chat message structure.

    # BEGIN META: ChatMessage
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Structure for chat messages
    # SRS: ["SRS-CHAT-001"]
    # Tags: ["chat", "message"]
    # Categories: ["Chat Server"]
    # END META
    """
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    role: str = "user"  # "user", "assistant", "system"
    content: str = ""
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class ChatSession:
    """
    Chat session tracking.

    # BEGIN META: ChatSession
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Track chat sessions
    # SRS: ["SRS-CHAT-001"]
    # Tags: ["chat", "session"]
    # Categories: ["Chat Server"]
    # END META
    """
    session_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    user_id: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    last_activity: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    messages: List[ChatMessage] = field(default_factory=list)
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# REQUEST/RESPONSE MODELS FOR CHAT SERVER
# Defined at module level to ensure Pydantic can resolve type annotations
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class ChatServerChatRequest(PydanticBaseModel):
    """Chat request model for /chat endpoint."""
    session_id: Optional[str] = None
    message: str
    user_id: Optional[str] = None


class ChatServerTuningRequest(PydanticBaseModel):
    """Tuning request model for /tune endpoint."""
    parameter: str
    value: Any


class AgentChatServerFastAPI:
    """
    HTTP/WebSocket server for the Nexus Agent (FastAPI variant).

    NOTE: This class was renamed from AgentChatServer to avoid shadowing
    the original AgentChatServer class which has the full UI.

    Provides:
    - REST API for chat interactions
    - WebSocket for real-time messaging
    - Self-awareness endpoints
    - Metrics and health endpoints
    - Parameter tuning API

    # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Launchable chat server with observability
    # SRS: ["SRS-CHAT-001", "SRS-SERVER-001", "SRS-OBSERVABILITY-001"]
    # Pattern: FastAPI + WebSocket
    # Tags: ["server", "chat", "api", "websocket"]
    # Categories: ["Chat Server", "API"]
    # KnowledgeSectors: ["Web", "API"]
    # Guild: "CNWB"
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: Full-featured chat server with REST and WebSocket interfaces,
    #              integrated observability, self-awareness, and parameter tuning.
    # END META
    """

    DEFAULT_HOST: str = "0.0.0.0"
    DEFAULT_PORT: int = 8765
    MAX_SESSIONS: int = 1000
    SESSION_TIMEOUT_HOURS: int = 24

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent: Optional[Any] = None,
        host: str = None,
        port: int = None,
        enable_observability: bool = True,
    ):
        """
        Initialize chat server.

        # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
