        # Purpose: Initialize server with agent and observability
        # SRS: ["SRS-SERVER-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "server"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.agent = agent

        # Handle being called with UIConfig object (backwards compatibility)
        if hasattr(host, 'host') and hasattr(host, 'port'):
            # host is actually a UIConfig object
            ui_config = host
            self.host = getattr(ui_config, 'host', self.DEFAULT_HOST) or self.DEFAULT_HOST
            self.port = getattr(ui_config, 'port', self.DEFAULT_PORT) or self.DEFAULT_PORT
            self._ui_config = ui_config
            self._response_config = port  # port param is actually ResponseLengthConfig
        else:
            self.host = host or self.DEFAULT_HOST
            self.port = port or self.DEFAULT_PORT
            self._ui_config = None
            self._response_config = None

        # Sessions
        self._sessions: Dict[str, ChatSession] = {}
        self._websocket_connections: Dict[str, Any] = {}

        # Observability
        self._enable_observability = enable_observability
        self._observability_hub: Optional[ObservabilityHub] = None
        self._observability_awareness: Optional[ObservabilitySelfAwareness] = None

        if enable_observability:
            agent_id = getattr(agent, 'agent_id', str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]) if agent else str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
            agent_name = getattr(agent, 'agent_name', 'NexusAgent') if agent else 'NexusAgent'
            self._observability_hub = ObservabilityHub(agent_id=agent_id, agent_name=agent_name)
            self._observability_awareness = ObservabilitySelfAwareness(
                agent=agent,
                observability_hub=self._observability_hub,
            )

        # Server state
        self._running = False
        self._start_time: Optional[datetime] = None
        self._request_count = 0
        self._error_count = 0

        # Alias for backwards compatibility
        self._self_awareness = self._observability_awareness

    def _track_request(self, endpoint: str, method: str = "GET") -> FunctionMetric:
        """Track a request for observability."""
        if self._observability_hub:
            return self._observability_hub.track_function_start(f"api_{endpoint}", "chat_server")
        return None

    def _complete_request(
        self,
        metric: Optional[FunctionMetric],
        success: bool = True,
        error: Optional[Exception] = None
    ) -> None:
        """Complete request tracking."""
        self._request_count += 1
        if not success:
            self._error_count += 1
        if metric and self._observability_hub:
            self._observability_hub.track_function_end(metric, success=success, error=error)

    def _generate_ui_html(self) -> str:
        """Generate HTML for the chat UI."""
        agent_name = getattr(self.agent, 'agent_name', 'Nexus Agent') if self.agent else 'Nexus Agent'
        return f'''<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>{agent_name} - Citadel Nexus</title>
    <style>
        * {{ margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }}
        body {{
            font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
            background: #0a0a1a;
            color: #fff;
            height: 100vh;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }}
        .header {{
            background: #1a1a2e;
            border-bottom: 1px solid #2a2a4a;
            padding: 16px 24px;
            display: flex;
            justify-content: space-between;
            align-items: center;
        }}
        .header h1 {{
            font-size: 18px;
            color: #00d9ff;
        }}
        .header .status {{
            font-size: 12px;
            color: #4ade80;
        }}
        .main {{
            flex: 1;
            display: flex;
            overflow: hidden;
        }}
        .chat-area {{
            flex: 1;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }}
        .messages {{
            flex: 1;
            overflow-y: auto;
            padding: 24px;
        }}
        .message {{
            margin-bottom: 16px;
            max-width: 80%;
        }}
        .message.user {{
            margin-left: auto;
        }}
        .message-content {{
            padding: 12px 16px;
            border-radius: 12px;
            line-height: 1.5;
        }}
        .message.user .message-content {{
            background: linear-gradient(135deg, #00d9ff20, #00d9ff10);
            border: 1px solid #00d9ff30;
        }}
        .message.assistant .message-content {{
            background: linear-gradient(135deg, #7B68EE20, #7B68EE10);
            border: 1px solid #7B68EE30;
        }}
        .input-area {{
            background: #1a1a2e;
            border-top: 1px solid #2a2a4a;
            padding: 16px 24px;
        }}
        .input-container {{
            display: flex;
            gap: 12px;
        }}
        textarea {{
            flex: 1;
            background: #0a0a1a;
            border: 1px solid #2a2a4a;
            border-radius: 12px;
            color: #fff;
            padding: 12px 16px;
            font-family: inherit;
            font-size: 14px;
            resize: none;
            min-height: 48px;
        }}
        textarea:focus {{
            outline: none;
            border-color: #00d9ff;
        }}
        button {{
            background: #00d9ff;
            border: none;
            color: #000;
            padding: 12px 24px;
            border-radius: 12px;
            cursor: pointer;
            font-weight: 600;
        }}
        button:hover {{
            background: #00c4e6;
        }}
        button:disabled {{
            opacity: 0.5;
            cursor: not-allowed;
        }}
        .sidebar {{
            width: 300px;
            background: #1a1a2e;
            border-left: 1px solid #2a2a4a;
            padding: 16px;
            overflow-y: auto;
        }}
        .sidebar h3 {{
            font-size: 14px;
            color: #00d9ff;
            margin-bottom: 12px;
        }}
        .zigzag-panel {{
            margin-bottom: 24px;
        }}
        .metric-grid {{
            display: grid;
            grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
            gap: 8px;
        }}
        .metric {{
            background: #0a0a1a;
            border: 1px solid #2a2a4a;
            border-radius: 8px;
            padding: 8px;
        }}
        .metric-label {{
            font-size: 10px;
            color: #888;
        }}
        .metric-value {{
            font-size: 16px;
            font-weight: 600;
            color: #00d9ff;
        }}
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <div class="header">
        <h1>{agent_name}</h1>
        <div class="status" id="status">Connecting...</div>
    </div>
    <div class="main">
        <div class="chat-area">
            <div class="messages" id="messages"></div>
            <div class="input-area">
                <div class="input-container">
                    <textarea id="input" placeholder="Type your message..." rows="1"></textarea>
                    <button id="send" onclick="sendMessage()">Send</button>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
        <div class="sidebar">
            <div class="zigzag-panel">
                <h3>ZigZag Observability</h3>
                <div class="metric-grid">
                    <div class="metric">
                        <div class="metric-label">Spans</div>
                        <div class="metric-value" id="zz-spans">0</div>
                    </div>
                    <div class="metric">
                        <div class="metric-label">Components</div>
                        <div class="metric-value" id="zz-components">0</div>
                    </div>
                    <div class="metric">
                        <div class="metric-label">Latency</div>
                        <div class="metric-value" id="zz-latency">0ms</div>
                    </div>
                    <div class="metric">
                        <div class="metric-label">XP</div>
                        <div class="metric-value" id="zz-xp">0</div>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>
    <script>
        let ws;
        const sessionId = 'session-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9);

        function connect() {{
            ws = new WebSocket(`ws://${{window.location.host}}/ws/${{sessionId}}`);

            ws.onopen = () => {{
                document.getElementById('status').textContent = 'Connected';
                document.getElementById('status').style.color = '#4ade80';
            }};

            ws.onmessage = (event) => {{
                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
                addMessage('assistant', data.response || data.message || JSON.stringify(data));
            }};

            ws.onclose = () => {{
                document.getElementById('status').textContent = 'Disconnected';
                document.getElementById('status').style.color = '#ef4444';
                setTimeout(connect, 3000);
            }};

            ws.onerror = (error) => {{
                console.error('WebSocket error:', error);
            }};
        }}

        function addMessage(role, content) {{
            const messages = document.getElementById('messages');
            const div = document.createElement('div');
            div.className = 'message ' + role;
            div.innerHTML = '<div class="message-content">' + content.replace(/\\n/g, '<br>') + '</div>';
            messages.appendChild(div);
            messages.scrollTop = messages.scrollHeight;
        }}

        function sendMessage() {{
            const input = document.getElementById('input');
            const message = input.value.trim();
            if (!message || !ws || ws.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN) return;

            addMessage('user', message);
            ws.send(message);
            input.value = '';
        }}

        document.getElementById('input').addEventListener('keypress', (e) => {{
            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {{
                e.preventDefault();
                sendMessage();
            }}
        }});

        async function refreshZigZag() {{
            try {{
                const response = await fetch('/zigzag/dashboard-data');
                if (response.ok) {{
                    const data = await response.json();
                    if (data.metrics) {{
                        document.getElementById('zz-spans').textContent = data.metrics.total_spans;
                        document.getElementById('zz-components').textContent = data.metrics.active_components;
                        document.getElementById('zz-latency').textContent = Math.round(data.metrics.avg_latency_ms) + 'ms';
                        document.getElementById('zz-xp').textContent = data.metrics.xp_awarded_total;
                    }}
                }}
            }} catch (e) {{
                console.error('ZigZag refresh failed:', e);
            }}
        }}

        connect();
        setInterval(refreshZigZag, 5000);
        refreshZigZag();
    </script>
</body>
</html>'''

    def create_session(self, user_id: Optional[str] = None) -> ChatSession:
        """Create a new chat session."""
        session = ChatSession(user_id=user_id)
        self._sessions[session.session_id] = session

        # Cleanup old sessions
        self._cleanup_sessions()

        # Track event
        if self._observability_hub:
            self._observability_hub.track_event("session_created", {"session_id": session.session_id})

        return session

    def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[ChatSession]:
        """Get an existing session."""
        return self._sessions.get(session_id)

    def _cleanup_sessions(self) -> None:
        """Remove expired sessions."""
        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=self.SESSION_TIMEOUT_HOURS)
        expired = [
            sid for sid, session in self._sessions.items()
            if datetime.fromisoformat(session.last_activity.replace("Z", "+00:00")) < cutoff
        ]
        for sid in expired:
            del self._sessions[sid]

        # Limit total sessions
        if len(self._sessions) > self.MAX_SESSIONS:
            oldest = sorted(
                self._sessions.items(),
                key=lambda x: x[1].last_activity
            )[:len(self._sessions) - self.MAX_SESSIONS]
            for sid, _ in oldest:
                del self._sessions[sid]

    async def handle_chat(
        self,
        session_id: str,
        user_message: str,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Handle a chat message.

        # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer.handle_chat
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Process chat message and return response
        # SRS: ["SRS-CHAT-001"]
        # Tags: ["chat", "message", "handler"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        metric = self._track_request("chat", "POST")

        try:
            session = self.get_session(session_id)
            if not session:
                session = self.create_session()

            # Add user message
            user_msg = ChatMessage(role="user", content=user_message)
            session.messages.append(user_msg)
            session.last_activity = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()

            # Get agent response
            response_text = ""
            if self.agent:
                response_text = self.agent.interact(user_message)
            else:
                response_text = f"Echo: {user_message}"

            # Add assistant message
            assistant_msg = ChatMessage(role="assistant", content=response_text)
            session.messages.append(assistant_msg)

            # Track interaction
            if self._observability_hub:
                self._observability_hub.track_event("chat_message", {
                    "session_id": session_id,
                    "input_length": len(user_message),
                    "output_length": len(response_text),
                })

            self._complete_request(metric, success=True)

            return {
                "session_id": session.session_id,
                "message_id": assistant_msg.id,
                "response": response_text,
                "timestamp": assistant_msg.timestamp,
            }

        except Exception as e:
            self._complete_request(metric, success=False, error=e)
            return {
                "error": str(e),
                "session_id": session_id,
            }

    async def handle_chat_streaming(
        self,
        session_id: str,
        user_message: str,
    ):
        """
        Handle a chat message with Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming.

        Returns an async generator that yields SSE-formatted events.

        # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer.handle_chat_streaming
        # SRS: ["SRS-STREAM-003"]
        # Purpose: Real-time token streaming via SSE for responsive chat UX
        # END META

        Usage with FastAPI:
            @app.get("/chat/stream/{session_id}")
            async def stream_chat(session_id: str, message: str):
                return StreamingResponse(
                    server.handle_chat_streaming(session_id, message),
                    media_type="text/event-stream"
                )
        """
        metric = self._track_request("chat_stream", "GET")

        try:
            session = self.get_session(session_id)
            if not session:
                session = self.create_session()

            # Add user message
            user_msg = ChatMessage(role="user", content=user_message)
            session.messages.append(user_msg)
            session.last_activity = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()

            # Check if agent has streaming capability
            if not self.agent or not hasattr(self.agent, '_generate_response_streaming'):
                # Fallback to non-streaming
                response_text = self.agent.interact(user_message) if self.agent else f"Echo: {user_message}"
                yield f"data: {json.dumps({'token': response_text})}\n\n"
                yield f"data: {json.dumps({'done': True, 'full_response': response_text})}\n\n"
                return

            # Build context from session history
            context = ""
            if session.messages:
                recent = session.messages[-4:]
                context = "\n".join([f"{m.role}: {m.content[:100]}" for m in recent])

            # Stream tokens
            full_response = ""
            async for token in self.agent._generate_response_streaming(user_message, context):
                full_response += token
                yield f"data: {json.dumps({'token': token})}\n\n"

            # Send completion
            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'done': True, 'full_response': full_response})}\n\n"

            # Add assistant message to session
            assistant_msg = ChatMessage(role="assistant", content=full_response)
            session.messages.append(assistant_msg)

            # Track interaction
            if self._observability_hub:
                self._observability_hub.track_event("chat_stream", {
                    "session_id": session_id,
                    "input_length": len(user_message),
                    "output_length": len(full_response),
                })

            self._complete_request(metric, success=True)

        except Exception as e:
            self._complete_request(metric, success=False, error=e)
            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'error': str(e)})}\n\n"

    def get_health(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get server health status."""
        uptime = 0.0
        if self._start_time:
            uptime = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - self._start_time).total_seconds()

        return {
            "status": "healthy" if self._running else "stopped",
            "uptime_seconds": uptime,
            "request_count": self._request_count,
            "error_count": self._error_count,
            "error_rate": self._error_count / self._request_count if self._request_count > 0 else 0,
            "active_sessions": len(self._sessions),
            "host": self.host,
            "port": self.port,
        }

    def get_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get comprehensive server metrics."""
        metrics = {
            "server": self.get_health(),
            "observability": {},
            "self_awareness": {},
        }

        if self._observability_hub:
            metrics["observability"] = self._observability_hub.get_observability_status()
            metrics["function_stats"] = self._observability_hub.get_function_stats()

        if self._self_awareness:
            self_metrics = self._self_awareness.collect_self_metrics()
            metrics["self_awareness"] = {
                "file_size_lines": self_metrics.file_size_lines,
                "function_count": self_metrics.function_count,
                "memory_mb": self_metrics.memory_usage_mb,
                "cpu_percent": self_metrics.cpu_percent,
                "total_interactions": self_metrics.total_interactions,
                "avg_response_ms": self_metrics.avg_response_time_ms,
            }
            metrics["tunable_parameters"] = self._self_awareness.get_tunable_parameters()

        return metrics

    def get_self_description(self) -> str:
        """Get agent self-description."""
        if self._self_awareness:
            return self._self_awareness.get_self_description()
        return "AgentChatServer (self-awareness not enabled)"

    def get_tuning_recommendations(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get parameter tuning recommendations."""
        if self._self_awareness:
            recommendations = self._self_awareness.analyze_and_recommend_tuning()
            return [
                {
                    "parameter": r.parameter_name,
                    "current": r.current_value,
                    "recommended": r.recommended_value,
                    "reason": r.reason,
                    "confidence": r.confidence,
                }
                for r in recommendations
            ]
        return []

    def apply_tuning(self, parameter: str, value: Any) -> bool:
        """Apply a parameter tuning."""
        if self._self_awareness:
            tuning = ParameterTuning(
                parameter_name=parameter,
                current_value=self._self_awareness._tunable_parameters.get(parameter, {}).get("current"),
                recommended_value=value,
                reason="manual_apply",
                source="api",
            )
            return self._self_awareness.apply_tuning(tuning)
        return False

    def create_fastapi_app(self):
        """
        Create a FastAPI application for the server.

        # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer.create_fastapi_app
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Build FastAPI app with all endpoints
        # SRS: ["SRS-SERVER-001", "SRS-CHAT-001"]
        # Tags: ["fastapi", "api", "routes"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        try:
            from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect
            from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
            from pydantic import BaseModel
        except ImportError:
            return None

        app = FastAPI(
            title="Citadel Nexus Agent Chat Server",
            description="REST and WebSocket API for Nexus Agent interactions",
            version="1.0.0",
        )

        # CORS
        app.add_middleware(
            CORSMiddleware,
            allow_origins=["*"],
            allow_credentials=True,
            allow_methods=["*"],
            allow_headers=["*"],
        )

        # Import Response for HTML
        from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse

        # Root route - serve UI
        @app.get("/", response_class=HTMLResponse)
        async def root():
            return self._generate_ui_html()

        # Routes (using module-level ChatServerChatRequest and ChatServerTuningRequest)
        @app.get("/health")
        async def health():
            return self.get_health()

        @app.get("/metrics")
        async def metrics():
            return self.get_metrics()

        @app.get("/self-description")
        async def self_description():
            return {"description": self.get_self_description()}

        @app.post("/chat")
        async def chat(request: ChatServerChatRequest):
            session_id = request.session_id
            if not session_id:
                session = self.create_session(request.user_id)
                session_id = session.session_id
            return await self.handle_chat(session_id, request.message)

        @app.post("/session")
        async def create_session(user_id: Optional[str] = None):
            session = self.create_session(user_id)
            return {"session_id": session.session_id}

        @app.get("/session/{session_id}")
        async def get_session(session_id: str):
            session = self.get_session(session_id)
            if not session:
                raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Session not found")
            return {
                "session_id": session.session_id,
                "message_count": len(session.messages),
                "created_at": session.created_at,
                "last_activity": session.last_activity,
            }

        @app.get("/tuning/recommendations")
        async def tuning_recommendations():
            return {"recommendations": self.get_tuning_recommendations()}

        @app.post("/tuning/apply")
        async def apply_tuning(request: ChatServerTuningRequest):
            success = self.apply_tuning(request.parameter, request.value)
            return {"success": success, "parameter": request.parameter, "value": request.value}

        @app.get("/observability/functions")
        async def function_stats():
            if self._observability_hub:
                return self._observability_hub.get_function_stats()
            return {}

        # WebSocket
        @app.websocket("/ws/{session_id}")
        async def websocket_endpoint(websocket: WebSocket, session_id: str):
            await websocket.accept()
            self._websocket_connections[session_id] = websocket

            try:
                while True:
                    data = await websocket.receive_text()
                    response = await self.handle_chat(session_id, data)
                    await websocket.send_json(response)
            except WebSocketDisconnect:
                if session_id in self._websocket_connections:
                    del self._websocket_connections[session_id]

        # ZigZag Observability Routes
        @app.get("/zigzag/dashboard-data")
        async def zigzag_dashboard_data():
            """Get combined ZigZag dashboard data."""
            try:
                if ZIGZAG_AVAILABLE and get_zigzag_tracer:
                    tracer = get_zigzag_tracer()
                    return {
                        "metrics": {
                            "total_traces": tracer.metrics.total_traces,
                            "total_spans": tracer.metrics.total_spans,
                            "active_components": tracer.metrics.active_components,
                            "avg_latency_ms": tracer.metrics.avg_latency_ms,
                            "error_rate": tracer.metrics.error_rate,
                            "xp_awarded_total": tracer.metrics.xp_awarded_total,
                            "tp_awarded_total": tracer.metrics.tp_awarded_total,
                            "professor_queries": tracer.metrics.professor_queries,
                            "uptime_seconds": tracer.metrics.uptime_seconds,
                        },
                        "topology": tracer.get_topology(),
                        "professors": tracer.get_professor_activity(),
                        "economy": tracer.get_economy_flow(),
                    }
            except Exception as e:
                return {"error": str(e), "metrics": {"total_spans": 0, "active_components": 0, "avg_latency_ms": 0, "xp_awarded_total": 0}}
            return {"metrics": {"total_spans": 0, "active_components": 0, "avg_latency_ms": 0, "xp_awarded_total": 0}}

        return app

    def launch(self, background: bool = False) -> None:
        """
        Launch the chat server.

        # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer.launch
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Start the HTTP/WebSocket server
        # SRS: ["SRS-SERVER-001"]
        # Tags: ["launch", "server", "start"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self._running = True
        self._start_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        app = self.create_fastapi_app()
        if not app:
            print("[ChatServer] FastAPI not available. Install with: pip install fastapi uvicorn")
            return

        # Track launch event
        if self._observability_hub:
            self._observability_hub.track_event("server_launched", {
                "host": self.host,
                "port": self.port,
            })

        print(f"\n{'='*70}")
        print("CITADEL NEXUS AGENT CHAT SERVER")
        print(f"{'='*70}")
        print(f"  Host: {self.host}")
        print(f"  Port: {self.port}")
        print(f"  Observability: {'Enabled' if self._enable_observability else 'Disabled'}")
        if self._self_awareness:
            metrics = self._self_awareness.collect_self_metrics()
            print(f"  Agent Size: {metrics.file_size_lines:,} lines")
            print(f"  Functions: {metrics.function_count}")
            print(f"  Classes: {metrics.class_count}")
        print(f"{'='*70}")
        print(f"  API Docs: http://{self.host}:{self.port}/docs")
        print(f"  Health:   http://{self.host}:{self.port}/health")
        print(f"  Metrics:  http://{self.host}:{self.port}/metrics")
        print(f"  WebSocket: ws://{self.host}:{self.port}/ws/{{session_id}}")
        print(f"{'='*70}\n")

        try:
            import uvicorn
            uvicorn.run(app, host=self.host, port=self.port)
        except ImportError:
            print("[ChatServer] uvicorn not available. Install with: pip install uvicorn")

    def stop(self) -> None:
        """Stop the chat server."""
        self._running = False
        if self._observability_hub:
            self._observability_hub.track_event("server_stopped")

    def launch_ui(self) -> None:
        """
        Launch the UI (alias for launch for backward compatibility).

        # BEGIN META: AgentChatServer.launch_ui
        # Purpose: Backward-compatible alias for launch()
        # END META
        """
        self.launch()


def launch_agent_server(
    agent: Optional[Any] = None,
    host: str = "0.0.0.0",
    port: int = 8765,
    enable_observability: bool = True,
) -> AgentChatServer:
    """
    Convenience function to launch the agent chat server.

    Usage:
        from citadel_nexus_pro import launch_agent_server, NexusTamagotchiAgentPro

        agent = NexusTamagotchiAgentPro()
        server = launch_agent_server(agent, port=8765)

    # BEGIN META: launch_agent_server
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Quick server launch function
    # SRS: ["SRS-SERVER-001"]
    # Tags: ["launch", "convenience"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    server = AgentChatServer(
        agent=agent,
        host=host,
        port=port,
        enable_observability=enable_observability,
    )
    server.launch()
    return server


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# END SECTION 106: AGENT CHAT SERVER
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 107: FUNCTION/ABILITY SELF-SCANNER (Operational Self-Awareness)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
"""
Self-scanning system for operational self-awareness.

The agent can introspect its own source code to discover:
- All functions and their purposes
- All classes and their capabilities
- All SRS requirements it satisfies
- All integrations it supports
- All abilities and skills it possesses

This enables the agent to:
- Answer questions about its own capabilities
- Understand its limitations
- Document itself for external systems
- Adapt behavior based on available functions
"""


@dataclass
class ScannedFunction:
    """
    A discovered function with metadata.

    # BEGIN META: ScannedFunction
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Represent a scanned function with its metadata
    # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
    # Tags: ["introspection", "metadata"]
    # END META
    """

    name: str
    module: str = ""
    docstring: str = ""
    parameters: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    return_type: str = "Any"
    line_number: int = 0
    is_async: bool = False
    is_method: bool = False
    parent_class: str = ""
    decorators: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)

    # Metadata from BEGIN META block
    srs_refs: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    purpose: str = ""
    caps_grade: str = ""
    categories: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "name": self.name,
            "module": self.module,
            "docstring": self.docstring[:200] if self.docstring else "",
            "parameters": self.parameters,
            "return_type": self.return_type,
            "line_number": self.line_number,
            "is_async": self.is_async,
            "is_method": self.is_method,
            "parent_class": self.parent_class,
            "decorators": self.decorators,
            "srs_refs": self.srs_refs,
            "tags": self.tags,
            "purpose": self.purpose,
            "caps_grade": self.caps_grade,
            "categories": self.categories,
        }


@dataclass
class ScannedClass:
    """
    A discovered class with its methods and metadata.

    # BEGIN META: ScannedClass
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Represent a scanned class with methods
    # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
    # Tags: ["introspection", "metadata"]
    # END META
    """

    name: str
    module: str = ""
    docstring: str = ""
    line_number: int = 0
    base_classes: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    methods: List[ScannedFunction] = field(default_factory=list)
    class_variables: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    decorators: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)

    # Metadata from BEGIN META block
    srs_refs: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    purpose: str = ""
    caps_grade: str = ""

    @property
    def method_count(self) -> int:
        return len(self.methods)

    @property
    def public_methods(self) -> List[ScannedFunction]:
        return [m for m in self.methods if not m.name.startswith("_")]

    @property
    def private_methods(self) -> List[ScannedFunction]:
        return [m for m in self.methods if m.name.startswith("_") and not m.name.startswith("__")]

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "name": self.name,
            "module": self.module,
            "docstring": self.docstring[:200] if self.docstring else "",
            "line_number": self.line_number,
            "base_classes": self.base_classes,
            "method_count": self.method_count,
            "public_method_count": len(self.public_methods),
            "class_variables": self.class_variables,
            "decorators": self.decorators,
            "srs_refs": self.srs_refs,
            "tags": self.tags,
            "purpose": self.purpose,
            "caps_grade": self.caps_grade,
        }


@dataclass
class ScannedAbility:
    """
    An agent ability/capability discovered from scanning.

    # BEGIN META: ScannedAbility
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Represent a discovered agent ability
    # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
    # Tags: ["introspection", "capabilities"]
    # END META
    """

    name: str
    category: str  # "core", "integration", "gamification", "governance", "memory", etc.
    description: str = ""
    implementing_functions: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    implementing_classes: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    srs_refs: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    enabled: bool = True
    requires_integration: Optional[str] = None  # e.g., "datadog", "posthog"

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "name": self.name,
            "category": self.category,
            "description": self.description,
            "implementing_functions": self.implementing_functions,
            "implementing_classes": self.implementing_classes,
            "srs_refs": self.srs_refs,
            "enabled": self.enabled,
            "requires_integration": self.requires_integration,
        }


@dataclass
class SelfScanResult:
    """
    Complete result of a self-scan operation.

    # BEGIN META: SelfScanResult
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Complete self-scan results
    # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
    # Tags: ["introspection", "results"]
    # END META
    """

    scan_timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    source_file: str = ""
    total_lines: int = 0
    total_functions: int = 0
    total_classes: int = 0
    total_abilities: int = 0
    total_sections: int = 0
    total_integrations: int = 0

    functions: List[ScannedFunction] = field(default_factory=list)
    classes: List[ScannedClass] = field(default_factory=list)
    abilities: List[ScannedAbility] = field(default_factory=list)

    # Aggregated metadata
    all_srs_refs: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    all_tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    sections: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    integrations: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)

    def __post_init__(self):
        """Compute totals from lists."""
        if self.sections and not self.total_sections:
            self.total_sections = len(self.sections)
        if self.integrations and not self.total_integrations:
            self.total_integrations = len(self.integrations)

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "scan_timestamp": self.scan_timestamp,
            "source_file": self.source_file,
            "total_lines": self.total_lines,
            "total_functions": self.total_functions,
            "total_classes": self.total_classes,
            "total_abilities": self.total_abilities,
            "all_srs_refs": self.all_srs_refs,
            "all_tags": self.all_tags,
            "sections": self.sections,
            "integrations": self.integrations,
        }

    def get_summary(self) -> str:
        """Generate human-readable summary."""
        return f"""
=== Agent Self-Scan Summary ===
Scanned: {self.source_file}
Timestamp: {self.scan_timestamp}

Code Statistics:
  - Total Lines: {self.total_lines:,}
  - Total Functions: {self.total_functions}
  - Total Classes: {self.total_classes}
  - Total Abilities: {self.total_abilities}
  - Total Sections: {len(self.sections)}

SRS Coverage:
  - {len(self.all_srs_refs)} unique requirements referenced

Categories:
  - {len(self.all_tags)} unique tags

Integrations Supported:
  - {', '.join(self.integrations) if self.integrations else 'None detected'}
"""


class FunctionAbilityScanner:
    """
    Self-scanner for operational self-awareness.

    Scans the agent's own source code to discover:
    - All function definitions
    - All class definitions
    - All abilities and capabilities
    - All SRS requirements
    - All integrations

    # BEGIN META: FunctionAbilityScanner
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Scan agent source for operational self-awareness
    # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002", "SRS-INTROSPECTION-001"]
    # Tags: ["core", "introspection", "self-awareness"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    # Known ability categories and their keywords
    ABILITY_CATEGORIES = {
        "core": ["interact", "process", "handle", "execute", "run"],
        "memory": ["remember", "recall", "store", "retrieve", "memory", "stm", "ltm"],
        "gamification": ["xp", "tp", "badge", "rank", "quest", "mission", "reward"],
        "governance": ["council", "policy", "verdict", "govern", "authority", "permission"],
        "integration": ["sync", "broadcast", "notify", "webhook", "api", "connect"],
        "analytics": ["track", "metric", "analytics", "posthog", "datadog", "observe"],
        "learning": ["learn", "train", "adapt", "improve", "feedback"],
        "communication": ["chat", "message", "respond", "reply", "conversation"],
        "security": ["auth", "validate", "verify", "secure", "encrypt", "token"],
        "persistence": ["save", "load", "persist", "restore", "serialize"],
    }

    # Integration detection patterns
    INTEGRATION_PATTERNS = {
        "datadog": ["datadog", "statsd", "dogstatsd", "dd_"],
        "posthog": ["posthog", "ph_"],
        "slack": ["slack", "slack_"],
        "discord": ["discord", "discord_"],
        "notion": ["notion", "notion_"],
        "linear": ["linear", "linear_"],
        "github": ["github", "gh_"],
        "gitlab": ["gitlab", "gl_"],
        "stripe": ["stripe", "stripe_"],
        "supabase": ["supabase", "sb_"],
        "openai": ["openai", "gpt", "chatgpt"],
        "anthropic": ["anthropic", "claude"],
        "intercom": ["intercom"],
        "aws": ["aws", "s3", "lambda", "dynamodb"],
    }

    def __init__(self, source_file: Optional[str] = None):
        """
        Initialize the scanner.

        Args:
            source_file: Path to source file. Defaults to this file.
        """
        if source_file:
            self._source_file = Path(source_file)
        else:
            # Default to this file
            self._source_file = Path(__file__)

        self._scan_result: Optional[SelfScanResult] = None
        self._last_scan_time: Optional[datetime] = None
        self._logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

    def scan(self, force: bool = False) -> SelfScanResult:
        """
        Perform a full self-scan of the source code.

        Args:
            force: Force rescan even if cached result exists

        Returns:
            SelfScanResult with all discovered metadata

        # BEGIN META: scan
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Main scan entry point
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["scan", "main"]
        # END META
        """
        # Check cache
        if not force and self._scan_result and self._last_scan_time:
            cache_age = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - self._last_scan_time
            if cache_age.total_seconds() < 300:  # 5-minute cache
                return self._scan_result

        # Read source file
        try:
            source_content = self._source_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        except Exception as e:
            self._logger.error(f"Failed to read source file: {e}")
            return SelfScanResult(source_file=str(self._source_file))

        # Parse source
        lines = source_content.split("\n")

        result = SelfScanResult(
            source_file=str(self._source_file),
            total_lines=len(lines),
        )

        # Scan for functions
        result.functions = self._scan_functions(source_content, lines)
        result.total_functions = len(result.functions)

        # Scan for classes
        result.classes = self._scan_classes(source_content, lines)
        result.total_classes = len(result.classes)

        # Scan for sections
        result.sections = self._scan_sections(lines)

        # Discover abilities from functions/classes
        result.abilities = self._discover_abilities(result.functions, result.classes)
        result.total_abilities = len(result.abilities)

        # Aggregate SRS refs
        all_srs = set()
        for func in result.functions:
            all_srs.update(func.srs_refs)
        for cls in result.classes:
            all_srs.update(cls.srs_refs)
        result.all_srs_refs = sorted(list(all_srs))

        # Aggregate tags
        all_tags = set()
        for func in result.functions:
            all_tags.update(func.tags)
        for cls in result.classes:
            all_tags.update(cls.tags)
        result.all_tags = sorted(list(all_tags))

        # Detect integrations
        result.integrations = self._detect_integrations(source_content)

        # Cache result
        self._scan_result = result
        self._last_scan_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        self._logger.info(f"Self-scan complete: {result.total_functions} functions, {result.total_classes} classes, {result.total_abilities} abilities")

        return result

    def _scan_functions(self, source: str, lines: List[str]) -> List[ScannedFunction]:
        """
        Scan source for function definitions.

        # BEGIN META: _scan_functions
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Extract all function definitions
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["scan", "functions"]
        # END META
        """
        functions = []

        # Regex patterns
        func_pattern = re.compile(
            r'^(\s*)(async\s+)?def\s+(\w+)\s*\(([^)]*)\)\s*(?:->\s*([^:]+))?\s*:',
            re.MULTILINE
        )

        # Find all function definitions
        for match in func_pattern.finditer(source):
            indent = match.group(1)
            is_async = bool(match.group(2))
            func_name = match.group(3)
            params_str = match.group(4)
            return_type = match.group(5).strip() if match.group(5) else "Any"

            # Calculate line number
            line_num = source[:match.start()].count("\n") + 1

            # Parse parameters
            params = self._parse_parameters(params_str)

            # Determine if method (has indent)
            is_method = len(indent) > 0

            # Find docstring (next line after def)
            docstring = self._extract_docstring(lines, line_num)

            # Find metadata from BEGIN META block
            meta = self._extract_meta_block(lines, line_num)

            # Find decorators
            decorators = self._find_decorators(lines, line_num - 1)

            func = ScannedFunction(
                name=func_name,
                module=self._source_file.stem,
                docstring=docstring,
                parameters=params,
                return_type=return_type,
                line_number=line_num,
                is_async=is_async,
                is_method=is_method,
                decorators=decorators,
                srs_refs=meta.get("srs", []),
                tags=meta.get("tags", []),
                purpose=meta.get("purpose", ""),
                caps_grade=meta.get("caps", ""),
                categories=meta.get("categories", []),
            )

            functions.append(func)

        return functions

    def _scan_classes(self, source: str, lines: List[str]) -> List[ScannedClass]:
        """
        Scan source for class definitions.

        # BEGIN META: _scan_classes
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Extract all class definitions
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["scan", "classes"]
        # END META
        """
        classes = []

        # Regex for class definitions
        class_pattern = re.compile(
            r'^class\s+(\w+)\s*(?:\(([^)]*)\))?\s*:',
            re.MULTILINE
        )

        for match in class_pattern.finditer(source):
            class_name = match.group(1)
            bases_str = match.group(2) or ""

            # Calculate line number
            line_num = source[:match.start()].count("\n") + 1

            # Parse base classes
            base_classes = [b.strip() for b in bases_str.split(",") if b.strip()]

            # Find docstring
            docstring = self._extract_docstring(lines, line_num)

            # Find metadata
            meta = self._extract_meta_block(lines, line_num)

            # Find decorators
            decorators = self._find_decorators(lines, line_num - 1)

            # Find class variables (simple heuristic)
            class_vars = self._find_class_variables(lines, line_num)

            cls = ScannedClass(
                name=class_name,
                module=self._source_file.stem,
                docstring=docstring,
                line_number=line_num,
                base_classes=base_classes,
                decorators=decorators,
                class_variables=class_vars,
                srs_refs=meta.get("srs", []),
                tags=meta.get("tags", []),
                purpose=meta.get("purpose", ""),
                caps_grade=meta.get("caps", ""),
            )

            classes.append(cls)

        # Associate methods with classes
        all_funcs = self._scan_functions(source, lines)
        for cls in classes:
            cls.methods = [
                f for f in all_funcs
                if f.is_method and f.line_number > cls.line_number
                and (not classes or all(f.line_number < c.line_number or c == cls for c in classes if c.line_number > cls.line_number))
            ]

        return classes

    def _scan_sections(self, lines: List[str]) -> List[str]:
        """
        Scan for section headers.

        # BEGIN META: _scan_sections
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Extract section headers
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["scan", "sections"]
        # END META
        """
        sections = []
        # Match SECTION with decimal numbers (e.g., 1.5, 2B, etc.) and letters
        section_pattern = re.compile(r'^#\s*SECTION\s+([\d.]+[A-Z]?):\s*(.+)', re.IGNORECASE)

        for line in lines:
            match = section_pattern.match(line)
            if match:
                section_num = match.group(1)
                section_name = match.group(2).strip()
                sections.append(f"Section {section_num}: {section_name}")

        # Also detect major system headers via comment patterns
        system_patterns = [
            (r'^#.*BOOK\s*MAKER.*SYSTEM', "Book Maker System"),
            (r'^#.*COLLEGE\s*(?:NETWORK|SYSTEM)', "College Network"),
            (r'^#.*COUNCIL\s*(?:SYSTEM|GOVERNANCE)', "Council System"),
            (r'^#.*SANCTUM\s*(?:SYSTEM|MEMORY)', "Sanctum System"),
            (r'^#.*BROTHERHOOD\s*(?:SYSTEM|ENGINE)', "Brotherhood Engine"),
            (r'^#.*ZIGZAG\s*(?:FLOW|VISUALIZ)', "ZigZag Visualization"),
            (r'^#.*FOUNDRY\s*(?:SYSTEM|SCAFFOLD)', "Agent Foundry"),
            (r'^#.*AUTO\s*INSTALL', "Auto Installation System"),
        ]

        for line in lines:
            for pattern, label in system_patterns:
                if re.match(pattern, line, re.IGNORECASE):
                    if label not in sections:
                        sections.append(f"System: {label}")
                    break

        return sections

    def _discover_abilities(
        self,
        functions: List[ScannedFunction],
        classes: List[ScannedClass]
    ) -> List[ScannedAbility]:
        """
        Discover agent abilities from scanned code.

        # BEGIN META: _discover_abilities
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Infer abilities from function/class patterns
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["discovery", "abilities"]
        # END META
        """
        abilities = []
        ability_map: Dict[str, ScannedAbility] = {}

        # Analyze functions for abilities
        for func in functions:
            func_lower = func.name.lower()

            for category, keywords in self.ABILITY_CATEGORIES.items():
                for keyword in keywords:
                    if keyword in func_lower:
                        ability_name = f"{category}_{keyword}"

                        if ability_name not in ability_map:
                            ability_map[ability_name] = ScannedAbility(
                                name=ability_name,
                                category=category,
                                description=func.purpose or func.docstring[:100] if func.docstring else "",
                            )

                        ability_map[ability_name].implementing_functions.append(func.name)
                        ability_map[ability_name].srs_refs.extend(func.srs_refs)
                        break

        # Analyze classes for abilities
        for cls in classes:
            cls_lower = cls.name.lower()

            for category, keywords in self.ABILITY_CATEGORIES.items():
                for keyword in keywords:
                    if keyword in cls_lower:
                        ability_name = f"{category}_{keyword}"

                        if ability_name not in ability_map:
                            ability_map[ability_name] = ScannedAbility(
                                name=ability_name,
                                category=category,
                                description=cls.purpose or cls.docstring[:100] if cls.docstring else "",
                            )

                        ability_map[ability_name].implementing_classes.append(cls.name)
                        ability_map[ability_name].srs_refs.extend(cls.srs_refs)
                        break

        # Deduplicate SRS refs
        for ability in ability_map.values():
            ability.srs_refs = list(set(ability.srs_refs))
            abilities.append(ability)

        return abilities

    def _detect_integrations(self, source: str) -> List[str]:
        """
        Detect which integrations are present in the source.

        # BEGIN META: _detect_integrations
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Detect integration patterns
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["detection", "integrations"]
        # END META
        """
        source_lower = source.lower()
        detected = []

        for integration, patterns in self.INTEGRATION_PATTERNS.items():
            for pattern in patterns:
                if pattern in source_lower:
                    detected.append(integration)
                    break

        return sorted(detected)

    def _parse_parameters(self, params_str: str) -> List[str]:
        """Parse function parameters from string."""
        if not params_str.strip():
            return []

        params = []
        depth = 0
        current = ""

        for char in params_str:
            if char in "([{":
                depth += 1
                current += char
            elif char in ")]}":
                depth -= 1
                current += char
            elif char == "," and depth == 0:
                if current.strip():
                    # Extract just the parameter name
                    param = current.strip().split(":")[0].split("=")[0].strip()
                    if param:
                        params.append(param)
                current = ""
            else:
                current += char

        if current.strip():
            param = current.strip().split(":")[0].split("=")[0].strip()
            if param:
                params.append(param)

        return params

    def _extract_docstring(self, lines: List[str], start_line: int) -> str:
        """Extract docstring following a definition."""
        if start_line >= len(lines):
            return ""

        # Look for docstring on next non-empty line
        for i in range(start_line, min(start_line + 5, len(lines))):
            line = lines[i].strip()
            if line.startswith('"""') or line.startswith("'''"):
                quote = line[:3]
                if line.endswith(quote) and len(line) > 6:
                    return line[3:-3].strip()
                # Multi-line docstring
                docstring_lines = [line[3:]]
                for j in range(i + 1, min(i + 50, len(lines))):
                    end_line = lines[j]
                    if quote in end_line:
                        docstring_lines.append(end_line.split(quote)[0])
                        return "\n".join(docstring_lines).strip()
                    docstring_lines.append(end_line.strip())
                break

        return ""

    def _extract_meta_block(self, lines: List[str], start_line: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Extract BEGIN META block data."""
        meta = {
            "srs": [],
            "tags": [],
            "purpose": "",
            "caps": "",
            "categories": [],
        }

        # Search in docstring or nearby lines
        search_range = range(max(0, start_line - 5), min(len(lines), start_line + 50))

        in_meta = False
        for i in search_range:
            line = lines[i].strip()

            if "BEGIN META:" in line:
                in_meta = True
                continue
            if "END META" in line:
                break
            if not in_meta:
                continue

            # Parse meta fields
            if line.startswith("# SRS:"):
                try:
                    srs_match = re.search(r'\[([^\]]+)\]', line)
                    if srs_match:
                        meta["srs"] = [s.strip().strip('"\'') for s in srs_match.group(1).split(",")]
                except:
                    pass
            elif line.startswith("# Tags:"):
                try:
                    tags_match = re.search(r'\[([^\]]+)\]', line)
                    if tags_match:
                        meta["tags"] = [t.strip().strip('"\'') for t in tags_match.group(1).split(",")]
                except:
                    pass
            elif line.startswith("# Purpose:"):
                meta["purpose"] = line.replace("# Purpose:", "").strip()
            elif line.startswith("# CAPS:"):
                meta["caps"] = line.replace("# CAPS:", "").strip()
            elif line.startswith("# Categories:"):
                try:
                    cat_match = re.search(r'\[([^\]]+)\]', line)
                    if cat_match:
                        meta["categories"] = [c.strip().strip('"\'') for c in cat_match.group(1).split(",")]
                except:
                    pass

        return meta

    def _find_decorators(self, lines: List[str], def_line: int) -> List[str]:
        """Find decorators above a definition."""
        decorators = []

        for i in range(def_line - 1, max(0, def_line - 10), -1):
            line = lines[i].strip()
            if line.startswith("@"):
                # Extract decorator name
                dec = line[1:].split("(")[0]
                decorators.insert(0, dec)
            elif line and not line.startswith("#"):
                break

        return decorators

    def _find_class_variables(self, lines: List[str], class_line: int) -> List[str]:
        """Find class-level variable definitions."""
        variables = []

        # Look at lines following class definition
        for i in range(class_line, min(class_line + 50, len(lines))):
            line = lines[i].strip()

            # Stop at method definition
            if line.startswith("def ") or line.startswith("async def "):
                break

            # Look for UPPER_CASE = or type: annotations
            if re.match(r'^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*\s*[:=]', line):
                var_name = line.split(":")[0].split("=")[0].strip()
                variables.append(var_name)

        return variables

    def get_function_by_name(self, name: str) -> Optional[ScannedFunction]:
        """Get a specific function by name."""
        if not self._scan_result:
            self.scan()

        for func in self._scan_result.functions:
            if func.name == name:
                return func
        return None

    def get_class_by_name(self, name: str) -> Optional[ScannedClass]:
        """Get a specific class by name."""
        if not self._scan_result:
            self.scan()

        for cls in self._scan_result.classes:
            if cls.name == name:
                return cls
        return None

    def get_abilities_by_category(self, category: str) -> List[ScannedAbility]:
        """Get all abilities in a category."""
        if not self._scan_result:
            self.scan()

        return [a for a in self._scan_result.abilities if a.category == category]

    def get_functions_by_srs(self, srs_code: str) -> List[ScannedFunction]:
        """Get all functions that implement an SRS requirement."""
        if not self._scan_result:
            self.scan()

        return [f for f in self._scan_result.functions if srs_code in f.srs_refs]

    def export_to_json(self, output_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> str:
        """
        Export scan results to JSON.

        # BEGIN META: export_to_json
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Export scan results for external use
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["export", "json"]
        # END META
        """
        if not self._scan_result:
            self.scan()

        export_data = {
            "meta": self._scan_result.to_dict(),
            "functions": [f.to_dict() for f in self._scan_result.functions],
            "classes": [c.to_dict() for c in self._scan_result.classes],
            "abilities": [a.to_dict() for a in self._scan_result.abilities],
        }

        json_str = json.dumps(export_data, indent=2, default=str)

        if output_path:
            output_path.write_text(json_str)
            self._logger.info(f"Exported scan results to {output_path}")

        return json_str

    def generate_capability_report(self) -> str:
        """
        Generate a human-readable capability report.

        # BEGIN META: generate_capability_report
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Generate readable capability documentation
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["report", "documentation"]
        # END META
        """
        if not self._scan_result:
            self.scan()

        result = self._scan_result
        report_lines = [
            "=" * 80,
            "CITADEL NEXUS AGENT - CAPABILITY REPORT",
            "=" * 80,
            "",
            f"Generated: {datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}",
            f"Source: {result.source_file}",
            "",
            "OVERVIEW",
            "-" * 40,
            f"Total Lines of Code: {result.total_lines:,}",
            f"Total Functions: {result.total_functions}",
            f"Total Classes: {result.total_classes}",
            f"Total Abilities: {result.total_abilities}",
            f"Total Sections: {len(result.sections)}",
            "",
            "SECTIONS",
            "-" * 40,
        ]

        for section in result.sections:
            report_lines.append(f"  • {section}")

        report_lines.extend([
            "",
            "ABILITIES BY CATEGORY",
            "-" * 40,
        ])

        abilities_by_cat: Dict[str, List[ScannedAbility]] = {}
        for ability in result.abilities:
            if ability.category not in abilities_by_cat:
                abilities_by_cat[ability.category] = []
            abilities_by_cat[ability.category].append(ability)

        for category, abilities in sorted(abilities_by_cat.items()):
            report_lines.append(f"\n  [{category.upper()}]")
            for ability in abilities:
                report_lines.append(f"    • {ability.name}")
                if ability.implementing_functions:
                    report_lines.append(f"      Functions: {', '.join(ability.implementing_functions[:5])}")
                if ability.implementing_classes:
                    report_lines.append(f"      Classes: {', '.join(ability.implementing_classes[:5])}")

        report_lines.extend([
            "",
            "INTEGRATIONS DETECTED",
            "-" * 40,
        ])

        for integration in result.integrations:
            report_lines.append(f"  ✓ {integration}")

        report_lines.extend([
            "",
            "SRS REQUIREMENTS COVERED",
            "-" * 40,
            f"  Total: {len(result.all_srs_refs)} requirements",
        ])

        for srs in sorted(result.all_srs_refs)[:20]:
            report_lines.append(f"  • {srs}")
        if len(result.all_srs_refs) > 20:
            report_lines.append(f"  ... and {len(result.all_srs_refs) - 20} more")

        report_lines.extend([
            "",
            "=" * 80,
            "END OF CAPABILITY REPORT",
            "=" * 80,
        ])

        return "\n".join(report_lines)


class AgentOperationalAwareness:
    """
    Aggregated operational self-awareness system.

    Combines:
    - FunctionAbilityScanner for code introspection
    - Self-metrics collection
    - Integration health checks
    - Parameter tuning recommendations

    # BEGIN META: AgentOperationalAwareness
    # Date: 2026-01-14
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Unified operational self-awareness
    # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-001", "SRS-AWARENESS-002", "SRS-INTROSPECTION-001"]
    # Tags: ["core", "self-awareness", "operational"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    def __init__(self, agent: Optional[Any] = None, source_file: Optional[str] = None):
        """
        Initialize operational awareness.

        Args:
            agent: Optional agent instance for runtime metrics
            source_file: Path to source file for scanning
        """
        self._agent = agent
        self._scanner = FunctionAbilityScanner(source_file)
        self._last_full_scan: Optional[datetime] = None
        self._operational_log: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    def get_full_awareness_report(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Generate comprehensive self-awareness report.

        # BEGIN META: get_full_awareness_report
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Complete operational awareness report
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-001", "SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["report", "comprehensive"]
        # END META
        """
        scan_result = self._scanner.scan()

        report = {
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            "identity": {
                "module": "citadel_nexus_pro",
                "version": "4.0",
                "type": "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro",
            },
            "code_metrics": {
                "total_lines": scan_result.total_lines,
                "total_functions": scan_result.total_functions,
                "total_classes": scan_result.total_classes,
                "total_abilities": scan_result.total_abilities,
                "sections": len(scan_result.sections),
            },
            "capabilities": {
                "categories": list(set(a.category for a in scan_result.abilities)),
                "ability_count_by_category": {},
                "top_abilities": [a.name for a in scan_result.abilities[:10]],
            },
            "integrations": {
                "detected": scan_result.integrations,
                "count": len(scan_result.integrations),
            },
            "compliance": {
                "srs_refs_count": len(scan_result.all_srs_refs),
                "srs_refs": scan_result.all_srs_refs[:20],
            },
            "sections": scan_result.sections,
        }

        # Count abilities by category
        for ability in scan_result.abilities:
            cat = ability.category
            if cat not in report["capabilities"]["ability_count_by_category"]:
                report["capabilities"]["ability_count_by_category"][cat] = 0
            report["capabilities"]["ability_count_by_category"][cat] += 1

        self._last_full_scan = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self._log_operation("full_awareness_report", {"success": True})

        return report

    def can_i_do(self, task_description: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Answer "Can I do X?" questions about capabilities.

        Args:
            task_description: Natural language description of the task

        Returns:
            Assessment of capability to perform the task

        # BEGIN META: can_i_do
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Self-assess capability for a task
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["query", "capability"]
        # END META
        """
        scan_result = self._scanner.scan()
        task_lower = task_description.lower()

        # Check for matching abilities
        matching_abilities = []
        for ability in scan_result.abilities:
            ability_keywords = ability.name.replace("_", " ").lower()
            if any(kw in task_lower for kw in ability_keywords.split()):
                matching_abilities.append(ability)

        # Check for matching functions
        matching_functions = []
        for func in scan_result.functions:
            if any(word in func.name.lower() for word in task_lower.split()):
                matching_functions.append(func)

        # Check for required integrations
        required_integrations = []
        for integration, patterns in self._scanner.INTEGRATION_PATTERNS.items():
            if any(p in task_lower for p in patterns):
                required_integrations.append(integration)

        # Assess capability
        can_do = len(matching_abilities) > 0 or len(matching_functions) > 0
        confidence = min(1.0, (len(matching_abilities) + len(matching_functions)) / 5.0)

        # Check if required integrations are available
        missing_integrations = [i for i in required_integrations if i not in scan_result.integrations]
        if missing_integrations:
            can_do = False
            confidence *= 0.5

        return {
            "task": task_description,
            "can_do": can_do,
            "confidence": round(confidence, 2),
            "matching_abilities": [a.name for a in matching_abilities],
            "matching_functions": [f.name for f in matching_functions[:5]],
            "required_integrations": required_integrations,
            "missing_integrations": missing_integrations,
            "recommendation": self._generate_recommendation(can_do, matching_abilities, missing_integrations),
        }

    def _generate_recommendation(
        self,
        can_do: bool,
        abilities: List[ScannedAbility],
        missing: List[str]
    ) -> str:
        """Generate a recommendation based on capability assessment."""
        if can_do and not missing:
            return f"Yes, I can do this using {abilities[0].name if abilities else 'available functions'}."
        elif missing:
            return f"I would need the {', '.join(missing)} integration(s) to do this."
        else:
            return "I don't have direct capability for this, but I may be able to help in a different way."

    def list_my_abilities(self, category: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        List all abilities, optionally filtered by category.

        # BEGIN META: list_my_abilities
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: List agent abilities
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["list", "abilities"]
        # END META
        """
        scan_result = self._scanner.scan()

        abilities = scan_result.abilities
        if category:
            abilities = [a for a in abilities if a.category == category]

        return [a.to_dict() for a in abilities]

    def what_do_i_know_about(self, topic: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Answer "What do I know about X?" questions.

        # BEGIN META: what_do_i_know_about
        # Date: 2026-01-14
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Self-query for knowledge about a topic
        # SRS: ["SRS-AWARENESS-002"]
        # Tags: ["query", "knowledge"]
        # END META
        """
        scan_result = self._scanner.scan()
        topic_lower = topic.lower()

        related_functions = []
        related_classes = []
        related_abilities = []

        # Search functions
        for func in scan_result.functions:
            if topic_lower in func.name.lower() or topic_lower in (func.docstring or "").lower():
                related_functions.append({
                    "name": func.name,
                    "purpose": func.purpose or func.docstring[:100] if func.docstring else "",
                    "line": func.line_number,
                })

        # Search classes
        for cls in scan_result.classes:
            if topic_lower in cls.name.lower() or topic_lower in (cls.docstring or "").lower():
                related_classes.append({
                    "name": cls.name,
                    "purpose": cls.purpose or cls.docstring[:100] if cls.docstring else "",
                    "methods": len(cls.methods),
                })

        # Search abilities
        for ability in scan_result.abilities:
            if topic_lower in ability.name.lower() or topic_lower in ability.description.lower():
                related_abilities.append(ability.to_dict())

        return {
            "topic": topic,
            "knowledge_found": len(related_functions) + len(related_classes) + len(related_abilities) > 0,
            "related_functions": related_functions[:10],
            "related_classes": related_classes[:10],
            "related_abilities": related_abilities[:10],
            "summary": self._summarize_knowledge(topic, related_functions, related_classes, related_abilities),
        }

    def _summarize_knowledge(
        self,
        topic: str,
        functions: List[Dict],
        classes: List[Dict],
        abilities: List[Dict]
    ) -> str:
        """Generate a summary of knowledge about a topic."""
        if not functions and not classes and not abilities:
            return f"I don't have specific knowledge about '{topic}' in my codebase."

        parts = []
        if functions:
            parts.append(f"{len(functions)} function(s) related to {topic}")
        if classes:
            parts.append(f"{len(classes)} class(es) related to {topic}")
        if abilities:
            parts.append(f"{len(abilities)} ability(ies) related to {topic}")

        return f"I have {', '.join(parts)}."

    def _log_operation(self, operation: str, details: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """Log an operation for audit trail."""
        self._operational_log.append({
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            "operation": operation,
            "details": details,
        })

        # Keep log bounded
        if len(self._operational_log) > 1000:
            self._operational_log = self._operational_log[-500:]

    def get_operational_log(self, limit: int = 50) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get recent operational log entries."""
        return self._operational_log[-limit:]


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# END SECTION 107: FUNCTION/ABILITY SELF-SCANNER
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 108: RPG ABILITY STATS SYSTEM (Trust & Knowledge Bars)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-ABILITY-STATS-001, SRS-ANALYTICS-002, SRS-GAMIFICATION-003
# Purpose: RPG-style stat bars for abilities based on operational trust and knowledge metrics
# Features: DataDog trust metrics, PostHog/Supabase knowledge scoring, visual stat bars
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


@dataclass
class AbilityStats:
    """
    RPG-style stats for an agent ability.

    # BEGIN META: AbilityStats
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Track trust and knowledge levels for abilities
    # SRS: ["SRS-ABILITY-STATS-001", "SRS-GAMIFICATION-003"]
    # Tags: ["rpg", "stats", "abilities", "trust", "knowledge"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # Description: RPG stat bars based on operational data from DataDog (trust)
    #              and PostHog/Supabase (knowledge) for each agent ability.
    #              XP gained only through good behavior; bad behavior reduces XP/TP.
    # END META
    """

    ability_name: str
    category: str

    # Trust Score (0-100) - Based on DataDog operational metrics
    # Higher trust = more reliable execution, fewer errors
    trust_score: float = 50.0
    trust_level: str = "NEUTRAL"  # UNTRUSTED, LOW, NEUTRAL, HIGH, LEGENDARY

    # Knowledge Score (0-100) - Based on PostHog/Supabase interaction outcomes
    # Higher knowledge = better understanding of the domain
    knowledge_score: float = 50.0
    knowledge_level: str = "NOVICE"  # NOVICE, APPRENTICE, JOURNEYMAN, EXPERT, MASTER

    # Usage metrics
    total_invocations: int = 0
    successful_invocations: int = 0
    failed_invocations: int = 0
    avg_latency_ms: float = 0.0

    # Learning metrics
    outcomes_positive: int = 0
    outcomes_negative: int = 0
    user_feedback_score: float = 0.5

    # Time tracking
    last_used: Optional[str] = None
    first_used: Optional[str] = None

    # Metadata
    datadog_metric_id: Optional[str] = None
    posthog_event_type: Optional[str] = None

    # XP/TP Impact - Calculated from operational behavior
    # Good behavior = positive XP/TP, Bad behavior = negative XP/TP
    xp_impact: int = 0  # Can be negative for poor performance
    tp_impact: int = 0  # Can be negative for poor performance
    behavior_grade: str = "NEUTRAL"  # EXCELLENT, GOOD, NEUTRAL, POOR, CRITICAL

    # Data Source Tracking
    data_source: str = "SYNTHETIC"  # SYNTHETIC or LIVE
    datadog_connected: bool = False
    posthog_connected: bool = False
    supabase_connected: bool = False

    # Analytics Counter - Total tracks from integrations
    analytics_tracks_datadog: int = 0
    analytics_tracks_posthog: int = 0
    analytics_tracks_supabase: int = 0

    def calculate_trust_level(self) -> str:
        """Calculate trust level from score."""
        if self.trust_score >= 90:
            return "LEGENDARY"
        elif self.trust_score >= 75:
            return "HIGH"
        elif self.trust_score >= 50:
            return "NEUTRAL"
        elif self.trust_score >= 25:
            return "LOW"
        else:
            return "UNTRUSTED"

    def calculate_knowledge_level(self) -> str:
        """Calculate knowledge level from score."""
        if self.knowledge_score >= 90:
            return "MASTER"
        elif self.knowledge_score >= 75:
            return "EXPERT"
        elif self.knowledge_score >= 50:
            return "JOURNEYMAN"
        elif self.knowledge_score >= 25:
            return "APPRENTICE"
        else:
            return "NOVICE"

    def calculate_behavior_grade(self) -> str:
        """
        Calculate behavior grade from operational metrics.
        Determines if XP/TP should be gained or lost.
        """
        success_rate = (self.successful_invocations / max(1, self.total_invocations)) * 100
        error_rate = (self.failed_invocations / max(1, self.total_invocations)) * 100

        # Combine trust, knowledge, and success metrics
        combined_score = (self.trust_score * 0.4 + self.knowledge_score * 0.3 + success_rate * 0.3)

        if combined_score >= 85 and error_rate < 5:
            return "EXCELLENT"
        elif combined_score >= 70 and error_rate < 10:
            return "GOOD"
        elif combined_score >= 50 and error_rate < 20:
            return "NEUTRAL"
        elif combined_score >= 30 or error_rate < 35:
            return "POOR"
        else:
            return "CRITICAL"

    def calculate_xp_tp_impact(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        """
        Calculate XP/TP impact based on operational behavior.
        Good behavior = positive gains, Bad behavior = negative (loss).

        Returns:
            Tuple of (xp_impact, tp_impact)
        """
        grade = self.calculate_behavior_grade()

        # Base values per invocation
        base_xp = self.total_invocations // 10  # 1 XP per 10 invocations base

        impact_multipliers = {
            "EXCELLENT": (3.0, 2.0),   # +300% XP, +200% TP
            "GOOD": (1.5, 1.0),        # +150% XP, +100% TP
            "NEUTRAL": (0.5, 0.25),    # +50% XP, +25% TP
            "POOR": (-0.5, -0.25),     # -50% XP, -25% TP (LOSS)
            "CRITICAL": (-1.5, -1.0),  # -150% XP, -100% TP (MAJOR LOSS)
        }

        xp_mult, tp_mult = impact_multipliers.get(grade, (0, 0))

        xp_impact = int(base_xp * xp_mult)
        tp_impact = int(base_xp * tp_mult * 0.5)  # TP is harder to earn

        return xp_impact, tp_impact
