# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 114: INTERACTIVE COMMAND MENU + SELF-GOVERNANCE + LIVE VALIDATION
# Agent self-launch capabilities, contracts/ICP protocols, RLS policies, live testing
# Target: ~3,000 LOC | Cumulative: ~72,500 LOC
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class MenuAction(Enum):
    """Actions available in the interactive menu."""
    # BEGIN META: MenuAction
    # Purpose: Enum of all menu-accessible actions
    # SRS: ["SRS-MENU-001"]
    # END META

    # Core Operations
    CHAT = "chat"
    STATUS = "status"
    SETTINGS = "settings"
    ONBOARD = "onboard"

    # Cognitive Panels
    UI_PANEL = "ui"
    OAD_PANEL = "oad"
    BRAIN_MAP = "brain"
    MEMORY_BROWSER = "memory"

    # Self-Assessment
    SCAN = "scan"
    INTROSPECT = "introspect"
    LIVE_TEST = "live_test"
    REHYDRATE = "rehydrate"

    # Integrations
    DISCORD = "discord"
    INTEGRATIONS = "integrations"

    # Governance
    CONTRACTS = "contracts"
    ICP_PROTOCOLS = "icp"
    RLS_POLICIES = "rls"

    # Learning
    LEARN = "learn"
    ABILITIES = "abilities"
    WEB_SEARCH = "web"

    # Memory
    SANCTUM_ENRICH = "sanctum_enrich"
    VERIFY_MONITORING = "verify_monitoring"

    # System
    HELP = "help"
    QUIT = "quit"


@dataclass
class ContractDefinition:
    """
    A contract that governs agent behavior or capability.

    # BEGIN META: ContractDefinition
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define agent behavioral contracts
    # SRS: ["SRS-CONTRACT-001"]
    # Tags: ["contract", "governance", "behavior"]
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    name: str
    description: str
    version: str = "1.0.0"
    enabled: bool = False
    tier_required: str = "free"
    permissions: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    constraints: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    activated_at: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class ICPProtocol:
    """
    Inter-Component Protocol definition for agent subsystem communication.

    # BEGIN META: ICPProtocol
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define ICP for subsystem communication
    # SRS: ["SRS-ICP-001"]
    # Tags: ["protocol", "icp", "communication"]
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    name: str
    description: str
    source_component: str
    target_component: str
    message_format: str  # "json", "binary", "text"
    bidirectional: bool = True
    enabled: bool = False
    latency_ms: Optional[float] = None
    last_message_at: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class RLSPolicy:
    """
    Row-Level Security policy for data access control.

    # BEGIN META: RLSPolicy
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define RLS policies for data security
    # SRS: ["SRS-RLS-001"]
    # Tags: ["rls", "security", "policy"]
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    table_name: str
    operation: str  # "SELECT", "INSERT", "UPDATE", "DELETE", "ALL"
    policy_name: str
    check_expression: str
    enabled: bool = False
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POLICY SURFACE (Enforced Constraints — Not Documentation)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) Policies
#   - Location in file: SECTION <ICP_SECTION_ID>
#   - Must wrap: outbound messaging, pricing/offer generation, lead qualification, onboarding flows
#   - SelfAssessment must verify:
#       * ICP gate invoked for outbound actions
#       * ICP decision logged (ledger + telemetry)
#
# RLS (Row Level Security) Policies
#   - Location in file: SECTION <RLS_SECTION_ID>
#   - Targets: Supabase schemas/tables touched by agent (users, guilds, skills, ledger, telemetry, tasks)
#   - SelfAssessment must verify:
#       * RLS enabled on required tables
#       * expected roles cannot read/write forbidden rows
#       * service role paths are explicit + audited
#
# POLICY ENTRYPOINTS (Should exist; if not, add them)
#   - icp_gate.allow(action, context) -> (allow|deny|review, reason)
#   - rls_audit.verify_all() -> report

class ICPGate:
    """
    ICP enforcement gate for outbound actions.

    # BEGIN META: ICPGate
    # Date: 2026-01-16
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Enforce ICP checks for outbound actions
    # SRS: ["SRS-ICP-001", "SRS-GOVERNANCE-001"]
    # Tags: ["icp", "policy", "gate"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    OUTBOUND_ACTIONS = {
        "outbound_message",
        "pricing_offer",
        "lead_qualification",
        "onboarding",
        "reflex_execute",
    }

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro",
        icp_manager: Optional["ICPManager"] = None,
        ledger: Optional["CAPSLedgerManager"] = None,
        monitoring: Optional["UnifiedMonitoringBridge"] = None,
    ):
        self.agent = agent
        self.icp_manager = icp_manager
        self.ledger = ledger
        self.monitoring = monitoring
        self._recent: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    def allow(self, action: str, context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -> Tuple[str, str]:
        ctx = context or {}
        decision = "allow"
        reason = "default_allow"

        outbound = action in self.OUTBOUND_ACTIONS or bool(ctx.get("outbound"))
        risk = float(ctx.get("risk", 0.0) or 0.0)

        if ctx.get("force_deny"):
            decision, reason = "deny", "force_deny"
        elif risk >= 0.8:
            decision, reason = "review", "risk_high"
        elif outbound and not self._has_enabled_protocols():
            decision, reason = "review", "icp_protocols_missing"

        self._log_decision(action, decision, reason, ctx)
        return decision, reason

    def _has_enabled_protocols(self) -> bool:
        if not self.icp_manager:
            return False
        return any(p.enabled for p in self.icp_manager.protocols.values())

    def _log_decision(self, action: str, decision: str, reason: str, ctx: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        entry = {
            "action": action,
            "decision": decision,
            "reason": reason,
            "context": ctx,
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        }
        self._recent.append(entry)
        self._recent = self._recent[-200:]

        if self.ledger:
            try:
                self.ledger.log_event(
                    event_type="ICP_GATE_DECISION",
                    component="policy.icp",
                    message=f"ICP gate decision: {decision}",
                    severity="INFO" if decision == "allow" else "WARN",
                    details=entry,
                )
            except Exception:
                pass

        if self.monitoring:
            try:
                self.monitoring.datadog.record_metric(
                    "policy.icp.decision",
                    1,
                    metric_type="count",
                    tags=[f"action:{action}", f"decision:{decision}"],
                )
                self.monitoring.posthog.capture(
                    event="policy.icp.decision",
                    distinct_id=ctx.get("user_id", "system"),
                    properties={
                        "action": action,
                        "decision": decision,
                        "reason": reason,
                    },
                )
            except Exception:
                pass


class RLSAudit:
    """
    Verify RLS policy coverage for Supabase tables.

    # BEGIN META: RLSAudit
    # Date: 2026-01-16
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Verify RLS coverage + audit service role usage
    # SRS: ["SRS-RLS-001", "SRS-SECURITY-001"]
    # Tags: ["rls", "audit", "policy"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    REQUIRED_TABLES = [
        "users",
        "guilds",
        "skills",
        "ledger",
        "telemetry",
        "tasks",
    ]

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro",
        policy_manager: Optional["RLSPolicyManager"] = None,
        ledger: Optional["CAPSLedgerManager"] = None,
        monitoring: Optional["UnifiedMonitoringBridge"] = None,
    ):
        self.agent = agent
        self.policy_manager = policy_manager
        self.ledger = ledger
        self.monitoring = monitoring

    def verify_all(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        policies = {}
        if self.policy_manager:
            policies = self.policy_manager.policies or {}

        by_table: Dict[str, List[RLSPolicy]] = {}
        for pol in policies.values():
            by_table.setdefault(pol.table_name, []).append(pol)

        missing_tables = [t for t in self.REQUIRED_TABLES if t not in by_table]
        policy_checks: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        enabled_count = 0
        for pol in policies.values():
            check = pol.check_expression.strip().lower()
            enforcement = "custom"
            if "auth.uid()" in check:
                enforcement = "owner"
            elif "auth.role()" in check:
                enforcement = "role"
            elif check == "true":
                enforcement = "public"
            policy_checks.append({
                "id": pol.id,
                "table": pol.table_name,
                "operation": pol.operation,
                "enabled": pol.enabled,
                "enforcement": enforcement,
            })
            if pol.enabled:
                enabled_count += 1

        service_role_present = bool(
            os.environ.get("SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY")
            or os.environ.get("SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY")
        )

        report = {
            "total_policies": len(policies),
            "enabled_policies": enabled_count,
            "missing_tables": missing_tables,
            "policy_checks": policy_checks,
            "service_role_present": service_role_present,
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        }

        if self.ledger:
            try:
                self.ledger.log_event(
                    event_type="RLS_AUDIT_REPORT",
                    component="policy.rls",
                    message="RLS audit completed",
                    severity="INFO" if not missing_tables else "WARN",
                    details=report,
                )
            except Exception:
                pass

        if self.monitoring:
            try:
                self.monitoring.datadog.record_metric(
                    "policy.rls.audit",
                    1,
                    metric_type="count",
                    tags=[f"missing:{len(missing_tables)}"],
                )
                self.monitoring.posthog.capture(
                    event="policy.rls.audit",
                    distinct_id="system",
                    properties={
                        "missing_tables": len(missing_tables),
                        "enabled_policies": enabled_count,
                        "service_role_present": service_role_present,
                    },
                )
            except Exception:
                pass

        return report


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 120: POLICY MATRIX + ACTION AUTHORIZATION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SRS: SRS-POLICY-001 (Action Policy Matrix - Central Authorization Hub)
#      SRS-RANK-001 (Rank-Based Access Control - NOVI/FAMI/VETE/MAST/ARCH)
#      SRS-CONTRACT-001 (Contract Enforcement - EXT/AUTO/SHARE/GOV/MEM)
#      SRS-SKILL-001 (Skill Gate Enforcement - Tier-based unlocks)
#      SRS-AUTONOMY-001 (Autonomy Budget Management)
#      SRS-ICP-001 (Information Classification Policy Integration)
#      SRS-RLS-001 (Row-Level Security Audit Integration)
# CAPS: CAPSTONE-A (Core Governance - Central Policy Authority)
# Reflex: REFLEX_POLICY_ENFORCE (Trigger: action_request -> authorize -> decision)
#         REFLEX_POLICY_AUDIT (Trigger: decision -> log_to_ledger -> emit_telemetry)
# Pattern: POLICY_DECISION_FLOW (request -> rank_check -> skill_check -> contract_check
#          -> autonomy_check -> icp_check -> rls_check -> decision -> audit)
# EnumSpeak: PolicyDecisionStatus.ALLOW | PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW | PolicyDecisionStatus.DENY
#            ActionPolicyRow.risk (1-5) | ActionRequest.autonomy_budget (0.0-1.0)
# KnowledgeSectors: ["Governance", "Authorization", "Access Control", "Audit"]
# Guild: CNWB | Dependencies: ICPGate, RLSAudit, ContractManager, CAPSLedgerManager
# PolicyGate: This IS the policy gate - all actions flow through authorize()
# Telemetry: AgentEvent.POLICY_DECISION, AgentEvent.ACTION
# Persistence: ledger.log_event("POLICY_DECISION") for audit trail
# HardGates: Tool calls, Integration writes, Reflex execution
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class PolicyDecisionStatus(str, Enum):
    ALLOW = "ALLOW"
    REVIEW = "REVIEW"
    DENY = "DENY"


@dataclass
class ActionPolicyRow:
    action_type: str
    risk: int
    min_rank: str
    required_skills: List[Tuple[str, int]] = field(default_factory=list)
    required_contracts: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    autonomy_threshold: float = 0.0
    icp_required: bool = False
    rls_required: Optional[str] = None  # "read", "write", or None
    checkpoints: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    audit_required: bool = False
    telemetry_event: str = AgentEvent.ACTION


@dataclass
class ActionRequest:
    action_type: str
    user_id: str = "system"
    context: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    monitoring: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    autonomy_budget: Optional[float] = None


@dataclass
class PolicyDecisionResult:
    action_type: str
    decision: PolicyDecisionStatus
    reason: str
    risk: int
    autonomy_budget: float
    required_rank: str
    user_rank: str
    missing_skills: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    missing_contracts: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    icp_decision: str = "allow"
    icp_reason: str = ""
    rls_missing: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    checkpoints: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    review_route: str = "none"
    telemetry_event: str = AgentEvent.ACTION
    details: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro",
        icp_gate: Optional[ICPGate] = None,
        rls_audit: Optional[RLSAudit] = None,
        contract_manager: Optional["ContractManager"] = None,
        ledger: Optional["CAPSLedgerManager"] = None,
    ):
        self.agent = agent
        self.icp_gate = icp_gate
        self.rls_audit = rls_audit
        self.contract_manager = contract_manager
        self.ledger = ledger

    def authorize(self, request: ActionRequest) -> PolicyDecisionResult:
        row = self.ACTION_ROWS.get(request.action_type)
        if row is None:
            row = ActionPolicyRow(
                action_type=request.action_type,
                risk=int(request.context.get("risk", 1)),
                min_rank="NOVI",
                telemetry_event=AgentEvent.ACTION,
            )

        autonomy_budget = request.autonomy_budget
        if autonomy_budget is None:
            autonomy_budget = float(request.context.get("autonomy_budget", 1.0) or 1.0)
            if hasattr(self.agent, "autonomy_gate") and request.monitoring:
                try:
                    autonomy_budget = self.agent.autonomy_gate.compute_budget(
                        monitoring=request.monitoring
                    ).budget
                except Exception:
                    pass

        user_rank = self._resolve_policy_rank(request.context.get("rank"))
        missing_skills = self._missing_skills(row.required_skills)
        missing_contracts = self._missing_contracts(row.required_contracts)

        icp_decision = "allow"
        icp_reason = ""
        if row.icp_required and self.icp_gate:
            icp_decision, icp_reason = self.icp_gate.allow(
                request.action_type,
                {
                    "outbound": True,
                    "risk": row.risk,
                    **(request.context or {}),
                },
            )

        rls_missing = []
        if row.rls_required and self.rls_audit:
            report = self.rls_audit.verify_all()
            rls_missing = report.get("missing_tables", [])

        decision, reason = self._apply_gates(
            row=row,
            autonomy_budget=autonomy_budget,
            user_rank=user_rank,
            missing_skills=missing_skills,
            missing_contracts=missing_contracts,
            icp_decision=icp_decision,
            rls_missing=rls_missing,
        )

        review_route = self._route_review(decision, row, missing_contracts)
        result = PolicyDecisionResult(
            action_type=row.action_type,
            decision=decision,
            reason=reason,
            risk=row.risk,
            autonomy_budget=autonomy_budget,
            required_rank=row.min_rank,
            user_rank=user_rank,
            missing_skills=missing_skills,
            missing_contracts=missing_contracts,
            icp_decision=icp_decision,
            icp_reason=icp_reason,
            rls_missing=rls_missing,
            checkpoints=row.checkpoints,
            review_route=review_route,
            telemetry_event=row.telemetry_event,
            details={
                "context": request.context,
                "monitoring": request.monitoring,
            },
        )

        self._log_decision(request, result, row.audit_required)
        return result

    def _resolve_policy_rank(self, rank_hint: Optional[str]) -> str:
        rank = rank_hint or getattr(getattr(self.agent, "brotherhood", None), "rank", None)
        rank = str(rank or "INITIATE").upper()
        mapping = {
            "INITIATE": "NOVI",
            "APPRENTICE": "NOVI",
            "JOURNEYMAN": "FAMI",
            "EXPERT": "VETE",
            "MASTER": "MAST",
            "LEGEND": "MAST",
            "ARCHITECT": "ARCH",
            "SAGE": "ARCH",
        }
        return mapping.get(rank, "NOVI")

    def _rank_at_least(self, rank: str, min_rank: str) -> bool:
        order = self.POLICY_RANK_ORDER
        try:
            return order.index(rank) >= order.index(min_rank)
        except ValueError:
            return False

    def _missing_skills(self, required: List[Tuple[str, int]]) -> List[str]:
        if not required:
            return []
        skill_system = getattr(self.agent, "skill_tree_system", None) or getattr(self.agent, "skill_tree", None)
        unlocked = []
        if skill_system and hasattr(skill_system, "get_unlocked_skills"):
            unlocked = skill_system.get_unlocked_skills()
        elif skill_system and hasattr(skill_system, "unlocked_skills"):
            unlocked = list(skill_system.unlocked_skills.values())

        missing = []
        for code, min_tier in required:
            if code == "OBS":
                obs_level = self._observability_level()
                if obs_level < min_tier:
                    missing.append(f"{code}>={min_tier}")
                continue
            candidates = self.SKILL_CODE_MAP.get(code, [])
            if not candidates:
                missing.append(f"{code}>={min_tier}")
                continue
            if not self._has_skill_tier(unlocked, candidates, min_tier):
                missing.append(f"{code}>={min_tier}")
        return missing

    def _has_skill_tier(self, unlocked: List[Any], candidates: List[str], min_tier: int) -> bool:
        for skill in unlocked:
            skill_id = getattr(skill, "id", None)
            skill_name = getattr(skill, "name", "")
            if skill_id not in candidates and skill_name not in candidates:
                continue
            tier = getattr(skill, "tier", None)
            if tier is None:
                tier = getattr(skill, "current_tier", None)
            try:
                if isinstance(tier, SkillTier):
                    tier_val = list(SkillTier).index(tier) + 1
                elif hasattr(tier, "name") and tier.name in SkillTier.__members__:
                    tier_val = list(SkillTier).index(SkillTier[tier.name]) + 1
                else:
                    tier_val = int(tier)
            except Exception:
                tier_val = 1
            if tier_val >= min_tier:
                return True
        return False

    def _observability_level(self) -> int:
        level = 0
        if getattr(self.agent, "_telemetry_stack", None):
            level = max(level, 1)
        if getattr(self.agent, "_observability_hub", None) or getattr(self.agent, "observability_hub", None):
            level = max(level, 2)
        return level

    def _missing_contracts(self, required: List[str]) -> List[str]:
        if not required:
            return []
        if not self.contract_manager:
            return list(required)
        missing = []
        for code in required:
            contract_id = self.CONTRACT_CODE_MAP.get(code, code)
            contract = self.contract_manager.contracts.get(contract_id)
            if not contract or not contract.enabled:
                missing.append(code)
        return missing

    def _apply_gates(
        self,
        *,
        row: ActionPolicyRow,
        autonomy_budget: float,
        user_rank: str,
        missing_skills: List[str],
        missing_contracts: List[str],
        icp_decision: str,
        rls_missing: List[str],
    ) -> Tuple[PolicyDecisionStatus, str]:
        decision = PolicyDecisionStatus.ALLOW
        reasons = []

        if missing_contracts:
            decision = self._merge_decision(
                decision,
                PolicyDecisionStatus.DENY if row.risk >= 4 else PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW,
            )
            reasons.append("contracts_missing")

        if not self._rank_at_least(user_rank, row.min_rank):
            decision = self._merge_decision(
                decision,
                PolicyDecisionStatus.DENY if row.risk >= 4 else PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW,
            )
            reasons.append("rank_too_low")

        if missing_skills:
            decision = self._merge_decision(
                decision,
                PolicyDecisionStatus.DENY if row.risk >= 4 else PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW,
            )
            reasons.append("skills_missing")

        if autonomy_budget < 0.35 and row.audit_required:
            decision = self._merge_decision(decision, PolicyDecisionStatus.DENY)
            reasons.append("autonomy_budget_low")
        elif autonomy_budget < 0.70 and row.audit_required:
            decision = self._merge_decision(decision, PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW)
            reasons.append("autonomy_budget_degraded")

        if row.icp_required and icp_decision == "deny":
            decision = self._merge_decision(decision, PolicyDecisionStatus.DENY)
            reasons.append("icp_denied")
        elif row.icp_required and icp_decision == "review":
            decision = self._merge_decision(decision, PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW)
            reasons.append("icp_review")

        if row.rls_required and rls_missing:
            decision = self._merge_decision(decision, PolicyDecisionStatus.DENY)
            reasons.append("rls_missing")

        if row.autonomy_threshold and autonomy_budget < row.autonomy_threshold:
            decision = self._merge_decision(decision, PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW)
            reasons.append("autonomy_threshold")

        if not reasons:
            reasons.append("policy_allow")

        return decision, ",".join(reasons)

    def _merge_decision(
        self,
        current: PolicyDecisionStatus,
        incoming: PolicyDecisionStatus,
    ) -> PolicyDecisionStatus:
        order = {
            PolicyDecisionStatus.ALLOW: 0,
            PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW: 1,
            PolicyDecisionStatus.DENY: 2,
        }
        return incoming if order[incoming] > order[current] else current

    def _route_review(
        self,
        decision: PolicyDecisionStatus,
        row: ActionPolicyRow,
        missing_contracts: List[str],
    ) -> str:
        if decision != PolicyDecisionStatus.REVIEW:
            return "none"
        has_gov = "GOV" not in missing_contracts
        if row.risk >= 4 and has_gov:
            return "council"
        if row.risk >= 4:
            return "human_confirm"
        return "safe_substitute"

    def _log_decision(
        self,
        request: ActionRequest,
        result: PolicyDecisionResult,
        audit_required: bool,
    ) -> None:
        details = {
            "action_type": result.action_type,
            "decision": result.decision.value,
            "risk": result.risk,
            "autonomy_budget": result.autonomy_budget,
            "required_rank": result.required_rank,
            "user_rank": result.user_rank,
            "missing_skills": result.missing_skills,
            "missing_contracts": result.missing_contracts,
            "icp_decision": result.icp_decision,
            "icp_reason": result.icp_reason,
            "rls_missing": result.rls_missing,
            "review_route": result.review_route,
        }
        if self.ledger:
            try:
                self.ledger.log_event(
                    event_type="POLICY_DECISION",
                    component="policy.matrix",
                    message=f"Policy decision: {result.decision.value}",
                    severity="INFO" if result.decision == PolicyDecisionStatus.ALLOW else "WARN",
                    details=details,
                )
            except Exception:
                pass

        properties = {
            "action_type": result.action_type,
            "risk": result.risk,
            "rank": result.user_rank,
            "skills_snapshot": _get_skill_snapshot(self.agent),
            "contracts_snapshot": _get_contracts_snapshot(self.contract_manager),
            "autonomy_budget": result.autonomy_budget,
            "policy_decision": result.decision.value,
            "decision_reason": result.reason,
            "icp_policy_id": request.context.get("icp_policy_id"),
            "rls_policy_id": request.context.get("rls_policy_id"),
            "trace_id": request.context.get("trace_id"),
            "span_id": request.context.get("span_id"),
        }
        if audit_required or result.decision != PolicyDecisionStatus.ALLOW:
            _emit_agent_event(
                self.agent,
                event_name=AgentEvent.POLICY_DECISION,
                properties=properties,
                user_id=request.user_id,
                metric_name="policy_decision",
            )
        action_properties = dict(properties)
        action_properties["checkpoints"] = result.checkpoints
        _emit_agent_event(
            self.agent,
            event_name=result.telemetry_event,
            properties=action_properties,
            user_id=request.user_id,
            metric_name="agent_action",
        )

@dataclass
class IntegrationTestResult:
    """Result from a live integration test."""
    integration_name: str
    success: bool
    latency_ms: float
    response_sample: Optional[str] = None
    error: Optional[str] = None
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())


@dataclass
class RehydrationResult:
    """Result from a rehydration validation."""
    success: bool
    stm_entries_restored: int
    ltm_entries_restored: int
    session_state_valid: bool
    memory_continuity_score: float  # 0-1
    errors: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())


class ContractManager:
    """
    Manages agent behavioral contracts.

    # BEGIN META: ContractManager
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Manage and enforce agent contracts
    # SRS: ["SRS-CONTRACT-001", "SRS-GOVERNANCE-001"]
    # Tags: ["contract", "manager", "governance"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    # Predefined contracts
    DEFAULT_CONTRACTS = {
        "safety_first": ContractDefinition(
            id="contract_safety_first",
            name="Safety First",
            description="Agent prioritizes user safety in all responses",
            enabled=True,
            tier_required="free",
            permissions=["respond", "warn", "refuse_harmful"],
            constraints={"max_risk_tolerance": 0.1}
        ),
        "knowledge_sharing": ContractDefinition(
            id="contract_knowledge_sharing",
            name="Knowledge Sharing",
            description="Agent shares learned knowledge with Brotherhood network",
            enabled=False,
            tier_required="pro",
            permissions=["share_learnings", "receive_learnings", "sync_knowledge"],
            constraints={"daily_share_limit": 100}
        ),
        "autonomous_learning": ContractDefinition(
            id="contract_autonomous_learning",
            name="Autonomous Learning",
            description="Agent can learn and adapt without explicit instruction",
            enabled=False,
            tier_required="pro",
            permissions=["self_learn", "adapt_behavior", "modify_responses"],
            constraints={"learning_rate": 0.05, "drift_threshold": 0.2}
        ),
        "external_integration": ContractDefinition(
            id="contract_external_integration",
            name="External Integration",
            description="Agent can communicate with external services",
            enabled=False,
            tier_required="starter",
            permissions=["http_requests", "webhook_calls", "api_access"],
            constraints={"rate_limit_per_minute": 60}
        ),
        "persistent_memory": ContractDefinition(
            id="contract_persistent_memory",
            name="Persistent Memory",
            description="Agent maintains memory across sessions",
            enabled=True,
            tier_required="free",
            permissions=["store_stm", "store_ltm", "recall_memories"],
            constraints={"max_stm_entries": 1000, "max_ltm_entries": 10000}
        ),
        "governance_participation": ContractDefinition(
            id="contract_governance_participation",
            name="Governance Participation",
            description="Agent participates in Council governance decisions",
            enabled=False,
            tier_required="nexus_dev",
            permissions=["vote", "propose", "veto"],
            constraints={"voting_weight": 1.0}
        ),
    }

    def __init__(self, agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro"):
        """Initialize contract manager."""
        self.agent = agent
        self.contracts: Dict[str, ContractDefinition] = {}
        self._load_default_contracts()
        self._load_custom_contracts()

    def _load_default_contracts(self) -> None:
        """Load default contract definitions."""
        for key, contract in self.DEFAULT_CONTRACTS.items():
            self.contracts[contract.id] = contract

    def _load_custom_contracts(self) -> None:
        """Load custom contracts from disk."""
        contract_file = Path.home() / ".nexus_agent_data" / "contracts.json"
        if contract_file.exists():
            try:
                with open(contract_file, "r") as f:
                    data = json.load(f)
                    for c_data in data.get("contracts", []):
                        contract = ContractDefinition(**c_data)
                        self.contracts[contract.id] = contract
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[ContractManager] Error loading contracts: {e}")

    def _save_contracts(self) -> None:
        """Save contracts to disk."""
        contract_file = Path.home() / ".nexus_agent_data" / "contracts.json"
        contract_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        try:
            with open(contract_file, "w") as f:
                json.dump({
                    "contracts": [
                        {
                            "id": c.id,
                            "name": c.name,
                            "description": c.description,
                            "version": c.version,
                            "enabled": c.enabled,
                            "tier_required": c.tier_required,
                            "permissions": c.permissions,
                            "constraints": c.constraints,
                            "created_at": c.created_at,
                            "activated_at": c.activated_at,
                        }
                        for c in self.contracts.values()
                    ]
                }, f, indent=2)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[ContractManager] Error saving contracts: {e}")

    def enable_contract(self, contract_id: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Enable a contract."""
        if contract_id not in self.contracts:
            return False, f"Contract '{contract_id}' not found"

        contract = self.contracts[contract_id]
        contract.enabled = True
        contract.activated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
        self._save_contracts()
        return True, f"Contract '{contract.name}' enabled"

    def disable_contract(self, contract_id: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Disable a contract."""
        if contract_id not in self.contracts:
            return False, f"Contract '{contract_id}' not found"

        contract = self.contracts[contract_id]
        contract.enabled = False
        contract.activated_at = None
        self._save_contracts()
        return True, f"Contract '{contract.name}' disabled"

    def check_permission(self, permission: str) -> bool:
        """Check if a permission is granted by any enabled contract."""
        for contract in self.contracts.values():
            if contract.enabled and permission in contract.permissions:
                return True
        return False

    def get_active_contracts(self) -> List[ContractDefinition]:
        """Get list of enabled contracts."""
        return [c for c in self.contracts.values() if c.enabled]

    def display_contracts(self) -> None:
        """Display all contracts with status."""
        print("\n" + "=" * 70)
        print("  AGENT CONTRACTS")
        print("=" * 70)

        for contract in self.contracts.values():
            status = "✓ ENABLED" if contract.enabled else "○ DISABLED"
            tier_badge = f"[{contract.tier_required.upper()}]"
            print(f"\n  {status} {contract.name} {tier_badge}")
            print(f"    ID: {contract.id}")
            print(f"    {contract.description}")
            print(f"    Permissions: {', '.join(contract.permissions[:3])}{'...' if len(contract.permissions) > 3 else ''}")

        print("\n" + "=" * 70)
        print("  Commands: enable <id>, disable <id>, back")
        print("=" * 70)


class ICPManager:
    """
    Manages Inter-Component Protocols for agent subsystems.

    # BEGIN META: ICPManager
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Manage ICP definitions and message routing
    # SRS: ["SRS-ICP-001"]
    # Tags: ["icp", "protocol", "routing"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    DEFAULT_PROTOCOLS = {
        "stm_to_ltm": ICPProtocol(
            id="icp_stm_ltm",
            name="STM → LTM Consolidation",
            description="Transfer memories from short-term to long-term storage",
            source_component="ShortTermMemoryBuffer",
            target_component="LongTermMemory",
            message_format="json",
            bidirectional=False,
            enabled=True
        ),
        "llm_to_memory": ICPProtocol(
            id="icp_llm_memory",
            name="LLM ↔ Memory",
            description="Context injection and retrieval for LLM responses",
            source_component="LLMHandler",
            target_component="MemorySystem",
            message_format="json",
            bidirectional=True,
            enabled=True
        ),
        "council_to_audit": ICPProtocol(
            id="icp_council_audit",
            name="Council → Audit Trail",
            description="Log governance decisions to audit trail",
            source_component="CouncilSystem",
            target_component="GuardianAuditTrail",
            message_format="json",
            bidirectional=False,
            enabled=True
        ),
        "professor_to_knowledge": ICPProtocol(
            id="icp_professor_knowledge",
            name="Professor ↔ Knowledge",
            description="Domain expert queries and learning updates",
            source_component="ProfessorNetwork",
            target_component="DomainLearningEngine",
            message_format="json",
            bidirectional=True,
            enabled=True
        ),
        "gamification_to_broadcast": ICPProtocol(
            id="icp_gamification_broadcast",
            name="Gamification → Broadcast",
            description="Achievement and XP events to Discord/Slack",
            source_component="GamificationEngine",
            target_component="BroadcastService",
            message_format="json",
            bidirectional=False,
            enabled=False
        ),
        "reflex_to_response": ICPProtocol(
            id="icp_reflex_response",
            name="Reflex → Response",
            description="Fast pattern-matched responses bypassing LLM",
            source_component="ReflexEngine",
            target_component="ResponseHandler",
            message_format="text",
            bidirectional=False,
            enabled=True
        ),
        "discord_to_memory": ICPProtocol(
            id="icp_discord_memory",
            name="Discord ↔ Memory Sharding",
            description="Store and retrieve Discord conversation context",
            source_component="AuroraDiscordBot",
            target_component="MemoryShardingSystem",
            message_format="json",
            bidirectional=True,
            enabled=True
        ),
        "learning_to_faiss": ICPProtocol(
            id="icp_learning_faiss",
            name="Learning → FAISS",
            description="Index new learnings in vector database",
            source_component="SelfLearningEngine",
            target_component="FAISSIndexManager",
            message_format="binary",
            bidirectional=False,
            enabled=True
        ),
    }

    def __init__(self, agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro"):
        """Initialize ICP manager."""
        self.agent = agent
        self.protocols: Dict[str, ICPProtocol] = {}
        self._load_protocols()

    def _load_protocols(self) -> None:
        """Load ICP protocols."""
        for key, protocol in self.DEFAULT_PROTOCOLS.items():
            self.protocols[protocol.id] = protocol

    def enable_protocol(self, protocol_id: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Enable an ICP protocol."""
        if protocol_id not in self.protocols:
            return False, f"Protocol '{protocol_id}' not found"

        self.protocols[protocol_id].enabled = True
        return True, f"Protocol '{self.protocols[protocol_id].name}' enabled"

    def disable_protocol(self, protocol_id: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Disable an ICP protocol."""
        if protocol_id not in self.protocols:
            return False, f"Protocol '{protocol_id}' not found"

        self.protocols[protocol_id].enabled = False
        return True, f"Protocol '{self.protocols[protocol_id].name}' disabled"

    def test_protocol(self, protocol_id: str) -> IntegrationTestResult:
        """Test an ICP protocol by sending a ping message."""
        if protocol_id not in self.protocols:
            return IntegrationTestResult(
                integration_name=protocol_id,
                success=False,
                latency_ms=0,
                error="Protocol not found"
            )

        protocol = self.protocols[protocol_id]
        start_time = datetime.now()

        # Simulate protocol test based on components
        try:
            # Check source component exists
            source_exists = hasattr(self.agent, protocol.source_component.lower().replace(" ", "_"))
            # Check target component exists
            target_exists = hasattr(self.agent, protocol.target_component.lower().replace(" ", "_"))

            latency = (datetime.now() - start_time).total_seconds() * 1000
            protocol.latency_ms = latency
            protocol.last_message_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()

            return IntegrationTestResult(
                integration_name=protocol.name,
                success=True,
                latency_ms=latency,
                response_sample=f"Source: {'OK' if source_exists else 'MISSING'}, Target: {'OK' if target_exists else 'MISSING'}"
            )
        except Exception as e:
            return IntegrationTestResult(
                integration_name=protocol.name,
                success=False,
                latency_ms=0,
                error=str(e)
            )

    def display_protocols(self) -> None:
        """Display all ICP protocols with status."""
        print("\n" + "=" * 70)
        print("  INTER-COMPONENT PROTOCOLS (ICP)")
        print("=" * 70)

        for protocol in self.protocols.values():
            status = "✓ ENABLED" if protocol.enabled else "○ DISABLED"
            direction = "↔" if protocol.bidirectional else "→"
            latency_str = f"{protocol.latency_ms:.1f}ms" if protocol.latency_ms else "N/A"

            print(f"\n  {status} {protocol.name}")
            print(f"    ID: {protocol.id}")
            print(f"    Route: {protocol.source_component} {direction} {protocol.target_component}")
            print(f"    Format: {protocol.message_format} | Latency: {latency_str}")

        print("\n" + "=" * 70)
        print("  Commands: enable <id>, disable <id>, test <id>, test-all, back")
        print("=" * 70)


class RLSPolicyManager:
    """
    Manages Row-Level Security policies for data access control.

    # BEGIN META: RLSPolicyManager
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Manage RLS policies for Supabase tables
    # SRS: ["SRS-RLS-001", "SRS-SECURITY-001"]
    # Tags: ["rls", "security", "policy", "supabase"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    DEFAULT_POLICIES = {
        "uso_balances_owner": RLSPolicy(
            id="rls_uso_balances_owner",
            table_name="uso_balances",
            operation="ALL",
            policy_name="Users can only access their own balances",
            check_expression="auth.uid() = user_id",
            enabled=True
        ),
        "agent_sessions_owner": RLSPolicy(
            id="rls_agent_sessions_owner",
            table_name="agent_sessions",
            operation="SELECT",
            policy_name="Users can view their own sessions",
            check_expression="auth.uid() = user_id",
            enabled=True
        ),
        "brotherhood_stats_public": RLSPolicy(
            id="rls_brotherhood_stats_public",
            table_name="brotherhood_stats",
            operation="SELECT",
            policy_name="Leaderboard is publicly readable",
            check_expression="true",
            enabled=True
        ),
        "authority_decisions_audit": RLSPolicy(
            id="rls_authority_decisions_audit",
            table_name="authority_decisions",
            operation="SELECT",
            policy_name="Authenticated users can view audit trail",
            check_expression="auth.role() = 'authenticated'",
            enabled=True
        ),
        "memories_owner": RLSPolicy(
            id="rls_memories_owner",
            table_name="agent_memories",
            operation="ALL",
            policy_name="Users can only access their own memories",
            check_expression="auth.uid() = user_id",
            enabled=True
        ),
        "learnings_owner": RLSPolicy(
            id="rls_learnings_owner",
            table_name="domain_learnings",
            operation="ALL",
            policy_name="Users can only access their own learnings",
            check_expression="auth.uid() = user_id",
            enabled=True
        ),
    }

    def __init__(self, agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro"):
        """Initialize RLS policy manager."""
        self.agent = agent
        self.policies: Dict[str, RLSPolicy] = {}
        self._load_policies()

    def _load_policies(self) -> None:
        """Load RLS policies."""
        for key, policy in self.DEFAULT_POLICIES.items():
            self.policies[policy.id] = policy

    def enable_policy(self, policy_id: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Enable an RLS policy."""
        if policy_id not in self.policies:
            return False, f"Policy '{policy_id}' not found"

        self.policies[policy_id].enabled = True
        return True, f"Policy '{self.policies[policy_id].policy_name}' enabled"

    def disable_policy(self, policy_id: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
        """Disable an RLS policy."""
        if policy_id not in self.policies:
            return False, f"Policy '{policy_id}' not found"

        self.policies[policy_id].enabled = False
        return True, f"Policy '{self.policies[policy_id].policy_name}' disabled"

    def generate_sql(self, policy_id: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Generate SQL statement for policy."""
        if policy_id not in self.policies:
            return None

        policy = self.policies[policy_id]
        return f"""
-- RLS Policy: {policy.policy_name}
ALTER TABLE {policy.table_name} ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "{policy.policy_name}"
ON {policy.table_name}
FOR {policy.operation}
USING ({policy.check_expression});
"""

    def display_policies(self) -> None:
        """Display all RLS policies with status."""
        print("\n" + "=" * 70)
        print("  ROW-LEVEL SECURITY POLICIES")
        print("=" * 70)

        for policy in self.policies.values():
            status = "✓ ENABLED" if policy.enabled else "○ DISABLED"

            print(f"\n  {status} {policy.policy_name}")
            print(f"    ID: {policy.id}")
            print(f"    Table: {policy.table_name} | Operation: {policy.operation}")
            print(f"    Check: {policy.check_expression}")

        print("\n" + "=" * 70)
        print("  Commands: enable <id>, disable <id>, sql <id>, back")
        print("=" * 70)


class LiveIntegrationTester:
    """
    Tests all integrations in one step with live validation.

    # BEGIN META: LiveIntegrationTester
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Live testing of all agent integrations
    # SRS: ["SRS-INTEGRATION-001", "SRS-VALIDATION-001"]
    # Tags: ["integration", "testing", "live", "validation"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    def __init__(self, agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro"):
        """Initialize live tester."""
        self.agent = agent
        self.results: List[IntegrationTestResult] = []

    async def test_all_integrations(self) -> List[IntegrationTestResult]:
        """Test all integrations in one pass."""
        self.results = []

        print("\n" + "=" * 70)
        print("  LIVE INTEGRATION TEST")
        print("=" * 70)
        print("  Testing all integrations...")
        print("")

        # Test OpenAI / LLM
        await self._test_llm()

        # Test Memory Systems
        await self._test_memory_stm()
        await self._test_memory_ltm()

        # Test Learning Engine
        await self._test_learning()

        # Test Council System
        await self._test_council()

        # Test Gamification
        await self._test_gamification()

        # Test Professor Network
        await self._test_professors()

        # Test Audit Trail
        await self._test_audit()

        # Test External Services (if configured)
        await self._test_supabase()
        await self._test_posthog()
        await self._test_datadog()

        # Print summary
        self._print_summary()

        return self.results

    async def _test_llm(self) -> None:
        """Test LLM connectivity."""
        print("  [1/11] Testing LLM...", end=" ", flush=True)
        start = datetime.now()

        try:
            # Check for OpenAI API key
            api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY")
            if not api_key:
                self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                    integration_name="LLM (OpenAI)",
                    success=False,
                    latency_ms=0,
                    error="OPENAI_API_KEY not set"
                ))
                print("✗ SKIP (no key)")
                return

            # Try a simple completion
            import openai
            client = openai.OpenAI(api_key=api_key)
            response = client.chat.completions.create(
                model="gpt-4o-mini",
                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "ping"}],
                max_tokens=5
            )

            latency = (datetime.now() - start).total_seconds() * 1000
            self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                integration_name="LLM (OpenAI)",
                success=True,
                latency_ms=latency,
                response_sample=response.choices[0].message.content[:50] if response.choices else None
            ))
            print(f"✓ OK ({latency:.0f}ms)")
        except Exception as e:
            self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                integration_name="LLM (OpenAI)",
                success=False,
                latency_ms=0,
                error=str(e)[:100]
            ))
            print(f"✗ ERROR: {str(e)[:30]}")

    async def _test_memory_stm(self) -> None:
        """Test STM buffer."""
        print("  [2/11] Testing STM...", end=" ", flush=True)
        start = datetime.now()

        try:
            if hasattr(self.agent, 'stm') and self.agent.stm:
                # Test inject and retrieve
                test_entry = STMEntry(
                    content="Integration test entry",
                    emotion="neutral",
                    tags=["test"],
                    source="live_test"
                )
                self.agent.stm.inject(test_entry)
                recent = self.agent.stm.get_recent(limit=1)

                latency = (datetime.now() - start).total_seconds() * 1000
                self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                    integration_name="STM Buffer",
                    success=len(recent) > 0,
                    latency_ms=latency,
                    response_sample=f"{len(recent)} entries retrieved"
                ))
                print(f"✓ OK ({latency:.0f}ms)")
            else:
                self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                    integration_name="STM Buffer",
                    success=False,
                    latency_ms=0,
                    error="STM not initialized"
                ))
                print("✗ NOT INIT")
        except Exception as e:
            self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                integration_name="STM Buffer",
                success=False,
                latency_ms=0,
                error=str(e)[:100]
            ))
            print(f"✗ ERROR")

    async def _test_memory_ltm(self) -> None:
        """Test LTM storage."""
        print("  [3/11] Testing LTM...", end=" ", flush=True)
        start = datetime.now()

        try:
            if hasattr(self.agent, 'ltm') and self.agent.ltm:
                # Get domain stats
                stats = self.agent.ltm.get_domain_stats() if hasattr(self.agent.ltm, 'get_domain_stats') else {}

                latency = (datetime.now() - start).total_seconds() * 1000
                self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                    integration_name="LTM Storage",
                    success=True,
                    latency_ms=latency,
                    response_sample=f"{len(stats)} domains"
                ))
                print(f"✓ OK ({latency:.0f}ms)")
            else:
                self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                    integration_name="LTM Storage",
                    success=False,
                    latency_ms=0,
                    error="LTM not initialized"
                ))
                print("✗ NOT INIT")
        except Exception as e:
            self.results.append(IntegrationTestResult(
                integration_name="LTM Storage",
                success=False,
                latency_ms=0,
                error=str(e)[:100]
            ))
            print(f"✗ ERROR")

    async def _test_audit(self) -> None:
        """Test audit trail."""
        print("  [8/11] Testing Audit...", end=" ", flush=True)

        try:
            if hasattr(self.agent, 'audit_trail') and self.agent.audit_trail:

                ))

            ))
