                self._init_error = "datadog library not installed (pip install datadog)"
            except Exception as e:
                self._init_error = f"Datadog init error: {str(e)[:100]}"
        elif config.datadog_enabled and not config.datadog_api_key:
            self._init_error = "DD_API_KEY environment variable not set"

    @property
    def is_connected(self) -> bool:
        return self._connected and (self._statsd is not None or self._api is not None)

    @property
    def init_error(self) -> Optional[str]:
        return self._init_error

    def send_metric_via_api(self, name: str, value: float, tags: List[str] = None) -> bool:
        """Send metric via Datadog API (fallback when agent unavailable)."""
        if not self._api or not self.config.datadog_api_key:
            return False
        try:
            import time
            metric_data = {
                "metric": name,
                "points": [(time.time(), value)],
                "type": "gauge",
                "tags": tags or [],
            }
            self._api.Metric.send(metrics=[metric_data])
            return True
        except Exception:
            return False

    def send_metric(self, name: str, value: float, tags: List[str] = None, metric_type: str = "gauge") -> bool:
        """Send metric to Datadog."""
        if not self.is_connected:
            return False

        try:
            tags = tags or []
            if metric_type == "gauge":
                self._statsd.gauge(name, value, tags=tags)
            elif metric_type == "increment":
                self._statsd.increment(name, value, tags=tags)
            elif metric_type == "histogram":
                self._statsd.histogram(name, value, tags=tags)
            elif metric_type == "timing":
                self._statsd.timing(name, value, tags=tags)
            return True
        except Exception:
            return False

    def send_decision_metrics(self, metrics: TelemetryMetrics, tags: List[str] = None) -> None:
        """Send all 5 decision metrics to Datadog."""
        prefix = self.METRIC_PREFIXES["decision"]
        tags = tags or []
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.count", metrics.decision_count, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.confidence_avg", metrics.decision_confidence_avg, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.latency_ms", metrics.decision_latency_ms, tags, "timing")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.reversal_rate", metrics.decision_reversal_rate, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.quality_score", metrics.decision_quality_score, tags, "gauge")

    def send_trace_metrics(self, metrics: TelemetryMetrics, tags: List[str] = None) -> None:
        """Send all 5 trace/APM metrics to Datadog."""
        prefix = self.METRIC_PREFIXES["trace"]
        tags = tags or []
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.span_count", metrics.trace_span_count, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.error_rate", metrics.trace_error_rate, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.p50_latency_ms", metrics.trace_p50_latency_ms, tags, "histogram")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.p95_latency_ms", metrics.trace_p95_latency_ms, tags, "histogram")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.throughput_rps", metrics.trace_throughput_rps, tags, "gauge")

    def send_llm_metrics(self, metrics: TelemetryMetrics, tags: List[str] = None) -> None:
        """Send all 5 LLM behavior metrics to Datadog."""
        prefix = self.METRIC_PREFIXES["llm"]
        tags = tags or []
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.call_count", metrics.llm_call_count, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.token_usage", metrics.llm_token_usage_total, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.response_time_ms", metrics.llm_avg_response_time_ms, tags, "timing")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.hallucination_score", metrics.llm_hallucination_score, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.coherence_score", metrics.llm_coherence_score, tags, "gauge")

    def send_user_metrics(self, metrics: TelemetryMetrics, tags: List[str] = None) -> None:
        """Send all 5 user context metrics to Datadog."""
        prefix = self.METRIC_PREFIXES["user"]
        tags = tags or []
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.satisfaction", metrics.user_satisfaction_score, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.engagement_rate", metrics.user_engagement_rate, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.sentiment", metrics.user_response_sentiment, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.context_relevance", metrics.user_context_relevance, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.session_depth", metrics.user_session_depth, tags, "gauge")

    def send_outcome_metrics(self, metrics: TelemetryMetrics, tags: List[str] = None) -> None:
        """Send all 5 outcome metrics to Datadog."""
        prefix = self.METRIC_PREFIXES["outcome"]
        tags = tags or []
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.good_count", metrics.outcome_good_count, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.bad_count", metrics.outcome_bad_count, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.neutral_count", metrics.outcome_neutral_count, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.success_rate", metrics.outcome_success_rate, tags, "gauge")
        self.send_metric(f"{prefix}.impact_score", metrics.outcome_impact_score, tags, "gauge")

    def send_all_metrics(self, metrics: TelemetryMetrics, agent_name: str = "") -> None:
        """Send all 25 telemetry metrics to Datadog."""
        tags = [f"agent:{agent_name}"] if agent_name else []
        self.send_decision_metrics(metrics, tags)
        self.send_trace_metrics(metrics, tags)
        self.send_llm_metrics(metrics, tags)
        self.send_user_metrics(metrics, tags)
        self.send_outcome_metrics(metrics, tags)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 117.5: POSTHOG TELEMETRY ADAPTER
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class PostHogTelemetryAdapter:
    """
    PostHog adapter for product analytics and user behavior tracking.

    # BEGIN META: PostHogTelemetryAdapter
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Send telemetry to PostHog for product analytics
    # SRS: ["SRS-TELEMETRY-001", "SRS-POSTHOG-001"]
    # Tags: ["posthog", "telemetry", "analytics", "behavior"]
    # Categories: ["Telemetry", "Analytics"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    EVENT_CATEGORIES = {
        "decision": "Agent Decision",
        "trace": "Agent Trace",
        "llm": "LLM Interaction",
        "user": "User Interaction",
        "outcome": "Outcome Recorded",
        "progression": "Progression Update",
        "skill": "Skill Update",
        "xp": "XP Earned",
        "tp": "TP Earned",
    }

    def __init__(self, config: TelemetryConfig):
        """Initialize PostHog telemetry adapter."""
        self.config = config
        self._client = None
        self._connected = False

        if config.posthog_enabled and config.posthog_api_key:
            try:
                import posthog
                posthog.project_api_key = config.posthog_api_key
                posthog.host = config.posthog_host
                self._client = posthog
                self._connected = True
            except Exception:
                pass

    @property
    def is_connected(self) -> bool:
        return self._connected and self._client is not None

    def capture(self, event_name: str, distinct_id: str, properties: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> bool:
        """Capture event in PostHog."""
        if not self.is_connected:
            return False

        try:
            self._client.capture(
                distinct_id=distinct_id,
                event=event_name,
                properties=properties or {}
            )
            return True
        except Exception:
            return False

    def identify(self, distinct_id: str, properties: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> bool:
        """Identify user/agent in PostHog."""
        if not self.is_connected:
            return False

        try:
            self._client.identify(
                distinct_id=distinct_id,
                properties=properties or {}
            )
            return True
        except Exception:
            return False

    def capture_decision(self, agent_id: str, decision: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """Capture decision event."""
        return self.capture(
            self.EVENT_CATEGORIES["decision"],
            agent_id,
            {
                "decision_type": decision.get("type", "unknown"),
                "confidence": decision.get("confidence", 0),
                "latency_ms": decision.get("latency_ms", 0),
                "outcome": decision.get("outcome", "neutral"),
            }
        )

    def capture_llm_interaction(self, agent_id: str, llm_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """Capture LLM interaction event."""
        return self.capture(
            self.EVENT_CATEGORIES["llm"],
            agent_id,
            {
                "model": llm_data.get("model", "unknown"),
                "tokens_used": llm_data.get("tokens", 0),
                "response_time_ms": llm_data.get("response_time_ms", 0),
                "coherence_score": llm_data.get("coherence", 0.5),
            }
        )

    def capture_outcome(self, agent_id: str, outcome: str, score: float, context: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> bool:
        """Capture outcome event."""
        return self.capture(
            self.EVENT_CATEGORIES["outcome"],
            agent_id,
            {
                "outcome": outcome,
                "score": score,
                "context": context or {},
            }
        )

    def capture_progression(self, agent_id: str, xp: int, tp: int, rank: str, skills: Dict[str, float]) -> bool:
        """Capture progression update event."""
        return self.capture(
            self.EVENT_CATEGORIES["progression"],
            agent_id,
            {
                "xp_total": xp,
                "tp_total": tp,
                "rank": rank,
                "top_skills": dict(sorted(skills.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]),
            }
        )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 117.6: UNIFIED TELEMETRY STACK
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class UnifiedTelemetryStack:
    """
    Unified telemetry stack combining Supabase + Datadog + PostHog.

    # BEGIN META: UnifiedTelemetryStack
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Unified interface for all telemetry backends
    # SRS: ["SRS-TELEMETRY-001", "SRS-UNIFIED-001"]
    # Tags: ["telemetry", "unified", "stack", "supabase", "datadog", "posthog"]
    # Categories: ["Telemetry"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    def __init__(self, agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro"):
        """Initialize unified telemetry stack."""
        self.agent = agent
        self.config = TelemetryConfig.from_env()
        self.metrics = TelemetryMetrics()

        # Initialize adapters
        self.supabase = SupabaseTelemetryAdapter(self.config)
        self.datadog = DatadogTelemetryAdapter(self.config)
        self.posthog = PostHogTelemetryAdapter(self.config)

        # Event buffer for batching
        self._event_buffer: List[TelemetryEvent] = []
        self._last_flush: float = time.time()

        # Context chain for linking
        self._context_chain: List[str] = []
        self._current_trace_id: Optional[str] = None

    def _create_event(self, event_type: str, category: str, action: str, value: float = 0.0,
                      properties: Dict[str, Any] = None, outcome: str = "neutral") -> TelemetryEvent:
        """Create a telemetry event with full context."""
        return TelemetryEvent(
            event_type=event_type,
            agent_id=self.agent.agent_id,
            agent_name=self.agent.agent_name,
            session_id=self.agent.agent_id,
            category=category,
            action=action,
            value=value,
            properties=properties or {},
            outcome=outcome,
            parent_event_id=self._context_chain[-1] if self._context_chain else None,
            context_chain=self._context_chain.copy(),
        )

    def track_decision(self, decision_type: str, confidence: float, latency_ms: float,
                       outcome: str = "neutral", properties: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> str:
        """
        Track a decision event (D1-D5 metrics).

        Returns event_id for context linking.
        """
        event = self._create_event(
            event_type="decision",
            category="decision",
            action=decision_type,
            value=confidence,
            properties={
                "confidence": confidence,
                "latency_ms": latency_ms,
                **(properties or {}),
            },
            outcome=outcome,
        )

        # Update metrics
        self.metrics.decision_count += 1
        self.metrics.decision_confidence_avg = (
            (self.metrics.decision_confidence_avg * (self.metrics.decision_count - 1) + confidence)
            / self.metrics.decision_count
        )
        self.metrics.decision_latency_ms = latency_ms

        # Push to all backends
        self._push_event(event, "decisions")
        self._context_chain.append(event.event_id)

        return event.event_id

    def track_trace(self, span_name: str, latency_ms: float, error: bool = False,
                    properties: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> str:
        """
        Track a trace span event (T1-T5 metrics).

        Returns event_id for context linking.
        """
        outcome = "bad" if error else "good"
        event = self._create_event(
            event_type="trace",
            category="trace",
            action=span_name,
            value=latency_ms,
            properties={
                "latency_ms": latency_ms,
                "error": error,
                **(properties or {}),
            },
            outcome=outcome,
        )

        # Update metrics
        self.metrics.trace_span_count += 1
        if error:
            self.metrics.trace_error_rate = (
                (self.metrics.trace_error_rate * (self.metrics.trace_span_count - 1) + 1)
                / self.metrics.trace_span_count
            )

        self._push_event(event, "traces")
        return event.event_id

    def track_llm_call(self, model: str, tokens: int, response_time_ms: float,
                       coherence_score: float = 0.5, hallucination_score: float = 0.0,
                       properties: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> str:
        """
        Track an LLM call event (L1-L5 metrics).

        Returns event_id for context linking.
        """
        event = self._create_event(
            event_type="llm",
            category="llm",
            action="llm_call",
            value=response_time_ms,
            properties={
                "model": model,
                "tokens": tokens,
                "response_time_ms": response_time_ms,
                "coherence_score": coherence_score,
                "hallucination_score": hallucination_score,
                **(properties or {}),
            },
        )

        # Update metrics
        self.metrics.llm_call_count += 1
        self.metrics.llm_token_usage_total += tokens
        self.metrics.llm_avg_response_time_ms = (
            (self.metrics.llm_avg_response_time_ms * (self.metrics.llm_call_count - 1) + response_time_ms)
            / self.metrics.llm_call_count
        )
        self.metrics.llm_coherence_score = coherence_score
        self.metrics.llm_hallucination_score = hallucination_score

        self._push_event(event, "llm")
        self.posthog.capture_llm_interaction(self.agent.agent_id, event.properties)

        return event.event_id

    def track_user_response(self, satisfaction: float, engagement: float, sentiment: float,
                            context_relevance: float, properties: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> str:
        """
        Track user response/context event (U1-U5 metrics).

        Returns event_id for context linking.
        """
        event = self._create_event(
            event_type="user",
            category="user",
            action="user_response",
            value=satisfaction,
            properties={
                "satisfaction": satisfaction,
                "engagement": engagement,
                "sentiment": sentiment,
                "context_relevance": context_relevance,
                **(properties or {}),
            },
        )

        # Update metrics
        self.metrics.user_satisfaction_score = satisfaction
        self.metrics.user_engagement_rate = engagement
        self.metrics.user_response_sentiment = sentiment
        self.metrics.user_context_relevance = context_relevance
        self.metrics.user_session_depth += 1

        self._push_event(event, "context")
        return event.event_id

    def track_outcome(self, outcome: str, score: float, properties: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> str:
        """
        Track an outcome event (O1-O5 metrics).

        outcome: "good", "bad", or "neutral"

        Returns event_id for context linking.
        """
        event = self._create_event(
            event_type="outcome",
            category="outcome",
            action=f"outcome_{outcome}",
            value=score,
            properties=properties or {},
            outcome=outcome,
        )
        event.outcome_score = score

        # Update metrics
        if outcome == "good":
            self.metrics.outcome_good_count += 1
        elif outcome == "bad":
            self.metrics.outcome_bad_count += 1
        else:
            self.metrics.outcome_neutral_count += 1

        total_outcomes = (
            self.metrics.outcome_good_count +
            self.metrics.outcome_bad_count +
            self.metrics.outcome_neutral_count
        )
        if total_outcomes > 0:
            self.metrics.outcome_success_rate = self.metrics.outcome_good_count / total_outcomes

        self.metrics.outcome_impact_score += score

        self._push_event(event, "outcomes")
        self.posthog.capture_outcome(self.agent.agent_id, outcome, score, properties)

        return event.event_id

    def _push_event(self, event: TelemetryEvent, table: str = "events") -> None:
        """Push event to all backends."""
        # Add to buffer
        self._event_buffer.append(event)

        # Push to Supabase
        self.supabase.push_event(event, table)

        # Check if we should flush to Datadog
        if len(self._event_buffer) >= self.config.batch_size:
            self._flush_to_datadog()

    def _flush_to_datadog(self) -> None:
        """Flush accumulated metrics to Datadog."""
        if not self.datadog.is_connected:
            return

        self.datadog.send_all_metrics(self.metrics, self.agent.agent_name)
        self._last_flush = time.time()

    def get_metrics_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get summary of all 25 telemetry metrics."""
        return {
            "decision": {
                "D1_count": self.metrics.decision_count,
                "D2_confidence_avg": self.metrics.decision_confidence_avg,
                "D3_latency_ms": self.metrics.decision_latency_ms,
                "D4_reversal_rate": self.metrics.decision_reversal_rate,
                "D5_quality_score": self.metrics.decision_quality_score,
            },
            "trace": {
                "T1_span_count": self.metrics.trace_span_count,
                "T2_error_rate": self.metrics.trace_error_rate,
                "T3_p50_latency_ms": self.metrics.trace_p50_latency_ms,
                "T4_p95_latency_ms": self.metrics.trace_p95_latency_ms,
                "T5_throughput_rps": self.metrics.trace_throughput_rps,
            },
            "llm": {
                "L1_call_count": self.metrics.llm_call_count,
                "L2_token_usage": self.metrics.llm_token_usage_total,
                "L3_avg_response_ms": self.metrics.llm_avg_response_time_ms,
                "L4_hallucination": self.metrics.llm_hallucination_score,
                "L5_coherence": self.metrics.llm_coherence_score,
            },
            "user": {
                "U1_satisfaction": self.metrics.user_satisfaction_score,
                "U2_engagement": self.metrics.user_engagement_rate,
                "U3_sentiment": self.metrics.user_response_sentiment,
                "U4_context_relevance": self.metrics.user_context_relevance,
                "U5_session_depth": self.metrics.user_session_depth,
            },
            "outcome": {
                "O1_good_count": self.metrics.outcome_good_count,
                "O2_bad_count": self.metrics.outcome_bad_count,
                "O3_neutral_count": self.metrics.outcome_neutral_count,
                "O4_success_rate": self.metrics.outcome_success_rate,
                "O5_impact_score": self.metrics.outcome_impact_score,
            },
        }


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 117.7: TELEMETRY → PROGRESSION BRIDGE (XP/TP/Skills)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TelemetryProgressionBridge:
    """
    Bridge between telemetry metrics and XP/TP/Skill progression.

    # BEGIN META: TelemetryProgressionBridge
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Convert telemetry outcomes into XP/TP/Skill rewards
    # SRS: ["SRS-TELEMETRY-001", "SRS-PROGRESSION-001", "SRS-GAMIFY-001"]
    # Tags: ["telemetry", "progression", "xp", "tp", "skills"]
    # Categories: ["Telemetry", "Gamification"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    # XP multipliers based on telemetry metrics
    XP_MULTIPLIERS = {
        "good_outcome": 1.5,
        "bad_outcome": 0.5,
        "high_confidence": 1.2,
        "low_latency": 1.1,
        "high_satisfaction": 1.3,
        "high_coherence": 1.2,
    }

    # TP multipliers
    TP_MULTIPLIERS = {
        "good_outcome": 2.0,
        "community_impact": 1.5,
        "streak_bonus": 1.25,
    }

    # Skill mappings from telemetry categories
    SKILL_MAPPINGS = {
        "decision": ["reasoning", "analysis", "judgment"],
        "trace": ["performance", "efficiency", "reliability"],
        "llm": ["communication", "language", "synthesis"],
        "user": ["empathy", "engagement", "rapport"],
        "outcome": ["effectiveness", "impact", "results"],
    }

    def __init__(self, agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro", telemetry: UnifiedTelemetryStack):
        """Initialize telemetry-progression bridge."""
        self.agent = agent
        self.telemetry = telemetry
        self._xp_from_telemetry: int = 0
        self._tp_from_telemetry: int = 0
        self._skill_gains: Dict[str, float] = {}

    def calculate_xp_from_outcome(self, outcome: str, base_xp: int = 10) -> int:
        """Calculate XP from outcome telemetry."""
        multiplier = 1.0

        if outcome == "good":
            multiplier *= self.XP_MULTIPLIERS["good_outcome"]
        elif outcome == "bad":
            multiplier *= self.XP_MULTIPLIERS["bad_outcome"]

        # Apply decision confidence bonus
        if self.telemetry.metrics.decision_confidence_avg > 0.7:
            multiplier *= self.XP_MULTIPLIERS["high_confidence"]

        # Apply latency bonus
        if self.telemetry.metrics.decision_latency_ms < 100:
            multiplier *= self.XP_MULTIPLIERS["low_latency"]

        # Apply satisfaction bonus
        if self.telemetry.metrics.user_satisfaction_score > 0.7:
            multiplier *= self.XP_MULTIPLIERS["high_satisfaction"]

        xp = int(base_xp * multiplier)
        self._xp_from_telemetry += xp
        return xp

    def calculate_tp_from_outcome(self, outcome: str, base_tp: int = 5) -> int:
        """Calculate TP from outcome telemetry."""
        multiplier = 1.0

        if outcome == "good":
            multiplier *= self.TP_MULTIPLIERS["good_outcome"]

        # Community impact bonus (based on session depth)
        if self.telemetry.metrics.user_session_depth > 5:
            multiplier *= self.TP_MULTIPLIERS["community_impact"]

        tp = int(base_tp * multiplier)
        self._tp_from_telemetry += tp
        return tp

    def calculate_skill_gain(self, category: str, performance_score: float) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Calculate skill gains from telemetry category performance."""
        skills = self.SKILL_MAPPINGS.get(category, [])
        gains = {}

        for skill in skills:
            # Base gain scaled by performance
            base_gain = 0.1 * performance_score
            gains[skill] = min(base_gain, 0.25)  # Cap at 0.25 per interaction

            # Track cumulative gains
            if skill not in self._skill_gains:
                self._skill_gains[skill] = 0.0
            self._skill_gains[skill] += gains[skill]

        return gains

    def process_telemetry_for_progression(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Process all telemetry metrics and return progression rewards.

        This should be called periodically (e.g., after each interaction).
        """
        metrics = self.telemetry.metrics
        results = {
            "xp_earned": 0,
            "tp_earned": 0,
            "skills_gained": {},
        }

        # Calculate XP from success rate
        if metrics.outcome_success_rate > 0.6:
            results["xp_earned"] += self.calculate_xp_from_outcome("good", 15)
        elif metrics.outcome_success_rate < 0.4:
            results["xp_earned"] += self.calculate_xp_from_outcome("bad", 5)
        else:
            results["xp_earned"] += self.calculate_xp_from_outcome("neutral", 10)

        # Calculate TP from engagement
        if metrics.user_engagement_rate > 0.5:
            results["tp_earned"] += self.calculate_tp_from_outcome("good", 8)

        # Calculate skill gains from each category
        category_scores = {
            "decision": metrics.decision_quality_score,
            "trace": 1.0 - metrics.trace_error_rate,
            "llm": metrics.llm_coherence_score,
            "user": metrics.user_satisfaction_score,
            "outcome": metrics.outcome_success_rate,
        }

        for category, score in category_scores.items():
            gains = self.calculate_skill_gain(category, score)
            for skill, gain in gains.items():
                if skill not in results["skills_gained"]:
                    results["skills_gained"][skill] = 0.0
                results["skills_gained"][skill] += gain

        return results

    def get_progression_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get summary of progression from telemetry."""
        return {
            "total_xp_from_telemetry": self._xp_from_telemetry,
            "total_tp_from_telemetry": self._tp_from_telemetry,
            "skill_gains": self._skill_gains.copy(),
            "telemetry_metrics": self.telemetry.get_metrics_summary(),
        }


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 117.8: CONTEXT LINKING ENGINE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class ContextLinkingEngine:
    """
    Deep context linking for telemetry event chains.

    # BEGIN META: ContextLinkingEngine
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Link telemetry events into causal chains for deep analysis
    # SRS: ["SRS-CONTEXT-001", "SRS-LINKING-001"]
    # Tags: ["context", "linking", "causal", "chain"]
    # Categories: ["Telemetry", "Analysis"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    def __init__(self, telemetry: UnifiedTelemetryStack):
        """Initialize context linking engine."""
        self.telemetry = telemetry
        self.event_graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}  # parent -> [children]
        self.event_metadata: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
        self.chains: List[List[str]] = []

    def link_events(self, parent_id: str, child_id: str) -> None:
        """Link two events in the context graph."""
        if parent_id not in self.event_graph:
            self.event_graph[parent_id] = []
        self.event_graph[parent_id].append(child_id)

    def get_event_chain(self, event_id: str, direction: str = "down") -> List[str]:
        """Get the chain of events starting from event_id."""
        chain = [event_id]

        if direction == "down":
            # Get all descendants
            children = self.event_graph.get(event_id, [])
            for child in children:
                chain.extend(self.get_event_chain(child, "down"))
        elif direction == "up":
            # Get all ancestors
            for parent, children in self.event_graph.items():
                if event_id in children:
                    chain = self.get_event_chain(parent, "up") + chain
                    break

        return chain

    def analyze_chain_outcome(self, chain: List[str]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Analyze the outcome pattern of an event chain."""
        outcomes = []
        categories = []

        for event_id in chain:
            metadata = self.event_metadata.get(event_id, {})
            outcomes.append(metadata.get("outcome", "neutral"))
            categories.append(metadata.get("category", "unknown"))

        good_count = outcomes.count("good")
        bad_count = outcomes.count("bad")
        total = len(outcomes)

        return {
            "chain_length": total,
            "outcomes": outcomes,
            "categories": categories,
            "success_rate": good_count / total if total > 0 else 0,
            "failure_rate": bad_count / total if total > 0 else 0,
            "dominant_category": max(set(categories), key=categories.count) if categories else "unknown",
        }

    def find_failure_patterns(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Find patterns that lead to bad outcomes."""
        patterns = []

        for chain in self.chains:
            analysis = self.analyze_chain_outcome(chain)
            if analysis["failure_rate"] > 0.5:
                patterns.append({
                    "chain": chain,
                    "analysis": analysis,
                    "root_cause": chain[0] if chain else None,
                })

        return patterns

    def get_context_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get summary of context linking."""
        return {
            "total_events": len(self.event_metadata),
            "total_chains": len(self.chains),
            "graph_edges": sum(len(children) for children in self.event_graph.values()),
            "failure_patterns": len(self.find_failure_patterns()),
        }


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 117.9: SIMULATED BRAIN DISPLAY
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class SimulatedBrainDisplay:
    """
    ASCII/Unicode visualization of agent brain state.

    # BEGIN META: SimulatedBrainDisplay
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Visual representation of agent cognitive state
    # SRS: ["SRS-DISPLAY-001", "SRS-BRAIN-001"]
    # Tags: ["display", "brain", "visualization", "ascii"]
    # Categories: ["Display", "Visualization"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    # Brain region activity indicators
    ACTIVITY_LEVELS = {
        0: " ",   # Inactive
        1: ".",   # Low
        2: "o",   # Medium
        3: "O",   # High
        4: "@",   # Peak
    }

    # Brain regions mapped to telemetry categories
    BRAIN_REGIONS = {
        "prefrontal": "decision",   # Decision making
        "temporal": "llm",          # Language processing
        "parietal": "trace",        # Spatial/performance
        "occipital": "user",        # Visual/user perception
        "limbic": "outcome",        # Emotional/outcome
    }

    def __init__(self, telemetry: UnifiedTelemetryStack):
        """Initialize brain display."""
        self.telemetry = telemetry

    def _get_activity_level(self, value: float, max_value: float = 1.0) -> int:
        """Convert value to activity level (0-4)."""
        if max_value == 0:
            return 0
        ratio = min(value / max_value, 1.0)
        return int(ratio * 4)

    def _get_region_activity(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
        """Get activity level for each brain region."""
        metrics = self.telemetry.metrics
        return {
            "prefrontal": self._get_activity_level(metrics.decision_quality_score),
            "temporal": self._get_activity_level(metrics.llm_coherence_score),
            "parietal": self._get_activity_level(1.0 - metrics.trace_error_rate),
            "occipital": self._get_activity_level(metrics.user_satisfaction_score),
            "limbic": self._get_activity_level(metrics.outcome_success_rate),
        }

    def render_brain_ascii(self) -> str:
        """Render brain state as ASCII art."""
        activity = self._get_region_activity()

        pf = self.ACTIVITY_LEVELS[activity["prefrontal"]]
        tm = self.ACTIVITY_LEVELS[activity["temporal"]]
        pr = self.ACTIVITY_LEVELS[activity["parietal"]]
        oc = self.ACTIVITY_LEVELS[activity["occipital"]]
        lb = self.ACTIVITY_LEVELS[activity["limbic"]]

        brain = f"""
    SIMULATED BRAIN STATE
    =====================

         .---.---.---.
        /  {pf} \\ {pf} /  {pf} \\
       | PREFRONTAL  |  [DECISION: {activity['prefrontal']}/4]
       |    {pf} {pf} {pf}    |
        \\  .-----.  /
         |/{tm}\\   /{pr}\\|   [TEMPORAL: {activity['temporal']}/4] [PARIETAL: {activity['parietal']}/4]
        /|{tm} {tm}| |{pr} {pr}|\\
       | | TMP | |PAR| |
       |  \\___/   \\___/  |
       |      {lb}{lb}{lb}      |  [LIMBIC: {activity['limbic']}/4]
       |    LIMBIC     |
        \\      {oc}      /   [OCCIPITAL: {activity['occipital']}/4]
         \\  {oc} {oc} {oc}  /
          \\OCCIP./
           '---'

    LEGEND: [ ]=Inactive [.]=Low [o]=Med [O]=High [@]=Peak
    """
        return brain

    def render_metrics_panel(self) -> str:
        """Render metrics panel alongside brain."""
        metrics = self.telemetry.metrics
        summary = self.telemetry.get_metrics_summary()

        panel = """
    TELEMETRY METRICS (25 VALUES)
    =============================

    DECISIONS (D1-D5)
    -----------------
    D1 Count:        {d1}
    D2 Confidence:   {d2:.2f}
    D3 Latency:      {d3:.1f}ms
    D4 Reversal:     {d4:.2f}
    D5 Quality:      {d5:.2f}

    TRACES (T1-T5)
    --------------
    T1 Spans:        {t1}
    T2 Error Rate:   {t2:.2f}
    T3 P50:          {t3:.1f}ms
    T4 P95:          {t4:.1f}ms
    T5 Throughput:   {t5:.2f}/s

    LLM (L1-L5)
    -----------
    L1 Calls:        {l1}
    L2 Tokens:       {l2}
    L3 Avg Time:     {l3:.1f}ms
    L4 Hallucinate:  {l4:.2f}
    L5 Coherence:    {l5:.2f}

    USER (U1-U5)
    ------------
    U1 Satisfaction: {u1:.2f}
    U2 Engagement:   {u2:.2f}
    U3 Sentiment:    {u3:.2f}
    U4 Relevance:    {u4:.2f}
    U5 Depth:        {u5}

    OUTCOMES (O1-O5)
    ----------------
    O1 Good:         {o1}
    O2 Bad:          {o2}
    O3 Neutral:      {o3}
    O4 Success:      {o4:.2f}
    O5 Impact:       {o5:.2f}
    """.format(
            d1=metrics.decision_count,
            d2=metrics.decision_confidence_avg,
            d3=metrics.decision_latency_ms,
            d4=metrics.decision_reversal_rate,
            d5=metrics.decision_quality_score,
            t1=metrics.trace_span_count,
            t2=metrics.trace_error_rate,
            t3=metrics.trace_p50_latency_ms,
            t4=metrics.trace_p95_latency_ms,
            t5=metrics.trace_throughput_rps,
            l1=metrics.llm_call_count,
            l2=metrics.llm_token_usage_total,
            l3=metrics.llm_avg_response_time_ms,
            l4=metrics.llm_hallucination_score,
            l5=metrics.llm_coherence_score,
            u1=metrics.user_satisfaction_score,
            u2=metrics.user_engagement_rate,
            u3=metrics.user_response_sentiment,
            u4=metrics.user_context_relevance,
            u5=metrics.user_session_depth,
            o1=metrics.outcome_good_count,
            o2=metrics.outcome_bad_count,
            o3=metrics.outcome_neutral_count,
            o4=metrics.outcome_success_rate,
            o5=metrics.outcome_impact_score,
        )
        return panel

    def display_full(self) -> None:
        """Display full brain visualization with metrics."""
        print(self.render_brain_ascii())
        print(self.render_metrics_panel())


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 117.10: TELEMETRY UI DISPLAY COMMANDS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def display_telemetry_dashboard(agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro") -> None:
    """
    Display comprehensive telemetry dashboard.

    # BEGIN META: display_telemetry_dashboard
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: UI command for telemetry dashboard
    # SRS: ["SRS-DISPLAY-001", "SRS-TELEMETRY-001"]
    # Tags: ["display", "dashboard", "telemetry"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    if not hasattr(agent, '_telemetry_stack') or not agent._telemetry_stack:
        print("\n[Telemetry] Not initialized. Enable telemetry in agent config.")
        return

    telemetry = agent._telemetry_stack

    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
    print("              UNIFIED TELEMETRY DASHBOARD")
    print("=" * 70)

    # Backend status with detailed error info
    print("\nBACKEND STATUS:")

    # Supabase
    sb_status = "[CONNECTED]" if telemetry.supabase.is_connected else "[OFFLINE]"
    sb_error = getattr(telemetry.supabase, 'init_error', None)
    print(f"  Supabase:  {sb_status}")
    if not telemetry.supabase.is_connected and sb_error:
        print(f"             └─ {sb_error}")

    # Datadog
    dd_status = "[CONNECTED]" if telemetry.datadog.is_connected else "[OFFLINE]"
    dd_error = getattr(telemetry.datadog, 'init_error', None)
    print(f"  Datadog:   {dd_status}")
    if not telemetry.datadog.is_connected:
        if dd_error:
            print(f"             └─ {dd_error}")
        else:
            print("             └─ Install: pip install datadog")

    # PostHog
    ph_status = "[CONNECTED]" if telemetry.posthog.is_connected else "[OFFLINE]"
    ph_error = getattr(telemetry.posthog, 'init_error', None)
    print(f"  PostHog:   {ph_status}")
    if not telemetry.posthog.is_connected and ph_error:
        print(f"             └─ {ph_error}")

    # Metrics summary
    summary = telemetry.get_metrics_summary()

    print("\n25 TELEMETRY METRICS:")
    print("-" * 70)

    for category, values in summary.items():
        print(f"\n  {category.upper()}:")
        for key, value in values.items():
            if isinstance(value, float):
                print(f"    {key}: {value:.3f}")
            else:
                print(f"    {key}: {value}")

    print("\n" + "=" * 70)


def display_brain_simulation(agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro") -> None:
    """
    Display simulated brain visualization.

    # BEGIN META: display_brain_simulation
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: UI command for brain simulation display
    # SRS: ["SRS-DISPLAY-001", "SRS-BRAIN-001"]
    # Tags: ["display", "brain", "simulation"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    if not hasattr(agent, '_telemetry_stack') or not agent._telemetry_stack:
        print("\n[Brain] Telemetry not initialized. Cannot render brain state.")
        return

    brain = SimulatedBrainDisplay(agent._telemetry_stack)
    brain.display_full()


def display_context_chains(agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro") -> None:
    """
    Display context linking analysis.

    # BEGIN META: display_context_chains
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: UI command for context chain analysis
    # SRS: ["SRS-CONTEXT-001", "SRS-LINKING-001"]
    # Tags: ["display", "context", "chains"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    if not hasattr(agent, '_context_linking') or not agent._context_linking:
        print("\n[Context] Context linking not initialized.")
        return

    linking = agent._context_linking
    summary = linking.get_context_summary()

    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
    print("              CONTEXT LINKING ANALYSIS")
    print("=" * 70)

    print(f"\n  Total Events Tracked:    {summary['total_events']}")
    print(f"  Total Chains:            {summary['total_chains']}")
    print(f"  Graph Edges:             {summary['graph_edges']}")
    print(f"  Failure Patterns Found:  {summary['failure_patterns']}")

    patterns = linking.find_failure_patterns()
    if patterns:
        print("\nFAILURE PATTERNS:")
        for i, pattern in enumerate(patterns[:5], 1):
            analysis = pattern["analysis"]
            print(f"\n  Pattern {i}:")
            print(f"    Chain Length:     {analysis['chain_length']}")
            print(f"    Failure Rate:     {analysis['failure_rate']:.2f}")
            print(f"    Dominant Category: {analysis['dominant_category']}")
            print(f"    Root Cause Event:  {pattern['root_cause']}")

    print("\n" + "=" * 70)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 117.11: TELEMETRY INTEGRATION METHODS FOR AGENT
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def init_telemetry_stack(agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro") -> None:
    """
    Initialize telemetry stack for agent.

    # BEGIN META: init_telemetry_stack
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Wire telemetry stack into agent
    # SRS: ["SRS-TELEMETRY-001"]
    # Tags: ["init", "telemetry", "integration"]
    # END META
    """
    try:
        # Initialize unified telemetry stack
        agent._telemetry_stack = UnifiedTelemetryStack(agent)

        # Initialize progression bridge
        agent._telemetry_progression = TelemetryProgressionBridge(agent, agent._telemetry_stack)

        # Initialize context linking
        agent._context_linking = ContextLinkingEngine(agent._telemetry_stack)

        # Initialize brain display
        agent._brain_display = SimulatedBrainDisplay(agent._telemetry_stack)

        # Identify agent in PostHog
        agent._telemetry_stack.posthog.identify(
            agent.agent_id,
            {
                "agent_name": agent.agent_name,
                "created_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            }
        )

        print(f"[Telemetry] Stack initialized with 25 metrics")

    except Exception as e:
        agent._telemetry_stack = None
        agent._telemetry_progression = None
        agent._context_linking = None
        agent._brain_display = None
        print(f"[Telemetry] Init failed: {e}")


def process_telemetry_on_interaction(
    agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro",
    user_input: str,
    response: str,
    latency_ms: float,
    outcome: str = "neutral"
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Process telemetry for an interaction and return progression rewards.

    # BEGIN META: process_telemetry_on_interaction
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Track interaction telemetry and calculate rewards
    # SRS: ["SRS-TELEMETRY-001", "SRS-PROGRESSION-001"]
    # Tags: ["telemetry", "interaction", "progression"]
    # END META
    """
    if not hasattr(agent, '_telemetry_stack') or not agent._telemetry_stack:
        return {"xp_earned": 0, "tp_earned": 0, "skills_gained": {}}

    telemetry = agent._telemetry_stack

    # Track decision
    decision_id = telemetry.track_decision(
        decision_type="response_generation",
        confidence=0.8,  # Could be derived from LLM confidence
        latency_ms=latency_ms,
        outcome=outcome,
        properties={"input_length": len(user_input), "response_length": len(response)}
    )

    # Track trace
    trace_id = telemetry.track_trace(
        span_name="interaction",
        latency_ms=latency_ms,
        error=outcome == "bad",
    )

    # Track user response (estimate from input analysis)
    user_id = telemetry.track_user_response(
        satisfaction=0.7 if outcome == "good" else 0.3,
        engagement=min(len(user_input) / 100, 1.0),
        sentiment=0.6,
        context_relevance=0.7,
    )

    # Track outcome
    outcome_id = telemetry.track_outcome(
        outcome=outcome,
        score=1.0 if outcome == "good" else (0.0 if outcome == "bad" else 0.5),
        properties={"decision_id": decision_id, "trace_id": trace_id}
    )

    # Link events in context chain
    if hasattr(agent, '_context_linking') and agent._context_linking:
        agent._context_linking.link_events(decision_id, trace_id)
        agent._context_linking.link_events(trace_id, user_id)
        agent._context_linking.link_events(user_id, outcome_id)

    # Calculate progression rewards
    if hasattr(agent, '_telemetry_progression') and agent._telemetry_progression:
        return agent._telemetry_progression.process_telemetry_for_progression()

    return {"xp_earned": 0, "tp_earned": 0, "skills_gained": {}}


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# END SECTION 117: UNIFIED TELEMETRY STACK (Supabase + Datadog + PostHog)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 117.5: RAG SYSTEM WITH DOMAIN MANAGEMENT
# SRS: SRS-RAG-001, SRS-DOMAIN-001, SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001
# Purpose: Retrieval Augmented Generation with multi-domain knowledge management
# Pattern: MIRA-style nested learning with domain-specific indexing
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

@dataclass
class KnowledgeDomain:
    """
    Knowledge domain for RAG organization.

    # BEGIN META: KnowledgeDomain
    # Date: 2026-01-18
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Define knowledge domain with metadata and retrieval config
    # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001", "SRS-DOMAIN-001"]
    # Tags: ["rag", "domain", "knowledge"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    name: str
    description: str = ""
    embedding_model: str = "text-embedding-ada-002"
    chunk_size: int = 512
    chunk_overlap: int = 50
    relevance_threshold: float = 0.7
    max_results: int = 5
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    document_count: int = 0
    total_chunks: int = 0
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class RAGDocument:
    """
    Document stored in RAG system.

    # BEGIN META: RAGDocument
    # Date: 2026-01-18
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Represent a document with chunks and metadata
    # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001"]
    # Tags: ["rag", "document"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    id: str
    domain: str
    title: str
    content: str
    source: str = "manual"
    source_url: Optional[str] = None
    chunks: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    embeddings: List[List[float]] = field(default_factory=list)
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    updated_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())


@dataclass
class RAGRetrievalResult:
    """
    Result from RAG retrieval.

    # BEGIN META: RAGRetrievalResult
    # Date: 2026-01-18
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Structured result from RAG query
    # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001"]
    # Tags: ["rag", "retrieval", "result"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    query: str
    domain: Optional[str]
    chunks: List[Dict[str, Any]]
    relevance_scores: List[float]
    context: str
    latency_ms: float
    total_searched: int


class RAGDomainManager:
    """
    Multi-domain RAG system with MIRA-style nested learning integration.

    # BEGIN META: RAGDomainManager
    # Date: 2026-01-18
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Manage knowledge domains for RAG with retrieval and indexing
    # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001", "SRS-DOMAIN-001", "SRS-KNOWLEDGE-001"]
    # Tags: ["rag", "domain", "knowledge", "retrieval"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """

    DEFAULT_DOMAINS = [
        KnowledgeDomain(name="general", description="General knowledge and conversations"),
        KnowledgeDomain(name="code", description="Code snippets, programming knowledge"),
        KnowledgeDomain(name="documentation", description="System documentation and guides"),
        KnowledgeDomain(name="user_context", description="User-specific information and preferences"),
        KnowledgeDomain(name="memory", description="Agent memory and past interactions"),
    ]

    def __init__(
        self,
        agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro",
        storage_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
    ):
        """
        Initialize RAG Domain Manager.

        # BEGIN META: RAGDomainManager.__init__
        # Date: 2026-01-18
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Initialize RAG system with domains and storage
        # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001"]
        # Tags: ["init", "rag"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        self.agent = agent
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger(__name__)
        self.storage_dir = storage_dir or Path(".nexus_rag") / (agent.agent_name if agent else "default")
        self.storage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        # Domain registry
        self.domains: Dict[str, KnowledgeDomain] = {}
        for domain in self.DEFAULT_DOMAINS:
            self.domains[domain.name] = domain

        # Document storage (in-memory + persisted)
        self.documents: Dict[str, RAGDocument] = {}

        # Vector index per domain (simple in-memory for now)
        self._domain_vectors: Dict[str, List[Tuple[str, List[float], Dict[str, Any]]]] = {
            d.name: [] for d in self.DEFAULT_DOMAINS
        }

        # Load persisted state
        self._load_state()

        # Statistics
        self._query_count = 0
        self._retrieval_latency_sum = 0.0

    def _load_state(self) -> None:
        """Load persisted RAG state."""
        state_file = self.storage_dir / "rag_state.json"
        if state_file.exists():
            try:
                with open(state_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                    state = json.load(f)
                    # Load domains
                    for d in state.get("domains", []):
                        self.domains[d["name"]] = KnowledgeDomain(**d)
                    self.logger.debug(f"RAG: Loaded {len(self.domains)} domains")
            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.warning(f"RAG: Failed to load state ({e})")

    def _save_state(self) -> None:
        """Persist RAG state."""
        state_file = self.storage_dir / "rag_state.json"
        try:
            state = {
                "domains": [asdict(d) for d in self.domains.values()],
                "document_count": len(self.documents),
                "updated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            }
            with open(state_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                json.dump(state, f, indent=2, default=str)
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.warning(f"RAG: Failed to save state ({e})")

    def create_domain(
        self,
        name: str,
        description: str = "",
        **kwargs
    ) -> KnowledgeDomain:
        """
        Create a new knowledge domain.

        # BEGIN META: RAGDomainManager.create_domain
        # Date: 2026-01-18
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Create new domain for knowledge organization
        # SRS: ["SRS-DOMAIN-001"]
        # Tags: ["domain", "create"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        domain = KnowledgeDomain(name=name, description=description, **kwargs)
        self.domains[name] = domain
        self._domain_vectors[name] = []
        self._save_state()
        self.logger.info(f"RAG: Created domain '{name}'")
        return domain

    def get_domain(self, name: str) -> Optional[KnowledgeDomain]:
        """Get domain by name."""
        return self.domains.get(name)

    def list_domains(self) -> List[KnowledgeDomain]:
        """List all domains."""
        return list(self.domains.values())

    def _chunk_text(self, text: str, chunk_size: int = 512, overlap: int = 50) -> List[str]:
        """Split text into overlapping chunks."""
        if len(text) <= chunk_size:
            return [text]

        chunks = []
        start = 0
        while start < len(text):
            end = start + chunk_size
            chunk = text[start:end]

            # Try to break at sentence boundary
            if end < len(text):
                last_period = chunk.rfind('. ')
                if last_period > chunk_size // 2:
                    chunk = chunk[:last_period + 1]
                    end = start + last_period + 1

            chunks.append(chunk.strip())
            start = end - overlap

        return chunks

    def _get_embedding(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
        """Get embedding for text using agent's embedding service."""
        if hasattr(self.agent, 'embedding_service') and self.agent.embedding_service:
            try:
                return self.agent.embedding_service.embed(text)
            except Exception:
                pass

        # Fallback: simple hash-based pseudo-embedding (for testing)
        import hashlib
        hash_val = hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest()
        return [float(int(hash_val[i:i+2], 16)) / 255.0 for i in range(0, 128, 2)][:64]

    def _cosine_similarity(self, vec1: List[float], vec2: List[float]) -> float:
        """Compute cosine similarity between two vectors."""
        if len(vec1) != len(vec2):
            return 0.0

        dot_product = sum(a * b for a, b in zip(vec1, vec2))
        norm1 = sum(a * a for a in vec1) ** 0.5
        norm2 = sum(b * b for b in vec2) ** 0.5

        if norm1 == 0 or norm2 == 0:
            return 0.0

        return dot_product / (norm1 * norm2)

    def ingest_document(
        self,
        content: str,
        domain: str = "general",
        title: str = "",
        source: str = "manual",
        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> RAGDocument:
        """
        Ingest a document into the RAG system.

        # BEGIN META: RAGDomainManager.ingest_document
        # Date: 2026-01-18
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Index document for RAG retrieval
        # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001"]
        # Tags: ["ingest", "document", "index"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        if domain not in self.domains:
            self.create_domain(domain)

        domain_config = self.domains[domain]

        # Generate document ID
        doc_id = f"doc_{uuid4().hex[:12]}"

        # Chunk the document
        chunks = self._chunk_text(content, domain_config.chunk_size, domain_config.chunk_overlap)

        # Generate embeddings for each chunk
        chunk_data = []
        embeddings = []
        for i, chunk_text in enumerate(chunks):
            embedding = self._get_embedding(chunk_text)
            embeddings.append(embedding)
            chunk_data.append({
                "chunk_id": f"{doc_id}_chunk_{i}",
                "text": chunk_text,
                "position": i,
                "doc_id": doc_id,
            })

            # Add to domain vector index
            self._domain_vectors[domain].append((
                f"{doc_id}_chunk_{i}",
                embedding,
                {"text": chunk_text, "doc_id": doc_id, "position": i, "title": title}
            ))

        # Create document record
        doc = RAGDocument(
            id=doc_id,
            domain=domain,
            title=title or f"Document {doc_id}",
            content=content,
            source=source,
            chunks=chunk_data,
            embeddings=embeddings,
            metadata=metadata or {},
        )

        self.documents[doc_id] = doc

        # Update domain stats
        self.domains[domain].document_count += 1
        self.domains[domain].total_chunks += len(chunks)
        self._save_state()

        self.logger.info(f"RAG: Ingested document '{doc.title}' with {len(chunks)} chunks into '{domain}'")
        return doc

    def retrieve(
        self,
        query: str,
        domain: Optional[str] = None,
        max_results: Optional[int] = None,
        relevance_threshold: Optional[float] = None,
    ) -> RAGRetrievalResult:
        """
        Retrieve relevant context for a query.

        # BEGIN META: RAGDomainManager.retrieve
        # Date: 2026-01-18
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Retrieve relevant chunks for query
        # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001"]
        # Tags: ["retrieve", "search", "query"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        start_time = time.time()

        # Get search configuration
        if domain and domain in self.domains:
            domain_config = self.domains[domain]
            max_results = max_results or domain_config.max_results
            relevance_threshold = relevance_threshold or domain_config.relevance_threshold
            search_domains = [domain]
        else:
            max_results = max_results or 5
            relevance_threshold = relevance_threshold or 0.7
            search_domains = list(self.domains.keys())

        # Get query embedding
        query_embedding = self._get_embedding(query)

        # Search across domains
        all_results = []
        total_searched = 0

        for search_domain in search_domains:
            vectors = self._domain_vectors.get(search_domain, [])
            total_searched += len(vectors)

            for chunk_id, embedding, metadata in vectors:
                similarity = self._cosine_similarity(query_embedding, embedding)
                if similarity >= relevance_threshold:
                    all_results.append({
                        "chunk_id": chunk_id,
                        "domain": search_domain,
                        "similarity": similarity,
                        "text": metadata.get("text", ""),
                        "doc_id": metadata.get("doc_id"),
                        "title": metadata.get("title", ""),
                    })

        # Sort by similarity and take top results
        all_results.sort(key=lambda x: x["similarity"], reverse=True)
        top_results = all_results[:max_results]

        # Build context string
        context_parts = []
        for r in top_results:
            context_parts.append(f"[{r['domain']}] {r['text']}")

        context = "\n\n".join(context_parts) if context_parts else ""

        latency_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000
        self._query_count += 1
        self._retrieval_latency_sum += latency_ms

        return RAGRetrievalResult(
            query=query,
            domain=domain,
            chunks=top_results,
            relevance_scores=[r["similarity"] for r in top_results],
            context=context,
            latency_ms=latency_ms,
            total_searched=total_searched,
        )

    def augment_prompt(self, user_query: str, system_prompt: str = "", domain: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
        """
        Augment a prompt with retrieved context (RAG).

        # BEGIN META: RAGDomainManager.augment_prompt
        # Date: 2026-01-18
        # Maintainer: CNWB
        # Purpose: Add RAG context to prompt for enhanced responses
        # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001"]
        # Tags: ["augment", "prompt", "context"]
        # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
        # END META
        """
        retrieval = self.retrieve(user_query, domain=domain)

        if not retrieval.context:
            return user_query

        augmented = f"""CONTEXT (from knowledge base):
{retrieval.context}

USER QUERY:
{user_query}"""

        if system_prompt:
            augmented = f"{system_prompt}\n\n{augmented}"

        return augmented

    def get_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get RAG system statistics."""
        return {
            "domains": len(self.domains),
            "documents": len(self.documents),
            "total_chunks": sum(d.total_chunks for d in self.domains.values()),
            "query_count": self._query_count,
            "avg_retrieval_latency_ms": (
                self._retrieval_latency_sum / self._query_count if self._query_count > 0 else 0
            ),
            "domain_stats": {
                name: {"documents": d.document_count, "chunks": d.total_chunks}
                for name, d in self.domains.items()
            },
        }


def init_rag_system(agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro") -> RAGDomainManager:
    """
    Initialize RAG system for agent.

    # BEGIN META: init_rag_system
    # Date: 2026-01-18
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Create and wire RAG domain manager to agent
    # SRS: ["SRS-RAG-001"]
    # Tags: ["init", "rag", "setup"]
    # CAPS: CAPSTONE-A
    # END META
    """
    rag = RAGDomainManager(agent, logger=agent.logger if hasattr(agent, 'logger') else None)
    agent.rag = rag
    agent.rag_manager = rag  # Alias

    # Auto-ingest any cold storage data if available
    if hasattr(agent, '_cold_storage') and agent._cold_storage:
        try:
            # Ingest recent artifacts as context
            artifacts = agent._cold_storage.list_artifacts(artifact_type="memory", limit=50)
            for artifact in artifacts[:10]:  # Limit initial ingest
                if hasattr(artifact, 'data') and artifact.data:
                    content = str(artifact.data.get('content', ''))
                    if content and len(content) > 20:
                        rag.ingest_document(
                            content=content,
                            domain="memory",
                            title=f"Memory {artifact.artifact_id[:8]}",
                            source="cold_storage",
                        )
        except Exception:
            pass  # Non-critical

    agent.logger.debug(f"RAG: Initialized with {len(rag.domains)} domains") if hasattr(agent, 'logger') else None
    return rag


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# END SECTION 117.5: RAG SYSTEM WITH DOMAIN MANAGEMENT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SECTION 118: DIAGNOSTIC TEST SUITE - ULMS, TASKIR, COLLEGE INTEGRATION
# Self-assessment with Datadog, PostHog, Supabase verification and cold storage aggregation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# --- 118.1: Governance Tiers (ULMS Pattern) ---
class AGSTier(Enum):
    """Governance severity tiers from ULMS pattern."""
    T0_CRITICAL = "T0_CRITICAL"  # Critical system operations
    T1_HIGH = "T1_HIGH"          # High priority actions
    T2_NORMAL = "T2_NORMAL"      # Normal operations
    T3_LOW = "T3_LOW"            # Low priority/info

class EmotionalTone(Enum):
    """Emotional tone detection from ULMS pattern."""
    NEUTRAL = "neutral"
    POSITIVE = "positive"
    NEGATIVE = "negative"
    URGENT = "urgent"
    CURIOUS = "curious"
    FRUSTRATED = "frustrated"

# --- 118.2: REFLEX_ACTION_MAP (College System Pattern) ---
DIAGNOSTIC_REFLEX_MAP: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {
    "DIGEST_WORDS": {"action": "professor_text_analysis", "caps": "B_90", "tier": "T2_NORMAL"},
    "RANK_MEMORY": {"action": "faiss_semantic_search", "caps": "A_95", "tier": "T1_HIGH"},
    "VECTOR_STORE": {"action": "embed_and_persist", "caps": "A_95", "tier": "T1_HIGH"},
    "REFLEX_TRIGGER": {"action": "auto_response_chain", "caps": "B_85", "tier": "T2_NORMAL"},
    "GOVERNANCE_CHECK": {"action": "council_validation", "caps": "A_99", "tier": "T0_CRITICAL"},
    "XP_AWARD": {"action": "brotherhood_progression", "caps": "B_90", "tier": "T2_NORMAL"},
    "TELEMETRY_EMIT": {"action": "platform_tracking", "caps": "B_85", "tier": "T2_NORMAL"},
    "COLD_STORE": {"action": "persist_to_sanctum", "caps": "A_95", "tier": "T1_HIGH"},
    "SELF_ASSESS": {"action": "diagnostic_introspection", "caps": "A_99", "tier": "T0_CRITICAL"},
    "CONNECTIVITY_TEST": {"action": "platform_health_check", "caps": "A_95", "tier": "T1_HIGH"},
}

# --- 118.3: ENUM_TO_REFLEX_MAP (College System Pattern) ---
ENUM_TO_DIAGNOSTIC_REFLEX: Dict[str, List[str]] = {
    "MEMORY": ["DIGEST_WORDS", "RANK_MEMORY", "VECTOR_STORE"],
    "GOVERNANCE": ["GOVERNANCE_CHECK", "REFLEX_TRIGGER"],
    "PROGRESSION": ["XP_AWARD", "TELEMETRY_EMIT"],
    "PERSISTENCE": ["COLD_STORE", "VECTOR_STORE"],
    "DIAGNOSTICS": ["SELF_ASSESS", "CONNECTIVITY_TEST"],
}

# --- 118.4: TaskIR Schema for Diagnostic Results ---
@dataclass
class DiagnosticTaskIR:
    """
    TaskIR for diagnostic test results (from taskir_blueprint pattern).

    # BEGIN META: DiagnosticTaskIR
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Intermediate representation for diagnostic results
    # SRS: ["SRS-TASKIR-001", "SRS-DIAGNOSTIC-001"]
    # Pattern: TaskIR Blueprint v2.0
    # END META
    """
    task_id: str
    source: Dict[str, Any]  # Where the test originated
    analysis: Dict[str, Any]  # Test findings
    enums: Dict[str, List[str]]  # Enum tags
    reflex: Dict[str, Any]  # Suggested reflex triggers
    actions: List[Dict[str, str]]  # Recommended actions
    evidence: Dict[str, Any]  # Supporting evidence
    governance: Dict[str, Any]  # Governance tier and policy
    confidence: float
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())

    def root_hash(self) -> str:
        """Generate SHA-256 hash for governance ledger."""
        content = json.dumps({
            "task_id": self.task_id,
            "source": self.source,
            "analysis": self.analysis,
            "confidence": self.confidence,
            "timestamp": self.timestamp
        }, sort_keys=True)
        return hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()

# --- 118.5: ULMS Memory Bridge ---
@dataclass
class ULMSMemoryEntry:
    """
    ULMS-style memory entry with governance tier.

    # BEGIN META: ULMSMemoryEntry
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Memory entry with ULMS governance integration
    # SRS: ["SRS-ULMS-001", "SRS-MEMORY-001"]
    # Pattern: ULMS v1.0 Integration
    # END META
    """
    key: str
    value: Any
    mca_signal_id: str
    emotional_tone: EmotionalTone
    governance_tier: AGSTier
    reflex_triggered: Optional[str]
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())
    fingerprint: str = field(default_factory=lambda: uuid4().hex[:16])

class ULMSMemoryBridge:
    """
    Bridge to ULMS (Unified Lingo Memory System) pattern.

    # BEGIN META: ULMSMemoryBridge
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Connect agent memory to ULMS webhook/reflex pattern
    # SRS: ["SRS-ULMS-001", "SRS-MEMORY-002"]
    # Pattern: ULMS v1.0 + MCA Webhook Integration
    # END META
    """

    def __init__(self, agent: "NexusTamagotchiAgentPro"):
        self.agent = agent
        self.memory_log: List[ULMSMemoryEntry] = []
        self.reflex_history: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    def detect_emotional_tone(self, text: str) -> EmotionalTone:
        """Detect emotional tone from text (simple pattern matching)."""
        text_lower = text.lower()
        if any(w in text_lower for w in ["urgent", "critical", "emergency", "asap"]):
            return EmotionalTone.URGENT
        if any(w in text_lower for w in ["thanks", "great", "awesome", "perfect"]):
            return EmotionalTone.POSITIVE
        if any(w in text_lower for w in ["error", "fail", "wrong", "bad"]):
            return EmotionalTone.NEGATIVE
        if any(w in text_lower for w in ["why", "how", "what", "?"]):
            return EmotionalTone.CURIOUS
        if any(w in text_lower for w in ["annoying", "stuck", "hate"]):
            return EmotionalTone.FRUSTRATED
        return EmotionalTone.NEUTRAL

    def determine_governance_tier(self, content: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> AGSTier:
        """Determine governance tier based on content and context."""
        # Check for critical keywords
        if any(w in content.lower() for w in ["delete", "production", "deploy", "critical"]):
            return AGSTier.T0_CRITICAL
        if any(w in content.lower() for w in ["security", "auth", "password", "api_key"]):
            return AGSTier.T1_HIGH
        if context.get("requires_approval"):
            return AGSTier.T1_HIGH
        return AGSTier.T2_NORMAL

    def remember(
        self,
        key: str,
        value: Any,
        mca_signal_id: str,
        context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
    ) -> ULMSMemoryEntry:
        """
        Store a memory with ULMS metadata.

        # BEGIN META: ULMSMemoryBridge.remember
        # Pattern: ulms.remember(key, value, mca_signal_id, emotional_tone, governance_tier, reflex_triggered)
        # END META
        """
        context = context or {}
        content_str = str(value) if not isinstance(value, str) else value

        emotional_tone = self.detect_emotional_tone(content_str)
        governance_tier = self.determine_governance_tier(content_str, context)

        # Check if reflex should be triggered
        reflex_triggered = self._check_reflex_trigger(key, content_str, governance_tier)

        entry = ULMSMemoryEntry(
            key=key,
            value=value,
            mca_signal_id=mca_signal_id,
            emotional_tone=emotional_tone,
            governance_tier=governance_tier,
            reflex_triggered=reflex_triggered
        )

        self.memory_log.append(entry)

        # If reflex triggered, log it
        if reflex_triggered:
            self.reflex_history.append({
                "reflex": reflex_triggered,
                "trigger_key": key,
                "tier": governance_tier.value,
                "timestamp": entry.timestamp
            })

        return entry

    def _check_reflex_trigger(self, key: str, content: str, tier: AGSTier) -> Optional[str]:
        """Check if any reflex should be triggered."""
        # Map key patterns to reflexes
        key_reflex_map = {
            "vector": "VECTOR_STORE",
            "memory": "RANK_MEMORY",
            "text": "DIGEST_WORDS",
            "governance": "GOVERNANCE_CHECK",
            "xp": "XP_AWARD",
        }
        for pattern, reflex in key_reflex_map.items():
            if pattern in key.lower():
                return reflex

        # Tier-based reflexes
        if tier == AGSTier.T0_CRITICAL:
            return "GOVERNANCE_CHECK"

        return None

    def get_memory_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get statistics about ULMS memory usage."""
        tone_counts = {}
        tier_counts = {}
        for entry in self.memory_log:
            tone_counts[entry.emotional_tone.value] = tone_counts.get(entry.emotional_tone.value, 0) + 1
            tier_counts[entry.governance_tier.value] = tier_counts.get(entry.governance_tier.value, 0) + 1

        return {
            "total_entries": len(self.memory_log),
            "tone_distribution": tone_counts,
            "tier_distribution": tier_counts,
            "reflexes_triggered": len(self.reflex_history),
            "unique_reflexes": list(set(r["reflex"] for r in self.reflex_history))
        }

# --- 118.6: Diagnostic Test Result ---
@dataclass
class DiagnosticTestResult:
    """Result of a single diagnostic test."""
    test_name: str
    component: str
    status: str  # PASS, FAIL, WARN, SKIP
    latency_ms: float
    message: str
    caps_rating: str
    reflex_triggered: Optional[str]
    details: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    timestamp: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())

# --- 118.7: Platform Connectivity Verifier ---
class PlatformConnectivityVerifier:
    """
    Verify connectivity to Datadog, PostHog, and Supabase.

    # BEGIN META: PlatformConnectivityVerifier
    # Date: 2026-01-15
    # Maintainer: CNWB
    # Purpose: Verify platform connectivity for diagnostic suite
    # SRS: ["SRS-CONNECTIVITY-001", "SRS-DIAGNOSTIC-001"]
    # Pattern: OAD Integration Check
    # END META
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.results: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}

    def verify_datadog(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Verify Datadog connectivity."""
        result = {
            "platform": "datadog",
            "status": "UNKNOWN",
            "latency_ms": 0.0,
            "message": "",
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
        }

        start_time = time.time()
        try:
            dd_api_key = os.environ.get("DD_API_KEY") or os.environ.get("DATADOG_API_KEY")
            if not dd_api_key:
                result["status"] = "WARN"
                result["message"] = "DD_API_KEY not configured"
            else:
                # Test API connectivity with a lightweight call
                try:
                    from datadog import initialize, api
                    initialize(api_key=dd_api_key)
                    # Try to validate the key
                    api.Metric.send(metrics=[{
                        "metric": "citadel.nexus.agent.diagnostic.ping",
                        "points": [(time.time(), 1)],
                        "type": "gauge",
                        "tags": ["diagnostic:connectivity_test"]
                    }])
                    result["status"] = "PASS"
                    result["message"] = "Datadog API connected and metric sent"
                except ImportError:
                    result["status"] = "WARN"
                    result["message"] = "datadog library not installed"
                except Exception as e:
                    result["status"] = "FAIL"
                    result["message"] = f"Datadog API error: {str(e)[:100]}"
        except Exception as e:
            result["status"] = "FAIL"
            result["message"] = f"Configuration error: {str(e)[:100]}"

        result["latency_ms"] = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000
        self.results["datadog"] = result
        return result

    def verify_posthog(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Verify PostHog connectivity."""
        result = {
            "platform": "posthog",
            "status": "UNKNOWN",
            "latency_ms": 0.0,
            "message": "",
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
        }

        start_time = time.time()
        try:
            posthog_key = os.environ.get("POSTHOG_API_KEY")
            posthog_host = os.environ.get("POSTHOG_HOST", "https://app.posthog.com")

            if not posthog_key:
                result["status"] = "WARN"
                result["message"] = "POSTHOG_API_KEY not configured"
            else:
                try:
                    from posthog import Posthog
                    ph = Posthog(project_api_key=posthog_key, host=posthog_host)
                    ph.capture(
                        distinct_id="diagnostic_test",
                        event="diagnostic_connectivity_test",
                        properties={"test_type": "ping"}
                    )
                    ph.flush()
                    result["status"] = "PASS"
                    result["message"] = "PostHog connected and event captured"
                except ImportError:
                    result["status"] = "WARN"
                    result["message"] = "posthog library not installed"
                except Exception as e:
                    result["status"] = "FAIL"
                    result["message"] = f"PostHog API error: {str(e)[:100]}"
        except Exception as e:
            result["status"] = "FAIL"
            result["message"] = f"Configuration error: {str(e)[:100]}"

        result["latency_ms"] = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000
        self.results["posthog"] = result
        return result

    def verify_supabase(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Verify Supabase connectivity."""
        result = {
            "platform": "supabase",
            "status": "UNKNOWN",
            "latency_ms": 0.0,
            "message": "",
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
        }

        start_time = time.time()
        try:
            supabase_url = os.environ.get("SUPABASE_URL")
            supabase_key = os.environ.get("SUPABASE_KEY") or os.environ.get("SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY")

            if not supabase_url or not supabase_key:
                result["status"] = "WARN"
                result["message"] = "SUPABASE_URL or SUPABASE_KEY not configured"
            else:
                try:
                    from supabase import create_client
                    client = create_client(supabase_url, supabase_key)
                    # Try a simple query
                    response = client.table("health_check").select("*").limit(1).execute()
                    result["status"] = "PASS"
                    result["message"] = "Supabase connected successfully"
                except ImportError:
                    result["status"] = "WARN"
                    result["message"] = "supabase library not installed"
                except Exception as e:
                    # Even if table doesn't exist, connection might work
                    if "does not exist" in str(e).lower() or "relation" in str(e).lower():
                        result["status"] = "PASS"
                        result["message"] = "Supabase connected (health_check table not found)"
                    else:
                        result["status"] = "FAIL"
                        result["message"] = f"Supabase error: {str(e)[:100]}"
        except Exception as e:
            result["status"] = "FAIL"
            result["message"] = f"Configuration error: {str(e)[:100]}"

        result["latency_ms"] = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000
        self.results["supabase"] = result
        return result

    def verify_all(self) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Verify all platform connections."""
        self.verify_datadog()
        self.verify_posthog()
        self.verify_supabase()
        return self.results

    def get_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get connectivity summary."""
        if not self.results:
            self.verify_all()

        passed = sum(1 for r in self.results.values() if r["status"] == "PASS")
        warned = sum(1 for r in self.results.values() if r["status"] == "WARN")
        failed = sum(1 for r in self.results.values() if r["status"] == "FAIL")

        return {
            "total_platforms": len(self.results),
            "passed": passed,
            "warned": warned,
            "failed": failed,
            "overall_status": "PASS" if failed == 0 and warned == 0 else ("WARN" if failed == 0 else "FAIL"),
            "avg_latency_ms": sum(r["latency_ms"] for r in self.results.values()) / max(len(self.results), 1),
